Name: AK1A PacketCluster
Rig Control Backend: Hardware TNC (KISS / terminal-host mode) on each RF port; no HF-rig CAT
How to Set CAT: No transceiver CAT. As a DX-cluster node, each RF link is a radio+TNC set in the node config (SET/PORT): assign the serial COM port and baud for the hardware TNC. Most modern use is telnet with no radio.
Audio Device Select: n/a — a hardware TNC provides the AFSK modem; the PC has no sound-card role.
PTT Config: Handled entirely by the TNC keying the attached FM radio; nothing in the app.
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a — FM packet is constant-envelope; set the radio's normal FM power.
Companion Needed: Hardware packet TNC per RF port (or none if running telnet-only).
Notes: Classic DOS DX PacketCluster by AK1A (Dick Newell). Radio is only involved on legacy RF cluster links; today it is almost purely Internet/telnet.
Source: AK1A PacketCluster / DX-cluster node documentation (packet-radio.net historical references)
Source Note: Architecture verified against packet/cluster-node documentation; no located AK1A manual online, but the TNC-based, no-rig-CAT model is definitional for a PacketCluster node.

Name: APRS Messenger
Rig Control Backend: Sound-card modem (built-in PSK/MFSK/GMSK, or via UZ7HO soundmodem); no HF-rig CAT
How to Set CAT: No rig CAT. It sends APRS as sound-card tones (PSK63/MFSK/GMSK) or hands the AX.25 mode to an external UZ7HO-style soundmodem, plus APRS-IS. Set the sound device and PTT in its options, not a Hamlib rig model.
Audio Device Select: Chosen in the program's sound-card settings (or in the UZ7HO soundmodem it shares the transmitter with).
PTT Config: PTT via a serial COM port (RTS/DTR) or VOX; the AX.25 mode keys the PTT port for a fixed period so a shared soundmodem can use the same transmitter.
ALC/Power Guidance: For HF PSK/MFSK/GMSK keep audio drive low so ALC barely moves (rig in DATA/USB); GMSK/FM is constant-envelope with no ALC concern.
Companion Needed: Optional AGWPE/UZ7HO soundmodem; a PTT interface or VOX.
Notes: Windows APRS text-messaging app that can beacon over RF sound-card modes or over APRS-IS.

Name: aprs.fi (mobile app)
Rig Control Backend: Bluetooth-LE KISS TNC (e.g. Mobilinkd TNC3); no CAT
How to Set CAT: No rig CAT. Pair a BLE KISS TNC (Mobilinkd TNC3 or compatible) as the radio interface; the TNC + attached HT do the RF. Primary use is viewing APRS-IS on the map.
Audio Device Select: n/a — the external BLE TNC contains the modem; phone audio is not used for RF.
PTT Config: Handled by the external TNC keying the HT via its cable; nothing in the app.
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a — VHF FM APRS, no ALC.
Companion Needed: BLE KISS TNC (Mobilinkd TNC2/TNC3/NW Digital) + an HT.
Notes: iOS/Android companion to the aprs.fi site; RF TX/RX is optional and only via a paired BLE TNC.
Source: aprs.fi blog — iPhone app / BLE KISS TNC support (OH7LZB)

Name: aprsd
Rig Control Backend: KISS TNC (serial or TCP) for the RF/iGate port; no rig CAT
How to Set CAT: No transceiver CAT. aprsd is an APRS-IS server/iGate daemon; when gating to RF it opens a KISS/TNC port (serial device+baud, or KISS-over-TCP to a soundmodem) defined in its config, not a Hamlib rig.
Audio Device Select: n/a with a hardware TNC; if a soundmodem is used, the audio device is set in that soundmodem (Direwolf/UZ7HO), not aprsd.
PTT Config: Handled by the TNC/soundmodem when gating to RF.
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a — VHF FM packet, no ALC.
Companion Needed: Hardware KISS TNC or a soundmodem (Direwolf) for the RF side.
Notes: Runs headless as an iGate/messaging daemon; mostly APRS-IS with an optional RF port.
Source Note: Two programs share the name (WA4DSY's Linux aprsd APRS-IS server and the modern Python 'aprsd'); both fit the KISS-TNC, no-rig-CAT description.

Name: APRSdos
Rig Control Backend: Serial hardware TNC (KISS or TNC2 command mode); no rig CAT
How to Set CAT: No rig CAT. The original APRS (WB4APR): set the COM port and baud for a hardware TNC in setup; it also reads a serial NMEA GPS on another port. No Hamlib/rig model.
Audio Device Select: n/a — hardware TNC provides the modem.
PTT Config: Handled by the TNC keying the FM radio.
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a — VHF FM packet, no ALC.
Companion Needed: Hardware TNC (KISS/TNC2); optional serial GPS.
Notes: Bob Bruninga WB4APR's original DOS APRS; purely serial-TNC + GPS, no sound-card or CAT.

Name: APRSdroid
Rig Control Backend: Built-in AFSK sound-card TNC, or external KISS TNC (Bluetooth SSP / USB), or TCP (Direwolf); no rig CAT
How to Set CAT: No rig CAT. In Connection Preferences pick the Connection Protocol/Type: AFSK (phone audio to the radio), TNC(KISS) over Bluetooth SSP / USB serial, or TCP (Direwolf/network). No rig model is set.
Audio Device Select: For AFSK the phone's own audio in/out is used (wired interface or acoustic coupling); no explicit device picker.
PTT Config: VOX on the radio, or a cable/interface keyed by the phone; external TNCs key their own radio.
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a — VHF FM APRS. Keep audio drive below overdeviation on FM.
Companion Needed: Optional Mobilinkd/Bluetooth KISS TNC or Direwolf; a wired audio/PTT interface for AFSK.
Notes: Android APRS client; simplest mode is acoustic/AFSK to an HT with VOX.

Name: APRSISCE/32 (APRSIS32)
Rig Control Backend: KISS/AGW TNC over serial or TCP; sound-card via AGW; no rig CAT
How to Set CAT: No rig CAT. Configure > Ports adds a port: Serial KISS (COM/baud), AGWPE (TCP to a soundmodem), or a TCP KISS TNC (host:port); add a GPS (NMEA) port too.
Audio Device Select: n/a in the app; with AGW, the sound device is chosen in the AGWPE/UZ7HO soundmodem it connects to.
PTT Config: Hardware TNC keys the radio; a soundmodem handles PTT (RTS/CAT/VOX) on its side.
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a — VHF FM APRS, no ALC.
Companion Needed: AGWPE/UZ7HO soundmodem or a hardware/TCP KISS TNC.
Notes: Popular Windows/CE APRS client (APRSIS32); connects to any KISS/AGW TNC plus APRS-IS.

Name: APRSpoint
Rig Control Backend: Serial KISS/TNC; no rig CAT
How to Set CAT: No rig CAT. Set the serial COM port/baud for a KISS or command-mode TNC in its TNC settings; also APRS-IS. No Hamlib rig model.
Audio Device Select: n/a — hardware TNC provides the modem.
PTT Config: Handled by the TNC keying the radio.
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a — VHF FM APRS, no ALC.
Companion Needed: Hardware KISS/TNC2 TNC.
Notes: APRS mapping client (built on Microsoft MapPoint); connects to a serial TNC to view/transmit.
Source Note: No official manual located online; TNC-based, no-rig-CAT architecture is definitional for this APRS mapping client and consistent with its documentation.

Name: APRSPro
Rig Control Backend: External TNC via audio cable or Bluetooth-LE (e.g. Mobilinkd); no rig CAT
How to Set CAT: No rig CAT. RF is via an external TNC connected by audio cable or BLE; select it in the app's connection settings. Also uses APRS-IS.
Audio Device Select: n/a in-app for a BLE TNC; an audio-cabled TNC handles the modem.
PTT Config: Handled by the external TNC keying the radio.
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a — VHF FM APRS, no ALC.
Companion Needed: BLE/audio KISS TNC + a radio.
Notes: iOS APRS app; RF operation requires an external TNC, otherwise APRS-IS only.
Source Note: No standalone official manual located; description matches this iOS APRS client's known BLE/audio-TNC architecture.

Name: aprx
Rig Control Backend: KISS TNC over serial or TCP (Direwolf/soundmodem); no rig CAT
How to Set CAT: No rig CAT. Edit aprx.conf: each <interface> is a serial KISS device (tty + baud, e.g. /dev/ttyUSB1 19200 8n1 KISS) or a TCP KISS (host:port to Direwolf/UZ7HO). No rig model.
Audio Device Select: n/a in aprx; the sound device is set inside the Direwolf/soundmodem it links to.
PTT Config: Handled by the TNC/soundmodem (Direwolf PTT: GPIO/RTS/CM108).
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a — VHF FM packet, no ALC.
Companion Needed: Hardware KISS TNC or Direwolf soundmodem.
Notes: Lightweight Linux APRS iGate/digipeater; config-file driven, headless.

Name: BPQ32 / LinBPQ
Rig Control Backend: KISS/AGW/soundmodem TNCs and PACTOR modems per PORT; optional RIGCONTROL for freq/PTT; no operating HF CAT
How to Set CAT: No general HF-rig CAT for QSOs; BPQ32.cfg PORT stanzas define each channel (KISS serial/TCP, AGW, hardware TNC, PACTOR/soundmodem) with device/host, baud and modem params. RIGCONTROL lines can drive a radio's frequency/PTT for multi-freq ports.
Audio Device Select: n/a for hardware TNC/PACTOR; the sound device is set in the linked soundmodem (Direwolf/UZ7HO).
PTT Config: Handled by each TNC/modem; RIGCONTROL can add PTT/freq control to a port.
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a for FM packet; for PACTOR/HF keep drive so ALC barely moves.
Companion Needed: KISS/soundmodem TNC and/or a PACTOR modem (SCS).
Notes: G8BPQ node/switch software; ties multiple radios into an AX.25/IP network node.

Name: DIGI_NED
Rig Control Backend: AGWPE or KISS TNC; no rig CAT
How to Set CAT: No rig CAT. Configure the AGWPE connection or a KISS serial TNC in its .ini files (device/host, baud/port). No rig model.
Audio Device Select: n/a in DIGI_NED; sound device is set in AGWPE/soundmodem.
PTT Config: Handled by the TNC/soundmodem.
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a — VHF FM APRS, no ALC.
Companion Needed: AGWPE soundmodem or a hardware KISS TNC.
Notes: APRS digipeater engine; runs headless keying via its TNC.

Name: DIXPRS
Rig Control Backend: KISS serial TNC or sound-card modem; no rig CAT
How to Set CAT: No rig CAT. Configure a serial KISS TNC (device/baud) or a soundcard modem in its config. No rig model.
Audio Device Select: Set in the soundcard modem it uses (or n/a with a hardware TNC).
PTT Config: Handled by the TNC/soundmodem.
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a — VHF FM APRS, no ALC.
Companion Needed: KISS TNC or a soundcard modem.
Notes: Python APRS iGate/digipeater (DIXPRS); typically headless.

Name: DXSpider / AR-Cluster / CC Cluster
Rig Control Backend: KISS/AGW TNC on RF ports; no rig CAT (mostly telnet)
How to Set CAT: No transceiver CAT. These are DX-cluster node servers; an RF port is a KISS/AGW TNC defined in the node config (serial device or AGW host:port). Nearly all use is telnet/Internet with no radio.
Audio Device Select: n/a — hardware TNC provides the modem (or a soundmodem set on its own).
PTT Config: Handled by the TNC on the RF port.
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a — VHF FM packet, no ALC.
Companion Needed: KISS/AGW TNC only if running an RF port; otherwise none.
Notes: DX cluster node software (DXSpider on Linux, AR-Cluster/CC Cluster on Windows); radio is a legacy option.

Name: EasyTerm
Rig Control Backend: AGWPE / UZ7HO soundmodem or AGW-capable TNC; no rig CAT
How to Set CAT: No rig CAT. Point EasyTerm at an AGW host:port (UZ7HO soundmodem or AGWPE, or a hardware AGW TNC). No rig model is set.
Audio Device Select: Chosen inside the UZ7HO/AGWPE soundmodem, not EasyTerm.
PTT Config: Handled by the soundmodem/TNC (UZ7HO PTT: COM RTS/DTR or VOX).
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a — VHF FM packet, no ALC.
Companion Needed: UZ7HO SoundModem or AGWPE (or an AGW-capable hardware TNC).
Notes: Packet terminal front-end that slaves to an AGW-compatible modem.

Name: F6FBB (FBB)
Rig Control Backend: Hardware TNC (KISS/host mode, incl. KAM/TF/TFPCX) or AGWPE; no rig CAT
How to Set CAT: No rig CAT. FBB's PORT.SYS defines each channel: a hardware TNC in KISS or host mode on a COM port (device/baud), or TFPCX/TFWIN/AGWPE link. No rig model.
Audio Device Select: n/a for hardware TNC; sound device set in AGWPE if used.
PTT Config: Handled by the TNC.
Companion Needed: Hardware TNC or AGWPE soundmodem.
Notes: F6FBB packet BBS (DOS/Windows); radio is via its TNC ports.

Name: FBB / LinFBB
Rig Control Backend: Hardware or sound-modem KISS TNC via Linux AX.25 / KISS ports; no rig CAT
How to Set CAT: No rig CAT. LinFBB attaches to Linux AX.25 kernel/KISS ports; configure the AX.25 interface (kissattach a serial TNC or bind a soundmodem) and reference it in the FBB port config. No rig model.
Audio Device Select: n/a in FBB; a soundmodem (Direwolf) selects the sound device.
PTT Config: Handled by the TNC/soundmodem.
Companion Needed: KISS TNC or soundmodem (Direwolf) bound to the kernel AX.25 stack.
Notes: Linux port of FBB BBS; rides on the Linux AX.25 stack.

Name: GP (GraphicPacket)
Rig Control Backend: Baycom modem / sound-card via TFPCX or FlexNet; no rig CAT
How to Set CAT: No rig CAT. GP talks to a TFPCX/FlexNet TSR that drives a Baycom (serial) modem or a sound-card modem; configure the modem/port in TFPCX, not in GP. No rig model.
Audio Device Select: Handled by the underlying TFPCX/sound-card driver, not in GP.
PTT Config: Via the modem's port line (Baycom PTT on a serial pin) keyed by TFPCX.
Companion Needed: TFPCX/FlexNet + a Baycom or sound-card modem.
Notes: DOS graphical packet terminal; sits on top of a TFPCX host-mode modem driver.

Name: iBCNU
Rig Control Backend: External TNC for RF; no rig CAT
How to Set CAT: No rig CAT. RF is via an external TNC selected in the app; primary connection is APRS-IS over the network. No rig model.
Audio Device Select: n/a — external TNC handles the modem.
PTT Config: Handled by the external TNC keying the radio.
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a — VHF FM APRS, no ALC.
Companion Needed: External (BLE/serial) KISS TNC for RF.
Notes: iOS APRS tracker; RF optional, otherwise Internet APRS-IS.
Source Note: No standalone official manual located; matches this iOS APRS client's known external-TNC + APRS-IS architecture.

Name: javAPRS
Rig Control Backend: Serial TNC data stream or APRS-IS feed; no rig CAT
How to Set CAT: No rig CAT. A Java applet/app that connects to a live TNC data stream (a server opens the TNC serial port) or an APRS-IS feed for display; no Hamlib/rig model.
Audio Device Select: n/a — hardware TNC provides the modem.
PTT Config: Handled by the TNC (display/RX-focused; typically no TX).
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a — VHF FM APRS, no ALC.
Companion Needed: Hardware TNC for RF, otherwise none (APRS-IS).
Notes: Steve Dimse K4HG's Java APRS applet/server; mainly a map display fed by APRS-IS or a TNC.

Name: JNOS
Rig Control Backend: KISS/AXUDP/AGW TNC or soundmodem (Direwolf); optional rig freq control on HF ports; no operating CAT
How to Set CAT: No transceiver CAT for casual ops. autoexec.nos 'attach' lines define each interface: KISS on a serial port, AXUDP/AGW, or a soundmodem (device/baud/host). HF ports can add a rig line for scanning.
Audio Device Select: n/a in JNOS; sound device set in Direwolf/soundmodem.
PTT Config: Handled by the TNC/soundmodem.
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a for FM; on HF PACTOR/packet keep drive so ALC barely moves.
Companion Needed: KISS/AGW TNC or Direwolf soundmodem.
Notes: KA9Q NOS derivative (TCP/IP + BBS over AX.25); headless server.

Name: KA9Q NOS (NET/NOS)
Rig Control Backend: KISS TNC; no rig CAT
How to Set CAT: No rig CAT. 'attach asy' / KISS lines in autoexec.net bind a serial KISS TNC (port/baud) to the AX.25 stack. No rig model.
Audio Device Select: n/a — hardware TNC provides the modem.
PTT Config: Handled by the TNC.
Companion Needed: Hardware KISS TNC.
Notes: Phil Karn KA9Q's original TCP/IP-over-packet NOS; the ancestor of JNOS/TNOS.

Name: KISSet
Rig Control Backend: KISS serial TNC (e.g. NinoTNC) or soundmodem; no rig CAT
How to Set CAT: No rig CAT. Select the KISS interface — a serial TNC (COM/baud, e.g. NinoTNC) or a soundmodem — in KISSet's connection settings. No rig model.
Audio Device Select: n/a for hardware TNC; a soundmodem selects its own sound device.
PTT Config: Handled by the TNC/soundmodem.
Companion Needed: KISS TNC (NinoTNC) or a soundmodem.
Notes: Simple modern KISS packet terminal (G7TAJ); pairs well with the NinoTNC.
Source Note: No formal online manual located; the KISS-TNC, no-rig-CAT architecture is definitional for this AX.25 terminal.

Name: LinPac
Rig Control Backend: Linux kernel AX.25 via a serial KISS TNC or soundmodem; no rig CAT
How to Set CAT: No rig CAT. LinPac uses a kernel AX.25 port set up with kissattach (serial KISS TNC) or a soundmodem; pick that AX.25 port name in LinPac, not a rig. No rig model.
Audio Device Select: n/a in LinPac; a soundmodem (Direwolf) selects the sound device.
PTT Config: Handled by the TNC/soundmodem.
Companion Needed: KISS TNC or Direwolf bound to the kernel AX.25 stack.
Notes: Linux packet terminal with a mail client; rides the kernel AX.25 stack.

Name: Linux AX.25 (ax25-tools / ax25-apps + kernel stack)
Rig Control Backend: Kernel AX.25 with a KISS TNC or soundmodem attached; no rig CAT
How to Set CAT: No rig CAT. Edit /etc/ax25/axports (portname, callsign, baud, paclen), then kissattach a serial KISS TNC or bind a soundmodem (Direwolf as KISS/pty) to that port. No rig model.
Audio Device Select: n/a in the stack; the soundmodem (Direwolf) chooses ADEVICE.
PTT Config: Handled by the TNC/soundmodem (Direwolf PTT: GPIO/RTS/CM108).
Companion Needed: Hardware KISS TNC or Direwolf; ax25-tools/ax25-apps.
Notes: The base Linux packet framework other apps (LinFBB, URONode, LinPac) sit on.

Name: MacAPRS
Rig Control Backend: Serial hardware TNC; no rig CAT
How to Set CAT: No rig CAT. Set the serial TNC's port/baud in its setup, plus a serial NMEA GPS. No rig model.
Audio Device Select: n/a — hardware TNC provides the modem.
PTT Config: Handled by the TNC keying the FM radio.
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a — VHF FM APRS, no ALC.
Companion Needed: Hardware TNC; optional serial GPS.
Notes: Classic Mac APRS (K4HG/KE6RK); serial-TNC + GPS, no sound-card/CAT.

Name: MSYS
Rig Control Backend: Serial hardware TNC (AX.25 over radio); no rig CAT
How to Set CAT: No rig CAT. Configure the TNC's serial port/baud in MSYS setup. No rig model.
Audio Device Select: n/a — hardware TNC provides the modem.
PTT Config: Handled by the TNC.
Companion Needed: Hardware packet TNC.
Notes: DOS packet BBS (WD6CMU); serial TNC only.

Name: Outpost Packet Message Manager (Outpost PMM)
Rig Control Backend: Packet TNC — Serial (KISS or text/command-mode hardware TNC), AGWPE, or Telnet; no rig CAT
How to Set CAT: No rig CAT. Setup > Interfaces lets you add a Serial Comm Port (hardware TNC, incl. KISS), an AGWPE interface, or Telnet; set the COM port/baud or AGW host:port. No rig model.
Audio Device Select: n/a in Outpost; with AGWPE the sound device is set in AGWPE.
PTT Config: Handled by the TNC/soundmodem.
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a — VHF FM packet, no ALC.
Companion Needed: AGWPE soundmodem or a hardware KISS TNC.
Notes: Emergency/ARES packet messaging client; abstracts the TNC so ops just write messages.
Source: Outpost Packet Message Manager User Guide (outpostpm.org)

Name: PinPoint APRS
Rig Control Backend: KISS/TNC2 serial TNC, or network (TCP) KISS TNC (e.g. Direwolf); no rig CAT
How to Set CAT: No rig CAT. Settings > TNC: choose the TNC Type — Kenwood/Kantronics/TinyTrak, any serial KISS/TNC2 (COM/baud), or network KISS (127.0.0.1:8100 to SoundModem/VARA/Direwolf); plus a serial NMEA GPS. No rig model.
Audio Device Select: n/a in PinPoint; a soundmodem (Direwolf) selects the sound device.
PTT Config: Handled by the TNC/soundmodem.
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a — VHF FM APRS, no ALC.
Companion Needed: KISS/TNC2 TNC or Direwolf over TCP.
Notes: Windows APRS mapping client with a clean TNC/GPS setup dialog.

Name: PocketPacket
Rig Control Backend: Built-in software AFSK TNC (device audio) or external Bluetooth KISS TNC (e.g. Mobilinkd TNC3); no rig CAT
How to Set CAT: No rig CAT. Choose the built-in software TNC (1200-baud AFSK through the device's audio to a VHF radio) or an external Bluetooth KISS TNC in settings; also APRS-IS via Tier-2 servers. No rig model.
Audio Device Select: Uses the iOS/Mac device audio in/out (via a wired interface) for the software TNC; no separate device picker.
PTT Config: VOX on the radio or a cabled interface for the software TNC; an external TNC keys its own radio.
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a — VHF FM APRS, no ALC.
Companion Needed: A wired audio/PTT interface (or an external KISS TNC).
Notes: iOS/macOS APRS app with a self-contained sound-card TNC.

Name: PoPT
Rig Control Backend: KISS over TCP/Serial, AXIP/UDP, or Linux AX.25; no rig CAT
How to Set CAT: No rig CAT. In the connection settings pick the device type: KISS-over-Serial (hardware TNC on a COM port), KISS-over-TCP (Direwolf), AXIP-over-UDP, or a Linux AX.25 device; set port/baud or host:port. No rig model.
Audio Device Select: n/a in PoPT; a soundmodem (Direwolf) chooses the sound device.
PTT Config: Handled by the TNC/soundmodem.
Companion Needed: Hardware KISS TNC or Direwolf (TCP KISS).
Notes: AX25_POPT (DerHirschi): a modern multi-platform Python packet terminal supporting many KISS transports.

Name: QtTermTCP
Rig Control Backend: KISS or AGW TNC (incl. Direwolf) over serial/TCP; no rig CAT
How to Set CAT: No rig CAT. Setup connects to a TNC via Serial KISS (COM/baud), TCP KISS, or AGW (host:port). No rig model.
Audio Device Select: n/a in QtTermTCP; a soundmodem selects the sound device.
PTT Config: Handled by the TNC/soundmodem.
Companion Needed: Direwolf/UZ7HO soundmodem or a hardware KISS/AGW TNC.
Notes: G8BPQ's cross-platform packet terminal; pairs naturally with BPQ32/Direwolf.

Name: SARTrack
Rig Control Backend: KISS/AGW TNC or Direwolf (AGWPE); no rig CAT
How to Set CAT: No rig CAT. Under Connections select AGWPE (TCP to Direwolf, e.g. localhost:8000) or a KISS serial TNC (COM/baud), plus a serial NMEA GPS. No rig model.
Audio Device Select: n/a in SARTrack; a soundmodem (Direwolf) selects the sound device.
PTT Config: Handled by the TNC/soundmodem.
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a — VHF FM APRS, no ALC.
Companion Needed: KISS/AGW TNC or Direwolf; optional GPS.
Notes: Search-and-rescue APRS tracking/mapping (ZL); TNC-based like other APRS clients.

Name: TNOS
Rig Control Backend: KISS TNC; no rig CAT
How to Set CAT: No rig CAT. 'attach' lines in the config bind a serial KISS TNC (port/baud) to the AX.25/IP stack. No rig model.
Audio Device Select: n/a — hardware TNC provides the modem.
PTT Config: Handled by the TNC.
Companion Needed: Hardware KISS TNC.
Notes: NOS derivative (KO4KS) with BBS features; headless server.

Name: TSTHOST (The Station Terminal)
Rig Control Backend: Hardware TNC in host mode, or software TNC via TFPCX; no rig CAT
How to Set CAT: No rig CAT. Point TSTHOST at a hardware TNC running host mode on a COM port (device/baud), or at a TFPCX TSR driving a Baycom/sound modem. No rig model.
Audio Device Select: n/a for hardware TNC; handled by TFPCX/modem if soft-modem.
PTT Config: Handled by the TNC/modem.
Companion Needed: Host-mode TNC (or TFPCX + Baycom/sound modem).
Notes: DOS packet terminal/mini-BBS; classic TNC-host-mode client.

Name: U2APRS
Rig Control Backend: AFSK audio via cable to the radio; no rig CAT
How to Set CAT: No rig CAT. U2APRS generates APRS AFSK through the device's audio out to a radio over a cable; select the audio/serial interface in settings. No rig model.
Audio Device Select: Uses the device (Android/PC) audio in/out via the interface cable; no rig-specific picker.
PTT Config: VOX on the radio, or PTT via a cable/serial line.
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a — VHF FM APRS. Keep audio below overdeviation.
Companion Needed: An audio/PTT interface cable to a VHF radio.
Notes: Lightweight AFSK APRS beacon app; simplest with VOX.

Name: UI-View32 (and UI-View16)
Rig Control Backend: TNC in KISS, AGWPE, BPQ, or WA8DED/TF host mode (or terminal-mode TNC); no rig CAT
How to Set CAT: No rig CAT. Setup > Comms Setup: choose the host mode (KISS on a COM port, AGWPE, BPQ, WA8DED/TF, or WINPDDE) and set COM/baud (with a KISS 'Setup' button) or the AGW/BPQ link. No rig model.
Audio Device Select: n/a in UI-View; with AGWPE the sound device is set in AGWPE.
PTT Config: Handled by the TNC/soundmodem.
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a — VHF FM APRS, no ALC.
Companion Needed: AGWPE soundmodem or a hardware KISS/BPQ TNC.
Notes: G4IDE's classic Windows APRS client; still widely used with AGWPE.

Name: URONode
Rig Control Backend: Linux kernel AX.25 with a KISS TNC or soundmodem; no rig CAT
How to Set CAT: No rig CAT. URONode uses the kernel AX.25 ports (/etc/ax25/axports) you set up by kissattaching a serial TNC or binding a soundmodem; it references those port names. No rig model.
Audio Device Select: n/a in URONode; a soundmodem (Direwolf) selects the sound device.
PTT Config: Handled by the TNC/soundmodem.
Companion Needed: KISS TNC or Direwolf on the kernel AX.25 stack.
Notes: Linux node/switch front-end; rides the kernel AX.25 stack.

Name: W0RLI MailBox (PBBS)
Rig Control Backend: Serial hardware TNC (AX.25 over radio); no rig CAT
How to Set CAT: No rig CAT. Configure the TNC's serial port/baud in the mailbox config. No rig model.
Audio Device Select: n/a — hardware TNC provides the modem.
PTT Config: Handled by the TNC.
Companion Needed: Hardware packet TNC.
Notes: Hank Oredson W0RLI's foundational packet BBS; serial-TNC based.

Name: WinAPRS
Rig Control Backend: Serial hardware TNC (KISS or TNC2 command mode); no rig CAT
How to Set CAT: No rig CAT. Set the TNC's COM port/baud and mode (KISS or TNC2) in setup, plus a serial GPS. No rig model.
Audio Device Select: n/a — hardware TNC provides the modem.
PTT Config: Handled by the TNC keying the FM radio.
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a — VHF FM APRS, no ALC.
Companion Needed: Hardware TNC; optional serial GPS.
Notes: Windows APRS (K4HG/KE6RK), sibling of MacAPRS.

Name: WinPack
Rig Control Backend: Hardware TNC in host mode or via AGWPE; no rig CAT
How to Set CAT: No rig CAT. Configure a host-mode hardware TNC on a COM port (device/baud) or an AGWPE link in setup. No rig model.
Audio Device Select: n/a in WinPack; with AGWPE the sound device is set in AGWPE.
PTT Config: Handled by the TNC/soundmodem.
Companion Needed: Host-mode TNC or AGWPE soundmodem.
Notes: Popular Windows packet terminal (G4IDE); works with AGWPE for soundcard packet.

Name: WINTNC
Rig Control Backend: KISS TNC or soundmodem; no rig CAT
How to Set CAT: No rig CAT. Set the KISS TNC's COM/baud (or the soundmodem link) in its config. No rig model.
Audio Device Select: n/a for hardware TNC; a soundmodem selects its sound device.
PTT Config: Handled by the TNC/soundmodem.
Companion Needed: KISS TNC or a soundmodem.
Notes: Windows packet terminal; KISS/soundmodem based.
Source Note: No formal online manual located; KISS-TNC, no-rig-CAT architecture is definitional for this packet terminal.

Name: Xastir
Rig Control Backend: Kernel AX.25, serial TNC, or AGW/Direwolf; Hamlib is used for optional rig control INCLUDING PTT keying (and HF frequency) on rig-controlled interfaces
How to Set CAT: No general CAT for the packet data. Interface > Interface Control > Add: choose Serial TNC (device/baud), Networked AGWPE, a KISS/Direwolf interface, or kernel AX.25; add a GPS interface. Rig-controlled interface types let you select a Hamlib-supported radio for PTT control and HF frequency.
Audio Device Select: n/a in Xastir; a soundmodem (Direwolf) selects the sound device.
PTT Config: For a hardware TNC the TNC keys PTT; on interfaces without TNC PTT, Xastir keys PTT via Hamlib (or a serial control line) — Hamlib should be installed if you need PTT control.
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a — VHF FM APRS, no ALC.
Companion Needed: Direwolf/serial TNC/AGWPE; optional Hamlib for HF freq set.
Notes: Powerful cross-platform APRS client; many interface types, GPS-aware.
Source Note: Corrected the claim that Hamlib in Xastir is 'for setting a HF frequency, not for keying.' The official docs state Hamlib should be installed if you need PTT-Control, so Xastir uses Hamlib for PTT keying as well as frequency on rig-controlled interfaces.

Name: XRouter (XRPi / XRLin / XRWin)
Rig Control Backend: KISS/BPQKISS TNCs, sound modems, and YAM modems (keys PTT for AX.25); no operating CAT
How to Set CAT: No transceiver CAT for QSOs. XROUTER.CFG PORT directives define each channel: a serial KISS/BPQKISS TNC (device/baud), a sound modem, or a YAM modem; set the interface and modem params. No rig model.
Audio Device Select: n/a for hardware TNC; the sound modem selects its own device.
PTT Config: XRouter/its modem keys PTT (serial RTS or the TNC) for each AX.25 port.
Companion Needed: KISS TNC or a sound modem.
Notes: G8PZT's multi-port AX.25/NET-ROM/IP router node; ties multiple radios into a network.

Name: XRouter / XRPi
Rig Control Backend: KISS/BPQKISS TNC, sound modem, or YAM modem; no operating CAT
How to Set CAT: No transceiver CAT for QSOs. Same as XRouter: PORT directives in XROUTER.CFG map each radio port to a KISS TNC (device/host + baud), a sound modem, or YAM. No rig model.
Audio Device Select: n/a for hardware TNC; the sound modem selects its own device.
PTT Config: Keyed by the TNC/sound modem per AX.25 port (serial RTS or TNC).
Companion Needed: KISS/AGW TNC or a soundmodem.
Notes: The Raspberry Pi build (XRPi) of G8PZT's XRouter; duplicate listing of the XRouter node software.
Source Note: Duplicate of the XRouter entry (same G8PZT software); confirmed identically.

Name: YAAC (Yet Another APRS Client)
Rig Control Backend: KISS or AGW TNC (incl. Direwolf) over serial/TCP; no rig CAT
How to Set CAT: No rig CAT. Configure > Ports > Add lets you add a Serial TNC (KISS, COM/baud), AGWPE (host:port, default 8000), a KISS-over-TCP port (Direwolf, default 8001), plus a serial GPS. No rig model.
Audio Device Select: n/a in YAAC; a soundmodem (Direwolf) selects the sound device.
PTT Config: Handled by the TNC/soundmodem.
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a — VHF FM APRS, no ALC.
Companion Needed: Direwolf/KISS/AGW TNC; optional GPS.
Notes: KA2DDO's Java APRS client; cross-platform, works well with Direwolf over TCP KISS.

Name: Band Master
Rig Control Backend: Omni-Rig (CAT hub) — Afreet Software (VE3NEA)
How to Set CAT: Configure Omni-Rig once (rig model, COM port, baud, data/stop bits); Band Master reads/tunes the radio through Omni-Rig. In Band Master select which Omni-Rig radio (1 or 2) to follow.
Audio Device Select: n/a — this is a bandmap/spot display, not a sound-card app.
PTT Config: n/a — no keying; it only reads frequency and click-tunes the rig.
Companion Needed: Omni-Rig; a DX-cluster/spot source (and typically a logger).
Notes: VE7CC-style bandmap/spot viewer; click-to-tune via Omni-Rig, shares the rig with your logger.
Source: Band Master (Afreet Software / DX Atlas, VE3NEA)
Source Note: Confirmed Omni-Rig backend; author is VE3NEA (Afreet Software / DX Atlas), the same developer as Omni-Rig and CW Skimmer.

Name: CC User (VE7CC / AR-User)
Rig Control Backend: Omni-Rig (CAT hub)
How to Set CAT: Set up Omni-Rig (rig model, COM, baud) and enable Omni-Rig in CC User's settings; spots then click-tune the radio via Omni-Rig. Also links to a logger.
Audio Device Select: n/a — cluster/spot client, no sound-card role.
PTT Config: n/a — no keying; frequency read/tune only.
Companion Needed: Omni-Rig; the VE7CC cluster feed; a logger for integration.
Notes: VE7CC's cluster client (AR-User); click-to-tune and spot filtering, shares the rig via Omni-Rig.
Source: CC User Manual (VE7CC, hosted by G4IFB / BCDXC)
Source Note: Could not verify the claimed 'Omni-Rig (CAT hub)' backend in the CC User manual; documented behavior is spot distribution to a separate logger which tunes the radio. Left as uncertain pending confirmation of any built-in Omni-Rig option.

Name: CQ/X
Rig Control Backend: Native CAT (freq/mode) over a serial COM port (FT-1000MP, Kenwood, Icom CAT radios)
How to Set CAT: In CQ/X radio/serial settings pick the radio type and set the COM port, baud, and data/stop bits; it reads/sets frequency and mode. GPS-enabled mobile QSO-party logger. No audio.
Audio Device Select: n/a — logging app; digital modes not part of it.
PTT Config: PTT/CW keying via a serial COM port (RTS/DTR) if used for CW/voice memories.
ALC/Power Guidance: For SSB keep mic/voice-keyer drive so ALC barely moves; CW is constant-carrier — set power on the rig.
Companion Needed: None required; a serial CAT/keying interface for radio control.
Notes: Rover/QSO-party logger (state QSO parties); tracks county lines and reads rig frequency.
Source Note: Confirmed; developer is Chuck, NO5W.

Name: CT
Rig Control Backend: Native CAT (later versions) over serial (Icom/Kenwood/Yaesu/TenTec); CW/PTT via serial or parallel (LPT) port; external DVK
How to Set CAT: In CT's config set the radio type and the COM port for CAT (later DOS/Windows versions). Frequency/mode are read/set from the rig; it also interfaces to a packet TNC/PacketCluster. Earlier versions were log-only.
Audio Device Select: n/a — no sound-card digital; DVK is external hardware.
PTT Config: CW keying and PTT on a serial (RTS/DTR) or parallel (LPT) port; also drives an external DVK.
ALC/Power Guidance: CW is constant-carrier — set power on the rig; SSB voice-keyer drive kept so ALC barely moves.
Companion Needed: A serial/LPT keying interface; external DVK for voice.
Notes: K1EA's classic DOS contest logger; a foundational SO2R/keying design.
Source: CT User Manual v9 (Ken Wolff K1EA)

Name: CTESTWIN
Rig Control Backend: Native CAT over serial; CW keying via serial/USB
How to Set CAT: Option > RIG (COM setup): choose the transceiver maker/model and set the COM port and baud; CTESTWIN reads/sets frequency and mode.
Audio Device Select: n/a — CW/SSB logger; no built-in sound-card digital.
PTT Config: CW keying and PTT on a serial COM port (RTS/DTR) or a USB/CI-V interface.
ALC/Power Guidance: CW is constant-carrier — set power on the rig; keep any voice-keyer drive so ALC barely moves.
Companion Needed: A serial/USB CAT+keying interface.
Notes: JE3HHT (Makoto Mori) contest logger, popular in JA; pairs with his other tools.
Source Note: Confirmed against the program's known design; developer is JE3HHT (Makoto Mori, author of MMTTY). No stable official manual URL located.

Name: DXLog.net
Rig Control Backend: Native radio drivers + Omni-Rig; SO2R
How to Set CAT: SETUP > (Configure Ports/Radios): assign each radio to a COM port with the driver/model, baud, data/stop bits — or select OmniRig; set PTT/CW pins per port and band-data output there. With OmniRig, set up the COM/baud in Omni-Rig first, then choose Radio Type: OmniRig in DXLog.
Audio Device Select: Windows default device for QSO recording; digital modes handed to external engines (MMTTY/2Tone/WSJT-X).
PTT Config: PTT via CAT or a COM DTR/RTS pin; CW via serial/LPT or WinKeyer, configured per port (CW+PTT can share one COM port's pins).
ALC/Power Guidance: CW/RTTY constant-carrier — set power on the rig; for SSB/AFSK keep audio drive so ALC barely moves.
Companion Needed: Omni-Rig (optional); WinKeyer (optional); MMTTY/2Tone/WSJT-X for digital.
Notes: Modern multi-op contest logger (fork lineage of Win-Test); full SO2R and band decoders.

Name: DXTelnet
Rig Control Backend: DX-cluster telnet client; click-to-tune via rig control (Omni-Rig / DDE link to a logger)
How to Set CAT: A Windows telnet cluster client; clicking a spot tunes the radio through its rig-control link (Omni-Rig, or DDE to a companion logger). Configure the rig model/COM/baud in the rig-control engine, not for TX.
Audio Device Select: n/a — telnet cluster client, no sound-card role.
PTT Config: n/a — no keying; frequency tune only.
Companion Needed: Omni-Rig; a DX-cluster telnet feed; optional logger.
Notes: IK4VYX's DX-cluster telnet client; click-to-tune via Omni-Rig, feeds spots to a logger.
Source Note: Core role (telnet cluster client, click-to-tune, no TX/audio) confirmed; the exact rig-control path (Omni-Rig vs DDE-to-logger) is not fully pinned down in the located docs. Developer is Fabrizio, IK4VYX.

Name: GenLog
Rig Control Backend: No rig CAT / no frequency control; CW and PTT keying only, via a serial COM port (RTS/DTR)
How to Set CAT: There is NO CAT/rig-frequency configuration — GenLog does not read or set radio frequency ('GenLog does not support QRG'). In the CW setup you select the serial COM port and can swap the CW and PTT lines; PTT can be turned off.
Audio Device Select: n/a — general logging app, no sound-card digital.
PTT Config: CW keying and PTT on a serial COM port (RTS/DTR); option to swap CW/PTT lines and to disable PTT.
ALC/Power Guidance: CW is constant-carrier — set power on the rig; SSB voice-keyer drive kept so ALC barely moves.
Companion Needed: A serial CAT/keying interface.
Notes: W3KM's general-purpose contest/general logger; lightweight rig-read + CW.
Source Note: Corrected: the claim that GenLog does 'Native CAT (freq/mode read) over serial' and that you 'pick the radio and set the COM port/baud for CAT; it reads frequency and mode' is wrong. Per W3KM's FAQ, GenLog does not control radios/does not support QRG; it only does CW/PTT keying via serial RTS/DTR. The CW/PTT and ALC guidance are correct.

Name: HamRacer
Rig Control Backend: Native CAT over a COM port; synthesized voice keyer routed via a PC sound card / DAX
How to Set CAT: In HamRacer's radio settings choose the rig and set the COM port/baud for CAT (frequency/mode readback and control). Its unique synthesized voice keyer plays through the Windows sound card; for a Flex you route that audio to DAX (e.g. via VoiceMeeter).
Audio Device Select: HamRacer uses the PC sound card for synthesized voice; Windows audio takes its output, which you route to the transmitter (e.g. DAX on a Flex).
PTT Config: PTT via CAT over the COM port (keys the rig for voice-message playback).
ALC/Power Guidance: SSB voice — set the synthesized-audio level so the rig's ALC barely moves at the desired power; CW is constant-carrier, set on the rig.
Companion Needed: A CAT interface; for Flex/SDR a DAX virtual audio device.
Notes: Contest logger notable for a built-in synthesized voice keyer (works well with Flex/DAX).

Name: MINOS
Rig Control Backend: Hamlib (rigtype 2, hamlib-net localhost:4532) or a serial/virtual-serial COM port
How to Set CAT: In MINOS rig-control settings select the radio and set the interface: Hamlib-net (localhost:4532) or a serial/USB COM port and baud (a virtual serial port is sometimes used, e.g. for a KX3). It reads/sets frequency and mode; digital modes (FT8 etc.) run in external apps.
Audio Device Select: n/a — VHF/UHF contest logger; no built-in sound-card digital.
PTT Config: PTT via CAT/Hamlib or a serial line if configured; primarily a logging app.
ALC/Power Guidance: For SSB keep drive so ALC barely moves; CW is constant-carrier, set on the rig.
Companion Needed: A CAT/serial interface; WSJT-X separately for VHF digital.
Notes: G0GJV VHF/UHF/SHF contest logger (RSGB/Region 1); open-source, Minos 2 line.
Source Note: Clarified: MINOS does support two interfaces — Hamlib rigtype 2 at localhost:4532 and a (virtual) serial port. The original 'direct Hamlib-net support has been limited' wording understates that hamlib-net IS a supported interface.

Name: N1MM Logger+
Rig Control Backend: Native radio drivers (per-COM); SO2R
How to Set CAT: Config > Configure Ports, Mode Control, Winkey, etc. — on the Hardware tab assign each radio to a COM port, pick the radio model, and set baud/data/stop/parity via the Set button. Choose PTT and CW pins per port; enable SO2R and band-data (LPT/OTRSP) as needed.
Audio Device Select: Windows default device for QSO/contest audio recording; digital is handed to MMTTY/fldigi/WSJT-X, which own the sound card.
PTT Config: PTT via CAT or a COM DTR/RTS pin; CW via a serial port or WinKeyer, set per port on the Hardware tab.
ALC/Power Guidance: CW/RTTY (FSK) is constant-carrier — set power on the rig; for SSB/AFSK keep audio drive so ALC barely moves.
Companion Needed: Optional WinKeyer; MMTTY/2Tone (RTTY), fldigi/WSJT-X for other digital.
Notes: The dominant Windows contest logger; native drivers plus tight external-engine integration.
Source: N1MM Logger+ — The Configurer / Interfacing (official docs)

Name: N1MM Logger+ Spectrum Display
Rig Control Backend: Native CAT of the transceiver via N1MM (not configured separately); ingests native spectrum or SDR I/Q
How to Set CAT: Not configured separately — it uses the radio already set in N1MM's Configure Ports. It reads the rig's native spectrum (Icom/Flex/etc.) or an SDR I/Q stream and click-tunes the same rig through N1MM's CAT.
Audio Device Select: For SDR I/Q it takes the SDR data stream/device; otherwise it uses the radio's native spectrum data, no audio device.
PTT Config: n/a — it drives tuning only; keying stays in the main N1MM window.
Companion Needed: N1MM Logger+ (host); a spectrum-capable rig or an SDR for I/Q.
Notes: A waterfall panadapter inside N1MM+; turns the rig's or an SDR's spectrum into click-to-tune.
Source: N1MM Logger+ documentation — Spectrum Display window

Name: N3FJP Contest Log suite
Rig Control Backend: Native CAT (freq/mode) over a COM port; WSJT-X integration via UDP API
How to Set CAT: Settings > Rig Interface: pick the radio and set the COM port, baud, data/stop bits; enable frequency/mode polling. Only one program can own the rig, so set WSJT-X's rig to None if AC Log controls it (or vice versa). WSJT-X links via Settings > Application Program Interface > WSJT Interface (UDP).
Audio Device Select: n/a in N3FJP itself; digital comes from WSJT-X (via its UDP API), which owns the sound card.
PTT Config: PTT/CW via a serial COM port (RTS/DTR) or WinKeyer; set in the CW/interface config.
ALC/Power Guidance: CW is constant-carrier — set power on the rig; SSB voice-keyer drive kept so ALC barely moves.
Companion Needed: A serial CAT/keying interface; WSJT-X for FT8/FT4 logging into the suite.
Notes: Scott Davis N3FJP's family of contest/general loggers; simple rig setup, popular for casual contests.
Source: N3FJP — WSJT-X integration / Rig Interface help (official)
Source Note: Minor: the CAT dialog is labeled 'Settings > Rig Interface' (not 'Interface Configuration'); WSJT-X integration is under Settings > Application Program Interface > WSJT Interface. Substance confirmed.

Name: NA
Rig Control Backend: Native CAT over serial; CW/PTT/DVK via serial or parallel (LPT) port
How to Set CAT: In NA's config choose the radio type and CAT COM port/baud; it reads/sets frequency and mode (log-only for unsupported radios).
Audio Device Select: n/a — no sound-card digital; DVK is external hardware.
PTT Config: CW keying, PTT, and DVK control via a serial (RTS/DTR) or parallel (LPT) port; the 'NA-standard' LPT DVK pulse (pins 7/8 for radio switching) was adopted by CT/TR/WriteLog.
ALC/Power Guidance: CW is constant-carrier — set power on the rig; SSB voice-keyer drive so ALC barely moves.
Companion Needed: A serial/LPT keying interface; external DVK for voice.
Notes: K8CC (Datom) DOS contest logger; contemporary of CT/TR.
Source: NA Contest Software (DATOM Engineering, K8CC) manual

Name: QARTest
Rig Control Backend: Native CAT + Omni-Rig; built-in sound-card RTTY/PSK/CW
How to Set CAT: Setup > Interfaces: select the rig (native driver or via Omni-Rig) and set the COM port/baud, plus the PTT and CW COM pins. RTTY/PSK/CW use the sound card configured in the digital section.
Audio Device Select: Setup selects the sound-card input/output used for the built-in RTTY/PSK/CW decode and transmit.
PTT Config: PTT via CAT or a COM DTR/RTS pin; CW via serial or WinKeyer.
ALC/Power Guidance: AFSK RTTY/PSK — keep audio drive so ALC barely moves (rig in DATA/USB); CW/FSK is constant-carrier, set on the rig.
Companion Needed: Omni-Rig optional; WinKeyer optional; a sound-card interface for digital.
Notes: IK3QAR contest logger with integrated multimode sound-card decode/transmit.

Name: RCKLog
Rig Control Backend: Native CAT + Omni-Rig; CW/PTT keying via COM/LPT
How to Set CAT: In RCKLog's configuration choose the radio (native driver or Omni-Rig) and set the COM port/baud; set the CW and PTT COM/LPT pins there too. Also supports real-time packet-radio spotting.
Audio Device Select: n/a for basic logging; digital handled by companion RCKRtty/MMTTY.
PTT Config: PTT via CAT or a COM DTR/RTS pin; CW via serial/LPT or WinKeyer.
ALC/Power Guidance: CW/RTTY constant-carrier — set power on the rig; SSB drive so ALC barely moves.
Companion Needed: Omni-Rig optional; RCKRtty/MMTTY for RTTY.
Notes: DL4RCK contest logger; pairs with RCKRtty for RTTY contests.

Name: RCKRtty
Rig Control Backend: Native CAT + Omni-Rig; MMTTY engine for RTTY (sound-card AFSK) or FSK; CW/PTT keying
How to Set CAT: Configure the rig (native driver or via Omni-Rig) with COM/baud for frequency/mode; the RTTY signal is generated by the MMTTY engine you attach.
Audio Device Select: The sound-card device is selected inside the MMTTY engine (AFSK) that RCKRtty drives; or use true FSK via a COM/EXTFSK port.
PTT Config: PTT via CAT or a COM DTR/RTS pin; FSK keying via a serial port (EXTFSK).
ALC/Power Guidance: AFSK RTTY — keep audio drive so ALC barely moves (rig in DATA/USB); true FSK is constant-carrier, set power on the rig.
Companion Needed: MMTTY engine; Omni-Rig optional.
Notes: DL4RCK's RTTY contest program built around the MMTTY engine.
Source Note: Confirmed against the RCK suite's known MMTTY/EXTFSK architecture; developer is DL4RCK. No dedicated RCKRtty manual URL located.

Name: RoverLog
Rig Control Backend: Hamlib
How to Set CAT: In RoverLog's rig-control settings enable Hamlib, choose the rig model, and set the serial port and baud; it reads frequency/mode for logging.
Audio Device Select: n/a — VHF/UHF/microwave contest logger; no sound-card digital.
PTT Config: PTT via Hamlib/CAT if the rig supports it; primarily a logging app.
ALC/Power Guidance: For SSB keep drive so ALC barely moves; CW is constant-carrier, set on the rig.
Companion Needed: Hamlib; WSJT-X separately for VHF digital.
Notes: Cross-platform VHF+ contest logger; Hamlib-based rig read.
Source Note: Hamlib backend corroborated by the Hamlib supported-applications list; RoverLog is a cross-platform VHF/UHF/microwave contest logger.

Name: RTTY by WF1B
Rig Control Backend: Hardware terminal unit / TNC (AEA PK-900, MFJ-1278, AMT-1, HAL) on a COM port; native CAT for freq/mode
How to Set CAT: In WF1B's setup select the radio for CAT (COM/baud) for frequency/mode, and separately select the RTTY interface: a hardware terminal unit/TNC on a COM port.
Audio Device Select: Hardware TUs need no PC audio (this is a DOS-era TU/TNC-based contest program).
PTT Config: PTT/FSK keying via the TU/TNC or a serial COM port (RTS/DTR / FSK line).
ALC/Power Guidance: FSK RTTY is constant-carrier — set power on the rig; if using AFSK keep audio so ALC barely moves.
Companion Needed: A hardware terminal unit/TNC (classic) or a sound-card RTTY modem.
Notes: WF1B's classic DOS RTTY contest program; defined RTTY contesting for years.
Source Note: Hardware-TU/TNC + CAT core confirmed. The claim's 'and later sound-card modems' is not well documented for WF1B RTTY, which is fundamentally a hardware-terminal-unit program — treat the sound-card capability as unverified.

Name: RttyRite
Rig Control Backend: Rig CAT via its host WriteLog; modems: WinRTTY (native), MMTTY, 2Tone, external TU, or FSK on a COM port
How to Set CAT: RttyRite runs inside WriteLog and uses WriteLog's rig CAT (set radio/COM/baud in WriteLog's Ports). In RttyRite pick the TU/modem type: WinRTTY, MMTTY (XMMT.ocx), 2Tone, an external TU, or FSK on a COM port. The FSK port is set via RttyRite's Port menu (not MMTTY's TX tab).
Audio Device Select: For MMTTY/2Tone AFSK the sound device is chosen in that plug-in; hardware TUs use no PC audio.
PTT Config: PTT via WriteLog's CAT or a COM pin; FSK keying via a serial port (or the TU). 2Tone requires WinRTTY for FSK.
ALC/Power Guidance: FSK RTTY is constant-carrier — set power on the rig; AFSK keep audio so ALC barely moves.
Companion Needed: WriteLog (host); MMTTY/2Tone plug-in or a hardware TU.
Notes: The RTTY window of WriteLog; leans on MMTTY/2Tone engines for decode.

Name: RUMped
Rig Control Backend: Native CAT engine shared with RUMlog (Serial/USB, TCP, or microHAM interface); CW/PTT keying via a serial/USB interface
How to Set CAT: In RUMped's transceiver/CAT preferences select the interface type (Serial, TCP, or microHAM), pick the manufacturer/model, and set the serial port and baud; reads/sets frequency and mode (macOS companion to RUMlog).
Audio Device Select: n/a - contest logger; digital modes are run in external apps.
PTT Config: PTT/CW via a serial (USB-serial) interface, or via a WinKey-class keyer (e.g. K3NG with OPTION_WINKEY_FREQUENT_STATUS_REPORT).
ALC/Power Guidance: CW constant-carrier - set power on the rig; SSB voice-keyer drive so ALC barely moves.
Companion Needed: A USB-serial CAT/keying interface.
Notes: DL2RUM's macOS contest logger, the contest sibling of RUMlogNG.
Source Note: Verified against DL2RUM docs; backend is the RUMlog/RUMlogNG CAT engine (Serial/TCP/microHAM), which the original loosely called 'Hamlib-style'.

Name: SD (Super Duper)
Rig Control Backend: Native CAT (radio control over serial reads band/frequency/mode); CW keying (internal serial/parallel or WinKey)
How to Set CAT: In SD set the radio's COM port; SD reads the frequency/band from the rig into the log. CW Port is set to 'internal' (serial/parallel) or 'WinKey'.
Audio Device Select: n/a - HF CW/SSB logger, no sound-card digital.
PTT Config: CW keying via internal serial/parallel port, or WinKey (recommended) on a serial/USB-serial port; PTT lines on that interface.
ALC/Power Guidance: CW constant-carrier - set power on the rig; SSB voice-keyer drive so ALC barely moves.
Companion Needed: A serial CAT/keying interface (WinKeyer optional).
Notes: EI5DI Paul O'Kane's famously simple, fast contest logger.
Source: SD - the Contest Logger (Paul O'Kane EI5DI)
Source Note: Confirmed CW port (internal vs WinKey) and serial radio control; note CAT is primarily frequency/band read rather than full rig setting.

Name: Skookum Logger
Rig Control Backend: Native CAT over serial only; CW sent only via a K1EL WinKeyer or YCCC SO2R Box; outputs band data
How to Set CAT: In SkookumLogger (macOS) radio preferences pick the transceiver and its serial/USB port and baud (install a driver if the USB-serial adapter is not natively supported by macOS); it reads/sets frequency and mode.
Audio Device Select: n/a - CW/SSB contest logger; no sound-card digital.
PTT Config: CW keying and PTT via a K1EL WinKeyer (USB) or YCCC SO2R Box; a WinKeyer/SO2R box is required to send CW.
ALC/Power Guidance: CW constant-carrier - set power on the rig; SSB drive so ALC barely moves.
Companion Needed: WinKeyer (USB) recommended for CW/PTT.
Notes: K1GQ Bill Myers's macOS CW-contest logger; WinKeyer-centric.
Source Note: Confirmed CAT-via-serial-only and that CW requires a WinKeyer or YCCC SO2R Box (the original said 'WinKeyer' generically).

Name: so2sdr
Rig Control Backend: Hamlib for rig CAT; SDR audio/I-Q for the so2sdr-bandmap spectral display
How to Set CAT: In so2sdr Config choose the rig model and set the serial device and baud per radio (two radios for SO2R). The SDR bandmap (so2sdr-bandmap) takes a separate sound-card I/Q or SDR input.
Audio Device Select: Select the SDR's sound-card I/Q input that feeds the so2sdr-bandmap; QSO audio is the OS default.
PTT Config: CW is generated ONLY by a K1EL WinKey (or compatible) or N6TR's So2rmini - configured via Config > Winkey. cwdaemon/direct-port keying is NOT supported. PTT is handled via the WinKey/So2rmini (and Hamlib for rig control).
ALC/Power Guidance: CW constant-carrier - set power on the rig; SSB drive so ALC barely moves.
Companion Needed: Hamlib; WinKeyer or cwdaemon; an SDR (e.g. SoftRock/Afedri) for the bandmap.
Notes: Linux SO2R contest logger with a built-in SDR bandmap.
Source Note: CORRECTED: the docs explicitly state so2sdr uses WinKey or So2rmini for CW and that 'no other keying method (cwdaemon, direct port keying, etc.) is currently supported' - the original's cwdaemon claim is wrong.

Name: TACLog
Rig Control Backend: Serial/parallel keying interface (CW/PTT/voice) - a keying interface, not a full CAT rig-control program
How to Set CAT: TACLog does not do full transceiver CAT; it uses external interface circuitry: the serial port keys CW and PTT; the parallel port keys CW/PTT and can trigger external voice-keyer messages (or Sound Blaster voice). Set the port in its interface config.
Audio Device Select: n/a for digital modes; a Sound Blaster can be used only for stored voice messages.
PTT Config: PTT and CW keyed via the serial (RTS/DTR) or parallel (LPT) interface; the parallel port can key an external voice keyer.
ALC/Power Guidance: CW constant-carrier - set power on the rig; SSB drive so ALC barely moves.
Companion Needed: A serial/parallel keying interface (and an external voice keyer for voice memories).
Notes: OZ2M's DOS VHF/UHF/SHF Region-1 contest logger; keying via port hardware, not modern CAT.
Source Note: Confirmed keying model (serial=CW+PTT; parallel=CW+PTT+voice-keyer); note it has essentially no CAT frequency control, so 'limited CAT' means keying-only.

Name: tlf
Rig Control Backend: Hamlib (CAT/PTT); cwdaemon for CW; fldigi/gMFSK for RTTY
How to Set CAT: Edit tlf's logcfg.dat: set RIGMODEL (Hamlib id), RIGPORT (device, e.g. /dev/ttyUSB0), and RIGSPEED (baud). tlf polls the rig via Hamlib for frequency/mode.
Audio Device Select: n/a in tlf; RTTY audio is handled by fldigi/gMFSK, which tlf controls.
PTT Config: PTT via Hamlib; CW keying via cwdaemon (serial/LPT, or a WinKeyer via winkeydaemon).
ALC/Power Guidance: CW/RTTY constant-carrier - set power on the rig; SSB drive so ALC barely moves.
Companion Needed: Hamlib; cwdaemon (CW); fldigi/gMFSK (RTTY).
Notes: Linux ncurses contest logger; Unix-toolchain keying/digital via daemons.
Source: TLF contest logger man page / documentation
Source Note: Verified logcfg.dat keywords and Hamlib+cwdaemon model against tlf man page/docs.

Name: TR Log
Rig Control Backend: Native CAT over serial; CW/PTT via serial or parallel port (real-time DOS)
How to Set CAT: In TR Log's config set the radio type and CAT COM port/baud; it reads/sets frequency and mode. It needs true real-time port access (issues under some Windows versions).
Audio Device Select: n/a - no sound-card digital; DVK is external.
PTT Config: CW keying and PTT on a serial (RTS/DTR) or parallel (LPT) port (e.g. W1WEF DB-25 keying interfaces); drives an external DVK.
ALC/Power Guidance: CW constant-carrier - set power on the rig; SSB voice-keyer drive so ALC barely moves.
Companion Needed: A serial/LPT keying interface; external DVK for voice.
Notes: N6TR Larry Tyree's DOS contest logger; renowned for SO2R and keyboard-driven flow.
Source Note: Verified DOS real-time serial/parallel CAT+keying model.

Name: TR4W (TR for Windows)
Rig Control Backend: Native CAT + Omni-Rig; CW/PTT keying via serial/LPT/WinKey; band output
How to Set CAT: Settings > 'CAT and CW Keying' > Radio 1 / Radio 2: choose a native model on a COM port (baud/data/stop) or select Omni-Rig. WinKey is enabled with WK ENABLE=TRUE and WK PORT set (then CW/PTT set to NONE/OFF).
Audio Device Select: Windows default for recording; digital handed to external engines (MMTTY / WSJT-X / 2Tone).
PTT Config: PTT via CAT or a COM DTR/RTS pin; CW via serial, LPT, or a WinKeyer; TR4W can also send CW via the CAT interface (CWByCAT).
ALC/Power Guidance: CW/RTTY constant-carrier - set power on the rig; SSB/AFSK keep drive so ALC barely moves.
Companion Needed: Omni-Rig optional; WinKeyer optional; MMTTY for RTTY.
Notes: UA4WLI's Windows re-implementation of TR Log; free and actively used.
Source Note: Confirmed; the CAT menu is Settings > 'CAT and CW Keying' (Radio 1/2), and native+Omni-Rig are both supported.

Name: Tucnak
Rig Control Backend: Hamlib (CAT for freq/mode and PTT); sound-card for WSJT/meteor-scatter and voice keyer
How to Set CAT: In Tucnak's rig settings enable Hamlib, pick the rig model (rigctl -l), and set the serial device (e.g. /dev/ttyUSB0 or COMx) and speed, per radio.
Audio Device Select: Tucnak's sound settings pick the input/output device used for recording, WSJT/meteor-scatter audio, and the voice keyer.
PTT Config: PTT via Hamlib (CAT), or a serial line; CW via a serial/LPT keyer or WinKeyer.
ALC/Power Guidance: CW set power on the rig; keep voice/WSJT audio drive so ALC barely moves.
Companion Needed: Hamlib; optional WinKeyer; WSJT for MS integration.
Notes: OK1ZIA's Linux/Windows VHF contest logger; strong meteor-scatter/EME features.
Source Note: Verified Hamlib CAT+PTT and sound-card usage.

Name: Win-Test
Rig Control Backend: Native drivers + Omni-Rig; SO2R + band decoder
How to Set CAT: Options > Configure Interfaces: add each radio on a COM port, choose a native model or Omni-Rig, set baud/data/stop; assign PTT/CW pins and the LPT/microHAM band decoder. SO2R via the same interface manager.
Audio Device Select: Windows default for QSO recording; digital (RTTY/PSK) handed to external engines (MMTTY/2Tone) or WSJT-X.
PTT Config: PTT via CAT or a COM DTR/RTS pin; CW via serial/LPT or WinKeyer, per interface.
ALC/Power Guidance: CW/RTTY constant-carrier - set power on the rig; SSB/AFSK keep drive so ALC barely moves.
Companion Needed: Omni-Rig optional; WinKeyer optional; MMTTY/2Tone for RTTY.
Notes: Widely used multi-op contest logger with a networked multi-station design.
Source Note: Verified Options > Configure Interfaces and native+Omni-Rig support.

Name: WriteLog
Rig Control Backend: Native drivers + Omni-Rig; built-in sound-card RTTY/PSK via RttyRite (MMTTY/2Tone/RITTY)
How to Set CAT: Setup > Ports: assign each radio to a COM port with its model or Omni-Rig, set baud/data/stop, choose PTT/CW pins. RTTY/PSK run in RttyRite using MMTTY/2Tone (via XMMT.ocx) or true FSK.
Audio Device Select: In RttyRite/PSK the sound-card device is selected for the MMTTY/2Tone modem; QSO recording uses the Windows default.
PTT Config: PTT via CAT or a COM DTR/RTS pin; CW via serial or WinKeyer; FSK via a serial port for RTTY.
ALC/Power Guidance: AFSK RTTY/PSK - keep audio drive so ALC barely moves (rig in DATA/USB); FSK/CW constant-carrier set on the rig.
Companion Needed: Omni-Rig optional; WinKeyer optional; MMTTY/2Tone bundled for RTTY.
Notes: W5XD's contest logger; strong integrated RTTY (RttyRite) and SO2R.
Source Note: Verified Setup > Ports and RttyRite engine model.

Name: YFKtest
Rig Control Backend: Hamlib (CAT); CW keying via cwdaemon (Perl/ncurses, Linux)
How to Set CAT: In YFKtest's config enable rig control, set the Hamlib rig model, serial device, and baud; it polls the rig for frequency/mode.
Audio Device Select: n/a - CW/SSB console logger; no built-in digital.
PTT Config: PTT via Hamlib; CW keying via cwdaemon (serial/LPT).
ALC/Power Guidance: CW constant-carrier - set power on the rig; SSB drive so ALC barely moves.
Companion Needed: Hamlib; cwdaemon for CW.
Notes: Linux console (ncurses) contest logger; Unix-daemon keying.
Source Note: Verified Hamlib + cwdaemon model (YFKtest is Perl-based, uses ncurses).

Name: zLog
Rig Control Backend: Native CAT over serial; CW keying via serial/USB/parallel; PacketCluster (telnet/TNC)
How to Set CAT: In zLog's rig settings pick the transceiver and set the COM port/baud for CAT (frequency/mode). PacketCluster is a separate telnet/TNC connection for spots.
Audio Device Select: n/a - CW/SSB contest logger; no built-in sound-card digital.
PTT Config: CW keying on a serial (RTS/DTR), USB, or parallel (LPT) port; separate CW PTT line for amplifier control.
ALC/Power Guidance: CW constant-carrier - set power on the rig; SSB voice-keyer drive so ALC barely moves.
Companion Needed: A serial/USB/LPT keying interface; a cluster feed for spots.
Notes: Popular JA contest logger (zLogWin / modern zLog); strong CW keying.
Source Note: Verified CW via serial/USB/parallel, rig control, and PacketCluster.

Name: 2Tone
Rig Control Backend: Sound-card RTTY modem (no rig CAT). Decodes RX audio AND can transmit its own AFSK/FSK/DOOK/Pseudo-FSK - it is not RX-only; when embedded in a host logger the host manages TX.
How to Set CAT: n/a - 2Tone does no transceiver CAT. Frequency is owned by the host logger (N1MM+/WriteLog/Win-Test) that embeds it via XMMT.ocx.
Audio Device Select: In 2Tone's setup choose the RX sound-card (and left/right/mono) carrying the RTTY audio, and a TX sound-card if transmitting AFSK from 2Tone's own window.
PTT Config: Corrected: 2Tone has selectable TX modes (AFSK TX, FSK TX, DOOK, Pseudo-FSK) and can key PTT/FSK itself; but as an MMTTY-replacement engine in a host logger, PTT/FSK is normally driven by the host.
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a for RX. On AFSK TX keep audio so ALC barely moves; FSK is constant-carrier (power set on the rig).
Companion Needed: A host contest logger (WriteLog, N1MM+, Win-Test) that pipes audio to 2Tone and does keying.
Notes: G3YYD's high-performance RTTY decoder; usually run as a second decoder alongside MMTTY.
Source Note: CORRECTED: original called it strictly RX-only with TX handled solely by the host. 2Tone (G3YYD) does have its own transmit capability with several TX modes and can key the rig; it simply does no CAT.

Name: AndFlmsg
Rig Control Backend: None (sound-card only; Android)
How to Set CAT: n/a - no rig CAT. AndFlmsg (VK2ETA's fldigi/flmsg port) couples to the radio by acoustic coupling or a wired audio interface; the operator sets frequency/mode manually.
Audio Device Select: Uses the Android device's own audio in/out (built-in mic/speaker acoustically, or through an interface cable / USB sound card via OTG).
PTT Config: VOX on the radio, or PTT via a wired interface; no CAT PTT.
ALC/Power Guidance: Keep transmit audio drive moderate so ALC barely moves (rig in USB/DATA); on FM avoid overdeviation.
Companion Needed: None required; a wired audio/PTT interface improves reliability over acoustic coupling.
Notes: NBEMS/Flmsg messaging on Android; MFSK/MT63/PSK sound-card modes for EmComm.
Source Note: Verified audio-coupled/no-CAT model against the AndFlmsg user manual.

Name: cocoaModem
Rig Control Backend: Native CAT over serial (per-rig); sound-card multimode
How to Set CAT: In cocoaModem's preferences select the radio and its serial (USB-serial) port and baud for CAT frequency/mode; separately configure the FSK/PTT serial lines. (macOS.)
Audio Device Select: Preferences select the Mac sound-card input/output used for RTTY/PSK/CW/MFSK decode and transmit.
PTT Config: PTT via a serial RTS/DTR line (or CAT); FSK RTTY keying via a serial port.
ALC/Power Guidance: AFSK PSK/RTTY — keep audio drive so ALC barely moves (rig in DATA/USB); true FSK/CW constant-carrier, set power on the rig.
Companion Needed: A serial CAT/PTT/FSK interface.
Notes: W7AY (Kok Chen) macOS multimode sound-card program (RTTY/PSK/CW/MFSK).
Source Note: UNCERTAIN: sound-card + serial FSK/PTT is confirmed, but I could not confirm 'Native CAT over serial (per-rig)' frequency control in the W7AY manual - the documented interfacing is FSK/PTT/microHAM keying.

Name: CW Decoder (mobile)
Rig Control Backend: None (receive-only; microphone/audio input from a receiver)
How to Set CAT: n/a - no rig CAT. It listens to receiver audio (phone mic or an audio cable) and decodes Morse on screen.
Audio Device Select: Uses the phone's microphone or line-in; typically no device picker beyond the OS input.
PTT Config: n/a - receive-only; it never transmits.
Companion Needed: None; just a receiver whose audio the phone can hear.
Notes: Generic mobile Morse-decoder app; RX display only, no keying or CAT.
Source: Generic mobile CW decoder (e.g. Google Play listings)
Source Note: Generic mobile CW-decoder category; claims (RX-only, mic input, no CAT) hold for such apps. No single official manual applies.

Name: CW Player
Rig Control Backend: None for frequency CAT; keys the rig via a COM-port line, or via sound-card AFCW tone
How to Set CAT: n/a - no rig-model/CAT selection. Options only let you pick the COM port used for keying (DTR or RTS to the rig KEY/PTT jack).
Audio Device Select: Uses the Windows default sound card to generate the keyed CW tone (AFCW / SSB tone-keying); no explicit device picker.
PTT Config: Keying/PTT via COM-port DTR or RTS to the rig's KEY/PTT line, or feed the keyed sidetone into the mic/data input and use VOX.
ALC/Power Guidance: If tone-keying through SSB, keep the audio tone low so ALC barely moves; if hard-keying the KEY jack, RF power is set on the radio (ALC n/a).
Companion Needed: none
Notes: Primarily a keyboard/text CW sender and code trainer; decode is not its focus.
Source Note: Verified F6DQM CW_Player: sound card + COM-port keying, no CAT.

Name: CW Skimmer
Rig Control Backend: Omni-Rig (external CAT) for transceiver/receiver frequency; direct control of SoftRock/SDR-IQ/QS1R/SDR-IP-class hardware
How to Set CAT: Settings > Radio: pick the Hardware Type (SoftRock-IF, SDR-IQ, QS1R, SDR-IP, etc.); then in the CAT section select Omni-Rig (Rig 1 or Rig 2) so Skimmer reads/sets frequency. Telnet spot port defaults to 7300.
Audio Device Select: Settings > Audio: choose the I/Q sound-card input (Left/Right = I/Q) and sample rate for SoftRock-class SDRs.
PTT Config: n/a - receive-only skimmer, no PTT.
Companion Needed: Omni-Rig for CAT; an SDR or transceiver IF/audio source; feeds telnet spots to RBN Aggregator / loggers.
Notes: VE3NEA/Afreet decoder; outputs decoded CW (and RTTY) telnet spots.
Source Note: Verified Hardware-Type + Omni-Rig CAT, I/Q audio, and telnet port 7300.

Name: CW Skimmer Server
Rig Control Backend: Wideband SDR: QS1R is supported natively; other SDRs (Hermes/HPSDR/Red Pitaya, RX-888, Perseus, etc.) via third-party drivers/plugins (e.g. HermesIntf.dll) or ExtIO. No transceiver CAT.
How to Set CAT: SkimSrv.ini / Settings > Radio: select the SDR device and the band segments to watch; no rig-model CAT. The built-in Telnet server port is configured here (default 7300).
Audio Device Select: n/a for direct I/Q hardware; it also exposes the CW Skimmer Waterfall (CWSL) shared memory that other skimmers/CWSL_DIGI consume.
PTT Config: n/a - receive only.
Companion Needed: Wideband SDR hardware; RBN Aggregator to relay spots; CWSL_DIGI/RTTY Skimmer Server can read its shared memory.
Notes: Multi-band CW skimmer; publishes CWSL shared memory for downstream decoders.
Source Note: CORRECTED: only the QS1R is natively supported; RX-888/Perseus/Red Pitaya/HPSDR are added via third-party DLL drivers or ExtIO, not native drivers as the original implied.

Name: CW Studio
Rig Control Backend: none (audio-only receive decoder)
How to Set CAT: n/a — no CAT/rig control.
Audio Device Select: Pick the sound-card/microphone input (receiver speaker/line audio) in the app's audio input settings.
PTT Config: n/a — receive-only decoder, no transmit.
Companion Needed: none
Notes: Receive-only CW decoder; set the rig's frequency/mode manually.
Source Note: UNCERTAIN: could not locate an authoritative official manual/site for a program named 'CW Studio'; the RX-only, sound-card, no-CAT description is plausible for a CW audio decoder but is unverified.

Name: cwdaemon
Rig Control Backend: None (Morse/PTT keying only via libcw; not CAT)
How to Set CAT: n/a - no CAT. Launch with -d <device> where device is a serial (ttyS0/ttyUSB0) or parallel (parport0, the default) port; accepts keying text over UDP (default port 6789, changeable with -p).
Audio Device Select: Optional console/OSS sidetone only; it is not a sound-card data app.
PTT Config: Keys CW and PTT on the serial DTR/RTS (or parallel-port) lines to the rig's KEY/PTT jacks via a simple transistor switch; -t sets PTT delay.
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a - hard CW keying; power set on the radio.
Companion Needed: A logger that speaks the cwdaemon protocol (tlf, Xlog, etc.); WinKeyer variant drives K1EL hardware.
Notes: Linux keying server accepting requests over the network.
Source Note: Verified UDP 6789, -d device (default parport0), libcw keying model against the man page.

Name: CwGet
Rig Control Backend: None for decode; optional CAT frequency read/set via Omni-Rig (not a bespoke 'DXsoft rig' driver)
How to Set CAT: Setup: enable CAT and let CwGet tune/read the transceiver frequency through the Omni-Rig component (installed separately). Most users leave CAT off.
Audio Device Select: Setup > Sound card: select the input device carrying receiver audio.
PTT Config: n/a - receive-only decoder (pair with CwType for transmit).
Companion Needed: CwType for transmit; optional external CAT app.
Notes: DXsoft's CW receive decoder.
Source Note: CORRECTED: CwGet's CAT tuning is done via Omni-Rig, not an internal DXsoft rig driver.

Name: CWSL_DIGI
Rig Control Backend: None - no transceiver CAT; reads I/Q from CW Skimmer Server's CWSL shared memory
How to Set CAT: Edit config.ini: add decoder lines 'frequency mode' (Hz, e.g. FT8/FT4/WSPR/Q65/JT65/JS8), suffix the line with 1/2 to select the source SkimSrv instance (sharedmem), set freq calibration, and set callsign/gridsquare for spotting.
Audio Device Select: n/a - uses CW Skimmer Server shared memory, not a sound card.
PTT Config: n/a - decode/spot only (receive).
Companion Needed: CW Skimmer Server running (provides CWSL shared memory) plus the WSJT-X decoder DLLs; uploads spots to PSKReporter/RBN.
Notes: alexranaldi's multi-band FT8/FT4/JT65/WSPR/FST4/Q65/JS8 skimmer.
Source Note: Verified config.ini decoder-line format, sharedmem, callsign/grid, and spotting to PSK Reporter/RBN/WSPRNet.

Name: CwType
Rig Control Backend: Primarily CW keying; optional CAT frequency read via Omni-Rig (not a bespoke 'DXsoft rig' driver)
How to Set CAT: Setup: optional CAT/transceiver frequency via the Omni-Rig component; mostly you just select the Comm-port used for keying.
Audio Device Select: Can generate keyed sidetone via the sound card (Setup > Sound) for AFCW, or hard-key the rig.
PTT Config: Keys CW and PTT via COM (DTR/RTS) or LPT lines to the rig.
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a for hard CW keying; if using sound-card AFCW keep the tone low so ALC barely moves.
Companion Needed: CwGet for receive decoding.
Notes: DXsoft keyboard/paddle CW sender.
Source Note: CORRECTED: any CAT frequency read is via Omni-Rig, not an internal DXsoft driver.

Name: Digital Master 780 (DM780)
Rig Control Backend: HRD Rig Control (Ham Radio Deluxe's CAT engine)
How to Set CAT: Set the radio in Ham Radio Deluxe's Connect dialog (Company/Radio model, COM port, baud). DM780 slaves to HRD Rig Control automatically; configure the Radio Pane/TX button in DM780 (View > Radio).
Audio Device Select: DM780 > Program Options > Soundcard: select the Transmit (Output) and Receive (Input) devices.
PTT Config: DM780 > Program Options > PTT: 'via Ham Radio Deluxe' (CAT), a COM-port RTS/DTR line, or VOX.
ALC/Power Guidance: Put the rig in DATA/USB-D mode; raise TX audio/drive until you reach desired RF out with ALC just moving; keep ALC low.
Companion Needed: Ham Radio Deluxe (HRD Rig Control) for CAT; logs to HRD Logbook. Supports FSK RTTY.
Notes: PSK/RTTY/multimode module of the HRD suite.
Source: Ham Radio Deluxe support - Configuring DM-780 (soundcard/PTT)
Source Note: Verified Program Options > Soundcard and PTT, and CAT via HRD Rig Control.

Name: DroidRTTY
Rig Control Backend: None - no CAT (audio-coupled Android app, Wolphi LLC)
How to Set CAT: n/a - set the rig's frequency and mode manually.
Audio Device Select: Android audio routing: acoustic coupling (phone mic/speaker), a wired Wolphi-Link into the headset jack, or a USB sound card via OTG, for RX audio and AFSK TX.
PTT Config: VOX, or Wolphi-Link (or similar) interface PTT triggered by the TX audio / mic voltage.
ALC/Power Guidance: AFSK into SSB - keep the audio level moderate so the rig's ALC barely moves.
Companion Needed: A Wolphi-Link (or similar) audio interface is recommended for reliable coupling/PTT.
Notes: AFSK RTTY only (no true FSK).
Source Note: Verified Wolphi audio-coupled/Wolphi-Link model, no CAT.

Name: EXTFSK / XMMT.ocx
Rig Control Backend: Two DISTINCT components, neither doing CAT: EXTFSK.dll = a software FSK/PTT keying driver; XMMT.ocx = the ActiveX/OCX build of the MMTTY engine (an audio RTTY modem) used to embed MMTTY/2Tone in host loggers.
How to Set CAT: n/a for both (no transceiver CAT). EXTFSK: in the host (MMTTY/MMVARI/2Tone/N1MM+) select 'EXTFSK', then pick the COM/LPT port and map FSK->TxD, PTT->RTS/DTR. XMMT.ocx: registered so a host logger (N1MM+/WriteLog) can drive MMTTY or 2Tone directly.
Audio Device Select: CORRECTED: XMMT.ocx IS the MMTTY audio modem, so it handles/decodes sound-card audio (the sound device is selected via the embedded MMTTY). EXTFSK provides only line keying, no audio.
PTT Config: EXTFSK keys true FSK and PTT over COM/LPT or USB-serial TxD/RTS/DTR (it bit-bangs FSK so USB adapters can do real FSK); XMMT.ocx invokes EXTFSK (or a COM port) for keying.
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a for FSK keying (RF power set on the radio); AFSK via the embedded MMTTY should keep audio so ALC barely moves.
Companion Needed: A host RTTY application (MMTTY, MMVARI, 2Tone, N1MM+, WinWarbler).
Notes: The common workaround to get real FSK out of USB-serial adapters.
Source Note: CORRECTED: the original treated both as 'an FSK/PTT keying interface component... not audio.' That is true for EXTFSK.dll but NOT for XMMT.ocx, which is JE3HHT's ActiveX MMTTY modem engine (audio decode + keying) that host loggers embed.

Name: Flwkey
Rig Control Backend: None for CAT; drives a K1EL WinKeyer; optional XML-RPC link to fldigi/flrig for frequency display only
How to Set CAT: Configure > WinKeyer: select the serial port and baud to the K1EL WinKeyer. Optional XML-RPC (default 127.0.0.1:7362) to fldigi/flrig for the frequency readout.
Audio Device Select: n/a - WinKeyer hardware keying, no sound card.
PTT Config: The WinKeyer asserts PTT and keys CW to the rig over its KEY/PTT lines.
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a - hard CW keying; power set on the radio.
Companion Needed: A K1EL WinKeyer; part of the fldigi/flrig family.
Notes: Standalone WinKeyer-based CW keyboard/keyer.
Source Note: Verified WinKeyer serial config + optional fldigi/flrig XML-RPC link.

Name: gMFSK
Rig Control Backend: Hamlib
How to Set CAT: Configure > Rig control (Hamlib): select rig model, serial device, and baud (or 'None').
Audio Device Select: Configure > Sound: choose the OSS/ALSA input and output device.
PTT Config: Hamlib CAT PTT, or serial RTS/DTR, or VOX.
ALC/Power Guidance: Use SSB DATA mode; keep the audio drive low so ALC is minimal.
Companion Needed: none
Notes: Legacy Linux multimode (PSK/MFSK/RTTY/THROB/Hell); predecessor of fldigi.
Source: gMFSK - A Gnome Multimode HF Terminal (OH2BNS)
Source Note: Verified Hamlib rig control and OSS/ALSA sound config (gMFSK by OH2BNS).

Name: GRITTY
Rig Control Backend: None (receive-only decoder)
How to Set CAT: n/a - tune the rig manually; it decodes whatever audio it is fed.
Audio Device Select: Select the sound-card input capturing a single RTTY signal from the transceiver's ~3 kHz audio.
PTT Config: n/a - receive only (it cannot be used from the main Digital Interface TX window in loggers).
Companion Needed: none
Notes: Multi-channel RTTY receive/super-decoder used as a copy/spotting aid.
Source Note: Verified K6STI GRITTY is a Windows RX-only single-signal RTTY decoder.

Name: Ham Morse
Rig Control Backend: none (audio-only trainer/decoder)
How to Set CAT: n/a — no CAT.
Audio Device Select: Device microphone/line-in captures the receiver's speaker audio.
PTT Config: n/a — receive only.
Companion Needed: none

Name: ARDOP (ardopc / ardopcf)
