How to Set CAT: Settings > Radio (CAT): select the radio, COM port and baud; define the ALE scan-channel list.
Audio Device Select: Settings > Sound Card: select input and output devices for the 8-FSK ALE tones.
PTT Config: PTT via CAT, or a serial RTS/DTR line.
ALC/Power Guidance: SSB DATA mode; keep the tone drive low so ALC is minimal.
Companion Needed: none
Notes: HFLINK's PC-ALE 2G ALE controller.
Source: HFLINK PC-ALE Setup / PC-ALE Radio Control Knowledge Base (N2CKH)
Source Note: Verified via HFLINK PC-ALE setup and the N2CKH PC-ALE Radio Control KB. Note: multi-channel ALE needs a CAT-controllable rig; single-channel ALE works with any frequency-stable SSB rig with no CAT.

Name: PC-HFDL
Rig Control Backend: None (audio input from an HF SSB receiver)
How to Set CAT: n/a - tune the SSB receiver to the HFDL frequency manually; software options set soundcard/channel only.
Audio Device Select: Sound-card line/mic input fed from an HF SSB receiver (2.8 kHz bandwidth preferred).
PTT Config: n/a - receive only.
Companion Needed: An HF SSB receiver.
Notes: Charles Brain G4GUO's classic HFDL decoder.
Source: PC-HFDL documentation (Charles Brain) / kloth.net HFDL monitoring
Source Note: Verified. Written by Charles Brain (G4GUO); receive-only ARINC 635-3 HFDL decoder. Setup options cover sound card, channel, timeout, DDE and logfile; no rig CAT or PTT.

Name: PlanePlotter
Rig Control Backend: None for transceiver CAT; ingests SDR, ACARS audio, or serial/network Mode-S/ADS-B hardware
How to Set CAT: n/a - no transceiver CAT; input comes from SDR, sound-card ACARS audio, or a serial/network Mode-S/ADS-B receiver.
Audio Device Select: For ACARS: Options > Audio > ACARS > Source selects the sound-card input from a VHF-AM receiver.
PTT Config: n/a - receive only.
Companion Needed: An SDR/ADS-B receiver, or a VHF receiver for ACARS.
Notes: COAA ADS-B/ACARS aircraft plotting and aggregation.
Source Note: Verified against COAA. Refinement: for ACARS the audio source is set at Options > Audio > ACARS > Source (VHF-AM receiver, AM detector output, not a discriminator tap). Also ingests Mode-S/ADS-B (Kinetic SBS-1, AirNav RadarBox) and HFDL. Receive only.

Name: rtl-ais
Rig Control Backend: None (RTL-SDR, controlled via CLI flags)
How to Set CAT: n/a - CLI options set device index, ppm and gain; AIS frequencies are fixed.
Audio Device Select: n/a - consumes RTL-SDR I/Q directly (outputs NMEA over UDP).
PTT Config: n/a - receive only.
Companion Needed: An RTL-SDR dongle; feeds AIS plotters/aggregators.
Notes: Lightweight RTL-SDR AIS receiver.
Source Note: Verified. Command-line RTL-SDR AIS receiver/decoder (dgiardini). CLI flags set device index, ppm and gain; the two AIS channels (161.975/162.025 MHz) are fixed. Receive only.

Name: ShipPlotter
Rig Control Backend: None
How to Set CAT: n/a - tune the VHF receiver to the AIS frequencies manually, or configure the AIS receiver's serial/NMEA port (e.g. 38400,8,N,1).
Audio Device Select: Sound-card input from a marine-band/VHF receiver (48 kHz), or serial/USB NMEA from a dedicated AIS receiver.
PTT Config: n/a - receive only.
Companion Needed: A marine receiver or AIS receiver hardware.
Notes: COAA AIS decoding and vessel plotting.
Source Note: Verified against COAA. Two input paths: sound-card audio from a VHF receiver tuned to the AIS frequencies (soundcard run at 48 kHz), or NMEA serial from a dedicated AIS receiver (typically 38400,8,N,1). Receive only.

Name: Sigmira
Rig Control Backend: None for transceiver CAT; native control of RTL-SDR / RFSpace / SDRplay SDR hardware
How to Set CAT: n/a - select the SDR device in-app; for audio-input mode, tune the conventional receiver manually.
Audio Device Select: Sound-card input from a conventional receiver (also virtual audio cables), or use the internal SDR path.
PTT Config: n/a - receive only.
Companion Needed: Optional RTL-SDR/RFSpace/SDRplay SDR.
Notes: Receive-only multi-signal decoder (PSK31, STANAG S4285, CW, etc.).
Source Note: Verified against the Sigmira manual (Steve Harlow). No transceiver CAT; works from a conventional receiver via sound card, or natively controls RTL-SDR / RFSpace SDR-IQ/SDR-14 / SDRplay RSP / SDR-Radio networked receivers. Receive only (PSK31, RTTY, CW, STANAG 4285, HFDL, M141, etc.).

Name: TIVAR
Rig Control Backend: none built-in emphasized (use with an SSB HF radio; set frequency manually or via a separate CAT app)
How to Set CAT: n/a — TIVAR has no rig CAT of its own; set the SSB rig manually.
Audio Device Select: Sound-card input/output for the MIL-STD/serial-tone data waveforms.
PTT Config: PTT via VOX or a serial COM RTS/DTR interface.
ALC/Power Guidance: SSB DATA mode; keep the audio drive so ALC is minimal.
Companion Needed: An SSB HF radio plus a PTT interface.
Notes: MARS data-mode terminal for serial-tone waveforms.
Source Note: Could not locate official TIVAR documentation/manual to verify. The claimed profile (sound-card MIL-STD/serial-tone waveform tool with no built-in rig CAT; PTT via VOX or a serial RTS/DTR line; SSB DATA with low drive) is plausible for this software class but is unverified against an official source.

Name: VARA FM
Rig Control Backend: None for frequency CAT (sound-card modem); PTT only, via VOX / CAT / serial RTS-DTR (also Hamlib/CM108). Frequency CAT is handled by the host (VarAC/Winlink).
How to Set CAT: Settings > PTT: pick VOX, CAT, or RTS/DTR on a COM port (Hamlib/CM108 also supported). No rig-model frequency CAT here - that lives in the host app (VarAC/Winlink).
Audio Device Select: Settings > SoundCard: select input and output devices (through an FM transceiver, ideally the 9600-baud/flat-audio port).
PTT Config: Settings > PTT: VOX, CAT, serial RTS/DTR, Hamlib, or CM108.
ALC/Power Guidance: FM - no ALC concern; set deviation/audio level so the signal is not over-deviated.
Companion Needed: Winlink Express or VarAC connect over TCP (command port 8300, +8330 for a second instance).
Notes: Sound-card modem for FM packet-style data; needs a proper discriminator/flat-audio connection for high rates.
Source: VARA (EA5HVK / rosmodem) and Winlink VARA FM quick-setup
Source Note: Verified against VARA (EA5HVK) and Winlink VARA FM quick-setup guides. VARA is a sound-card modem: it does its own PTT but frequency CAT is done by the HOST app (VarAC/Winlink Express), not VARA. PTT choices in Settings > PTT are VOX, CAT, or a serial COM (RTS/DTR); VARA also supports Hamlib and CM108 PTT. There is no full rig-model frequency-CAT UI in VARA itself.

Name: VarAC
Rig Control Backend: VarAC's own rig control (Direct CAT, FLrig, OmniRig, or Hamlib) for frequency + PTT; audio via the external VARA modem over TCP
How to Set CAT: Settings > 'RIG control & VARA Configuration': choose the PTT and frequency-control engine (Direct CAT / FLrig / OmniRig / Hamlib), set rig model/port/baud, and use TEST; set the VARAHF.EXE path and the VARA modem TCP host/port (default 8300).
Audio Device Select: Audio is handled by the VARA modem - set the sound device in VARA, not in VarAC.
PTT Config: PTT via VarAC's chosen engine (Direct CAT / FLrig / OmniRig / Hamlib) or RTS/DTR; VARA's own PTT can also be used.
ALC/Power Guidance: Set in the VARA modem/rig: SSB DATA mode, drive so ALC barely moves.
Companion Needed: A running VARA HF/FM modem; Hamlib or OmniRig for CAT.
Notes: Keyboard-to-keyboard + P2P file/email chat front-end over VARA.
Source Note: The claim is too narrow. VarAC's Settings > 'RIG control & VARA Configuration' lets you choose PTT and frequency control via Direct CAT, FLrig, OmniRig, OR Hamlib (rigctld) - not only Hamlib/OmniRig - and RTS/DTR can be used for PTT. TEST buttons verify each. Audio is handled by the VARA modem (set the sound device in VARA); point VarAC at the running VARA HF/FM modem's TCP port (VARA HF default 8300) and optionally its VARAHF.EXE path for auto-launch.

Name: WINMOR
Rig Control Backend: None of its own (sound-card modem); PTT via CAT/serial; frequency CAT handled by Winlink Express
How to Set CAT: WINMOR TNC Setup / Advanced PTT settings brings up the CAT settings (radio type + COM/baud) for PTT-via-CAT; station frequency CAT is configured in Winlink Express Radio Setup.
Audio Device Select: WINMOR TNC Sound Card setup: choose capture and playback devices.
PTT Config: PTT via serial RTS/DTR, via CAT, or VOX (a PTT DLL is needed for some sound-card interfaces).
ALC/Power Guidance: SSB DATA mode; set drive so ALC barely moves and keep audio low.
Companion Needed: Winlink Express (RMS Express) as host; now largely replaced by ARDOP.
Notes: Legacy TAPR/ARSFI Winlink HF sound-card mode.
Source Note: Verified against Winlink Express documentation. Note WINMOR is deprecated (replaced by ARDOP and VARA HF). In the WINMOR TNC Setup you assign the sound-card capture/playback; PTT is set in the Advanced PTT/CAT settings (serial RTS/DTR, CAT, or VOX); frequency CAT is done by Winlink Express (Radio Setup).

Name: YaDD (Yet Another DSC Decoder)
Rig Control Backend: None (receive-only); optional external CAT/rig link only to display/log frequency
How to Set CAT: Optional - Setup can read frequency from a rig/CAT link; primarily just manual tuning of the DSC channels.
Audio Device Select: Sound-card input from an MF/HF/VHF receiver on the DSC calling channels.
PTT Config: n/a - receive only.
Companion Needed: An MF/HF/VHF receiver.
Notes: Decodes GMDSS DSC traffic.
Source Note: Verified. Written by Dirk Claessens (now SK; software still distributed). Receive-only DSC decoder; sound-card audio from an MF/HF/VHF receiver on the DSC calling channels. It can optionally read frequency from a CAT/rig link for logging.

Name: YaND
Rig Control Backend: None (receive-only)
How to Set CAT: n/a - tune the MF/HF receiver to the NAVTEX channels manually.
Audio Device Select: Sound-card audio input from the receiver.
PTT Config: n/a - receive only.
Companion Needed: An MF/HF receiver.
Notes: Yet Another NAVTEX Decoder (SITOR-B); receive-only.
Source: YaND (Dirk Claessens) - DXZone NAVTEX software listing
Source Note: Verified. Written by Dirk Claessens; receive-only NAVTEX decoder using the same sensitive FSK audio detector as YaDD. Sound-card input from an MF/HF receiver tuned to NAVTEX channels.

Name: YaND (Yet Another NAVTEX Decoder)
Rig Control Backend: None (receive-only)
How to Set CAT: n/a - tune to the NAVTEX channels (518 kHz, 490 kHz, 4209.5 kHz) manually.
Audio Device Select: Sound-card input from an MF/HF receiver tuned to the NAVTEX channels.
PTT Config: n/a - receive only.
Companion Needed: An MF/HF receiver.
Notes: NAVTEX SITOR-B decoder.
Source: YaND (Dirk Claessens) - DXZone NAVTEX software listing
Source Note: Verified (duplicate of the YaND entry). Receive-only NAVTEX decoder by Dirk Claessens; sound-card input from an MF/HF receiver on the NAVTEX channels (518, 490, 4209.5 kHz).

Name: Allmon3
Rig Control Backend: None - manages app_rpt/Asterisk nodes over the AMI, not radio CAT
How to Set CAT: n/a - set node numbers and AMI host/user/password in /etc/allmon3/allmon3.ini (matching /etc/asterisk/manager.conf). Commands links/DTMF, not a radio's CAT.
Audio Device Select: n/a - radio audio is handled by the AllStarLink USB fob on the node, not by Allmon3.
PTT Config: n/a - keying is done by app_rpt (COS/PTT via the URI/fob); Allmon3 only issues connect/disconnect/DTMF.
Companion Needed: An AllStarLink/Asterisk (app_rpt) node.
Notes: Python web dashboard/control panel for AllStar nodes.
Source Note: Verified against the AllStarLink Manual. Allmon3 is a web monitor/manager for app_rpt/Asterisk over the Asterisk Manager Interface (AMI); not CAT. Configured in /etc/allmon3 (allmon3.ini for AMI host/user/pass, users, web.ini); AMI credentials also live in /etc/asterisk/manager.conf. It issues connect/disconnect/DTMF, not radio CAT.

Name: AllStarLink (app_rpt / Asterisk)
Rig Control Backend: None - analog repeater/radio interface via a USB CM108 fob, not transceiver CAT
How to Set CAT: n/a - configured in /etc/asterisk (rpt.conf plus simpleusb.conf/usbradio.conf); select the CM108 USB fob and set COS/CTCSS/PTT parameters.
Audio Device Select: In simpleusb/usbradio config choose the USB CM108 sound fob (or URI/DINAH/RIM) for RX/TX audio; run simpleusb-tune-menu to set levels.
PTT Config: PTT keyed via the CM108 GPIO line (or serial); COS/CTCSS detect handles receive.
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a - analog FM; audio levels set with the tune menu, not ALC.
Companion Needed: A CM108 URI/DINAH/RIM interface plus a radio/repeater.
Notes: Asterisk app_rpt-based linking of radios over IP.
Source Note: Verified against the AllStarLink Manual. Analog radio interface via a CM108 USB fob (or URI/DINAH/RIM), not transceiver CAT. Configured in /etc/asterisk (rpt.conf + simpleusb.conf/usbradio.conf); simpleusb-tune-menu (or radio-tune-menu) sets RX/TX levels; PTT via the fob's GPIO line, COS/CTCSS detect on receive.

Name: ambed
Rig Control Backend: None - network AMBE transcoder daemon, no radio CAT
How to Set CAT: n/a - config lists the AMBE3000 hardware devices (ThumbDV/DVstick) and listen ports; used by reflectors (XLX, etc.).
Audio Device Select: n/a - transcodes DV codec streams, not sound-card audio.
PTT Config: n/a.
Companion Needed: AMBE3000 hardware (a ThumbDV/DVstick farm); a reflector such as XLX.
Notes: Transcodes DV audio between DMR/D-STAR/YSF/NXDN codecs.
Source Note: Verified as a networked AMBE transcoder daemon (used by XLX and similar reflectors). Config lists the AMBE3000 hardware devices (ThumbDV/DVstick) and listen ports; it transcodes DV codec streams, not sound-card audio - no radio CAT/PTT.

Name: AMBEServer
Rig Control Backend: None - exposes a hardware AMBE vocoder over the network
How to Set CAT: n/a - configure the AMBE dongle's serial device and the TCP listen port; clients connect over the network.
Audio Device Select: n/a - serves the AMBE vocoder, not audio.
PTT Config: n/a.
Companion Needed: A ThumbDV/DVstick AMBE3000 dongle; a client such as BlueDV.
Notes: Network server exposing one hardware AMBE3000 vocoder.
Source Note: Verified. Exposes a hardware AMBE (DV3000/ThumbDV) vocoder over TCP; configure the AMBE dongle's serial device and the TCP listen port; clients connect over the network. No radio CAT/audio/PTT.

Name: Analog_Bridge
Rig Control Backend: None - DVSwitch audio/codec gateway, no radio CAT
How to Set CAT: n/a - Analog_Bridge.ini config; bridges USRP UDP audio to/from AllStarLink into AMBE/DV networks, with AMBE via md380-emu (software) or a hardware dongle.
Audio Device Select: Uses the USRP UDP audio link to/from AllStarLink (txPort/rxPort in [USRP]), not a local sound card.
PTT Config: n/a - bridging only; keying is handled by the AllStar/MMDVM sides.
Companion Needed: The DVSwitch suite (MMDVM_Bridge, AllStarLink) plus AMBE (md380-emu or a dongle).
Notes: Transcodes analog <-> DV in the DVSwitch chain.
Source Note: Verified against DVSwitch. Analog_Bridge.ini bridges an analog device (USRP UDP audio from AllStarLink's chan_usrp) to AMBE/DV; AMBE via md380-emu (software, default 127.0.0.1:2470) or a hardware dongle. No local sound card, no radio CAT.

Name: APRSGateway
Rig Control Backend: None - passes APRS/GPS frames between DV networks and APRS-IS
How to Set CAT: n/a - config file sets the APRS-IS connection; it forwards GPS/APRS frames embedded in DV traffic.
Audio Device Select: n/a.
PTT Config: n/a.
Companion Needed: MMDVMHost / DV networks; APRS-IS.
Notes: Routes APRS/GPS data carried in DV traffic to APRS-IS.
Source Note: Verified. G4KLX component that forwards APRS/GPS frames between DV networks and APRS-IS; config sets the APRS-IS connection. No radio CAT/audio/PTT.

Name: ASL3 (AllStarLink v3)
Rig Control Backend: None - same CM108/URI fob audio + PTT/COS interface as AllStarLink (not CAT)
How to Set CAT: n/a - v3 uses Debian packages and ASL3 tooling (asl-menu/node config); select the USB CM108/URI device in the simpleusb config.
Audio Device Select: CM108/URI/DINAH fob selected in the simpleusb config; use the asl3 tune tool for RX/TX levels.
PTT Config: PTT/COS keying via the fob's GPIO line.
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a - analog FM; levels set via the tune menu.
Companion Needed: A CM108 URI/DINAH interface plus a radio.
Notes: Modern (Debian 12) AllStarLink release.
Source Note: Verified against the AllStarLink Manual. ASL3 uses the same CM108/URI fob audio + PTT/COS interface as classic AllStarLink (not CAT); installed via Debian packages with ASL3 tooling (asl-menu/asl3 tools). Select the USB CM108/URI device in the simpleusb config; use the asl3 tune tool for RX/TX levels.

Name: BlueDV AMBE / BlueDV Server
Rig Control Backend: None - network AMBE vocoder server, no radio CAT
How to Set CAT: n/a - configure the AMBE3000 dongle's serial port and the network port; exposes the vocoder to BlueDV clients.
Audio Device Select: n/a - AMBE codec over the network, not a sound card.
PTT Config: n/a.
Companion Needed: An AMBE3000 dongle (ThumbDV/DVstick); a BlueDV client.
Notes: Serves a hardware AMBE vocoder over the network.
Source Note: Verified (PA7LIM). Network AMBE vocoder server; configure the AMBE3000 dongle's serial port and the network port to expose the vocoder to BlueDV clients. No radio CAT/audio/PTT.

Name: BlueDV for Android
Rig Control Backend: None - digital voice into internet reflectors (or via a dongle), not transceiver CAT
How to Set CAT: n/a - Settings: select DMR/D-STAR/Fusion networks and reflectors; choose a Bluetooth/USB AMBE dongle or network-only mode.
Audio Device Select: Phone audio for the decoded DV voice; hardware vocoding via a BT/USB AMBE dongle when present.
PTT Config: On-screen PTT keys into the DV reflector (no RF radio unless a dongle/hotspot is used).
Companion Needed: Optional AMBE dongle (network-only works without one); internet DV reflectors.
Notes: DMR/D-STAR/YSF client over internet or dongle.
Source Note: Verified (PA7LIM). Digital-voice into internet reflectors (or via a BT/USB AMBE dongle), not transceiver CAT. Settings select DMR/D-STAR/Fusion networks and reflectors; on-screen PTT keys into the DV reflector.

Name: BlueDV for Windows
Rig Control Backend: None - serial to a hotspot modem or AMBE dongle, not transceiver CAT
How to Set CAT: n/a - Menu > Setup: select the serial COM of a DVMega/BlueStack MMDVM modem (RF hotspot) OR an AMBE3000 dongle (ThumbDV/DVStick/ZUM), set AMBE baud 460800; enter DMR/D-STAR/Fusion IDs and reflectors.
Audio Device Select: PC sound card for decoded DV audio when using an AMBE dongle.
PTT Config: On-screen PTT; a DVMega/BlueStack modem keys the hotspot RF, a dongle stays network-only.
Companion Needed: A DVMega/BlueStack MMDVM modem or an AMBE3000 dongle.
Notes: Multimode digital-voice client for Windows.
Source Note: Verified (PA7LIM). Menu > Setup: enter the COM port for a DVMega/BlueStack MMDVM modem (RF hotspot) OR an AMBE3000 dongle (ThumbDV/DVStick/ZUM), set AMBE baud 460800, and enter DMR/D-STAR/Fusion IDs and reflectors. PC sound card carries decoded DV audio with a dongle. Not transceiver CAT.

Name: BrandMeister server (bmaster)
Rig Control Backend: None - DMR network core, no radio CAT
How to Set CAT: n/a - server-side configuration; routes DMR talkgroups for connected hotspots and repeaters.
Audio Device Select: n/a.
PTT Config: n/a.
Companion Needed: MMDVM hotspots/repeaters connecting in.
Notes: DMR master server software.
Source Note: Verified. DMR network core; server-side configuration routes talkgroups for connected hotspots/repeaters. No radio CAT/audio/PTT.

Name: Buster
Rig Control Backend: None - macOS D-STAR dongle client, not transceiver CAT
How to Set CAT: n/a - select D-STAR reflectors/gateways (DPlus/DExtra/DCS) in the app and point it at a ThumbDV/PiDV AMBE device.
Audio Device Select: Mac audio input/output for voice; hardware AMBE vocoding via a ThumbDV/PiDV.
PTT Config: On-screen/keyboard PTT into the D-STAR network (no RF radio).
Companion Needed: A ThumbDV/DVstick AMBE dongle; D-STAR reflectors.
Notes: macOS D-STAR dongle application.
Source Note: Verified. macOS D-STAR reflector client by Jeremy McDermond (NH6Z); supports DPlus/DExtra/DCS reflectors and repeater links. Uses a ThumbDV/PiDV hardware AMBE device (accessed over the network) for vocoding; Mac audio in/out; on-screen PTT into the D-STAR network. No transceiver CAT.

Name: cBridge (c-Bridge)
Rig Control Backend: None - DMR repeater interconnect/router, no radio CAT
How to Set CAT: n/a - web-admin configuration to interconnect DMR repeaters and route talkgroups.
Audio Device Select: n/a.
PTT Config: n/a.
Companion Needed: DMR repeaters/peers.
Notes: Commercial DMR c-Bridge routing platform.
Source Note: Verified as a DMR repeater interconnect/router (Rayfield Communications); web-admin configuration interconnects DMR repeaters and routes talkgroups. No radio CAT. (Official product page URL not confirmed, so left blank.)

Name: ClearNode app
Rig Control Backend: None - mobile control app for the ClearNode (AllStarLink-based) device
How to Set CAT: n/a - the app manages the ClearNode's links, favorites and DTMF; the node connects to node radios/handhelds.
Audio Device Select: n/a - audio is handled by the ClearNode hardware.
PTT Config: n/a - keying is done by the node.
Companion Needed: A ClearNode device.
Notes: Companion app for the ClearNode hotspot/node.
Source Note: Verified. Mobile control app for the ClearNode (AllStarLink-based hotspot, Node-Ventures). The app manages the node's links, favorites and DTMF; audio and keying are handled by the ClearNode hardware, not the app. No transceiver CAT.

Name: D-RATS
Rig Control Backend: None for voice CAT - a serial link to the D-STAR radio's DV low-speed data port (or a network/ratflector)
How to Set CAT: Preferences > Radio > Add: choose Serial (to the D-STAR radio's data jack) or Network/ratflector; set the port and baud to match the radio.
Audio Device Select: n/a - it uses the DV data channel, not sound-card audio.
PTT Config: No separate PTT line - the D-STAR radio transmits when D-RATS sends data over the DV-data stream.
Companion Needed: A D-STAR radio (with a data port) or a D-RATS ratflector.
Notes: Chat/file/forms over the D-STAR DV low-speed data channel.
Source Note: Verified. Preferences > Radio > Add: choose Type Serial (to the D-STAR radio's DV low-speed data jack) or Network (a ratflector such as ref.d-rats.com); set serial port and baud (must match the radio). It uses the DV data channel, not sound-card audio; the radio transmits when D-RATS sends data.

Name: DAPNET (MMDVM POCSAG paging)
Rig Control Backend: None - POCSAG paging via an MMDVM transmitter, not transceiver CAT
How to Set CAT: n/a - the DAPNET core plus a transmitter node; MMDVMHost config points at the DAPNET server, and the MMDVM modem keys the RF to page receivers.
Audio Device Select: n/a - POCSAG data goes through the MMDVM modem, not a sound card.
PTT Config: PTT asserted by the MMDVM modem/hardware when transmitting POCSAG.
Companion Needed: MMDVMHost plus an MMDVM modem/transmitter; the DAPNET network.
Notes: Amateur POCSAG paging network feeding MMDVMHost.
Source Note: Verified. POCSAG paging via an MMDVM transmitter, not transceiver CAT. A DAPNET core plus a transmitter node; MMDVMHost points at the DAPNET server and the MMDVM modem keys the RF to page receivers. No sound card; PTT asserted by the MMDVM hardware.

Name: DGIdGateway
Rig Control Backend: None - handles the C4FM DV stream by DG-ID, not transceiver CAT
How to Set CAT: n/a - config file maps System Fusion DG-IDs to YSF/FCS reflectors; runs alongside MMDVMHost.
Audio Device Select: n/a - it processes the C4FM DV stream, not sound-card audio.
PTT Config: n/a - RF is keyed by the MMDVM modem via MMDVMHost.
Companion Needed: MMDVMHost plus an MMDVM modem; YSF/FCS reflectors.
Notes: DG-ID-based reflector selection for Yaesu System Fusion.
Source Note: Verified (G4KLX). Config maps System Fusion DG-IDs to YSF/FCS reflectors and runs alongside MMDVMHost; it processes the C4FM DV stream, and RF is keyed by the MMDVM modem via MMDVMHost. No radio CAT.

Name: DMRGateway
Rig Control Backend: None - routes the hotspot's DMR frames between networks, not transceiver CAT
How to Set CAT: n/a - config file with rewrite rules routes DMR frames between multiple networks (BrandMeister/DMR+/TGIF) by talkgroup; sits between MMDVMHost and the networks.
Audio Device Select: n/a.
PTT Config: n/a - RF is handled by the MMDVM modem via MMDVMHost.
Companion Needed: MMDVMHost plus an MMDVM modem; the DMR networks.
Notes: Multi-network DMR router for hotspots.
Source Note: Verified (G4KLX). Config with rewrite rules (e.g. TGRewrite) routes DMR frames between multiple networks (BrandMeister/DMR+/TGIF) by talkgroup; sits between MMDVMHost and the networks. No radio CAT; RF handled by the MMDVM modem via MMDVMHost.

Name: DMRHub
Rig Control Backend: None - self-hosted DMR master/network, no radio CAT
How to Set CAT: n/a - server configuration; acts as a DMR master routing hotspot/repeater traffic (self-hosted, BrandMeister-like).
Audio Device Select: n/a.
PTT Config: n/a.
Companion Needed: MMDVM hotspots/repeaters connecting in.
Notes: Open-source self-hosted DMR master/network server.
Source Note: Verified. Self-hosted DMR master/network (USA-RedDragon/DMRHub); server configuration acts as a DMR master routing hotspot/repeater traffic. No radio CAT/audio/PTT.

Name: DMRlink
Rig Control Backend: none - DMR IPSC network router; links repeaters over IP, not RF.
How to Set CAT: n/a - no CAT. Configure dmrlink.cfg (self-documented INI): IPSC master/peer stanzas (IP, port, network auth, RADIO_ID). Python service.
Audio Device Select: n/a - passes DMR frames only, no audio.
PTT Config: n/a
Companion Needed: none - needs IPSC-speaking repeaters/peers (or HBlink bridge); no radio
Notes: N0MJS Python IPSC bridge/router; server-only. Radio interaction is entirely over the IPSC network protocol.
Source Note: Confirmed against N0MJS DMRlink source; config is INI-format and self-documented.

Name: DroidStar
Rig Control Backend: none (software AMBE/IMBE/Codec2 vocoder client over UDP); optional MMDVM modem in 'MMDVM Direct' stand-alone mode.
How to Set CAT: n/a - no rig CAT. In the app Settings pick Mode (DMR/D-STAR/YSF/FCS/NXDN/P25/M17/AllStar), Host/Reflector, module, callsign and DMR-ID. For MMDVM Direct select the modem's serial/USB port.
Audio Device Select: Phone/PC mic + speaker, or an AMBE USB dongle (ThumbDV/DVstick 30/DVSI) for hardware vocoding.
PTT Config: On-screen TX (PTT) button.
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a (digital-voice over UDP, or modem-set level in MMDVM Direct).
Companion Needed: a DV reflector/network; optional AMBE dongle or MMDVM modem + HT for actual RF
Notes: AD8DP. Talks DV over the internet; no traditional transceiver CAT.
Source Note: Corrected: MMDVM Direct (stand-alone transceiver via the MMDVM modem) is supported for M17 AND DMR, not M17 only. Otherwise confirmed against DroidStar README (nostar/AD8B).

Name: DStarGateway
Rig Control Backend: none - D-STAR gateway service (a wxWidgets-free port of ircddbGateway by F4FXL).
How to Set CAT: n/a - no CAT. Edit the .cfg (example.cfg/dstargateway.cfg): gateway/repeater callsign, ircDDB, DPlus/DExtra/DCS/G2 link settings, hosts files. Talks to an RF node over the network, not a rig.
Audio Device Select: n/a
PTT Config: n/a
Companion Needed: a D-STAR RF node (DStarRepeater/MMDVMHost/DVAP) to feed it
Notes: F4FXL modern replacement for ircDDBGateway; network gateway only.
Source Note: Confirmed against F4FXL/DStarGateway README; feature-parity with G4KLX ircddbGateway.

Name: DStarRepeater
Rig Control Backend: native D-STAR modem/node-adapter driver (not transceiver CAT).
How to Set CAT: n/a for a rig. In its config GUI / dstarrepeater.ini choose modem type (GMSK Modem, DVAP, DV-Mega, DV-RPTR v1/2/3, MMDVM) and its serial/USB port, plus RF frequency and repeater callsign (mmdvmPort, dvrptr1Port, RX/TX invert, levels, TX delay).
Audio Device Select: n/a - handles digital DV baseband via the modem.
PTT Config: Keyed by the RF modem over serial/USB.
Companion Needed: ircDDBGateway (for the network) + a D-STAR RF modem/node adapter
Notes: G4KLX/OpenDV. Drives the RF node hardware and feeds the gateway.
Source Note: Confirmed against G4KLX DStarRepeater source and example ini (supported modems match).

Name: DUDE-Star
Rig Control Backend: none (software vocoder client; predecessor of DroidStar, by nostar/AD8B).
How to Set CAT: n/a - no rig CAT. Desktop UI: pick mode (DMR/D-STAR/YSF/NXDN/P25/M17), host/reflector, module and callsign.
Audio Device Select: PC sound card mic/speaker, or a DVSI AMBE3000 USB dongle (ThumbDV) for hardware vocoding.
PTT Config: On-screen PTT.
Companion Needed: a DV reflector/network; optional AMBE dongle
Notes: AD8DP desktop predecessor of DroidStar; DV over UDP to reflectors, no local RF.
Source Note: Confirmed - DUDE-Star (nostar/dudestar) is the desktop forerunner of DroidStar; same config model.

Name: DudeShield
Rig Control Backend: none (software - or optional hardware AMBE - vocoder client, DUDE-Star family).
How to Set CAT: n/a - no rig CAT. App UI: select mode, reflector/talkgroup, callsign/DMR-ID.
Audio Device Select: PC/Android mic + speaker; optional hardware AMBE dongle.
PTT Config: On-screen PTT.
Companion Needed: DV reflectors; optional AMBE dongle
Notes: BlueDV-style client into DV reflectors; no local RF or CAT.
Source Note: Confirmed conceptually as a DUDE-Star-family DV client; exact upstream repo/URL not verified with certainty, so URL left blank.

Name: DummyRepeater
Rig Control Backend: none - uses a DV Dongle/ThumbDV (DV3000) AMBE hardware vocoder, not a rig (by G4KLX).
How to Set CAT: n/a - no rig CAT. Config: select the DV Dongle/ThumbDV serial port plus your callsign/module; connect through ircDDBGateway.
Audio Device Select: PC sound card mic/speaker for voice; the DV Dongle/ThumbDV supplies AMBE encode/decode.
PTT Config: On-screen PTT.
Companion Needed: ircDDBGateway + a DV Dongle/ThumbDV
Notes: G4KLX. Turns the PC into a 'repeater' onto the D-STAR network with no actual RF.
Source Note: Confirmed - G4KLX DummyRepeater acts as an RF-less D-STAR node via a DV Dongle/ThumbDV and ircDDBGateway.

Name: DV4mini control software
Rig Control Backend: native USB control of the DV4mini RF stick (a ~10-12 mW 70cm transceiver), not transceiver CAT.
How to Set CAT: n/a - no rig CAT. In the DV4mini Control Panel (or DV4MF2 by DL2MF) pick the DV4mini COM/USB port, mode (D-STAR/DMR/C4FM/P25/APRS), RF frequency (420-450 MHz) and reflector.
Audio Device Select: n/a - the low-power stick modulates DV to your handheld over RF; audio is your HT's.
PTT Config: Your DV handheld keys normally; the stick is a low-power transceiver.
Companion Needed: a DV-capable handheld tuned to the stick's frequency; the DV network
Notes: USB 10mW multimode hotspot stick.
Source Note: Confirmed against the DV4mini user guide / BrandMeister wiki; control software is the DV4mini Control Panel (Windows/Linux-Mono), plus the DV4MF2 alternative.

Name: DVAPTool
Rig Control Backend: native USB control of the DVAP (DV Access Point) dongle - a ~10 mW D-STAR GMSK access point, not transceiver CAT.
How to Set CAT: n/a - no rig CAT. DVAPTool: select the DVAP FTDI device from the dropdown and Open, set a simplex RF frequency (in Hz) and callsign (with 'A' in char 8). Links via ircDDBGateway (DPlus/DExtra/DCS/CCS).
Audio Device Select: n/a - your D-STAR handheld supplies voice/audio over RF.
PTT Config: Your D-STAR HT keys the DVAP; ~10 mW GMSK.
Companion Needed: a D-STAR handheld + ircDDBGateway/DVAPTool for the network
Notes: DVAP Dongle 2m/70cm ~10mW D-STAR access point.
Source Note: Confirmed against dvapdongle.com 'Using the DVAP'. DVAP is D-STAR-only.

Name: DVSwitch DIAL image
Rig Control Backend: none - bridge/transcode turnkey Debian image bundling the DVSwitch Server tools.
How to Set CAT: n/a - no CAT. Preconfigured image; configure Analog_Bridge.ini / MMDVM_Bridge.ini and the AMBE vocoder.
Audio Device Select: USRP audio between components; no direct sound card to a rig unless via an AllStar node.
PTT Config: n/a directly (keying happens on the bridged node/modem).
Companion Needed: AMBE dongle or ambed; an AllStar node radio or MMDVM modem for RF
Notes: 'DIAL' turnkey DVSwitch install image; same bridging/transcoding role.
Source Note: Confirmed against DVSwitch documentation.

Name: DVSwitch Mobile
Rig Control Backend: none - client app connecting to a DVSwitch/USRP server.
How to Set CAT: n/a - no CAT. App: enter the DVSwitch Server address/port and select talkgroup/mode.
Audio Device Select: Phone mic + speaker (full-duplex).
PTT Config: On-screen PTT (low-latency).
Companion Needed: a DVSwitch Server to connect to
Notes: Android front-end to DVSwitch Server; no local RF or CAT.
Source Note: Confirmed against DVSwitch documentation.

Name: DVSwitch Server (Analog_Bridge + MMDVM_Bridge)
Rig Control Backend: none - transcode/bridge server.
How to Set CAT: n/a - no CAT. Edit Analog_Bridge.ini ([USRP] address/txPort/rxPort/audio mode, [AMBE_AUDIO] ambeMode) and MMDVM_Bridge.ini (network endpoints). MMDVM_Bridge <-> Analog_Bridge on UDP 31100/31103.
Audio Device Select: USRP audio to AllStar node radios; MMDVM_Bridge handles the DV-network side.
PTT Config: Keying occurs via the bridged node/modem, not direct CAT.
Companion Needed: AMBE dongle or ambed; an AllStar node radio or MMDVM modem for RF
Notes: Core DVSwitch transcoder/bridge; reaches RF only through bridged systems.
Source Note: Confirmed against DVSwitch/Analog_Bridge source and wiki.

Name: DVTool (DV Dongle)
Rig Control Backend: none - AMBE hardware vocoder (DV Dongle, Internet Labs/AA4RC) over USB.
How to Set CAT: n/a - no rig CAT. DVTool: select the DV Dongle serial port, gateway/reflector and callsign.
Audio Device Select: PC sound card mic/speaker; the DV Dongle performs AMBE encode/decode.
PTT Config: On-screen PTT.
Companion Needed: the DV Dongle hardware + a D-STAR gateway/reflector
Notes: Original DVTool/DV Dongle app; network D-STAR only, no RF.
Source Note: Confirmed - DVTool is the PC app for the DV Dongle (dvdongle.com).

Name: EchoLink (Windows)
Rig Control Backend: none for single-user; in Sysop mode it keys a node radio via sound card + serial/VOX PTT (not rig CAT).
How to Set CAT: n/a - no rig CAT. Sysop mode: Tools > Sysop Settings > RX Ctrl (VOX or serial COS/CTS) and TX Ctrl (serial COM using RTS or DTR, e.g. RIGblaster); pick the audio device under Tools > Setup > Audio.
Audio Device Select: Windows sound device in Tools > Setup > Audio (line in/out to the node radio).
PTT Config: Serial COM RTS/DTR to the node radio (2400/9600 bps preset), or VOX; carrier/COS detect on RX.
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a - analog FM node; set audio so FM deviation is correct, not ALC.
Companion Needed: none for network use; Sysop mode needs an FM node radio + PC interface
Notes: Single-user is internet-only (no radio); Sysop mode is a link/repeater node.
Source Note: Confirmed against the EchoLink User's Guide / Sysop docs (RTS or DTR asserts PTT).

Name: EchoLink for Android
Rig Control Backend: none - pure VoIP client.
How to Set CAT: n/a - no CAT. Connects over the internet to remote RF EchoLink node radios.
Audio Device Select: Phone mic + speaker.
PTT Config: On-screen PTT.
Companion Needed: none - talks to distant EchoLink node radios
Notes: No local radio or CAT.
Source Note: Confirmed - official EchoLink mobile client (Synergenics/K1RFD).

Name: HAMVOIP
Rig Control Backend: none in the CAT sense; an AllStarLink distro using a USB CM108/CM119 sound-fob channel driver.
How to Set CAT: n/a - no rig CAT. Edit rpt.conf + simpleusb.conf (or usbradio.conf): choose the CM108 device, set RX (COS/carrier or VOX) and TX (PTT via the fob's GPIO, active-low default). Tune with simpleusb-tune-menu.
Audio Device Select: CM108/CM119 USB sound fob to the node radio's mic/speaker.
PTT Config: PTT line on the CM108 fob (GPIO), active-low by default; COS/COR detect on RX.
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a - analog FM; set audio/deviation levels, not ALC.
Companion Needed: an FM node radio + a USB sound-fob interface
Notes: Raspberry Pi AllStarLink build (app_rpt).
Source Note: Confirmed against hamvoip.org and the AllStarLink simpleusb.conf manual.

Name: HBLink3
Rig Control Backend: none - DMR (Homebrew/HBP) router/server.

Audio Device Select: n/a
PTT Config: n/a

Notes: Monitoring UI only; no radio.

Name: Icom RS-MS3A / RS-MS3W
Rig Control Backend: native Icom DV-Gateway link over USB serial (not generic Hamlib CAT).
How to Set CAT: n/a in the Hamlib sense. Connect the Icom D-STAR radio (ID-51/52/5100/705 etc.) by USB (or Bluetooth on Android for RS-MS3A); run Terminal mode or Access Point mode. Set Gateway Repeater address, Terminal/AP callsign, Gateway Type and UDP Hole Punch in the app.
Audio Device Select: n/a - the radio handles voice/AMBE over RF; the app carries the DV data over IP.
PTT Config: The radio keys normally; the app bridges to the internet.
Companion Needed: an Icom D-STAR radio with DV Gateway (Terminal/Access-Point) mode
Notes: Icom Android/Windows internet-gateway app for callsign routing/reflectors.
Source Note: Confirmed against the Icom RS-MS3A/RS-MS3W DV Gateway (Terminal/Access Point) instruction manuals. RS-MS3A = Android, RS-MS3W = Windows.

Name: IPSC2 / DMRplus master
Rig Control Backend: none - DMR master server.
How to Set CAT: n/a - no CAT. Routes DMR traffic + reflectors for connected repeaters/hotspots via server config.

Notes: DMRplus network master; server only.

Name: ircDDBGateway
Rig Control Backend: none - D-STAR network gateway (G4KLX).
How to Set CAT: n/a - no CAT. GUI/config: set the gateway callsign, repeater band(s) and links (ircDDB, DExtra/DCS/DPlus/CCS). Communicates with DStarRepeater/MMDVMHost/DVAP over the network, not a rig.
Audio Device Select: n/a
PTT Config: n/a
Companion Needed: a D-STAR RF node (DStarRepeater/MMDVMHost/DVAP)
Notes: G4KLX D-STAR network gateway.

Name: IRLP
Rig Control Backend: none - Linux node using the IRLP interface board.
How to Set CAT: n/a - no rig CAT. Node scripts/environment: sound card device + PTT via the board's parallel/serial-port lines; COS from the radio's COR into the board (COS HIGH/LOW jumper for active-high/low).
Audio Device Select: PC sound card to the node radio (line in/out).
PTT Config: Parallel/serial-port PTT via the IRLP interface board; COS/COR detect on RX.
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a - analog FM node; set audio/deviation, not ALC.
Companion Needed: the IRLP interface board + an FM node radio
Notes: Internet Radio Linking Project; dedicated hardware board + Linux node.
Source Note: Confirmed against irlp.net board manual (parallel-port interface board, COS jumper).

Name: m17-cxx-demod / M17 tools
Rig Control Backend: none - CLI baseband modem (mobilinkd).
How to Set CAT: n/a - no CAT. Command-line m17-mod/m17-demod pipe audio to/from the rig's sound path (m17-mod outputs 48 ksps 16-bit baseband; m17-demod reads it back); set the frequency manually on the rig.
Audio Device Select: Sound card baseband in/out (raw/WAV via stdin/stdout, e.g. sox/play) to the transceiver.
PTT Config: External - VOX, or a separate PTT script/interface.
ALC/Power Guidance: Treat like any 4-FSK digital signal: set TX audio so the rig isn't overdriven; keep it clean (ALC low).
Companion Needed: an FM/SSB rig with a sound interface; often paired with other M17 tools
Notes: Mobilinkd/M17 baseband encoder/decoder CLI.
Source Note: Confirmed against mobilinkd/m17-cxx-demod README (48 ksps 4-FSK baseband pipeline).

Name: M17Gateway
Rig Control Backend: none - M17 network gateway (G4KLX).
How to Set CAT: n/a - no CAT. Edit M17Gateway.ini (reflectors/hosts); works alongside MMDVMHost, which drives the modem/RF.
Audio Device Select: n/a
PTT Config: n/a

Notes: Relays the M17 stream to reflectors.
Source Note: Confirmed - part of the G4KLX MMDVM ecosystem.

Name: MMDVM cross-mode gateways (YSF2DMR, YSF2NXDN, YSF2P25, DMR2YSF, DMR2NXDN, NXDN2DMR)
Rig Control Backend: none - protocol transcoders (Andy CA6JAU / juribeparada, MMDVM_CM).
How to Set CAT: n/a - no CAT. Edit each gateway's .ini (network endpoints, DMR IDs); cross-vocoder transcoding uses a hardware AMBE dongle (ThumbDV) or software.

Companion Needed: MMDVMHost + a modem for RF; an AMBE dongle for cross-vocoder transcode
Notes: Convert the radio's DV frames between YSF/DMR/NXDN/P25 mid-stream.
Source Note: Confirmed - these are the MMDVM_CM cross-mode gateways.

Name: MMDVM firmware
Rig Control Backend: none - runs on the modem's MCU (G4KLX MMDVM / MMDVM_HS).
How to Set CAT: n/a - no CAT. Flashed to the MMDVM board (STM32 etc.); generates/receives the RF DV baseband and talks to MMDVMHost over serial. RX/TX levels tuned with MMDVMCal and stored in MMDVM.ini.
Audio Device Select: n/a - DV baseband through the modem's ADC/DAC to a radio's discriminator/mic.
PTT Config: Keyed by the modem hardware.
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a - the analog interface levels are set in calibration, not rig ALC.
Companion Needed: MMDVMHost on the host; a radio/board for RF
Notes: The on-modem firmware itself.
Source Note: Confirmed against G4KLX MMDVM firmware source/wiki.

Name: MMDVM_Bridge
Rig Control Backend: none - network bridge (DVSwitch).
How to Set CAT: n/a - no CAT. Edit MMDVM_Bridge.ini: presents as an MMDVMHost to DV networks but routes to Analog_Bridge/DVSwitch instead of a modem.
Audio Device Select: USRP audio to Analog_Bridge (no direct rig).
PTT Config: n/a
Companion Needed: DVSwitch Analog_Bridge; an AMBE dongle/ambed
Notes: DVSwitch network-side bridge; no RF of its own.
Source Note: Confirmed against DVSwitch MMDVM_Bridge.

Name: MMDVMCal
Rig Control Backend: none - modem calibration tool (G4KLX).
How to Set CAT: n/a - no rig CAT. Runs on the host over serial to the MMDVM/MMDVM_HS modem (add the serial port on the command line); keys test carriers/patterns and steps TX level, RX offset and deviation (Bessel-zero) for tuning.
Audio Device Select: n/a
PTT Config: Keys the modem's TX to emit test patterns.
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a - this IS the level-tuning tool for the modem; the resulting TX/RX level & offset values go into MMDVM.ini.
Companion Needed: an MMDVM modem to calibrate
Notes: Produces the RXOffset/TXLevel/deviation values you then put in MMDVM.ini.
Source Note: Confirmed against G4KLX MMDVMCal README and MMDVM calibration wiki.

Name: MMDVMHost
Rig Control Backend: none - drives the MMDVM/MMDVM_HS/DVMega modem (G4KLX).
How to Set CAT: n/a - no rig CAT. Edit MMDVM.ini [Modem]: Protocol (uart/udp/i2c) + UARTPort (e.g. /dev/ttyACM0) & UARTSpeed, RX/TX levels & offsets; enable modes (DMR/D-STAR/YSF/P25/NXDN/M17/POCSAG/FM); [Display] Nextion/OLED.
Audio Device Select: n/a - the modem handles the DV baseband/RF.
PTT Config: Keyed by the modem.
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a - modem TX level is set in the .ini / via MMDVMCal.
Companion Needed: an MMDVM/DVMega modem + network gateways (ircDDBGateway/YSFGateway/etc.)
Notes: G4KLX host that ties the modem to the DV networks.
Source Note: Confirmed; note current MMDVM.ini uses [Modem] Protocol=uart + UARTPort=... (older builds used a single 'Port=' key).

Name: Pi-Star
Rig Control Backend: none - drives an MMDVM/MMDVM_HS/DVMega modem.
How to Set CAT: n/a - no rig CAT. Web dashboard: Configuration > General > select Radio/Modem Type + (for display) port, enable modes, and set network/reflector options; this writes MMDVM.ini. The modem does the RF.
Audio Device Select: n/a
PTT Config: Keyed by the modem.
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a - modem RF level is set in configuration.
Companion Needed: an MMDVM/DVMega hotspot modem + a DV handheld
Notes: Turnkey hotspot distro (web UI over MMDVMHost).
Source Note: Confirmed against Pi-Star dashboard docs (Radio/Modem Type in General Configuration).

Name: QnetGateway (g2_ircddb / qnet)
Rig Control Backend: none - D-STAR node/gateway (n7tae).
How to Set CAT: n/a - no rig CAT. Config scripts (qn.cfg): choose the RF device (DVAP/MMDVM/DV-RPTR) or an AMBE dongle, plus callsign/modules; links to the D-STAR network.
Audio Device Select: PC sound only when using an AMBE-dongle voice path; otherwise DV via the modem.
PTT Config: Via the modem/dongle.
Companion Needed: a DVAP/MMDVM modem or a DVSI AMBE dongle
Notes: N7TAE QnetGateway D-STAR node/gateway.
Source Note: Confirmed against n7tae/QnetGateway.

Name: Qtel
Rig Control Backend: none - EchoLink VoIP client (part of SvxLink, SM0SVX).
How to Set CAT: n/a - no CAT. Qtel Settings dialog (shown on first run, or Settings > Qtel settings): callsign, name, password, location/info, and audio device (from 'arecord -L', e.g. alsa:plughw:CARD=Device,DEV=0). Callsign must be verified on echolink.org.
Audio Device Select: PC sound card mic/speaker (set AUDIO_DEV in Qtel settings; no in-app level controls - use alsamixer).
PTT Config: On-screen PTT.
Companion Needed: none - network to RF EchoLink nodes
Notes: No local radio or CAT.
Source Note: Confirmed against the SvxLink Qtel user docs.

Name: SharkRF openSPOT (firmware + web config)
Rig Control Backend: none - self-contained hotspot device (its own internal modem).
How to Set CAT: n/a - no rig CAT. Browse to the openSPOT web UI (or use the SharkRF app / AP mode wizard): set the modem RX/TX frequency and TX power, mode, reflector/talkgroup and network/connector profiles. Its modem talks to your handheld over RF.
Audio Device Select: n/a - your DV handheld provides voice over RF.
PTT Config: Your handheld keys the device.
Companion Needed: a DV handheld tuned to the openSPOT; a DV network
Notes: Standalone multimode hotspot; the SharkRF app assists configuration.
Source Note: Confirmed against the SharkRF openSPOT user manuals.

Name: Supermon / Allmon2 / AllScan
Rig Control Backend: none - AllStar/app_rpt web dashboards (no rig CAT)
How to Set CAT: n/a. These PHP dashboards issue DTMF/CLI commands to Asterisk app_rpt (link/monitor/key the node). The node channel driver (simpleusb/usbradio/USRP) handles the radio, not the dashboard.
Audio Device Select: n/a - handled by app_rpt / the node's USB fob (simpleusb.conf/usbradio.conf)
PTT Config: n/a - handled by app_rpt / the node interface
Companion Needed: an AllStarLink/app_rpt node
Notes: Management/monitor web UIs for AllStar nodes.
Source Note: Confirmed as claimed. AllScan/Supermon/Allmon2 are management front-ends for AllStarLink nodes; radio I/O is entirely in app_rpt.

Name: SvxLink server
Rig Control Backend: none in the rig-CAT sense; direct sound card + serial/GPIO/CM108 PTT to a transceiver
How to Set CAT: n/a - no rig CAT. Edit svxlink.conf [Rx]/[Tx] sections: AUDIO_DEV (alsa:plughw:x / pulse), SQL_DET (VOX/CTCSS/SERIAL/GPIO), PTT_TYPE (GPIO/GPIOD/SerialPin/Dummy) with PTT_PIN.
Audio Device Select: sound card to the transceiver via AUDIO_DEV (with AUDIO_CHANNEL 0/1 for stereo-as-2-mono)
PTT Config: PTT_TYPE = GPIO/GPIOD (PTT_PIN gpioNN), SerialPin (DTR/RTS), CM108 USB, or Dummy; squelch via SQL_DET
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a - analog FM; set audio/deviation levels, not ALC
Companion Needed: an FM transceiver + interface (GPIO/serial/CM108); can run a repeater
Notes: Linux repeater/link controller (also does EchoLink via ModuleEchoLink).
Source Note: Confirmed: AUDIO_DEV, PTT_TYPE (GPIO/SerialPin/Dummy) and PTT_PIN verified against the man page. Newer builds add PTT_TYPE=GPIOD (PTTGPIODCHIP/PTTGPIODLINE).

Name: SvxReflector
Rig Control Backend: none (audio reflector server for SvxLink nodes)
How to Set CAT: n/a - no CAT. Routes audio among SvxLink ReflectorLogic nodes over IP; edit svxreflector.conf.
Audio Device Select: n/a
PTT Config: n/a
Companion Needed: SvxLink nodes
Notes: Reflector for SvxLink networks; no radio itself.
Source Note: Confirmed - server-only, no radio interface.

Name: thebridge
Rig Control Backend: none (EchoLink conference/bridge server)
How to Set CAT: n/a - no CAT. Bridges/conferences EchoLink audio; config file (thebridge.conf).
Audio Device Select: n/a
PTT Config: n/a
Companion Needed: EchoLink clients/nodes
Notes: Conference bridge server; no radio.
Source Note: Confirmed - network conference server, no rig interface. No stable official URL found.

Name: thelinkbox
Rig Control Backend: none; sound card + PTT/COS interface to link radios (not rig CAT)
How to Set CAT: n/a - no rig CAT. In tlb.conf define each radio port's audio device and PTT/COS lines.
Audio Device Select: PC sound card / USB audio dongle (incl. CM108) to the link/repeater radio
PTT Config: PTT via serial RTS/DTR, parallel (IRLP), WB2REM/VA3TO board, or USB-dongle GPIO; COS via VOX, serial CTS/DSR/DCD, parallel, or USB HID/GPIO
Companion Needed: an FM link/repeater radio + interface
Notes: VoIP link/repeater controller (SvxLink-like).
Source Note: Confirmed: tlb.conf with serial/parallel/GPIO PTT and CTS/DSR/DCD/VOX COS verified.

Name: urfd (URF Reflector)
Rig Control Backend: none (multiprotocol DV reflector server)
How to Set CAT: n/a - no CAT. Routes/transcodes D-STAR/DMR/YSF/NXDN/P25/M17 among hotspots; transcoding via a vocoder (tcd/ambe).
Audio Device Select: n/a
PTT Config: n/a
Companion Needed: DV hotspots/repeaters + ambed for transcoding
Notes: Universal reflector (xlxd successor).
Source Note: Confirmed - reflector server.

Name: WinDV / DV-RPTR software
Rig Control Backend: none - drives the DV-RPTR modem board over serial/USB (the modem does the D-STAR RF)
How to Set CAT: n/a - no rig CAT. Select the DV-RPTR COM port and mode; the modem/attached radio handles RF.
Audio Device Select: n/a - DV baseband via the modem, not a PC sound card
PTT Config: keyed by the DV-RPTR modem / attached radio
Companion Needed: a DV-RPTR modem board + a D-STAR gateway
Notes: Config/host software for the DV-RPTR modem.
Source Note: Confirmed by design (hardware DV modem). No current official doc URL found.

Name: Wires-X (Yaesu)
Rig Control Backend: native Yaesu link via the HRI-200 connection kit (USB to PC; carries CAT + audio + PTT to the node radio)
How to Set CAT: n/a for generic Hamlib. Node = PC + HRI-200 + a Fusion node radio; the HRI-200 connects to the PC by USB and to the radio, and the WIRES-X software reads the radio/interface ID.
Audio Device Select: routed through the HRI-200 (or the radio's USB codec)
PTT Config: keyed via the HRI-200/CAT to the node radio
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a - C4FM digital node; set levels per Yaesu guidance
Companion Needed: an HRI-200 interface + a Fusion node radio (or DR-2X repeater)
Notes: Yaesu proprietary Fusion node/room software.
Source Note: Confirmed. HRI-200 is USB-connected and powered from the PC.

Name: WIRES-X Portable Digital Node (PDN)
Rig Control Backend: native Yaesu radio USB link (no HRI-200 required)
How to Set CAT: n/a for generic Hamlib. In PDN mode connect a PDN-capable C4FM radio (e.g. FTM-100/300, FT-70/FT3 via SCU-39/USB); no HRI-200 and no global IP needed. WIRES-X reads the radio ID.
Audio Device Select: via the radio's USB audio codec
PTT Config: keyed over the radio's USB/CAT
Companion Needed: a PDN-capable Yaesu Fusion radio + USB cable
Notes: HRI-200-less Portable Digital Node.
Source: Yaesu WIRES-X Portable Digital Node Function Manual
Source Note: Confirmed - PDN connects the transceiver directly by USB and does not use the HRI-200.

Name: WPSD (W0CHP-PiStar-Dash)
Rig Control Backend: none - drives an MMDVM/DVMega/hotspot modem (the modem does the RF)
How to Set CAT: n/a - no rig CAT. WPSD web dashboard > Configuration: set Radio/Modem type + serial port + modes + networks (writes MMDVMHost config). Use 'sudo wpsd-detectmodem' to identify the board.
Audio Device Select: n/a
PTT Config: keyed by the modem
Companion Needed: an MMDVM/DVMega hotspot modem + a DV handheld
Notes: Modern Pi-Star fork; same modem-over-serial control.
Source Note: Confirmed against the WPSD manual.

Name: xlxd (XLX Reflector)
Rig Control Backend: none (reflector server)
How to Set CAT: n/a - no CAT. Reflects D-STAR/DMR/YSF; transcodes via ambed (DVSI AMBE hardware).
Audio Device Select: n/a
PTT Config: n/a
Companion Needed: DV hotspots/repeaters; ambed + AMBE hardware for transcoding
Notes: XLX multiprotocol reflector.
Source Note: Confirmed - reflector server.

Name: YSFGateway
Rig Control Backend: none (network gateway)
How to Set CAT: n/a - no CAT. Edit YSFGateway.ini (YSF/FCS reflector lists); relays C4FM from MMDVMHost.
Audio Device Select: n/a
PTT Config: n/a
Companion Needed: MMDVMHost + a modem
Notes: Bridges a Fusion hotspot to YSF/FCS rooms.
Source Note: Confirmed - gateway, no radio interface.

Name: YSFReflector
Rig Control Backend: none (reflector server)
How to Set CAT: n/a - no CAT. Routes C4FM streams among Fusion hotspots/repeaters; config file (YSFReflector.ini).
Audio Device Select: n/a
PTT Config: n/a
Companion Needed: YSF hotspots/repeaters
Notes: Reflector server.
Source Note: Confirmed - reflector server.

Name: Codec 2
Rig Control Backend: none (codec library + CLI tools)
How to Set CAT: n/a - no CAT. c2enc/c2dec and freedv_tx/freedv_rx read/write raw or WAV audio via files or stdin/stdout; no sound-device or rig control of their own.
Audio Device Select: n/a - file/pipe based; you route to a sound card with other tools
PTT Config: n/a
Companion Needed: a host app/rig sound path if used live; it is a library
Notes: David Rowe's low-bitrate voice codec used by FreeDV/M17/etc.
Source Note: Confirmed - it is a vocoder/codec library, not a radio-control app.

Name: FreeDV / freedv-gui
Rig Control Backend: Hamlib (serial or TCP to rigctld/FLrig) for CAT/PTT; or direct serial RTS/DTR; or VOX
How to Set CAT: Tools > CAT and PTT Config: choose Hamlib, pick the rig model + serial port + serial rate (or a TCP host:port for SDRs/FLrig/rigctld); press Test to populate Serial Params. Hamlib also sets/reads frequency.
Audio Device Select: Tools > Audio Config - two sound cards: 'Transmit/Receive To/From Radio' (the interface) plus the mic/speaker/headset device
PTT Config: Tools > CAT and PTT Config - Hamlib CAT PTT, a serial port's RTS/DTR pin, or VOX (tone on the left Tx channel)
ALC/Power Guidance: FreeDV is a multi-carrier linear signal: use USB/DATA mode and raise TX drive so RF out is clean with ALC barely moving - never overdrive/clip, which wrecks the waveform
Companion Needed: none required (self-contained); needs an SSB rig + two audio paths
Notes: Reference FreeDV app (700D/700E/1600/2020 modes).
Source Note: Corrected: the CAT/PTT dialog is 'Tools > CAT and PTT Config' (audio is 'Tools > Audio Config') - the claimed 'Tools > Options' is not where CAT is set. Backend (Hamlib serial/TCP + serial RTS-DTR + VOX), two-soundcard model and ALC guidance are otherwise correct.

Name: mvoice (M17)
Rig Control Backend: none - software M17 gateway/client using David Rowe's Codec2 (connects to M17 reflectors over IP)
How to Set CAT: n/a - no rig CAT. In mvoice Settings set your callsign + codec on the M17 page and pick a reflector/module; audio uses the default PulseAudio/ALSA device.
Audio Device Select: USB headset via PulseAudio/ALSA (leave on 'default'); Codec2 vocoder in software
PTT Config: on-screen PTT (toggle) and Echo Test buttons; it is a network client, so no serial/rig PTT is involved
Companion Needed: the M17 reflector network; optional rig or hotspot for RF
Notes: AD8DP M17 client (same tool as the hotspot-category 'mvoice' entry).
Source Note: Confirmed, with clarification: mvoice is primarily a network M17 client driven by a USB headset and an on-screen PTT button, not an RF-rig driver; the claim's 'external interface for PTT when feeding a rig' overstates its role.

Name: QRadioLink
Rig Control Backend: native SDR transceiver control via GNU Radio / gr-osmosdr (RTL-SDR, USRP, HackRF, BladeRF, LimeSDR, Pluto, etc.)
How to Set CAT: n/a in the classic sense. In-app: select the SDR device, set RX/TX frequency, mode (Codec2 700/1400 DV, Opus, SSB, FM, etc.) and gains.
Audio Device Select: PC sound card mic/speaker; the SDR carries the RF I/Q; Codec2/Opus vocoders in software
PTT Config: on-screen TX / keyboard PTT that keys the SDR transmit path
ALC/Power Guidance: set TX gain so the SDR PA isn't clipped; keep the modulated signal clean (no analog ALC)
Companion Needed: an SDR transceiver (HackRF/LimeSDR/USRP/PlutoSDR)
Notes: YO4EGA Linux SDR transceiver app with Codec2 DV support.
Source Note: Confirmed - a GNU Radio-based SDR transceiver focused on Codec2/Opus digital voice on VHF+.

Name: Aether
Rig Control Backend: native built-in RS-232/USB rig drivers (Elecraft/Icom/Kenwood/Yaesu, some TEN-TEC); not Hamlib
How to Set CAT: Preferences > Rig Control tab: tick 'Enable Rig Control', select the radio brand, choose the serial port of your CAT interface, and set the baud to match the radio's menu. A USB-to-serial adapter is normally required on a Mac.
Audio Device Select: n/a for logging; digital modes are handled by companion apps (cocoaModem/fldigi) which own the sound device
PTT Config: not a primary function (a logger reading freq/mode/power); PTT via the digital companion if used
Companion Needed: none for logging; cocoaModem or fldigi for digital modes
Notes: macOS logger; auto-fills frequency/mode from the rig.
Source Note: Confirmed. Precise location is Preferences > 'Rig Control' tab (the claim said 'Radio (Rig)'). Native brand drivers + serial port + matched baud verified.

Name: BBLogger
Rig Control Backend: native CAT rig drivers built in
How to Set CAT: In the CAT/rig setup select the transceiver model + COM port + baud (+ data/stop bits); use its test/connect to confirm the frequency reads back. Reads freq/mode into the log.
Audio Device Select: n/a for logging (digital handled by WSJT-X/JTDX/MSHV, which BBLogger integrates with)
PTT Config: via CAT where the rig supports it; mainly reads freq/mode
Companion Needed: none required; integrates WSJT-X/JTDX/MSHV
Notes: Freeware IK2VIW/IZ2BKT/IK2UVR logger; captures freq/mode via CAT.
Source: BBLogger documentation (Windows logger with built-in rig control)
Source Note: Confirmed native rig-control backend (BBLogger is a free Win10/11 logger with rig control + cluster). No stable official-manual URL located; exact menu label not independently verified.

Name: Cloudlog
Rig Control Backend: none native - a companion CAT bridge POSTs freq/mode to Cloudlog's Radio API (CloudlogCAT+Omnirig on Windows; rigctld-based scripts on Linux/Mac)
How to Set CAT: Cloudlog is a self-hosted web app. In the web UI, create an API key (click your callsign > API keys). Then run a companion - CloudlogCAT (Omnirig) on Windows, or cloudlog-rigctl-interface/CloudlogBashCat/CloudLogPyCAT (rigctld/flrig) on Linux/Mac - and configure the rig model/port THERE; it POSTs to Cloudlog's /api/radio with the API key.
Audio Device Select: n/a
PTT Config: n/a
Companion Needed: Omni-Rig, or Hamlib rigctld + a bridge script (Cloudlog CAT / cloudlog-rigctl-interface)
Notes: PHP/MySQL self-hosted logger; live radio only via its Radio Interface API.
Source Note: Confirmed: CAT is external (Omnirig via CloudlogCAT, or rigctld scripts) posting to the Radio API; the rig itself is configured in the companion, not in Cloudlog.

Name: CommCat
Rig Control Backend: native built-in rig drivers, with PTT
How to Set CAT: File > Settings > Radio (Radio Connections): pick the radio model, the Serial Port (1-255) and Baud (fixed-parameter radios are set automatically); for Icom a Radio window sets the CI-V address/handshaking. Click Test - the rig's frequency appears if the link is good. (The Rig toolbar is shown via right-click on the Navigation toolbar > Rig.)
Audio Device Select: PC sound card for its digital/CW features
PTT Config: via CAT (plus a programmable CW keyboard)
ALC/Power Guidance: for digital/CW keep audio drive modest so ALC barely moves
Companion Needed: none required
Notes: COMMSOFT (W7DK) Windows suite: rig control + DX spotting + CW keyboard + maps.
Source: CommCat Help - Radio Connections and Settings
Source Note: Confirmed: serial port + baud + Test, and Icom CI-V address dialog verified against the CommCat help.

Name: CQRLOG
Rig Control Backend: Hamlib via rigctld
How to Set CAT: File > Preferences > TRX control: enable rig control, set the path to the rigctld binary, select the Rig model, and set Device (e.g. /dev/ttyUSB0) + baud; supports network rigs. Modern CQRLOG runs everything through rigctld (mind Hamlib 4.x model-number changes, e.g. IC-7300 373 -> 3073).
Audio Device Select: n/a for logging (fldigi/WSJT-X own the sound device)
PTT Config: via Hamlib if configured; mainly freq/mode capture
Companion Needed: Hamlib rigctld; integrates fldigi + WSJT-X
Notes: Linux MySQL logger.
Source Note: Confirmed: TRX control tab, rigctld path, rig model + /dev/ttyUSBx + baud verified.

Name: DX4WIN
Rig Control Backend: native built-in CAT rig drivers (rig files)
How to Set CAT: File > Preferences > Radio tab: set Radio Type, Address (e.g. CI-V for Icom), Baud, COM port, Poll Rate, Max Tries and control lines (DTR/RTS). It polls freq/mode. The CAT port cannot be shared with another app; use the 'enhanced' port with virtual serial ports.
Audio Device Select: n/a
PTT Config: via CAT where the rig supports it
Companion Needed: none
Notes: Long-standing Windows DX logger.
Source Note: Confirmed: File > Preferences > Radio (type/address/baud/COM/poll/DTR-RTS) verified.

Name: DXbase
Rig Control Backend: native CAT rig drivers (built-in)
How to Set CAT: Setup/Options > Radio (Transceiver): choose the rig model + COM port + baud; reads freq/mode into the log.
Audio Device Select: n/a
PTT Config: via CAT if the rig supports it
Companion Needed: none
Notes: Windows logging/station suite.
Source Note: Uncertain: DXbase is a legacy/largely discontinued Windows logger; no current official documentation could be located to confirm the exact CAT menu path. The native-CAT-logger characterization is consistent with its known behavior but unverified against a live source.

Name: DXKeeper
Rig Control Backend: none native - rig control is provided by DXLab Commander (which holds the CAT port)
How to Set CAT: No direct rig setup in DXKeeper. Run DXLab Commander and set the radio there (model/COM/baud/parameters). DXKeeper captures freq/mode from Commander and QSYs the rig through it.
Audio Device Select: n/a
PTT Config: via Commander
Companion Needed: DXLab Commander (required for rig control)
Notes: DXLab Suite logbook module.
Source Note: Confirmed: DXKeeper relies on Commander for all CAT.

Name: DXLab Suite
Rig Control Backend: Commander is the native CAT/PTT hub (built-in rig drivers); other modules share it
How to Set CAT: In Commander select the Radio (model) + Primary CAT Port + Baud/Parity/WordLength/StopBits once (for Icom, set exact baud, not auto). DXKeeper/DXView/SpotCollector/WinWarbler then use Commander for freq/mode/QSY; up to 4 radios can be switched.
Audio Device Select: WinWarbler uses the PC sound card for PSK/RTTY/CW
PTT Config: Commander PTT (CAT/RTS/DTR); WinWarbler keys via Commander
ALC/Power Guidance: for WinWarbler digital, keep audio drive low so ALC barely moves
Companion Needed: none - Commander is the built-in hub; modules interoperate
Notes: AA6YQ DXLab integrated suite; Commander can also serve CAT to third-party apps.
Source Note: Confirmed: Commander is the central CAT hub; Primary CAT Port + baud/parity settings verified.

Name: DXtreme Station Log
Rig Control Backend: Afreet Omni-Rig (external CAT hub), not native
How to Set CAT: In the log's Preferences > Rig Control, enable rig control via Omni-Rig; the actual rig model/COM/baud is set inside Omni-Rig. Freq/mode then auto-populate the log, and it can QSY the rig (e.g. schedule/spot tuning). A virtual serial port can share the CAT port with other apps.
Audio Device Select: n/a
PTT Config: via Omni-Rig if used
Companion Needed: Afreet Omni-Rig (required for CAT)
Notes: G4HFQ/DXtreme Windows logger.
Source Note: Confirmed: rig control is through Afreet Omni-Rig, configured in Omni-Rig, not natively in DXtreme.

Name: DXView
Rig Control Backend: none native - rig control via DXLab Commander; also does antenna-rotator control
How to Set CAT: No direct CAT: set the radio in Commander. DXView shows beam headings/maps and can QSY via Commander, and separately controls a rotator (its own rotator configuration).
Audio Device Select: n/a
PTT Config: via Commander
Companion Needed: DXLab Commander (rig) + a supported rotator/controller
Notes: DXLab mapping/rotator module.
Source Note: Confirmed: CAT via Commander; DXView adds map/rotator control.

Name: Fast Log Entry (FLE)
Rig Control Backend: none (offline text logger)
How to Set CAT: n/a - no CAT at all. You type contacts as fast-log shorthand text; there is no rig/CAT/audio/PTT connection.
Audio Device Select: n/a
PTT Config: n/a
Companion Needed: none - exports ADIF/Cabrillo to other loggers
Notes: DF3CB keyboard-only logger; listed in the logging category for completeness, but has no radio interface.
Source Note: Confirmed - FLE is a keyboard-only shorthand logger with no radio interface.

Name: Flcluster
Rig Control Backend: none native - relays to flrig/fldigi over XML-RPC
How to Set CAT: n/a directly. It is a DX-cluster client; double-clicking a spot QSYs the rig by sending that frequency to flrig/fldigi (which holds the CAT via XML-RPC).
Audio Device Select: n/a
PTT Config: n/a
Companion Needed: flrig or fldigi (which owns the CAT connection)
Notes: fldigi-suite cluster client.
Source Note: Confirmed: no CAT of its own; QSY via the flrig/fldigi XML-RPC server.

Name: Fllog
Rig Control Backend: none native - reads freq/mode from flrig/fldigi via XML-RPC
How to Set CAT: n/a directly. Logging server: captures the current frequency/mode from flrig/fldigi at log time (XML-RPC) and exposes an XML-RPC log interface to other apps.
Audio Device Select: n/a
PTT Config: n/a
Companion Needed: flrig or fldigi
Notes: fldigi-suite logbook server.
Source Note: Confirmed: flrig is the XML-RPC server; Fllog is a client that reads freq/mode.

Name: Flnet
Rig Control Backend: none native - can read the current frequency from flrig/fldigi over XML-RPC
How to Set CAT: n/a directly. Net-control/roster manager; optionally reads the rig frequency from flrig/fldigi. It does no CAT of its own.
Audio Device Select: n/a
PTT Config: n/a
Companion Needed: flrig or fldigi (optional)
Notes: fldigi-suite net-management database.
Source Note: Confirmed: net-control tool; optional freq read via flrig/fldigi XML-RPC.

Name: HAM Office
Rig Control Backend: native CAT rig drivers (built-in)
How to Set CAT: Optionen/Einstellungen > CAT/Transceiver (German UI): select the rig model + COM port + baud; it reads freq/mode into QSOs.
Audio Device Select: n/a
PTT Config: via CAT if the rig supports it
Companion Needed: none
Notes: German commercial logging suite (HAM OFFICE).
Source Note: Uncertain: HAM Office is a German logging suite (hamoffice.de) with built-in transceiver/CAT control, but the exact menu path could not be verified against its official documentation in this pass.

Name: Ham Radio Deluxe (HRD)
Rig Control Backend: native CAT via HRD Rig Control - its own CAT hub
How to Set CAT: HRD Rig Control > Connect: choose Company/Radio model + COM port + baud (USB radios auto-create a virtual COM plus a USB Audio CODEC; Yaesu 'Enhanced' port for CAT; Icom CI-V auto-detected). Other HRD modules and third-party apps can share it via HRD's virtual port.
Audio Device Select: the DM-780 module uses the PC sound card (or the radio's built-in USB codec) for digital modes
PTT Config: HRD Rig Control PTT (CAT/RTS/DTR); plus CW keying and a rotator-control module
ALC/Power Guidance: for DM-780 digital, use DATA/USB mode and keep audio drive low so ALC barely moves
Companion Needed: none (full suite); can also serve CAT to N1MM etc.
Notes: Integrated logging + rig control + DM-780 + rotor.
Source: Ham Radio Deluxe User Guide - Rig Control
Source Note: Confirmed: HRD Rig Control Connect dialog (Company/Radio + COM + baud) verified; USB radios create both a virtual COM and a USB audio codec.

Name: Ham Radio Deluxe - Digital Master (DM780)
Rig Control Backend: uses HRD Rig Control for CAT/PTT (companion module)
How to Set CAT: No separate CAT: DM-780 gets freq/mode and PTT from HRD Rig Control (set the rig there - model/COM/baud).
Audio Device Select: DM-780 > Program Options > Soundcard: select the input/output sound card (the radio's built-in USB codec or a second PC sound card) for PSK/RTTY/etc.
PTT Config: via HRD Rig Control (CAT/RTS/DTR), a COM port, or VOX
ALC/Power Guidance: use DATA/USB mode and set audio output so the rig's ALC barely moves
Companion Needed: HRD Rig Control (for CAT/PTT)
Notes: HRD's sound-card digital-modes module.
Source: HRD User Guide - Configuring DM-780 with Built-in Soundcard
Source Note: Confirmed: DM-780 is soundcard-based and takes CAT/PTT from HRD Rig Control; soundcard picked in Program Options.

Name: HamLog
Rig Control Backend: native CAT via Pignology Piglet (WiFi) or Pigtail (serial) hardware bridge
How to Set CAT: In HamLog (iOS): configure a Pignology Piglet (WiFi) or Pigtail (serial), which bridges to the rig's CAT; freq/mode then auto-populate the contact fields, and the app can tune the rig to a DX-spot frequency (the 'PT' toolbar button shows the control interface). Supports Elecraft K3/KX3/K2, Yaesu FT-817/857/897, Kenwood TS-480/590/2000.
Audio Device Select: n/a
PTT Config: n/a - freq/mode focus
Companion Needed: a Pignology Piglet or Pigtail CAT interface (rig control only works through it)
Notes: Pignology iOS logging app.
Source Note: Confirmed: rig control is via the Pignology Piglet/Pigtail bridge; freq/mode auto-populate.

Name: KLog
Rig Control Backend: Hamlib (rig control library / rigctld)
How to Set CAT: Settings > Preferences > Rig control (Hamlib): enable rig control, pick the Radio (Hamlib model), Serial/Device (COM or /dev/tty), Baud, Data/Stop bits, Flow control, and read/poll interval.
Audio Device Select: n/a - logging only, no sound-card/digital modes.
PTT Config: n/a - reads frequency/mode/S-meter only; does not key TX.
Companion Needed: none (Hamlib built in); optionally point at an external rigctld
Notes: Cross-platform Qt logbook (EA4TV). CAT used only to auto-fill freq/mode in the QSO entry.
Source Note: Verified (EA4TV). KLog uses Hamlib for rig control (reads frequency/mode/S-meter). Historical note: Hamlib support was disabled for a period (~2014, buggy) but is present and working again in current 2.x releases (e.g. 2.4.x, 2026). Logging only - no sound card, no TX keying.

Name: Log-EQF / Win-EQF
Rig Control Backend: Native built-in CAT drivers (Icom/Kenwood/Yaesu/Ten-Tec) over serial COM
How to Set CAT: Setup/Configure > Radio (station control): choose radio type, COM port, baud and data bits; enables freq/mode capture and click-to-tune.
Audio Device Select: n/a - logging only.
PTT Config: n/a - no keying.
Companion Needed: none
Notes: Win-EQF is the Win32 successor to the DOS Log-EQF (LA6VJA). Logging + basic station control (freq/mode) only; no longer actively maintained.
Source Note: Substance consistent with EQF documentation and long-standing behavior; the official EQF Software site was not directly reachable to cite. Native built-in CAT drivers (Icom/Kenwood/Yaesu/Ten-Tec) over a serial COM port; logging only (no sound card, no keying).

Name: Log4OM
Rig Control Backend: OmniRig OR Hamlib (acts as a CAT hub)
How to Set CAT: Settings > CAT Interface: choose Hamlib (select rig model + port + baud in Log4OM) or Omnirig (Rig1/Rig2 configured in OmniRig, Log4OM just attaches).
Audio Device Select: n/a - no built-in modem; digital audio is handled by the external app (WSJT-X/fldigi).
PTT Config: PTT via CAT command (Hamlib/Omnirig) or a dedicated RTS/DTR line; CW via WinKeyer.
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a - set on the external digital app / radio.
Companion Needed: OmniRig (Windows) or Hamlib/rigctld; external WSJT-X/fldigi for digital modes (linked via UDP)
Notes: Shares one CAT connection with other apps; double-click cluster spots to tune.
Source Note: Verified against the Log4OM 2 manual. Acts as a CAT hub: Program Configuration > CAT Interface, choose CAT Engine Hamlib (select rig model + port + baud inside Log4OM) or Omnirig (Rig1/Rig2 configured in OmniRig itself). PTT is via a CAT command (both Hamlib and Omnirig key PTT only via CAT) or a dedicated RTS/DTR line for older rigs. No internal modem - digital is handled by external WSJT-X/fldigi.

Name: Log4OM (v1 / v2)
Rig Control Backend: Omni-Rig OR Hamlib; CW via WinKeyer
How to Set CAT: v1: single CAT engine (Settings > CAT). v2: Settings > CAT Interface has separate CAT and PTT tabs - pick Hamlib or Omnirig, set rig model/port/baud (or configure in OmniRig).
Audio Device Select: n/a - no internal modem; digital handled by external WSJT-X/fldigi.
PTT Config: PTT via CAT or a dedicated PTT COM line; CW keying via WinKeyer.
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a - handled by the external digital app.
Companion Needed: OmniRig or Hamlib; WSJT-X/fldigi integrated over UDP/inbound
Notes: CAT hub for the shack; v2 adds a discrete PTT interface and richer external-app integration.
Source Note: Verified. v1 has a single CAT engine (Settings > CAT); v2 has separate CAT and PTT tabs under Settings > CAT Interface - pick Hamlib or Omnirig, set rig model/port/baud (or configure in OmniRig). PTT via CAT or a dedicated PTT COM line (Hamlib/Omnirig only key PTT via CAT); CW keying via WinKeyer. No internal modem.

Name: Logger32
Rig Control Backend: Native built-in per-radio CAT drivers + optional Omni-Rig (via L32OmniRigBridge); also rotor
How to Set CAT: Setup > Radio (Radio 1 / Radio 2): choose radio type, COM port, baud, data/stop bits (or use Omni-Rig via the L32OmniRigBridge helper); Setup > Rotor for the rotator COM.
Audio Device Select: Built-in sound-card RTTY/data terminal: select the sound-card input/output in its DSP/soundcard window (optional; most pair external MMTTY/WSJT-X).
PTT Config: For its data window, PTT via CAT or serial DTR/RTS.
ALC/Power Guidance: For its RTTY: DATA/USB mode, raise TX audio until desired RF with ALC barely moving.
Companion Needed: none (self-contained); optional MMTTY engine / external WSJT-X
Notes: K4CY. Auto freq/mode capture, rotor control, built-in sound-card RTTY.
Source Note: Verified (K4CY). Setup > Radio (Radio 1/Radio 2): native per-radio driver (radio type, COM, baud, data/stop bits). OmniRig is supported but via the external L32OmniRigBridge helper (set the radio COM port to None and run the bridge). Setup > Rotor for the rotator. Has a built-in sound-card RTTY/data terminal (select soundcard in/out in its DSP/soundcard window); PTT via CAT or serial DTR/RTS.

Name: Logic (LOGic)
Rig Control Backend: Native built-in CAT rig control (many radios) over serial
How to Set CAT: Setup/Tools > Radio Interface (radio control): select radio model, COM port, baud; enables freq/mode read and click-to-tune.
Audio Device Select: n/a - logging/awards suite, no digital modem.

Notes: Personal Database Applications logging/contest/awards package.
Source Note: Verified. LOGic (Personal Database Applications) is a logging/awards suite with native built-in CAT rig control for many radios over serial; no digital modem. Radio Interface selects radio model, COM and baud for freq/mode read and click-to-tune.

Name: MacLoggerDX
Rig Control Backend: Native CAT/CI-V direct control (macOS), its own per-radio drivers (not Hamlib)
How to Set CAT: MacLoggerDX > Preferences > Radio: select radio model, serial/USB port (or network host), baud; enable Doppler tracking for satellites.
Audio Device Select: n/a - not a digital modem (can feed WSJT-X).
PTT Config: Uses CAT to tune/track and can key for satellites; a separate Keyer popup sets DTR/RTS or a WinKeyer USB. No sound-card PTT of its own.
Companion Needed: none; commonly feeds WSJT-X/fldigi
Notes: Dog Park Software (VE3VRW). Click a DX-cluster spot to tune; satellite Doppler tracking.
Source Note: Verified against Dog Park Software's help (Don Agro VE3VRW). Clarification: MacLoggerDX uses its OWN native CAT/CI-V drivers, not Hamlib. Preferences > Radio: select radio model, serial/USB port (or network host), baud; Doppler tracking for satellites; a separate Keyer popup handles DTR/RTS or a WinKeyer USB. Not a digital modem (can feed WSJT-X).

Name: MixW / MixW2 / MixW3
Rig Control Backend: Built-in per-radio CAT rig control over COM (all-in-one)
How to Set CAT: Configure > TRCVR CAT/PTT: select rig model, COM port, baud, bits; Configure > Secondary CAT/PTT for an antenna rotator.
Audio Device Select: Configure > Sound Device Settings: select sound-card input and output.
PTT Config: Configure > TRCVR CAT/PTT: PTT via COM RTS/DTR, via CAT command, or VOX.
ALC/Power Guidance: Set radio to DATA/USB; raise TX audio (output level / Windows mixer) until desired RF with ALC minimal.

Source Note: Verified against the MixW help. Configure > TRCVR CAT/PTT: select rig model, COM, baud, bits (Configure > Secondary CAT/PTT for a rotator). Sound card is under Configure > Sound Device Settings. PTT via COM RTS/DTR, via a CAT command, or VOX.

Name: MixW4
Rig Control Backend: Hamlib / RigCAT built-in (device-manager based)
How to Set CAT: Devices > add a Transceiver device: choose Hamlib model (or RigCAT with a rig .xml), serial port, baud; add a PTT device.
Audio Device Select: Devices > Sound Card: pick the input and output audio device.
PTT Config: Hamlib CAT PTT or serial RTS/DTR; FSK/TinyFSK keying supported.
ALC/Power Guidance: DATA/USB mode, keep ALC low, adjust TX audio drive.
Companion Needed: none
Notes: Subscription rewrite of MixW using a device-based configuration model and Hamlib.
Source Note: Verified against the MixW4 help (RigExpert). Device-manager based: Devices > add a Transceiver (Hamlib model, or RigCAT with a downloaded rig .xml), serial port, baud; add a PTT device. Devices > Sound Card for the audio device. PTT via Hamlib CAT or serial RTS/DTR; FSK/TinyFSK keying supported.

Name: N3FJP Amateur Contact Log (AC Log)
Rig Control Backend: Built-in native CAT rig control, or via Hamlib (rigctld) / OmniRig
How to Set CAT: Settings > Rig Interface: choose radio type/model (auto-sets baud/parity/bits/polling), COM port, and CI-V address for Icom; or point at Hamlib/OmniRig.
Audio Device Select: n/a - no internal digital modem (pairs with WSJT-X).
PTT Config: n/a (CW keying via serial only in the contest logs).

Notes: Affirmatech (Scott Davis). Reads/sets freq and mode; shares rig data via its TCP API.

Settings > Rig Interface: pick the radio type/model (selecting the rig auto-sets baud/parity/data/stop bits and the polling string) and the COM port; CI-V address for Icom. Later versions also allow Hamlib (rigctld)/OmniRig. No internal digital modem (pairs with WSJT-X).

Name: N3FJP Amateur Contact Log (ACLog) + Rig Interface/Gateway
Rig Control Backend: Built-in native CAT, or Hamlib/OmniRig; API/Gateway relays rig data over TCP
How to Set CAT: Settings > Rig Interface: select radio/COM/baud (or Hamlib/OmniRig). The API/Gateway is enabled in Settings with a TCP port so other apps read the rig's freq/mode.
Audio Device Select: n/a - no internal modem.
PTT Config: n/a (CW via serial in the contest logs).
Companion Needed: none required; the Gateway feeds freq/mode to other software (and can consume Hamlib/OmniRig)
Notes: Same rig engine as AC Log plus a network gateway that rebroadcasts rig data to third-party apps.
Source Note: Verified. Same Rig Interface (native CAT, or Hamlib/OmniRig) as AC Log. The N3FJP API/Gateway is enabled in Settings with a TCP port so other apps can read the rig's freq/mode relayed by ACLog.

Name: ProLog
Rig Control Backend: Native built-in CAT rig control (many radios) + cluster
How to Set CAT: Setup > Radio / Rig control: select radio, COM port, baud; enables freq/mode read and click-to-tune from cluster spots.
