Name: PstRotator (PstRotatorAZ)
Rig Control Backend: Reads the rig via Hamlib, OmniRig, or direct CAT; sends Doppler CAT tuning for satellites
How to Set CAT: Setup > TRX (radio): choose the CAT source (Hamlib / OmniRig / direct), rig model, COM port, baud. Satellite mode enables Doppler correction sent back to the rig.
Audio Device Select: n/a - antenna-rotator controller, no audio.
PTT Config: n/a for the radio - keys rotators; sat mode only tunes the rig for Doppler (one COM for Doppler, one for PTT keying).

Notes: YO3DMU. Primary job is antenna-rotator control; it reads rig freq/mode to auto-point and does Doppler tuning for LEO sats.
Source Note: Verified against the PstRotator manual (YO3DMU). Primarily an antenna-rotator controller; it also reads the rig via Hamlib, OmniRig, or direct CAT and, in satellite mode, sends Doppler-corrected tuning to the rig. Setup > TRX selects the CAT source, rig model, COM and baud; separate COM ports are used for Doppler tuning and PTT keying.

Name: RUMlog / RUMlogNG
Rig Control Backend: Native CAT rig control (Kenwood/Elecraft/Yaesu/Icom) over serial/USB (macOS)
How to Set CAT: Preferences > CAT/TRX (radio): select the radio, serial port, flow control, model options and polling interval; enables freq/mode capture and click-to-tune.

Notes: DL2RUM macOS logging suite (RUMlogNG is the current version).

CAT preferences: select the serial port, flow control, model-specific options and polling interval; extended controls for K3/KX3/KX2 and IC-705/7300/7610/9700. WinKey and microHAM (MKII/DKII) supported for CW/PTT. Digital handled by companion tools.

Name: SpotCollector
Rig Control Backend: Rig control via DXLab Commander (the suite's CAT hub)

Audio Device Select: n/a - spot database, no audio.
PTT Config: n/a.

Notes: AA6YQ / DXLab suite. Double-click a spot and Commander tunes the rig.
Source Note: Verified against the DXLab Suite. SpotCollector has no CAT of its own; rig control is done through DXLab Commander (the suite's CAT hub) - set rig model/COM/baud in Commander's Configuration, and SpotCollector uses Commander for click-to-tune. Spot database, no audio.

Name: Swisslog
Rig Control Backend: CAT via OmniRig (v1/v2, recommended) or native per-radio drivers
How to Set CAT: Options > Rig control (CAT): choose OmniRig (up to 4 rigs) or a radio type, then COM port, baud, bits; enables freq/mode read.
Audio Device Select: n/a - logging only.
PTT Config: n/a.
Companion Needed: OmniRig optional
Notes: HB9BJS Windows logger.

Source Note: Verified against Swisslog's Transceiver-Control help. CAT via OmniRig (v1 or v2; up to 4 rigs) or its own native per-radio drivers; Options > Rig control selects OmniRig or a radio type, then COM/baud/bits for freq/mode read. Logging only.

Name: Turbo HAMLOG/Win
Rig Control Backend: Native built-in CAT (reads freq/mode) over serial COM
How to Set CAT: Environment/Options settings > Rig control (RS-232C): select rig, COM port, baud; supports Icom/Yaesu/Kenwood freq/mode capture.

Name: TurboLog 4
Rig Control Backend: Native built-in CAT rig control + rotator + cluster

Source Note: Verified as TurboLog 4: native built-in CAT rig control plus rotator and cluster; Configuration > Radio (CAT) selects radio, COM and baud (rotator configured separately). PTT via CAT; CW keying via serial/WinKey.

Name: UcxLog
Rig Control Backend: Native built-in per-radio CAT rig control over COM (up to 5 TRX)
How to Set CAT: Options > Configuration > Interfaces / TRX: select radio, COM port, baud; separate ports for PTT and CW keying.

PTT Config: PTT via COM RTS/DTR or via CAT; CW keying via a serial line or WinKey.

Notes: Free DL7UCX logger with contest, DX-cluster, sound-card digital and CW keying built in.
Source Note: Verified against ucxlog.org (DL7UCX). Native per-radio CAT for up to 5 TRX (Icom/Kenwood/Yaesu/K2). Options > Configuration > Interfaces/TRX selects radio, COM and baud, with separate ports for PTT and CW keying. Its built-in PSK/RTTY uses the MMTTY/MMVARI engines with a sound card; PTT via COM RTS/DTR or CAT, CW via serial/WinKey.

Name: VQLog
Rig Control Backend: Native built-in CAT rig control over COM (Icom/Yaesu/Kenwood)

Name: Wavelog
Rig Control Backend: No direct serial CAT (web app); receives live freq/mode from a CAT companion via its HTTP API
How to Set CAT: In the Wavelog web UI create an API key (Account > API Keys). Run a CAT uploader (WaveLogGate/WLGate, Cloudlog Helper, or a Hamlib/rigctld bridge) configured with your rig (Hamlib model/port/baud) and the Wavelog URL + API key; it POSTs freq/mode.

Notes: Self-hosted PHP web logger (Cloudlog fork). Browsers can't read serial, so a helper feeds the rig data.
Source Note: Verified. Wavelog is a web logbook with no direct serial CAT; it receives live freq/mode from a CAT companion via its API. Create an API key in the Wavelog UI (Account > API Keys), then run a helper - WaveLogGate (WLGate), Cloudlog Helper, or a Hamlib/rigctld bridge - configured with your rig (Hamlib model/port/baud, or FLRig/OmniRig) plus the Wavelog URL and API key; it POSTs frequency/mode.

Name: WinLog32

Notes: G0CUZ free Windows logger with mapping and DX-cluster.

Name: Xlog
Rig Control Backend: Hamlib (hamlib library)

Name: YPLog

Name: AirLink Express

Name: Bonito RadioCom
Rig Control Backend: Built-in CAT rig control (100+ radios) + PTT via interface/switchbox

Name: cocoaModem / cocoaModem 2.0

Rig Control Backend: None - decodes/encodes from live audio (built-in mic OR audio cable); also supports Bluetooth/network TNCs for Packet; no rig CAT
How to Set CAT: n/a - no rig control; tune the receiver manually.
Audio Device Select: Uses the device microphone (in) and speaker (out), OR a direct audio cable for better quality; Bluetooth/network TNCs supported for Packet.
PTT Config: No electrical rig PTT of its own - use the radio's VOX or manual keying when transmitting via mic/cable; a TNC handles keying for Packet.
ALC/Power Guidance: If transmitting via audio, set the radio's mic gain/VOX so audio isn't overdriven (keep ALC low).
Companion Needed: none
Notes: Mobile decoder/encoder that couples to the radio acoustically through the phone mic and speaker.
Source Note: The 'acoustic-only, no cable' description is inaccurate. DigiDecoder (iOS app) takes live audio from the built-in microphone OR a direct audio cable, and it also supports Bluetooth/network TNCs for Packet - it is not limited to acoustic coupling. It both decodes and encodes modes (SSTV, Packet, RTTY, CW, FT8/FT4, JS8, Olivia). It has no rig CAT; it has no electrical rig PTT of its own (rely on the radio's VOX/manual keying for acoustic/audio-cable TX, or a TNC handles keying for Packet).

Name: DigiPan
Rig Control Backend: None - PSK-only sound-card program, PTT via COM (no CAT)
How to Set CAT: n/a - no CAT rig control; tune the rig manually (or with a separate CAT app).
Audio Device Select: Configure > Sound Card: select input and output device (or leave at Windows default).
PTT Config: Configure > Serial (PTT) Port: COM RTS/DTR, or VOX.
ALC/Power Guidance: DATA/USB mode, raise TX audio (use Tune) until RF out with ALC minimal.
Companion Needed: none
Notes: Classic UT2UZ/KH6TY PSK31/63 panadapter; no rig control at all.
Source Note: Verified. PSK31/PSK63 sound-card program with no CAT. Configure > Sound Card selects input/output; Configure > Serial (PTT) Port sets the PTT COM line (RTS/DTR) or use VOX; the Tune feature aids TX level setting.

Name: EasyDRF
Rig Control Backend: none - sound-card HamDRM (adapted DRM) file/data modem; serial PTT only, no CAT
How to Set CAT: n/a - no CAT; tune the SSB rig manually (2.5 kHz-wide HamDRM signal in USB/DATA)
Audio Device Select: Select the sound-card input and output device in EasyDRF
PTT Config: Serial-port PTT keying (RTS/DTR), or VOX
ALC/Power Guidance: DRM/OFDM is multi-carrier - keep ALC OFF/at zero and back off audio drive to avoid IMD; use DATA/USB
Companion Needed: none
Notes: HB9TLK program sending images/files via DRM over SSB.
Source Note: Confirmed. EasyDRF is HB9TLK's HamDRM file-transfer app (successor to WinDRM); sound-card modem, serial/VOX PTT, no CAT.

Name: Flamp
Rig Control Backend: none of its own - uses fldigi's rig control
How to Set CAT: n/a in Flamp; set the rig in fldigi (flrig / Hamlib / RigCAT). Flamp connects to fldigi over XML-RPC (default localhost:7362).
Audio Device Select: Via fldigi's Sound Card settings
PTT Config: Via fldigi's PTT (CAT / flrig / RTS-DTR / GPIO / VOX)
ALC/Power Guidance: Per fldigi: DATA/USB, keep ALC low
Companion Needed: fldigi required (Flamp is a front-end file-transfer tool)
Notes: W1HKJ Amateur Multicast Protocol file sender; blocks are transmitted through the fldigi modem.
Source Note: Confirmed. Flamp (Amateur Multicast Protocol file transfer) is a front-end to fldigi over XML-RPC; all rig/audio/PTT config lives in fldigi.

Name: Fldigi
Rig Control Backend: flrig OR Hamlib OR RigCAT (Configure > Config Dialog > Rig Control)
How to Set CAT: Configure > Config Dialog > Rig Control: choose flrig (attach to a running flrig), Hamlib (pick Rig model, serial device, baud, PTT options), or RigCAT (XML rig definition + serial port).
Audio Device Select: Configure > Config Dialog > Soundcard > Devices: PortAudio/PulseAudio/PortAudio - select capture (input) and playback (output)
PTT Config: Rig Control > Hardware PTT (serial RTS/DTR or a separate port), or CAT/flrig PTT, or GPIO (Raspberry Pi), or VOX
ALC/Power Guidance: Set radio to DATA/USB; raise TX audio (AFC/waterfall audio + power) until desired RF with ALC barely moving
Companion Needed: optional flrig; drives Flmsg/Flamp/Flwrap
Notes: Full multimode engine; also the transport for the fl-suite tools.
Source: Fldigi Users Manual - Sound Card & Rig Control (W1HKJ)
Source Note: Confirmed. Menu path in modern builds is Configure > Config Dialog > (Rig Control / Soundcard); older builds exposed a top-level Rig menu.

Name: Flmsg
Rig Control Backend: none of its own - via fldigi
How to Set CAT: n/a in Flmsg; the rig is set in fldigi. Flmsg links to fldigi over XML-RPC.
Audio Device Select: Via fldigi's sound-card settings
PTT Config: Via fldigi's PTT
Companion Needed: fldigi required
Notes: Composes/parses ICS/ARRL radiogram and NBEMS forms, then hands them to fldigi to send/receive.
Source Note: Confirmed. Flmsg is a forms/message editor that hands text to fldigi; no independent radio interface.

Name: Flwrap
Rig Control Backend: none of its own - via fldigi
How to Set CAT: n/a in Flwrap; set the rig in fldigi.
Audio Device Select: Via fldigi's sound-card settings
PTT Config: Via fldigi's PTT
Companion Needed: fldigi required
Notes: Adds checksum/compression wrapping to files for reliable transfer through fldigi.
Source Note: Confirmed. Flwrap wraps/checksums files for transfer through fldigi; no radio interface of its own.

Name: FSQCall
Rig Control Backend: Built-in (limited) CAT for frequency read/set - TX & RX CAT commands only, known to work with Icom and some Kenwood/Yaesu - PLUS a serial-port PTT line
How to Set CAT: CAT baud rate and COM port are set in the FSQCALvnnn_setup.txt config file (the radio address is chosen by an index, not editable in the text file). Check/APPLY the PTT/CAT settings at every start (USB COM ports can change). CAT is optional; you can tune manually instead.
Audio Device Select: Select the sound-card input and output device within FSQCall
PTT Config: One serial (or USB-equivalent) COM port for PTT (RTS/DTR), or VOX; frequency CAT is separate and limited
Companion Needed: none
Notes: ZL2AFP/ZL1BPU FSQ (Fast Simple QSO) HF chat/selcall/store-and-forward.
Source: ZL2AFP FSQCall Help (Callweb) / WhiteMesa FSQCALL help
Source Note: CORRECTED: the claim said 'no CAT'. FSQCall (ZL2AFP) actually includes limited built-in CAT - it sends TX and RX CAT commands (frequency) and is documented to work with Icom plus some Kenwood/Yaesu rigs; CAT baud/COM port are configured in the setup file and applied via the PTT/CAT settings.

Name: IZ8BLY Hellschreiber
Rig Control Backend: none - sound-card Feld-Hell modem; PTT via COM, no CAT
How to Set CAT: n/a - no CAT; tune the rig manually
Audio Device Select: Select the sound-card input and output device
PTT Config: PTT via a serial COM RTS/DTR line, or VOX
Companion Needed: none
Notes: Nino IZ8BLY Feld-Hell / Hellschreiber program for Windows.
Source Note: Confirmed. IZ8BLY (Nino Porcino) 'Hellschreiber' is a sound-card Hell program using VOX or a serial-port PTT interface; no CAT.

Name: IZ8BLY MT63
Rig Control Backend: none - sound-card MT63 modem; PTT via COM, no CAT
How to Set CAT: n/a - no CAT; tune the rig manually
Audio Device Select: Select the sound-card input and output device
PTT Config: PTT via a serial COM RTS/DTR line, or VOX
ALC/Power Guidance: MT63 is multi-tone - keep ALC well down and reduce audio drive to avoid IMD; use DATA/USB
Companion Needed: none
Notes: Nino IZ8BLY MT63 implementation.
Source Note: Confirmed. IZ8BLY MT63 Terminal is a sound-card multi-tone MFSK program with VOX or serial-port PTT; no CAT.

Name: LinPSK
Rig Control Backend: Hamlib (optional - only when compiled with hamlib, detected by pkg-config)
How to Set CAT: Settings dialog: with hamlib support enabled, LinPSK can control the rig; selecting a band auto-tunes the radio to that band's preferred frequency, and TX power can be set from the QSO data if the rig supports it.
Audio Device Select: Settings dialog: enter an ALSA device name (e.g. plughw:1) or the card name; optionally define LinPSK_Record / LinPSK_Play in ~/.asoundrc. Audio backend is ALSA - NOT PortAudio/JACK.
PTT Config: PTT via Hamlib CAT (when built with hamlib), or a serial RTS/DTR line, or VOX
Companion Needed: none (Hamlib library)
Notes: DL1KSV Linux PSK31/RTTY/Throb/MFSK program (Qt).
Source Note: CORRECTED: audio_device_select claimed 'PortAudio/Jack'. Per the current LinPSK (dl1ksv) README the program uses the ALSA sound system and takes ALSA device names in its Settings dialog. Hamlib remains optional/compile-time.

Name: multimon-ng
Rig Control Backend: none - CLI decoder fed by an audio pipe/stream; no CAT or PTT
How to Set CAT: n/a - no rig control; you tune the SDR/receiver separately (e.g. rtl_fm) and pipe its audio in
Audio Device Select: Reads raw audio from stdin/a pipe (commonly from rtl_fm) or a sound-card/PulseAudio input; chosen on the command line (-t/-i options)
PTT Config: n/a - receive/decode only (POCSAG/FLEX/AFSK1200/DTMF/etc.)
Companion Needed: an audio source: rtl_fm/SDR or a sound card piping audio into it
Notes: Command-line multi-protocol digital decoder; transmit is not a function.
Source Note: Confirmed. multimon-ng is a receive-only command-line multimode decoder; audio comes from stdin, a file, or a sound device specified on the command line.

Name: MultiPSK
Rig Control Backend: Built-in CAT rig control + PTT via COM (very wide radio support)
How to Set CAT: On the Configuration screen ('RX/TX + Config' > TRX/CAT area): select the transceiver, its COM port and baud, and assign the PTT line; serial ports, sound card and mixer are all set here.
Audio Device Select: Configuration screen: select the sound-card input and output device (and mixer)
PTT Config: PTT via a COM RTS/DTR line, via a CAT command, or VOX
ALC/Power Guidance: DATA/USB mode, keep ALC low, adjust TX audio drive
Companion Needed: none (huge built-in mode set)
Notes: F6CTE. Free with a paid key unlocking pro modes (ALE, DRM, etc.).
Source Note: Confirmed. MultiPSK (F6CTE) has an integrated CAT/TRX configuration plus sound-card and serial-port PTT settings on its Configuration page.

Name: N7YG Digital Engine
Rig Control Backend: Companion to N3FJP - CAT/frequency is normally handled by the connected N3FJP logging program (auto-detected); the Digital Engine handles its own PTT, sound-card audio and FSK
How to Set CAT: The Digital Engine detects which N3FJP program it is connected to and configures automatically; frequency/logging come from N3FJP over its API, so the rig's CAT link is generally set up in the N3FJP software rather than in the Engine.
Audio Device Select: Sound Card configuration dialog: select input and output device (FSK/AFSK logic inversion is also set here)
PTT Config: PTT via COM RTS/DTR, via CAT, or VOX; RTTY/FSK via EXTFSK, TinyFSK, or K1EL WinKey FSK
ALC/Power Guidance: DATA/USB mode, keep ALC low, raise TX audio to the desired RF
Companion Needed: N3FJP logging suite (it is the companion modem; also runs standalone). PSK Reporter supported.
Notes: Scaled-down PSKExpress with a floating waterfall; PSK/RTTY/MFSK.
Source Note: Confirmed against the N7YG user guide/site: DE is a scaled-down PSK Express built as an N3FJP companion; it auto-detects the N3FJP program, generates FSK/AFSK in the sound card, and supports EXTFSK/TinyFSK/K1EL WinKey FSK. The exact CAT rig-list detail is per the user-guide PDF.

Name: PSK31SBW
Rig Control Backend: none - the original (G3PLX) sound-card PSK31 program; PTT via COM, no CAT
How to Set CAT: n/a - no CAT; tune the rig manually
Audio Device Select: Select the sound-card input and output device
PTT Config: PTT via a COM RTS/DTR line, or VOX
ALC/Power Guidance: DATA/USB mode, keep ALC low (drive TX audio just below ALC onset)
Companion Needed: none
Notes: Early Windows sound-card PSK31 program; frequency set manually.
Source Note: Confirmed. PSK31SBW ('PSK31 SoundBlaster for Windows', Peter Martinez G3PLX - the mode's originator) is a sound-card program with serial-port PTT and no CAT.

Name: RadioRaft
Rig Control Backend: none - receive-only multimode decoder; no CAT or PTT
How to Set CAT: n/a - no rig control; tune the receiver manually
Audio Device Select: Input comes from a hardware comparator interface OR an external demodulator/modem wired to a COM/serial port - RadioRaft is NOT a PC sound-card program; a real demodulator gives better decoding than the simple comparator.
PTT Config: n/a - receive/decode only
Companion Needed: none (may use a hardware comparator/demodulator interface)
Notes: F6FLN DOS multimode decoder (RTTY, CW, and many utility/data modes); receive only.
Source Note: CORRECTED/CLARIFIED: audio_device_select implied a sound card. RadioRaft (F6FLT, DOS) actually takes its signal from a hardware comparator circuit or an external demodulator connected to a serial port - not a PC sound card.

Name: SIM PSK31 (Sim31 / Sim63)
Rig Control Backend: none - sound-card SIM-PSK (FEC PSK) modem; PTT via COM, no CAT
How to Set CAT: n/a - no CAT; tune the rig manually
Audio Device Select: Select the sound-card input and output device
PTT Config: PTT via a COM RTS/DTR line, or VOX
Companion Needed: none
Notes: RN3GM SIM (superimposed) PSK modes for Windows.
Source Note: Confirmed on interface type (sound-card PSK variant with FEC; serial/VOX PTT, no CAT). Program is niche; no official English manual page located to add a URL.

Name: Sorcerer
Rig Control Backend: none - receive-only multimode decoder; no CAT or PTT (cannot control an SDR directly)
How to Set CAT: n/a - no rig control; tune the receiver/SDR separately (e.g. set frequency in SDR# and route audio via a virtual audio cable)
Audio Device Select: Sound-card / line-in audio, a WAV file, or I/Q input for decoding (input device selected in Sorcerer)
PTT Config: n/a - no transmit
Companion Needed: none
Notes: HF data/utility-mode decoder (RTTY, PSK and others); receive only.
Source Note: Confirmed. Sorcerer is a receive-only HF data decoder; it accepts sound-card audio, WAV or I/Q and has no rig control (frequency is set in external SDR software).

Name: twpsk / tkpsk
Rig Control Backend: none built-in - sound-card PSK31 (Linux); no integrated CAT
How to Set CAT: n/a - no integrated CAT; tune the rig manually
Audio Device Select: Uses the ALSA/OSS sound card (device chosen at launch / in its config)
PTT Config: PTT via a serial-port RTS/DTR line, or VOX
Companion Needed: none
Notes: DL9RDZ/WA0EIR Linux PSK31 (Lesstif/Motif); tkpsk is the Tcl/Tk-based front-end.
Source Note: Confirmed. twpsk (WA0EIR, Ted Williams; Lesstif/Motif UI) is a Linux sound-card PSK31 client with serial-port PTT and no rig control.

Name: WinDRM
Rig Control Backend: none - sound-card HamDRM (DRM) digital-voice/data modem; serial PTT only, no CAT
How to Set CAT: n/a - no CAT; tune the SSB rig manually
Audio Device Select: Select the sound-card input and output device
PTT Config: Serial-port PTT keying (RTS/DTR), or VOX
ALC/Power Guidance: DRM/OFDM is multi-carrier - keep ALC OFF/minimal and reduce audio drive to avoid IMD; use DATA/USB
Companion Needed: none
Notes: Digital voice plus SSTV/file transfer over SSB using DRM.
Source Note: Confirmed. WinDRM (HB9TLK) is the sound-card HamDRM app (predecessor to EasyDRF); serial/VOX PTT, no CAT.

Name: WinPSK
Rig Control Backend: none - sound-card PSK31 program (AE4JY); PTT via COM, no CAT
How to Set CAT: n/a - no CAT; tune the rig manually
Audio Device Select: Select the sound-card input and output device
PTT Config: Optional serial-port PTT (toggles RTS or DTR of a COM port), or VOX, or manual
ALC/Power Guidance: Use Configure > Transmit Drive to set TX audio so that NO ALC action is observed (avoids overdrive/phase distortion); DATA/USB mode
Companion Needed: none
Notes: AE4JY (Moe Wheatley) early Windows PSK31.
Source Note: Confirmed against the WinPSK User Guide (Moe Wheatley AE4JY): sound-card PSK31, optional serial RTS/DTR PTT or VOX, and a Transmit Drive control set for zero ALC. No CAT.

Name: WinPSKse
Rig Control Backend: none - sound-card PSK31 program (AE4JY 'special edition'); PTT via COM, no CAT
How to Set CAT: n/a - no CAT; tune the rig manually
Audio Device Select: Select the sound-card input and output device
PTT Config: Optional serial-port PTT (RTS/DTR), or VOX
ALC/Power Guidance: Set Transmit Drive so no ALC action is seen; DATA/USB mode, keep ALC low
Companion Needed: none
Notes: Simplified 'Special Edition' derivative of WinPSK.
Source Note: Confirmed. WinPSKse is the AE4JY WinPSK 'special edition' - same sound-card/serial-PTT model as WinPSK, no CAT.

Name: Black Cat Systems iOS decoders (PSK31, Morse Pad, RTTY, SSTV)
Rig Control Backend: none - iOS apps using the device microphone (acoustic input); no CAT
How to Set CAT: n/a - no rig control or cable; tune the radio manually
Audio Device Select: Device microphone (acoustic coupling from the radio speaker, or a headphone/line adapter); normally the iOS default input
PTT Config: n/a - primarily receive/decode; any transmit is acoustic via the speaker plus the radio's VOX
Companion Needed: none
Notes: iPhone/iPad decoders (Chris Smolinski); hold the device near the radio speaker.
Source Note: Confirmed. Black Cat Systems' iOS decoders use the phone/tablet mic (acoustic) and have no CAT/PTT cabling.

Name: FDMDV
Rig Control Backend: none built-in - sound-card digital-voice (FDM DV) modem; PTT via serial/VOX, no CAT
How to Set CAT: n/a - no CAT; tune the SSB rig manually
Audio Device Select: Select the sound-card input and output; two audio paths are used - one for the user's microphone/speaker and one for the radio (a second/USB sound device)
PTT Config: PTT via a serial RS-232 RTS/DTR line, or VOX
ALC/Power Guidance: Multi-carrier (14-subcarrier DQPSK) DV - keep ALC minimal and reduce drive to avoid IMD; use DATA/USB
Companion Needed: none
Notes: FreeDV's predecessor - 1600 bps digital voice over SSB.
Source Note: Confirmed. FDMDV (Francesco Lanza HB9TLK) is the sound-card FDM digital-voice modem that FreeDV was based on; two sound devices (mic + radio), serial/VOX PTT, no CAT.

Name: IZ8BLY Stream
Rig Control Backend: none - sound-card MFSK16 (Stream) modem; PTT via COM, no CAT
How to Set CAT: n/a - no CAT; tune the rig manually
Audio Device Select: Select the sound-card input and output device
PTT Config: PTT via a serial COM RTS/DTR line, or VOX
Companion Needed: none
Notes: Nino IZ8BLY 'Stream' - the original Olivia (and MFSK) implementation for Windows.
Source Note: Confirmed. IZ8BLY 'Stream' is the MFSK16 sound-card program with VOX or serial-port PTT; no CAT.

Name: Mobilinkd Config app
Rig Control Backend: n/a - configures a hardware Bluetooth KISS TNC, not the radio directly
How to Set CAT: n/a - no CAT. The radio link is the TNC's audio + PTT cable into the HT; the app only tunes TNC parameters over Bluetooth LE.
Audio Device Select: n/a on the PC/phone - the TNC handles radio audio via the HT cable; the app sets the TNC's output level (transmit volume) and input gain
PTT Config: App configures the TNC's PTT method/behavior (the TNC's cable keys the radio); this is not a PC PTT line
Companion Needed: a Mobilinkd hardware TNC (TNC2/3/4) plus an APRS/packet app (e.g. APRSdroid) that speaks KISS over BLE
Notes: Bluetooth config utility for levels, PTT and KISS params; does no CAT/audio on the host itself.
Source Note: Confirmed. The Mobilinkd config app tunes a BLE KISS TNC (output level, input gain, PTT behavior); the TNC keys the radio via its cable.

Name: NinoTNC (N9600A) host utilities
Rig Control Backend: n/a - hardware KISS TNC over USB serial; no CAT
How to Set CAT: n/a - no CAT. The TNC wires to the radio's data/mic port (audio + PTT); host utilities only read/set KISS params over the USB serial port.
Audio Device Select: n/a - audio is handled inside the TNC hardware; on-board DIP switches select modem type/speed (1200/9600/etc.)
PTT Config: The TNC keys the radio's PTT directly; nothing to configure on the PC beyond KISS params
Companion Needed: a packet/AX.25 stack or app (Direwolf-style, BPQ, Winlink) speaking KISS over the USB serial link
Notes: NinoTNC is a hardware TNC; the 'host utilities' configure/monitor it, they don't touch radio audio.
Source Note: Confirmed. The NinoTNC (TARPN N9600A) is a hardware KISS TNC; DIP switches set the modem and the TNC keys the radio - the PC only exchanges KISS frames.

Name: OTWincfg (OpenTracker config, Argent Data)
Rig Control Backend: n/a - serial config of a hardware APRS tracker; no CAT
How to Set CAT: n/a - no CAT. The tracker connects to the radio's audio + PTT; OTWincfg only reads/writes the tracker's settings over serial.
Audio Device Select: n/a - the tracker generates the AFSK; you set its TX audio level in the config, not a PC sound device
PTT Config: Config sets the tracker's PTT behavior/timing (it keys the radio); serial config only
Companion Needed: an Argent Data OpenTracker/Tracker2 hardware unit + GPS + radio
Notes: Windows serial configuration tool for OpenTracker APRS trackers; no computer in the RF path after setup.
Source Note: Confirmed. OTWincfg configures Argent Data OpenTracker hardware over serial (TX audio level, PTT/path/timing); the tracker keys the radio.

Name: PacFORMS
Rig Control Backend: n/a - no radio access of its own
How to Set CAT: n/a - no CAT; it only produces/parses standardized message forms (used with Outpost / a packet TNC for the actual radio link)
Audio Device Select: n/a
PTT Config: n/a
Companion Needed: Outpost Packet Message Manager (which drives the packet TNC/radio); PacFORMS hands messages to Outpost
Notes: Browser/HTML packet form templates for emergency messaging; relies entirely on Outpost + a TNC for the RF link.
Source Note: Confirmed. PacFORMS is HTML/browser-based form software for packet messaging; it has no radio, audio or PTT interface.

Name: TinyTrak Config (Byonics)
Rig Control Backend: n/a - serial config of a hardware APRS tracker; no CAT
How to Set CAT: n/a - no CAT. The tracker keys the radio (AFSK + PTT); TinyTrak Config only reads/writes tracker settings over serial.
Audio Device Select: n/a - the tracker generates AFSK; TX audio level is set in the config, not a PC sound device
PTT Config: Config sets the tracker's PTT behavior (it keys the radio); serial config only
Companion Needed: a Byonics TinyTrak3/4 hardware unit + GPS + radio
Notes: Windows serial configuration utility for TinyTrak APRS trackers.
Source Note: Confirmed. Byonics TinyTrak Config is a serial configuration tool for the TinyTrak hardware tracker; the tracker keys the radio.

Name: TNC-Pi / TNC-X utilities (pitnc_getparams, xtinit)
Rig Control Backend: n/a - hardware KISS TNC; no CAT
How to Set CAT: n/a - no CAT. The TNC connects to the radio's audio + PTT; the utilities read/set the TNC's KISS params over serial (TNC-X) or the Raspberry Pi serial/I2C (TNC-Pi).
Audio Device Select: n/a - audio is handled by the TNC hardware modem (trim pots set levels)
PTT Config: The TNC keys the radio's PTT; nothing to set on the host beyond KISS params
Companion Needed: an AX.25/packet stack (Linux ax25, BPQ, Direwolf-as-KISS) plus APRS/Winlink apps on top
Notes: pitnc_getparams/pitnc_setparams read/set TNC params; xtinit initializes a TNC-X.
Source Note: Confirmed. pitnc_getparams/xtinit only initialize/read KISS parameters on the TNC-Pi/TNC-X hardware; the TNC handles audio and keys the radio.

Name: Alinco Programmer / Clone Utility
Rig Control Backend: none - programming/cloning only (not CAT)
How to Set CAT: n/a - Communications/Radio menu > select the COM port; Read (clone from radio) / Write (clone to radio). The radio is placed in clone mode.
Audio Device Select: n/a
PTT Config: n/a
Companion Needed: an Alinco ERW-4C / ERW-7 / ERW-8 USB/serial programming cable + driver
Notes: Reads/writes memory channels and settings; no audio/PTT/CAT.
Source Note: Confirmed. Alinco's clone/programming utilities read/write the radio's memory image over the programming cable; no operating CAT, audio or PTT.

Name: Anytone CPS
Rig Control Backend: none - codeplug programming only (not CAT)
How to Set CAT: n/a - Program > Read from Radio / Write to Radio; select the port under Set > COM.
Audio Device Select: n/a
PTT Config: n/a
Companion Needed: an AnyTone USB programming cable; the CPS version must match the radio's firmware revision
Notes: Full DMR codeplug (channels, contacts, zones); model- and firmware-specific.
Source Note: Confirmed. AnyTone CPS reads/writes the codeplug over USB; it is not a CAT/operating tool.

Name: AnyTone Firmware/QX Upgrade Tool
Rig Control Backend: none - firmware flashing only (not CAT)
How to Set CAT: n/a - put the radio in boot/upgrade mode (hold PTT + a function key while powering on), select the COM port, load the .bin/.spi image, and Write.
Audio Device Select: n/a
PTT Config: n/a
Companion Needed: a USB cable; matching firmware + digital-contact (QX) database files
Notes: Flashes firmware; separate tool from the CPS codeplug software.
Source Note: Confirmed. The AnyTone firmware/QX upgrade tool flashes firmware over USB in bootloader mode; no CAT/audio/PTT.

Name: ARC series (BuTel)
Rig Control Backend: none for amateur transceiver CAT - scanner programming (and live control on some models) via serial/USB
How to Set CAT: n/a - per model: select the COM port, then Read/Write the scanner memory; some Uniden/AOR models also allow live control (scan/hold/tune) from the app.
Audio Device Select: n/a (receiver audio is separate; no sound-card modem role)
PTT Config: n/a (receive-only scanners)
Companion Needed: the BuTel programming cable for the specific scanner
Notes: BuTel 'Advanced Radio Control' programming software family for Uniden/AOR/etc. scanners.
Source Note: Confirmed. BuTel ARC titles program/control Uniden/AOR scanners over serial/USB; not transceiver CAT and no PTT.

Name: Baofeng CPS (factory / VIP)
Rig Control Backend: none - programming only (not CAT)
How to Set CAT: n/a - Program > Read/Write; select the COM port.
Audio Device Select: n/a
PTT Config: n/a
Companion Needed: a Baofeng programming cable (CH340/FTDI/Prolific driver)
Notes: Reads/writes channels and settings; the VIP variant covers newer models.
Source Note: Confirmed. The Baofeng factory/VIP CPS reads/writes memory channels over the programming cable; no CAT/audio/PTT.

Name: Baofeng Ola
Rig Control Backend: none - wireless codeplug programming over Bluetooth (not CAT)

Source: Airspy SpyServer documentation / airspy groups.io wiki
Source Note: bind_port default 5555 confirmed in spyserver.config.

Name: AOR control software (ARC/AR-family)
Rig Control Backend: native AOR serial CAT (receiver control)
How to Set CAT: Select the AOR receiver's COM port + baud in the ARC title, then tune/scan and manage memories (AR5000/AR-DV1/AR-One etc.).
Audio Device Select: n/a (receiver audio; no soundcard-modem role)
PTT Config: n/a (receivers)
Companion Needed: AOR serial/USB control cable
Notes: Receiver-control/monitoring software; receive only.
Source: AOR / Butel ARC receiver control software
Source Note: The ARC-series titles are authored by Butel for AOR receivers; serial receiver-control description is accurate.

Name: ARSVCOM
Rig Control Backend: none — EA4TX antenna-rotator control (not a radio/CAT device)
How to Set CAT: No radio CAT. Set the ARS-USB interface's COM/USB port (the driver makes a virtual COM), or use UDP/network sharing; then calibrate rotator limits. The virtual port emulates a Yaesu GS-232A (default) and can emulate other rotator drivers.
Audio Device Select: n/a
PTT Config: n/a
Companion Needed: EA4TX ARS-USB (or Prosistel 'D') rotator interface; runs alongside satellite/contest trackers
Notes: Rotator calibration/control utility, not a transceiver tool.
Source Note: GS-232A default emulation confirmed on EA4TX site; the specific UDP port 12040 was not documented on the official site (plausible but unverified).

Name: CalicoCAT
Rig Control Backend: virtual-serial CAT emulation (Kenwood TS-2000 command set) via com0com — inside SDR#
How to Set CAT: In SDR#, open the CalicoCat panel, select one of the com0com virtual COM ports at 19200 8N1 and enable CAT; the partner app (WSJT-X/logger) opens the paired virtual COM and is configured as a Kenwood TS-2000.
Audio Device Select: n/a in-plugin — route SDR#'s audio to the digital app with a Virtual Audio Cable (e.g., VB-Cable)
PTT Config: n/a (receive; SDR# doesn't transmit)
Companion Needed: SDR#, a com0com virtual serial pair, and a VAC for audio; optionally points at a real radio to keep it synced with SDR#
Notes: Bridges SDR# tuning to other apps over CAT, or slaves a real rig to SDR#'s tuning.
Source Note: TS-2000 emulation, 19200 8N1, com0com pair all confirmed.

Name: Cloud-SDR / CloudIQ software
Rig Control Backend: none — controls an RFSpace networked SDR (Cloud-IQ/CloudSDR) over Ethernet; no transceiver CAT
How to Set CAT: No rig CAT. Connect to the SDR by its IP; choose IQ-streaming mode (SpectraVue/SDR-Radio/SDR#/GNU Radio) or the built-in 'Cloud' server mode (radio tunes/demodulates and streams to remote clients); mode is switched with the CloudSetup app; set sample-rate/gain.
Audio Device Select: n/a — streams IQ (UDP) or demodulated data over the network, not a soundcard
PTT Config: n/a (receive-only SDR)
Companion Needed: SpectraVue or an SDR client (SDR Console/SDR#); optional Virtual Audio Cable for digital modes
Notes: RFSpace Cloud-IQ (0.009–56 MHz, 24-bit) / CloudSDR remote SDR software.
Source Note: Description matches the RFSpace Cloud-IQ/CloudSDR receivers and their SpectraVue/CloudSetup control (not F4GKR's separately-named 'Cloud-SDR' software).

Name: Commander (DXLab)
Rig Control Backend: native multi-brand CAT/CI-V (also acts as the DXLab CAT hub)
How to Set CAT: Configuration > Ports > set the Primary CAT serial port (radio model/COM/baud/word/parity/stop, + CI-V address for Icom); a Secondary CAT port lets other DXLab apps (SpotCollector, WinWarbler) and third-party apps tune through Commander.
Audio Device Select: n/a (control only; digital audio handled by WinWarbler/other apps)
PTT Config: PTT via CAT, or via a DTR/RTS pin selected on the PTT tab
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a (Commander sets freq/mode/split; drive/ALC set on the radio or in the digital app)
Companion Needed: Standalone hub within DXLab Suite; other DXLab/3rd-party apps slave to it
Notes: Same program as 'DXLab Commander'; the CAT engine for the whole DXLab Suite.

Name: DDUtil
Rig Control Backend: reads a radio (PowerSDR CAT or serial CAT) and re-shares band/frequency data to multiple apps/devices via virtual serial ports
How to Set CAT: Ports tab > pick the Radio CAT port pair (e.g., PowerSDR on COM11 <-> DDUtil COM10 made with VSP Manager/VSPE); set band-data outputs (BCD/USB) and the Passive Listener port for SteppIR/tuners/slave radios.
Audio Device Select: n/a (control/band-data director, not audio)
PTT Config: Keys amplifiers/relays/antenna switches via band-data and accessory outputs (not mic PTT)
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a (drive/ALC set in PowerSDR/on the radio)
Companion Needed: PowerSDR/FlexRadio + a virtual serial port program (VSP Manager or VSPE)
Notes: 'Device Director' utility that lets one radio drive many frequency-following accessories/apps.

Name: DXLab Commander
Rig Control Backend: native multi-brand CAT/CI-V; DXLab's shared CAT hub
How to Set CAT: Configuration > Ports > choose radio model + COM port + baud (+ CI-V address); enable a Secondary CAT port so SpotCollector and other DXLab/3rd-party apps tune the rig through Commander.
Audio Device Select: n/a (control only)
PTT Config: PTT via CAT or a chosen DTR/RTS pin
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a (set drive/ALC on the radio or in the digital app)
Companion Needed: Standalone within DXLab Suite; other apps share the rig through it
Notes: The CAT engine shared by all DXLab apps; identical to the 'Commander (DXLab)' entry.
Source Note: Same product as 'Commander (DXLab)'.

Name: EA4TX ARSWIN / ARS-USB software
Rig Control Backend: none — EA4TX antenna-rotator control (serial/USB)
How to Set CAT: No radio CAT. Set the ARS-USB interface COM/USB port + rotator limits/calibration; provides DDE to loggers (Logger32/HRD) for click-to-turn.
Audio Device Select: n/a
PTT Config: n/a
Companion Needed: EA4TX ARS-USB rotator interface
Notes: Rotator control, not a transceiver tool.

Name: flrig
Rig Control Backend: standalone rig-control server using flrig's own native rig drivers; exposes an XML-RPC server (default port 12345) so fldigi/WSJT-X share the rig
How to Set CAT: Config > Setup > Transceiver > select Rig, Serial/Update port, Baud, stop bits and RTS/CTS flow; PTT tab sets the keying method. Other apps then choose 'flrig' as their rig (TCP 127.0.0.1:12345).
Audio Device Select: n/a (control only; audio is set in fldigi/WSJT-X)
PTT Config: PTT via CAT, RTS/DTR, or a separate serial line, chosen on the PTT tab
ALC/Power Guidance: Set the radio to DATA/USB-D and raise TX audio in the digital app until desired RF out with ALC barely moving (keep ALC low)
Companion Needed: Standalone; fldigi selects it in Rig Control, WSJT-X can use it, and any app can hit XML-RPC localhost:12345
Notes: Companion CAT server most commonly paired with fldigi.
Source Note: XML-RPC default port 12345 confirmed.

Name: FRStack
Rig Control Backend: FlexRadio SmartSDR TCP/IP API (no COM port / no rig model)
How to Set CAT: No serial CAT. FRStack discovers the Flex on the LAN via the SmartSDR API; expose a REST port (default 13522, localhost-only by default with an IP whitelist) for Stream Deck/web/macro control and read/set slice freq, mode, TX and keying.
Audio Device Select: n/a (audio handled by SmartSDR DAX, not FRStack)
PTT Config: TX/keying issued through the SmartSDR API (no serial PTT line)
ALC/Power Guidance: TX drive/power is set with the SmartSDR power slider; the Flex manages ALC internally
Companion Needed: A FlexRadio 6000/8000-series + SmartSDR; optional Elgato Stream Deck
Notes: Companion automation/accessory controller for FlexRadio (amps, SteppIR, macros).
Source Note: REST default port 13522 (configurable in FRStack.exe.config, localhost-only by default) confirmed.

Name: FT-817 Commander
Rig Control Backend: native Yaesu CAT (serial)
How to Set CAT: Select the FT-817/818/857/897 COM port + baud (4800 or 38400 per the radio's menu) in the app; controls VFO/mode/PTT.
Audio Device Select: n/a (control only)
PTT Config: PTT via the Yaesu CAT command (CAT and PTT share the single COM port)
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a (set power/drive on the radio; use the digital app's audio level for ALC on data modes)
Companion Needed: CAT cable (CT-62 / USB-serial) for the FT-8x7
Notes: Lightweight dedicated controller for Yaesu FT-8x7 radios.

Name: Green Heron Everyware
Rig Control Backend: none — Green Heron antenna-rotator controller software (serial/network)
How to Set CAT: No radio CAT. Point it at the Green Heron RT-21 controller's COM port or network (GH Everyware Server manages shared/networked access); provides DDE/network to loggers for point-and-shoot rotator control.
Audio Device Select: n/a
PTT Config: n/a
Companion Needed: Green Heron RT-21 (or compatible) rotator controller
Notes: Rotator control, not a transceiver tool.
Source: Green Heron Engineering — Rotator Controllers Software

Name: Grig
Rig Control Backend: Hamlib (direct serial or rigctld)
How to Set CAT: Launch with Hamlib params, e.g. grig -m <model#> -r <port> -s <baud> (defaults to /dev/ttyS0, or localhost for RPC-rig); a simple GTK front panel to set frequency, mode and PTT. Config is via command-line options, not stored.
Audio Device Select: n/a (control only)
PTT Config: PTT via Hamlib; disabled by default, enable with -p/--enable-ptt (type follows the Hamlib backend)
Companion Needed: Hamlib installed (Grig is a thin GUI over it)
Notes: Minimal GTK rig-control GUI.
Source Note: Minor clarification: PTT is off by default and enabled with -p/--enable-ptt.

Name: Ham Radio Deluxe (HRD Rig Control + Remote)
Rig Control Backend: native HRD radio drivers; also acts as a CAT hub and IP-remote server/client
How to Set CAT: Connect dialog > choose Company + Radio model + COM port + baud (CI-V address for Icom); other apps read the rig through HRD's 3rd-party/virtual interface. HRD Remote runs a server at the radio and a client remotely.
Audio Device Select: Digital audio via DM-780 (its Soundcard settings) or over HRD Remote's audio companion
PTT Config: PTT via CAT, or a chosen RTS/DTR pin; DM-780 keys through HRD Rig Control
ALC/Power Guidance: Set radio power; for digital modes in DM-780 raise TX audio until ALC just moves (keep it low)
Companion Needed: DM780 (bundled) for digital modes; HRD Logbook for logging; HRD Remote for IP operation
Notes: Full suite (Rig Control + Logbook + DM780 + Remote); the rig-control module aims to expose every documented CAT command.
Source: Ham Radio Deluxe User Guide — Radio Connection / DM-780

Name: HamClock
Rig Control Backend: Hamlib rigctld (or flrig) over the network (reads/sets frequency and mode only)
How to Set CAT: Run rigctld -m <model> -r <port> -s <baud> on the host; on HamClock Setup page 3 enable the rigctld connection (defaults Host: Localhost, Port: 4532). Clicking a DX-cluster/On-The-Air spot then sends the frequency to the radio's VFO A.
Audio Device Select: n/a (no audio)
PTT Config: n/a (HamClock does not key PTT)
Companion Needed: A separately-running Hamlib rigctld daemon
Notes: Info/dashboard display; CAT is an optional convenience feature, not a control console.
Source: HamClock User Guide (Clear Sky Institute, ClearSkyInstitute.com)
Source Note: Default rigctld port 4532 confirmed; flrig is also supported as the simple rig-control backend.

Name: Hamlib (rigctl / rigctld / rigctlcom)
Rig Control Backend: itself — the reference CAT/CI-V library; the backend most other apps use
How to Set CAT: Start the daemon: rigctld -m <model#> -r <port> -s <baud> (serves TCP 4532); rigctl is the interactive/CLI client; rigctlcom emulates a Kenwood TS-2000 on a serial port (needs a com0com/socat virtual pair) so TS-2000-only apps can drive any Hamlib-supported radio.
Audio Device Select: n/a (control only; apps handle audio)
PTT Config: PTT via -P/--ptt-type: RIG (CAT), RTS, DTR, CM108, GPIO, or PARALLEL
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a (set drive/ALC in the client digital app or on the radio)
Companion Needed: None — it IS the CAT backend for WSJT-X, fldigi, CQRLOG, Gpredict, etc.
Notes: 'rigctld' TCP 4532 is the network entry point; find your model number with rigctl --list.
Source Note: rigctld TCP 4532 and rigctlcom TS-2000 emulation confirmed.

Name: Hamlib (rigctl / rigctld)
Rig Control Backend: itself — the Hamlib CAT/CI-V library and its rigctld TCP server
How to Set CAT: rigctld -m <model#> -r <port> -s <baud> exposes TCP 4532 for other apps; rigctl gives interactive/CLI control of frequency/mode/PTT over serial/USB/network.
Audio Device Select: n/a (control only)
PTT Config: PTT via -P RIG/RTS/DTR/CM108/GPIO
Companion Needed: None; consumed by WSJT-X/fldigi/CQRLOG/Gpredict as their CAT backend
Notes: Duplicate list entry of the Hamlib rigctl/rigctld tooling.
Source Note: Duplicate Hamlib entry; confirmed.

Name: Hamlib / rigctld
Rig Control Backend: itself — Hamlib; the CAT/PTT tuning backend behind Gqrx, SDRangel, SDR++, wfview and Gpredict
How to Set CAT: rigctld -m <model#> -r <port> -s <baud> serves TCP 4532; SDR apps and Gpredict point their 'Hamlib rigctld' host/port at it for frequency/mode/PTT of hundreds of transceivers.
Audio Device Select: n/a (control only; the SDR/digital app handles audio/IQ)
PTT Config: PTT via -P RIG/RTS/DTR/CM108/GPIO
Companion Needed: None; the shared tuning backend for the SDR apps listed
Notes: Same tool as the other Hamlib entries, framed as the SDR-app tuning backend.
Source Note: Duplicate Hamlib entry; confirmed.

Name: IC7100BKT / CatBkt (BktSoftware)
Rig Control Backend: native Icom CI-V (IC7100BKT is IC-7100-specific; CatBkt is a universal CAT engine)
How to Set CAT: Setup/COM > select the COM port + baud + Icom CI-V address; controls frequency/mode/PTT/tuner and accepts direct-frequency input from cluster/DX programs.
Audio Device Select: Only for the built-in audio-recording feature (PC soundcard); not a modem
PTT Config: PTT via the CI-V/CAT command
Companion Needed: Icom USB port or a CT-17/CI-V interface
Notes: IZ2BKT (Mauro Capelli) software; free/donationware; adds memory/D-STAR/scan management.
Source Note: Built-in audio-recording feature and CI-V control confirmed.

Name: Icom RS-BA1
Rig Control Backend: native Icom CI-V plus audio-over-IP (via the Icom Remote Utility server)
How to Set CAT: Remote Utility connects to the radio (USB or LAN) as the server; the RS-BA1 Remote Control app auto-loads the radio + CI-V address and can expose a virtual COM port so loggers see the rig.
Audio Device Select: Audio is carried over IP by the Remote Utility — pick the PC's playback/recording (speaker/mic) devices and the codec in Remote Utility's audio settings (it creates a virtual audio cable pair per rig)
PTT Config: PTT via the RS-BA1 TX button / MOD source setting
ALC/Power Guidance: Set TX power and mic/data gain on the radio (or in RS-BA1); keep ALC in the radio's normal zone
Companion Needed: Icom Remote Utility (server) + a compatible Icom transceiver
Notes: Icom's own remote-control package; audio + control + PTT over LAN/Internet.

Name: Icom RS-BA1 (Version 2)
Rig Control Backend: native Icom CI-V plus digital audio-over-IP (Remote Utility server; or direct-LAN for network-capable rigs)
How to Set CAT: As v1 — Remote Utility hosts the radio (USB/LAN) and the RS-BA1 v2 client selects the rig + CI-V address, with an optional virtual COM for loggers. v2 improves latency/codec and supports network-native Icoms (IC-7610/7300/9700/705).
Audio Device Select: Over IP via Remote Utility — choose the PC audio devices + codec there
PTT Config: PTT via the RS-BA1 TX button / selected MOD source
ALC/Power Guidance: Set TX power + mic/data gain on the radio; keep ALC in the normal range
Companion Needed: Icom Remote Utility + a compatible Icom rig (some network rigs need no separate server PC)
Notes: Version 2 of Icom's remote software.

Name: Icom RS-R8600
Rig Control Backend: native Icom CI-V over USB or LAN (receiver control)
How to Set CAT: Connect the IC-R8600 by USB or network; set the CI-V address/network credentials; the app gives full remote tuning/scan plus the spectrum scope.
Audio Device Select: Receiver audio over USB/LAN (IP) — select in the app; it is a receiver, no modem role
PTT Config: n/a (IC-R8600 is a receiver)
Companion Needed: Icom IC-R8600 wideband receiver
Notes: Remote-control + scope software for the IC-R8600 receiver.
Source: Icom RS-R8600 / IC-R8600 remote control software documentation

Name: jAReC
Rig Control Backend: native/Hamlib-style CAT within a Java app; serves control + bidirectional audio to remote clients
How to Set CAT: Choose the rig's CAT connection (serial/USB-serial/Bluetooth/network) + model, and set the server TCP port. Runs as server (at the radio), client, or bridge.
Audio Device Select: Select the PC capture/playback (soundcard) devices; audio is sent alongside CAT over the network
PTT Config: PTT via the rig's CAT command over the link
ALC/Power Guidance: Set drive/mic on the radio; keep ALC normal for the mode
Companion Needed: PocketRxTx (Android client) at the matching version for full remote+audio; a CAT-capable rig
Notes: (j)ava (A)udio and (Re)mote (C)ontrol by YO3GGX; server and client in one.
Source Note: In v4 audio used the same-numbered UDP port as CAT; from v5 audio + waterfall + CAT all share the single server TCP port (UDP no longer used).

Name: K3NG Rotator Controller
Rig Control Backend: none — open-source Arduino antenna-rotator controller firmware (speaks Yaesu GS-232A/B or EasyComm over USB serial)
How to Set CAT: No radio CAT. The host tracker (SatPC32/Gpredict/PstRotator/Win-Test) talks the GS-232A/B or EasyComm protocol over the Arduino's USB serial port to command the rotator; you set the port/baud in that host program.
Audio Device Select: n/a
PTT Config: n/a
Companion Needed: Arduino + motor-driver hardware, plus a satellite/rotator control program on the PC
Notes: Rotator controller firmware, not a transceiver tool.
Source: K3NG Rotator Controller Wiki — Software: Computer Interface (GitHub)
Source Note: GS-232A/B + EasyComm emulation confirmed.

Name: K4-Control
Rig Control Backend: native Elecraft K4 network control, with a built-in CAT server (rigctl/Hamlib + Kenwood/Icom CAT over the network)
How to Set CAT: Enter the K4's IP address to connect (home or over the Internet); enable K4-Control's built-in CAT server so any network-CAT app (Hamlib/Kenwood/Icom protocol) can share the rig.
Audio Device Select: Uses the K4's remote/network audio; select devices in the app (macOS/iPadOS)
PTT Config: PTT/keying (including CW via USB paddle/keyboard) through the app over the network
ALC/Power Guidance: Set TX power/drive on the K4; keep ALC normal
Companion Needed: An Elecraft K4 (network remote capable); optional CAT client apps
Notes: Roskosch macOS/iOS controller for the Elecraft K4.
Source: K4-Control for Elecraft (Marcus Roskosch) — Software User Guide

Name: Kenwood ARCP / ARHP / ARVP suite
Rig Control Backend: native Kenwood CAT (ARCP) + KNS LAN hosting (ARHP) + VoIP audio (ARVP)
How to Set CAT: ARCP-xxxx > Tool > Setup > select the COM port + baud (USB or RS-232C), or connect to ARHP over the network. For remote, ARHP hosts the rig on the LAN/Internet and the operator runs ARCP + ARVP at the remote end.
Audio Device Select: ARVP (ARVP-10/10H) carries the audio — select the PC mic/speaker devices in ARVP; the transceiver's ACC2 audio is wired to the host PC sound I/O
PTT Config: PTT via ARCP's CAT command (send/mic)
ALC/Power Guidance: Set TX power + mic/data gain on the radio; keep ALC in the normal zone
Companion Needed: ARHP + ARVP for remote operation; a KNS-capable Kenwood (TS-590/TS-990 etc.)
Notes: ARCP = Radio Control Program, ARHP = Radio Host Program, ARVP = VoIP audio; together they form Kenwood's remote suite.
Source: Kenwood Network Command System (KNS) Setting Manual

Name: Kenwood ARCP / ARHP series
Rig Control Backend: native Kenwood CAT (ARCP) with ARHP for LAN remote hosting
How to Set CAT: ARCP > Tool > Setup > COM port + baud, or connect to an ARHP host over the network; controls freq/mode/menus/PTT.
Audio Device Select: Via ARVP (VoIP companion) for remote audio; local operation uses the radio's own audio
PTT Config: PTT via ARCP's CAT command
ALC/Power Guidance: Set power + mic/data gain on the radio; keep ALC normal
Companion Needed: ARHP for LAN remote (and ARVP for audio); a KNS-capable Kenwood
Notes: Duplicate/short form of the Kenwood ARCP/ARHP/ARVP suite entry.
Source: Kenwood Network Command System (KNS) Setting Manual
Source Note: Same Kenwood ARCP/ARHP/ARVP family as the prior entry.

Name: KX3 Companion
Rig Control Backend: native Elecraft CAT over USB-serial (via Android USB-OTG) or Bluetooth — it is an Android app, not a PC/COM utility
How to Set CAT: On Android, connect the KX3/KX2 via the Elecraft USB serial cable + a USB-OTG adapter (or a Bluetooth serial link) and set the serial baud rate; the app then controls VFO/mode/menus, presets and macros.
Audio Device Select: n/a — no soundcard path: CW/PSK31/RTTY are done in-app using the KX3's built-in text-mode encoders/decoders over CAT (there is no separate digital-audio application)
PTT Config: PTT via the Elecraft CAT command
ALC/Power Guidance: Set drive (PWR) on the KX3 (KX3 Companion has power presets); because data modes run as CAT text through the KX3's internal encoders, there is no PC TX-audio level to manage
Companion Needed: A serial or Bluetooth link to the KX3/KX2
Notes: Third-party utility (macros/memories/logging) for Elecraft KX-series.
Source: KX3 Companion (Andrea Salvatore IU4APC) — official site
Source Note: Corrected: KX3 Companion is an Android application (USB-OTG serial or Bluetooth), not a desktop COM-port utility, and it performs CW/PSK31/RTTY itself via the KX3's built-in CAT text data modes — so the original claim that 'digital audio is handled by a separate app' and the ALC-via-audio-level guidance are wrong (no soundcard is involved).

Name: LP-Bridge
Rig Control Backend: native Elecraft K3 CAT with virtual-COM fan-out (maintains a mirror K3 and re-exposes multiple virtual K3 ports)
How to Set CAT: Set the real K3's COM port + baud once in LP-Bridge; it presents several virtual K3 ports (paired via com0com) that each app (LP-PAN/N1MM/DXLab, up to ~5) opens, keeping frequency/mode in sync across all of them.
Audio Device Select: n/a (control sharing only; LP-PAN's IF audio is set in the panadapter app)
PTT Config: PTT commands pass through transparently to the K3
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a (set on the K3/in the digital app)
Companion Needed: An Elecraft K3, com0com virtual serial pairs; commonly used with LP-PAN/NaP3
Notes: TelePost CAT splitter so multiple programs share one K3 CAT port.

Name: microHAM USB Device Router
Rig Control Backend: native passthrough to the rig plus virtual COM ports (routes CAT, keys PTT/CW/FSK) via microHAM interfaces
How to Set CAT: Router > Ports/Device tabs > set the radio type + baud, then create and assign virtual COM ports for CAT, 2nd-CAT, PTT, CW and FSK; logging/digital apps open those virtual ports to reach the rig through the microHAM hardware.
Audio Device Select: On interfaces with a built-in codec (microKEYER/MK2R), select that microHAM sound device as the app's input/output
PTT Config: PTT via the interface (footswitch, DTR/RTS, or CAT), configured per port in Router (DTR*/RTS* inhibit for 1s after the port opens)
ALC/Power Guidance: Set radio drive; for data modes raise TX audio until ALC just moves (keep it low)
Companion Needed: A microHAM interface (micro KEYER II / MK2R+ / DIGI KEYER etc.)
Notes: The 'glue' driver that maps one radio to many virtual ports for contesting/digital software.

Name: N4PY
Rig Control Backend: native multi-brand CAT (Icom/Elecraft/Ten-Tec/Kenwood/Yaesu) with virtual ports to share the rig
How to Set CAT: Interface/Setup > select the radio + COM port + baud (CI-V address/speed for Icom); enable second/virtual COM ports (5-8, Kenwood command emulation) so logging/digital software share the rig; supports panadapter and TMate2 knob control.
Audio Device Select: n/a (control software; digital audio handled by the slaved app)
PTT Config: PTT via the rig's CAT command
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a (set drive/ALC on the radio or in the digital app)
Companion Needed: Virtual serial pairs to share the rig; optional TMate2/panadapter
Notes: N4PY rig control by Carl Moreschi; strong Ten-Tec/Elecraft/Icom support.

Name: N4PY Rig Control
Rig Control Backend: native multi-brand CAT/CI-V; drives external panadapter programs/controllers alongside the radio
How to Set CAT: Setup > choose the radio + COM port + baud (+ CI-V address), add virtual/second ports for other apps; can also feed an external panadapter and hardware controllers (TMate2/FlexControl).
Audio Device Select: n/a (control only)
PTT Config: PTT via the rig's CAT command
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a (set on the radio / digital app)
Companion Needed: Virtual serial pairs for sharing; optional panadapter hardware/TMate2
Notes: Same product as the 'N4PY' entry (duplicate list line).
Source Note: Same product as 'N4PY'.

Name: NaP3
Rig Control Backend: native Elecraft K3 CAT (often shared via LP-Bridge) with an IF/IQ panadapter display
How to Set CAT: On NaP3's setup tab select 'Elecraft K3' rig type and the K3's CAT COM port (or a virtual port from LP-Bridge) + baud; NaP3 follows the K3 and lets you click-tune VFO/filters/bands/modes on the panadapter.
Audio Device Select: Select the soundcard/IQ input fed from LP-PAN or the K3's IF (the panadapter's I/Q source, not a modem), plus the decoded-audio output
PTT Config: n/a (display + tuning; the K3 handles TX)
Companion Needed: An I/Q source (LP-PAN or K3 IF) and LP-Bridge to share the K3 CAT port
Notes: Panadapter/spectrum software for the Elecraft K3/P3 family.

Name: OmniRig
Rig Control Backend: native CAT broker (a COM-automation server) that provides tuning/PTT to consuming apps
How to Set CAT: OmniRig Settings > Rig 1 / Rig 2 > choose Rig type + COM port + baud + poll/timeout; consuming apps (SDR#, HDSDR, SDRuno, NaP3, loggers) simply select 'OmniRig Rig 1/2' instead of a COM port.
Audio Device Select: n/a (control broker only)
PTT Config: PTT via CAT; the RTS/DTR pin behavior is set per consuming app
Companion Needed: Standalone broker; SDR/logging/digital apps consume it so the SDR panadapter and rig stay in sync
Notes: Ships with an .ini per rig type; the de-facto CAT hub on Windows for SDR sync.

Name: OmniRig (v1 & v2)
Rig Control Backend: native CAT/CI-V broker; shares one rig's COM port among many apps
How to Set CAT: Settings > Rig 1/Rig 2 (v2 adds Rig 3/4) > pick Rig type + COM port + baud + poll; HDSDR/SDR Console/loggers/digital apps then select 'OmniRig'. v2 supports more rigs/commands and user-editable command sets.
Audio Device Select: n/a (control broker)
PTT Config: PTT via CAT; RTS/DTR handled by the consuming app
Companion Needed: Standalone; consumed by SDR/logging/digital apps
Notes: v2 is the expanded rewrite of the original OmniRig broker.
Source Note: v2 adds Rig 3/4 and editable command sets, as claimed.

Name: PocketRxTx
Rig Control Backend: native per-brand CAT/CI-V (Yaesu/Kenwood/Icom/Elecraft) over Bluetooth, USB-OTG or network; can use a jAReC server for audio
How to Set CAT: Select the radio brand/model + connection (Bluetooth / USB-OTG serial / TCP) + baud; for remote+audio point it at a jAReC server IP/port. Controls mode/band/VFO/PTT/ATU.
Audio Device Select: Via a jAReC server or WebSDR (audio comes over the network); no local soundcard modem role otherwise
PTT Config: PTT via the rig's CAT command over the chosen link
ALC/Power Guidance: Set drive/mic on the radio; keep ALC normal for the mode
Companion Needed: USB-OTG/Bluetooth/serial link, or a jAReC server for remote+audio
Notes: Android transceiver/WebSDR remote-control app by YO3GGX.

Name: RCForb Client (RemoteHams)
Rig Control Backend: none locally — sends CAT + PTT and streams TX/RX audio to a remote radio via an RCForb Server
How to Set CAT: No local rig/COM. Log into the RemoteHams network or a specific RCForb Server, pick a radio, and tune/change mode/PTT in the client; all control is relayed to the remote station.
Audio Device Select: Uses the PC mic/speakers — choose them in the client's Audio settings; audio is streamed to/from the server
PTT Config: PTT via the client's TX button or a configured PTT key
ALC/Power Guidance: Managed/limited by the host station; you set mic gain, not the radio's ALC directly
Companion Needed: An RCForb Server hosting a radio + a free RemoteHams account
Notes: Client side of the RemoteHams platform.

Name: RCForb Server (RemoteHams)
Rig Control Backend: native CAT (broad Hamlib-like radio list) plus a sound card; keys PTT and relays control/audio to clients
How to Set CAT: Radios > Add Radio > select the radio model + COM port + baud + PTT method; select the rig's sound-card in/out; set user access/permissions. Then it publishes to RCForb clients.
Audio Device Select: Pick the radio's sound-card input and output devices in the server's audio config
PTT Config: PTT via CAT/CI-V, or an RTS/DTR/CTS serial line on a COM port, as chosen when adding the radio (VOX also possible)
ALC/Power Guidance: Set radio drive/mic gain; for data keep ALC low — the server passes client audio to the rig
Companion Needed: A physical radio + audio/CAT interface; publishes to RCForb clients
Notes: Host side of RemoteHams; the machine physically connected to the radio.

Name: Remote Ham Radio (RHR)
Rig Control Backend: none for the user — commercial, professionally-hosted remote stations; control is entirely server-side
How to Set CAT: No local CAT/COM. Use the RHR web/app console to select a hosted station, then tune and change mode/PTT in the browser/app; the rig, COM ports and interfaces live at RHR's sites.
Audio Device Select: Audio streams in the browser/app — use the PC/phone's own output and mic
PTT Config: PTT via the RHR console (and remote keyer support)
ALC/Power Guidance: Managed by RHR at the station; the operator has no direct ALC control
Companion Needed: An RHR membership/credits and a browser or the RHR app — nothing else local
Notes: Paid shared-remote-station service, not software you point at your own rig.

Name: RemoteHams RCForb
Rig Control Backend: the RCForb client/server platform: server drives a real radio's CAT + sound card + PTT; clients relay CAT/PTT/audio over IP
How to Set CAT: Server end: Add Radio > model + COM port + baud + PTT + sound-card device. Client end: log into RemoteHams, pick a radio, and tune/PTT — no local COM needed.
Audio Device Select: Server: the rig's sound-card in/out. Client: the PC's mic/speakers (audio is streamed).
PTT Config: Server keys PTT via CAT/RTS/DTR/CTS; client triggers it via its TX button
ALC/Power Guidance: Set drive/mic at the host station; keep ALC low for data
Companion Needed: RemoteHams account; a hosting PC + radio (server) or just a client
Notes: General 'RCForb' platform entry combining the client and server roles.
Source Note: Same RemoteHams RCForb platform as the separate Client/Server entries.

Name: RemoteRig / RRC-Nano
Rig Control Backend: transparent serial-CAT tunnel — the radio's own CAT/PTT/audio are transported over IP between the two RRC ends (RRC-Nano/PC-Client is the software control end)
How to Set CAT: Not a driver: configure the RemoteRig RRC boxes (radio-end and control-end) via their web pages with matching serial settings + audio codec; the PC-Client/RRC-Nano provides virtual COM ports + audio at the control end so your logger sees the rig's native CAT.
Audio Device Select: The RRC codec / PC-Client soundcard carries audio — select devices in the PC-Client/RRC-Nano
PTT Config: PTT is carried over the tunnel (PTT line / COM); the radio does the actual keying
ALC/Power Guidance: Set drive/mic on the radio at the remote end; keep ALC normal
Companion Needed: Microbit RemoteRig RRC-1258 hardware pair (or compatible split rig); RRC-Nano software
Notes: Transports control/PTT/audio between a control site and the radio site.

Name: RemoteTX
Rig Control Backend: Hamlib-based rig control running on a Raspberry Pi image, connecting to the rig's CAT/USB + audio; clients operate over the Internet via RemoteTx cloud (no port-forwarding)
How to Set CAT: In the RemoteTX web UI select the radio model + its USB/serial device; the Audio tab > choose the 'USB Audio Codec' sound card + Start Audio, then Call Radio for bidirectional audio.
Audio Device Select: Pi's 'USB Audio Codec' sound device (the rig's USB audio), selected in the Audio tab
PTT Config: PTT via the web client — CAT/Hamlib for CAT radios, or a GPIO relay (pin 12/physical 32) in the PTT-only mode for HTs/fixed-frequency radios
ALC/Power Guidance: Set TX power/mic on the radio; keep ALC in the normal zone
Companion Needed: A RemoteTX Raspberry Pi image + account/cloud; a CAT + USB-audio rig (IC-7300/FT-991A etc.)
Notes: Turnkey Pi-based cloud remote base.
Source Note: Minor clarification: for HT/fixed-frequency radios PTT is a GPIO relay; for CAT radios PTT is via Hamlib.

Name: rig-remote
Rig Control Backend: Hamlib rigctld (network/rigctl-protocol client over TCP/telnet)
How to Set CAT: Start rigctld -m <model> -r <port> (TCP 4532) separately; in rig-remote set the rigctld hostname/IP + port. Provides threaded scanning, bookmarks and frequency/mode control through that daemon.
Audio Device Select: n/a (no audio path)
PTT Config: n/a for most use (mainly RX/scanning; any PTT is via rigctld if the rig supports it)
Companion Needed: A separately-running Hamlib rigctld daemon
Notes: Lightweight Python bookmark/scanner front-end to rigctld.

Name: RigPi Station Server
Rig Control Backend: Hamlib (controls the rig's CAT port, keys PTT/CW) with browser-based audio streaming
How to Set CAT: RigPi web UI > Radio/Config > select the Hamlib rig model + serial device + baud; set the PTT method and CW keying; operate from any browser with audio streamed from the Pi.
Audio Device Select: A USB sound device on the Pi, streamed to the browser — chosen in RigPi's audio settings
PTT Config: PTT via Hamlib (CAT), or a GPIO/serial line; CW via the onboard K1EL WinKey keyer board
ALC/Power Guidance: Set TX power/mic on the radio; keep ALC normal
Companion Needed: A RigPi Raspberry Pi image; a CAT rig + USB audio interface
Notes: MFJ/Howard Nurse (W6HN) Pi web station server built on Hamlib.

Name: SDR-Control Mobile
Rig Control Backend: native Icom CI-V over USB or WLAN/Internet (no extra hardware)
How to Set CAT: Add the Icom model (IC-705/7300/9700/7610 etc.) and connect via USB or the radio's built-in WLAN/network; the CI-V address is handled automatically.
Audio Device Select: Audio over USB or WLAN (the Icom's own audio) — no soundcard cabling needed
PTT Config: PTT via the app (CI-V)
ALC/Power Guidance: Set TX power/mic on the radio; keep ALC in the normal zone
Companion Needed: A network/USB-audio-capable Icom (IC-705/7300/9700/7610)
Notes: Roskosch iOS/macOS Icom control + audio + PTT app.

Name: SmartSDR for iOS
Rig Control Backend: FlexRadio SmartSDR TCP/IP API + SmartLink (no COM/CAT, no rig model/baud)
How to Set CAT: Discover the FlexRadio on the LAN or log in via SmartLink for remote; the app controls slices/tune/mode/PTT directly over the API — there is no serial CAT to configure.
Audio Device Select: DAX/Opus audio over the API; the app plays/records through the device
PTT Config: PTT/keying issued through the SmartSDR API
ALC/Power Guidance: TX power/drive set in SmartSDR; the Flex manages ALC internally
Companion Needed: A FlexRadio 6000/8000-series + a SmartLink account for remote
Notes: Marcus Roskosch's iOS/iPadOS SmartSDR client for FlexRadio.
Source: SmartSDR for iOS User Guide (Marcus Roskosch)
Source Note: Note: DAX audio works on the local network but is not available over a SmartLink remote connection (per the user guide).

Name: SoapySDR / SoapyRemote
Rig Control Backend: none — a hardware-abstraction API for SDR devices; SoapyRemote proxies a device on another host over the network (not a transceiver CAT)
How to Set CAT: No rig CAT. Apps set SoapySDR 'device args' (e.g., driver=rtlsdr or driver=remote,remote=tcp://host:55132); on the remote machine run SoapySDRServer (default bind port 55132) to expose its SDR. Tuning happens through the client app's SDR controls.
Audio Device Select: n/a — moves IQ samples, not soundcard audio
PTT Config: n/a for RX SDRs; TX only on TX-capable SDRs via the client app
Companion Needed: A SoapySDR-supported SDR + a client (CubicSDR/SDRangel/GNU Radio)
Notes: Vendor-neutral SDR driver layer; SoapyRemote adds network transport.
Source Note: Default SoapySDRServer port 55132 confirmed.

Name: TMATE2 controller software
Rig Control Backend: none by itself — the ELAD TM-2/Woodbox TMate2 is a USB-HID knob console; the host SDR software translates its input into CAT
How to Set CAT: No direct rig link. For PowerSDR/HPSDR use the Tmate2CatControl helper to map the knob to CAT; N4PY, NaP3, FDM-SW1/2, Studio1 and SDR-Radio Console v2 accept the TM-2 natively — set the rig CAT in that host program.
Audio Device Select: n/a (controller only)
PTT Config: The knob's PTT/keys are sent to the host, which performs the actual PTT via its CAT
Companion Needed: Elad TM-2/TMate2 hardware + a host SDR program (N4PY/NaP3/PowerSDR/FDM-SW2)
Notes: A tactile tuning/mode/PTT console for SDR software, not a standalone CAT tool.
Source Note: Official vendor page not captured; description of USB-HID knob + host-CAT translation is accurate.

Name: TRX-Manager
Rig Control Backend: native multi-brand CAT/CI-V (150+ transceivers); also a control hub/remote server
How to Set CAT: Configuration > Transceiver > select brand/model + COM port + baud (+ CI-V address/poll; e.g. 19200 for Icom/Ten-Tec, AUTO baud off); a TCP/UDP server (TRXNET) lets it operate remotely and share control with other software.
Audio Device Select: Soundcard for its built-in digital/remote audio (selected in its audio/remote setup)
PTT Config: PTT via CAT, or a chosen RTS/DTR line
ALC/Power Guidance: Set drive on the radio; for data raise TX audio until ALC just moves (keep low)
Companion Needed: Standalone hub; optional remote client for over-IP operation
Notes: F6DEX comprehensive rig-control/logging suite.

Name: TS-Control
Rig Control Backend: native Kenwood CAT (serial/USB or network) with a built-in CAT server (rigctl/Hamlib + Kenwood/Icom protocol over the network)
How to Set CAT: Connect to the Kenwood (serial/USB or network); enable TS-Control's built-in CAT server so any network-CAT app (Hamlib/Kenwood/Icom) can share the rig. Offers dual-VFO waterfalls + DX-cluster spots.
Audio Device Select: Uses the radio's USB/network audio; select devices in the app (macOS/iPadOS)
PTT Config: PTT via the app, including CW keying (keyboard or a USB-connected paddle)
ALC/Power Guidance: Set TX power/drive on the Kenwood; keep ALC normal
Companion Needed: A waterfall-capable Kenwood (TS-590SG/TS-890/TS-990); optional CAT clients
Notes: Roskosch macOS/iOS SDR-style client for Kenwood transceivers.

Name: W4MQ Internet Remote Base
Rig Control Backend: native CAT on the Host (radio-specific, via per-radio definition files); Host controls the rig + audio, Client tunes/PTT over IP (UDP)
How to Set CAT: IRB Host: select the radio type + COM port + baud + PTT, then publish the station. IRB Client: connect by the host's address and tune/change mode; no local COM at the client.
Audio Device Select: IRB audio over IP — select the PC's mic/speaker devices at each end
PTT Config: PTT via the Client (relayed to the Host's CAT/keying)
ALC/Power Guidance: Set drive/mic on the host radio; keep ALC normal
Companion Needed: W4MQ IRB Host (server) + Client software; a CAT-capable rig at the host
Notes: Free W4MQ/Handiham remote-base toolkit (Host + Client).
Source: W4MQ Internet Remote Base software (Handiham Remote Base / Stan Schretter W4MQ)
Source Note: Clarification: the Host uses radio-specific command sets defined in radio-definition files (supports Kenwood, Icom, Ten-Tec, etc.), so 'Kenwood-style protocol' is an over-simplification — the client<->host link is W4MQ's own protocol.

Name: Waterfall Bandmap
Rig Control Backend: CAT via OmniRig (or a direct COM) for reading/setting frequency; an SDR/IF I/Q source drives the waterfall display
How to Set CAT: Set the CAT connection (usually OmniRig, or a direct COM port) so the bandmap follows the rig and can QSY it; select the SDR/soundcard I/Q input (or ExtIO) for the waterfall, then click a spot/waterfall to tune the radio. Spots/callsigns can come from DXLab Commander.
Audio Device Select: Soundcard/ExtIO I/Q input feeding the waterfall (from an SDR/IF tap) — select in its settings
PTT Config: n/a (tuning/spotting tool; no PTT)
Companion Needed: OmniRig (or direct CAT) + an I/Q source (SDR-IF/RTL); pairs with logger spot data
Notes: Point-and-click spot bandmap over an SDR waterfall.
Source: Interoperating with the Waterfall Bandmap (DXLab Wiki) / AC0C
Source Note: OmniRig (or direct COM) CAT confirmed via DXLab interop notes.

Name: wfview
Rig Control Backend: native Icom CI-V (serial) or Icom LAN/network control; can emulate a Hamlib rigctld server and expose a virtual serial + virtual audio for other apps
How to Set CAT: Settings > Radio Access > choose Serial (COM port + baud + CI-V address) or Network (IP/username/password for LAN-capable Icoms). Enable the built-in rigctld (default port 4532; preferred) and/or a virtual serial port so WSJT-X/fldigi/N1MM reach the rig through wfview.
Audio Device Select: Settings > audio input/output devices (e.g. USB CODEC), or the Icom LAN audio for network rigs
PTT Config: PTT via CI-V (and a mapped virtual serial line for slaved apps)
ALC/Power Guidance: Set TX power/mic in wfview or on the radio; for data keep ALC low
Companion Needed: An Icom rig; pairs with WSJT-X/fldigi via its rigctld + virtual serial/audio
Notes: Open-source cross-platform Icom control with local or LAN/Internet operation.
Source: wfview User Manual — Settings / Virtual Serial Ports (wfview.org)
Source Note: rigctld emulation on 4532 plus virtual serial/audio confirmed.

Name: Win4Icom / Win4Yaesu / Win4K3-K4 Suite
Rig Control Backend: native CI-V (Icom) / CAT (Yaesu/Elecraft) with a virtual-radio server (provides virtual radios to N1MM+/HRD/DXLab/Log4OM)
How to Set CAT: Setup > Radio > select the model + COM port + baud (or LAN for networked rigs); Tools > Settings > 3rd Party SW/HW tab creates Virtual/AUX radio ports (via com0com) that loggers/digital apps open. Handles PTT and band data.
Audio Device Select: DAX/soundcard for the integrated SDR panadapter/digital audio (selected in its audio setup)
PTT Config: Software PTT via CAT (band-data outputs for amps/switches); note the virtual ports do NOT pass DTR/RTS, so always use software-based PTT
ALC/Power Guidance: Set radio drive/mic; for data raise TX audio to near-zero ALC
Companion Needed: com0com virtual serial pairs so N1MM+/HRD/DXLab/Log4OM slave to its virtual radios
Notes: VA2FSQ Win4-series CAT hub + panadapter for Icom/Yaesu/Elecraft.
Source Note: Clarification per the manual: Win4 does not support DTR/RTS through its virtual ports — software (CAT) PTT is required.

Name: Win4K3Suite / Win4IcomSuite / Win4YaesuSuite
Rig Control Backend: Native CAT control of hardware Yaesu/Icom/Elecraft rigs with an integrated SDRPlay panadapter and a server interface (up to 4 auxiliary CAT ports) so external loggers/digital apps read frequency/band/mode.
How to Set CAT: Setup > Radio: select model + COM port + baud (or LAN); enable the auxiliary/virtual CAT ports (needs com0com virtual serial pairs) so external apps track frequency/band/mode. Panadapter is via an SDRPlay/RSP.
Audio Device Select: Digital/voice audio uses the transceiver's USB sound codec (or a VAC); the panadapter is an SDRPlay/RSP spectrum display, not an audio device. 'DAX' is FlexRadio-specific and not used here.
PTT Config: PTT via CAT; band-data outputs key amps/switches.
ALC/Power Guidance: Set drive/mic on the radio; keep ALC low for data.
Companion Needed: com0com virtual pairs; other loggers slave to its server/virtual radios
Notes: The Win4 suite by brand (K3/K4, Icom, Yaesu) — same family as the Win4Icom/Win4Yaesu/Win4K3-K4 entry.
Source Note: CAT setup, com0com virtual/auxiliary ports for third-party apps, and the integrated panadapter are confirmed against the va2fsq manuals. But 'DAX' is FlexRadio (6000-series) terminology and does not apply to Win4, which controls hardware Yaesu/Icom/Elecraft rigs. The panadapter is a spectrum DISPLAY fed by an SDRPlay/RSP receiver (not an audio device); digital/voice audio uses the transceiver's own USB sound codec or a virtual audio cable.

Name: Yaesu SCU-LAN10
Rig Control Backend: Native Yaesu control via the SCU-LAN10 hardware interface over LAN (CAT + audio + PTT).
How to Set CAT: Connect the SCU-LAN10 box to the radio's CAT + audio jacks; in the SCU-LAN10 PC software enter the unit's IP/credentials + radio model (CAT rate 115200). CAT rides the LAN through the box.
Audio Device Select: Audio is carried over LAN; select your PC playback/mic devices in the app (Windows volume also applies).
PTT Config: On-screen PTT button over the LAN link (right-click switches PTT/MOX); VOX Select set to USB for TX audio.
ALC/Power Guidance: Set TX power/mic on the radio; keep ALC in the normal zone.
Companion Needed: Yaesu SCU-LAN10 hardware + a supported Yaesu rig (FTDX101/FTDX10/FT-991A/FTDX3000 etc.)
Notes: Yaesu's official LAN remote-control interface + PC software.
Source Note: Confirmed against the Yaesu SCU-LAN10 Operation/Installation manuals: CAT rate 115200 bps, PC sound devices for audio, on-screen PTT (right-click toggles PTT/MOX).

Name: Yaesu SCU-LAN10 Network Remote
Rig Control Backend: Native Yaesu network remote (SCU-LAN10 hardware bridge) - not Hamlib; CAT + audio + PTT tunnel over LAN.
How to Set CAT: In the SCU-LAN10 PC software's connection/network settings enter the network unit's IP/host and password; radio type follows the connected rig. CAT (115200) rides the LAN - no local COM port.
Audio Device Select: Audio streamed over the network; in the app's Audio settings pick PC playback (RX) and microphone (TX) - standard PC sound devices, not a rig codec.
PTT Config: On-screen/keyboard TX button (or footswitch wired to the SCU-LAN10 unit); command sent over LAN.
ALC/Power Guidance: Use the radio's mic/DATA level plus RF Power; raise TX audio until rated RF out with ALC just deflecting.
Companion Needed: needs the SCU-LAN10 hardware interface at the radio; none other (can feed WSJT-X etc. only if you add a virtual COM/audio cable)
Notes: Remote-operation bridge for three Yaesu radios, not a shared CAT hub.
Source Note: Same product/manual as above; confirmed. CAT/audio/PTT tunnel over LAN through the SCU-LAN10 unit to FTDX101/FTDX10/FT-710; no local COM port; audio uses standard PC sound devices.

Name: AMSATDroid Free
Rig Control Backend: None - no CAT/rig control.
How to Set CAT: n/a - Android app only predicts passes and shows the pass on a compass/map view; you dial frequencies manually.
Audio Device Select: n/a
PTT Config: n/a
Companion Needed: none (manual tuning of your handheld/rig)
Notes: Display/prediction-only satellite tracker.
Source Note: Confirmed (G4DPZ, open source). Pass-prediction/display only - no CAT, no rig control, no automatic Doppler correction.

Name: CALSAT32 (Calsat)
Rig Control Backend: Native CAT (Icom CI-V, Yaesu, Kenwood) plus rotator control
How to Set CAT: In Calsat32's setup/config select the radio type, COM port and baud; it sets RX and TX VFOs for Doppler. Rotator gets its own COM port in the same config.
Audio Device Select: n/a — Calsat32 does not handle audio
PTT Config: n/a — full-duplex TX is done on the radio; Calsat32 only retunes
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a — no TX audio; set mic/DATA level and ALC on the radio itself for SSB/CW
Companion Needed: none for SSB/CW by ear; a separate soundcard app if you run digital
