Notes: JR1HUO Calsat32; Doppler tuning + antenna tracking.
Source Note: Confirmed to exist (JR1HUO, Windows satellite tracker with CAT rig control + rotator control for Doppler; no audio). Detailed CI-V/Yaesu/Kenwood brand support and per-menu CAT setup could not be verified from the official (Japanese) site during this audit, so the field values are marked uncertain pending the developer's own docs.

Name: CardSat
Rig Control Backend: Native CAT on the Cardputer ADV - Icom CI-V, Yaesu, Kenwood, plus native Icom LAN (RS-BA1) for the IC-9700 over WiFi.
How to Set CAT: On-device menu: choose the radio family and wire the CAT cable to the rig (or configure WiFi/LAN for IC-9700). Applies the AMSAT One True Rule.
Audio Device Select: n/a - no audio path
PTT Config: n/a - the radio transmits; CardSat only Doppler-tunes
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a - set drive/ALC on the radio
Companion Needed: the M5Stack Cardputer ADV hardware itself; none else
Notes: Pocket hardware controller (not PC software); also does GS-232/rotctl/PstRotator rotator control and VUCC/WAS/DXCC footprint.
Source Note: Confirmed against the CardSat GitHub. Self-contained M5Stack Cardputer ADV (ESP32-S3): Icom CI-V (incl. IC-820/821/910/9100/9700), Yaesu FT-847/FT-736R, Kenwood TS-790/TS-2000, plus native Icom LAN (RS-BA1) to the IC-9700 over WiFi. Applies the AMSAT 'One True Rule' (uplink+downlink together).

Name: FoxTelem
Rig Control Backend: Sound-card telemetry decoder; newer builds add optional Hamlib/SDR rig control for Find-Signal/Doppler.
How to Set CAT: In Settings enable the rig/Find-Signal option and point it at a Hamlib rigctld host:port or an SDR; older builds are audio-only with no CAT.
Audio Device Select: Settings > Input: select the soundcard input (left channel by default) from your receiver, or IQ from an SDR.
PTT Config: n/a - receive-only telemetry decoder
Companion Needed: needs a receiver or SDR (e.g., FUNcube dongle) feeding audio/IQ
Notes: AMSAT Fox/HuskySat/GOLF telemetry decoder + server upload.
Source Note: Confirmed against the AMSAT/G0KLA FoxTelem manual. Reads soundcard audio (left channel default) or IQ from an SDR; newer builds add optional rig control (Find Signal) via Hamlib.

Name: gp2icom
Rig Control Backend: Python glue script that sends CI-V CAT directly to an IC-9700 or IC-9100 (replaces Hamlib for these rigs).
How to Set CAT: Edit gp2icom.py: set the serial port (e.g. /dev/ttyUSB0 or COM5) and CI-V address (0xA2/162 for 9700, 0x7C/124 for 9100). On the radio set CI-V USB baud 115200 (9700)/19200 (9100), Transceive ON, Echo Back OFF. It listens on TCP 4532 for Gpredict.
Audio Device Select: n/a
PTT Config: n/a - full-duplex SSB done on the radio
Companion Needed: needs Gpredict (drives it) plus the IC-9700/9100
Notes: DL7OAP script; uses main/sub band (not the rig's sat mode) so internal Doppler doesn't fight Gpredict.
Source Note: Confirmed exactly against the gp2icom README: IC-9700 CI-V Transceive ON, USB baud 115200, USB Echo Back OFF, address 162 (0xA2); IC-9100 Transceive ON, baud 19200, address 124 (0x7C). Listens on 4532 for Gpredict; edit the serial port in gp2icom.py.

Name: Gpredict
Rig Control Backend: Hamlib rigctld over TCP for the rig (and rotctld for rotors); can also drive rigctld-speaking SDRs (e.g. Gqrx 7356).
How to Set CAT: Edit > Preferences > Interfaces > Radios: add a radio pointing at the rigctld host:port, choose the Doppler/RX-TX type (Simplex, Duplex TRX, or two devices). Start rigctld separately against your rig.
Audio Device Select: n/a - Gpredict tunes only, no audio
PTT Config: n/a - TX handled by the rig or the soundcard/digital app
Companion Needed: needs Hamlib rigctld running against a radio or SDR (Gqrx/SDR++); soundcard app for digital
Notes: De-facto Linux satellite tracker.
Source Note: Confirmed. Menu is Edit > Preferences > Interfaces > Radios (some third-party guides say 'Options'); connects to a separately-launched rigctld (default TCP 4532) / rotctld (4533).

Name: gr-satellites
Rig Control Backend: GNU Radio flowgraph - SDR source blocks (gr-osmosdr/UHD/SoapySDR) with Doppler-correction blocks; not a CAT app.
How to Set CAT: Not a GUI CAT program: set the SDR source device args and center frequency in the flowgraph/command line; Doppler applied via a gr-gpredict-doppler tag (from Gpredict) or a file of Doppler-vs-time.
Audio Device Select: IQ from the SDR (can also read a WAV/audio recording); no soundcard picker
PTT Config: n/a - receive/decode only
Companion Needed: needs an SDR + optionally Gpredict for Doppler; feeds SatNOGS
Notes: Daniel Estévez decoder framework for many satellites.
Source Note: Confirmed against the gr-satellites docs. GNU Radio flowgraph with SDR source blocks; Doppler applied via gr-gpredict-doppler (driven by Gpredict) or a file-based Doppler-correction block. Not a CAT app.

Name: GridTracker (GridTrackerSat)
Rig Control Backend: Optional Hamlib/rigctld (or FLRig) CAT for frequency/mode; primarily consumes WSJT-X UDP for spots.
How to Set CAT: Settings > Rig Control: enable Hamlib/rigctld (or FLRig) and set host/port/rig model. WSJT-X sends spots over UDP (2237/2238, multicast 224.0.0.73 for multi-PC).
Audio Device Select: n/a - audio belongs to WSJT-X/JTDX
PTT Config: n/a - TX handled by the linked WSJT-X, not GridTracker
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a - set in WSJT-X / on the radio
Companion Needed: needs WSJT-X or JTDX (UDP source); Hamlib optional
Notes: Map/logging/overlay tool, not a primary rig controller.
Source Note: Confirmed against docs.gridtracker.org. Optional Hamlib(rigctld)/FLRig rig control under Settings; primarily consumes WSJT-X UDP (default 127.0.0.1:2237, out 2238; multicast 224.0.0.73). Satellite view overlays Doppler/pass data; audio/PTT belong to WSJT-X.

Name: Ham Radio Deluxe Satellite Tracking
Rig Control Backend: HRD Rig Control - HRD's own native CAT engine over serial/USB (large built-in rig database).
How to Set CAT: Open HRD Rig Control's Connect dialog: pick radio company/model, COM port, baud; the Satellite Tracking module then retunes RX/TX for Doppler (shared data layer).
Audio Device Select: n/a in the tracker - digital audio handled separately by HRD DM-780
PTT Config: PTT via HRD Rig Control CAT / DM-780
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a in the tracker; on the radio use standard mic/DATA level with ALC just moving for SSB/digital
Companion Needed: needs the HRD suite (Rig Control); optional DM-780 for digital
Notes: Commercial suite; tracker is one HRD component.
Source Note: Confirmed against HRD's SatTrack feature/user guide. HRD Rig Control's native CAT engine; Connect dialog picks company/model/COM/baud; Satellite Tracking retunes RX/TX for Doppler; digital audio via DM-780.

Name: Ham Satting
Rig Control Backend: None - no rig CAT/rigctld. Tracking + Doppler-corrected frequency display only; the operator tunes the radio manually.
How to Set CAT: n/a - no rig control; read the displayed Doppler-corrected RX/TX frequencies and set them by hand.
Audio Device Select: n/a for control; iOS build can record received signals to WAV (five AR modes).
PTT Config: n/a
Companion Needed: optional rigctld server or SDR; otherwise manual tuning
Notes: Multi-platform (iOS/macOS/Android/Win/Linux) app by A46UNX; tracking, passes, logging with Doppler display.
Source Note: Not a rig-control app. Per the App Store description, Ham Satting is tracking/pass-prediction and reference DISPLAY only: it shows Doppler-corrected uplink/downlink frequencies (stepping in 100 Hz) for MANUAL tuning and has NO CAT/rigctld radio control. The claimed 'optional rigctld network rig-control' is incorrect. On iPhone/iPad it can record received audio to WAV (SSTV/SSDV/Digital/Weather/Music).

Name: Hamlib (rigctld / rotctld)
Rig Control Backend: IS the backend - native drivers to hundreds of rigs over serial/USB/CI-V/network; rotctld daemon for rotators.
How to Set CAT: Launch rigctld -m <model> -r <port> -s <baud> -P <PTT method> (or rotctld for a rotor). Client apps connect over TCP 4532 (rig) / 4533 (rotor).
Audio Device Select: n/a - control library only
PTT Config: Yes - rigctl 'T 1/0' keys PTT via CAT/RTS/DTR per the -P flag
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a - sets level only if the rig CAT supports it
Companion Needed: none; it is the conduit that Gpredict/SatDump/SatNOGS/WSJT-X talk to
Notes: Library plus rigctld/rotctld daemons; the universal CAT layer.
Source Note: Confirmed against Hamlib docs. rigctld TCP 4532 / rotctld 4533; PTT via 'T 1/0' using CAT/RTS/DTR per the -P flag.

Name: HamSat
Rig Control Backend: None - satellite tracker/pass predictor that displays Doppler-corrected dial frequencies; no CAT, Bluetooth-IC-705, or rigctld rig control.
How to Set CAT: n/a - tune the radio manually to the displayed RX/TX dial frequencies.
Audio Device Select: n/a
PTT Config: On the radio (full-duplex); HamSat only displays.
Companion Needed: needs an IC-705 (BT) or a rigctld server; none else
Notes: Mobile satellite Doppler app.
Source Note: HamSat (iOS satellite tracker) is DISPLAY-ONLY: a full-screen in-pass tracker showing Doppler-corrected RX/TX DIAL frequencies for manual tuning, with AMSAT TLEs and Live Activity. It does NOT control a transceiver via CAT, Bluetooth to an IC-705, or rigctld - that description matches other apps (e.g. SatHunter/SatPathFinder), not HamSat.

Name: InstantTrack
Rig Control Backend: Rig and rotor control via external hardware-interface driver TSRs (Kansas City Tracker standard, PacComm, TrakBox, FODTRACK, etc.).
How to Set CAT: In the DOS config select the tracking interface card/driver TSR for the radio and the rotor; it then sets Doppler and antenna pointing through that hardware.
Audio Device Select: n/a
PTT Config: n/a - radio handles TX
Companion Needed: needs a hardware tracking interface (KCT/PacComm/TrakBox)
Notes: Classic AMSAT DOS tracker.
Source Note: Confirmed against AMSAT/InstantTrack docs (INTSPEC.TXT). DOS-era; uses Kansas City Tracker-standard rotor driver TSRs (Brooks Van Pelt KB2CST) and a similar interface for Doppler rig tuning; FODTRACK etc. adapt to the KCT standard.

Name: iridium-toolkit / gr-iridium
Rig Control Backend: SDR hardware (HackRF/Airspy/USRP/SoapySDR) at L-band via GNU Radio - no transceiver CAT.
How to Set CAT: Configure gr-iridium's .conf with the SDR device args, center frequency (~1626 MHz) and sample rate (>=2 MSPS, higher BW captures more band); pipe detected frames into iridium-toolkit for parsing.
Audio Device Select: n/a - raw IQ, no soundcard
PTT Config: n/a - receive/decode only
Companion Needed: needs an L-band-capable SDR + GNU Radio
Notes: Research/decoder tool for the Iridium network.
Source Note: Confirmed against muccc/gr-iridium. SDR (HackRF/Airspy/USRP/SoapySDR) at Iridium L-band (1616-1626.5 MHz); .conf config; frames piped to iridium-toolkit. Receive/decode only, no transceiver CAT.

Name: KlaTrack
Rig Control Backend: None - pass prediction/visualization only; no rig CAT and no rotator control.
How to Set CAT: n/a - no radio or rotator interface.
Audio Device Select: n/a
PTT Config: n/a
Companion Needed: none for SSB; a separate soundcard app for digital
Notes: Cross-platform (Win/Mac/Linux) tracker with an elevation-vs-time pass graph.
Source Note: Confirmed against g0kla.com/klatrack that KlaTrack (G0KLA / ac2cz - NOT DL4KA) is a satellite PASS PREDICTOR/visualizer only (elevation-vs-time plot, real-time updates, Java). It has NO CAT rig control and NO rotator control - the claimed 'Native CAT rig control plus rotator control' is incorrect.

Name: Look4Sat
Rig Control Backend: Network rig control - rigctld / Gpredict-compatible TCP (plus rotator over TCP); no on-device Hamlib.
How to Set CAT: Look4Sat Settings > Radio (and Rotator): enter the rigctld/rotctld IP:port on your LAN; the Android app sends Doppler-tuned frequencies over the network.
Audio Device Select: n/a - Android app, no audio path
PTT Config: n/a
Companion Needed: needs a rigctld server on the LAN (e.g., a Pi running Hamlib)
Notes: Open-source Android tracker by Arty Bishop.
Source Note: Confirmed against the Look4Sat GitHub. Network-only control (no on-device Hamlib): rigctld/rotctld over TCP; Settings > Radio and Rotator take IP:port; sends Doppler-tuned frequencies / \set_pos over the LAN.

Name: MacDoppler
Rig Control Backend: Native CAT over serial/USB/network plus rotator control.
How to Set CAT: Preferences > Radios: choose the radio model and the serial/USB/network port; it sets RX/TX Doppler and PTT. The Rotators tab configures the antenna controller.
Audio Device Select: n/a
PTT Config: CAT PTT for full-duplex satellite operation
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a - set mic/DATA level and ALC on the radio
Companion Needed: none required; optional rotator
Notes: Dog Park Software, macOS.
Source Note: Confirmed against the MacDoppler manual (Dog Park Software). Preferences > Radios (model + serial/USB/network port), Rotators tab; CAT PTT for full-duplex; auto Doppler on RX and TX.

Name: MacDopplerPRO
Rig Control Backend: Native CAT plus rotator control (legacy MacDoppler branding).
How to Set CAT: Preferences > Radios: model + serial/USB/network port; Rotators tab for the antenna.
Audio Device Select: n/a
PTT Config: CAT PTT for full-duplex
Companion Needed: none required; optional rotator
Notes: Older name for the MacDoppler application.
Source Note: Confirmed - 'MacDopplerPRO' is the legacy branding of MacDoppler; same Preferences > Radios / Rotators configuration and CAT PTT full-duplex.

Name: MoonSked
Rig Control Backend: Antenna rotator control (many controllers) plus Doppler-shift display and Az/El export; native transceiver CAT tuning not confirmed.
How to Set CAT: Configure the rotator interface/COM port in setup; MoonSked shows Doppler for manual tuning and exports Az/El. No documented CAT rig frequency control.
Audio Device Select: n/a
PTT Config: n/a - TX on the radio
Companion Needed: needs a rotator controller; WSJT-X (Q65/JT65) alongside for the EME digital audio
Notes: GM4JJJ EME scheduling/tracking program.
Source Note: MoonSked (GM4JJJ) provides EME scheduling, Moon/Sun tracking, extensive antenna ROTATOR control (GS-232, SPID, EasyComm, W2DRZ, C.A.T.S., Green Heron, etc.) and Doppler-shift DISPLAY plus Az/El export. Direct transceiver CAT frequency control is NOT an established MoonSked feature - the claim that it 'can drive supported rigs' could not be confirmed and Doppler is shown for manual/rotator tuning. (Official page uses a mismatched TLS cert, blocking direct fetch.)

Name: Nova for Windows
Rig Control Backend: Native CAT rig control plus rotor control; can also act as a DDE tracking source for SDR software.
How to Set CAT: In Nova's Setup select the rig driver, COM port and baud, and the rotor interface (Setup > Antenna rotator); DDE output feeds SDR programs for Doppler.
Audio Device Select: n/a
PTT Config: n/a - radio handles TX
Companion Needed: rotor/rig hardware; optional SDR via DDE
Notes: Northern Lights Software tracker.
Source: Nova for Windows (Northern Lights Software Associates)
Source Note: Confirmed against Northern Lights Software Associates (nlsa.com). Setup selects rig driver/COM/baud and the rotor interface (Setup > Antenna rotator); DDE output feeds SDR programs for Doppler.

Name: Orbitron
Rig Control Backend: No direct CAT - exports RX/TX frequency via DDE (MyDDE / WispDDE driver) to a CAT rig-control driver or to SDR#/SDRuno.
How to Set CAT: Setup > Rotor/Radio: point Orbitron at the DDE driver EXE (MyDDE, WispDDE, or the SDR# DDE plugin). The actual COM/CAT (and rotor) settings live inside that driver, not in Orbitron.
Audio Device Select: n/a
PTT Config: n/a
Companion Needed: needs a DDE bridge driver (MyDDE/WispDDE) plus a CAT app or SDR#
Notes: Sebastian Stoff; the classic front-end paired with the SDR# DDE Tracking plugin.
Source Note: Confirmed. Orbitron (Sebastian Stoff) has no direct CAT: it exports RX/TX frequency via DDE to a driver (MyDDE, WispDDE) or to SDR#/SDRuno; COM/CAT settings live in that driver (e.g. WispDDE, configured from its own icon), not in Orbitron.

Name: OscarWatch-Tracker
Rig Control Backend: Optional native serial CAT (Icom IC-910/9100/9700/705, Yaesu FT-847/817/818/991, Kenwood TS-2000 beta) plus rotator (GS-232/EasyComm/SPID).
How to Set CAT: Settings > Radio: set COM port, baud and model (dual-radio for full-duplex, with sat/split and CTCSS). Settings > Rotator for the rotor; each device (rig, rotor, GPS) gets its own port.
Audio Device Select: n/a
PTT Config: CAT for sat/split modes; the radio performs TX
Companion Needed: none required; optional rotator
Notes: Peter Goodhall MM9SQL; multi-platform desktop, map-centered.
Source Note: Confirmed against the OscarWatch-Tracker GitHub (Peter Goodhall MM9SQL). Optional serial CAT: Icom IC-910/9100/9700/705, Yaesu FT-847/817/818/991, Kenwood TS-2000 (beta); rotator GS-232/SPID/EasyComm; dual-radio full-duplex with sat/split and CTCSS.

Name: PREDICT
Rig Control Backend: EasyComm rotor control plus a UDP/socket Doppler server that feeds rig-control clients/scripts.
How to Set CAT: Run predict with its socket/network server (-s); the rotor is driven via EasyComm, while rig frequency control is done by external clients/scripts that read az/el/Doppler from PREDICT's socket.
Audio Device Select: n/a
PTT Config: n/a
Companion Needed: needs a client/script (e.g., gsat) to push Doppler to Hamlib, plus rotor hardware
Notes: KD2BD Linux ncurses tracker.
Source Note: Confirmed against KD2BD's PREDICT docs. EasyComm rotor control (PIC/TRACK code by I3VFJ) plus a socket/network server (contributed by KD4HBO) that feeds external rig-control clients/scripts (e.g. gsat).

Name: PstRotator
Rig Control Backend: Antenna rotator control (RS232/TCP) is the core; radio control is via OmniRig using Doppler.sqf for satellite up/downlink + Doppler. Also EME.
How to Set CAT: Rig model/COM/baud are set in OmniRig (PstRotator uses OmniRig); enable Sat tracking with Doppler.sqf, or link SatPC32/Orbitron/Gpredict for tracking data. The rotor controller is set under Communication (COM RS232 / TCP).
Audio Device Select: n/a
PTT Config: n/a - radio handles TX
Companion Needed: typically SatPC32/Orbitron/Gpredict for tracking data, plus rotor hardware
Notes: YO3DMU; primarily a rotor controller with a rig-CAT add-on.
Source Note: Radio CAT is via OmniRig, not a native rig driver with its own COM/baud in PstRotator. PstRotator reads Doppler.sqf (same file as SatPC32); in AUTO it sets Up/Downlink + Doppler on the OmniRig-controlled rig, in MANUAL it adds Doppler only to TX. It is primarily a ROTATOR program (rotor via RS232/TCP under Communication); also EME.

Name: raspberry-noaa v2
Rig Control Backend: RTL-SDR direct (scheduled rtl_fm captures; also Airspy/HackRF/SDRplay) - no transceiver CAT.
How to Set CAT: n/a for CAT - config files / web UI set the SDR device index, gain, frequency offset, bias-tee and pass schedule (predict-driven cron); there is no rig to control.
Audio Device Select: RTL-SDR captured to WAV then decoded by wxtoimg (NOAA APT) and meteor_demod/medet (Meteor LRPT); no soundcard picker.
PTT Config: n/a - receive only
Companion Needed: needs an RTL-SDR dongle + wxtoimg/SatDump/predict on a Raspberry Pi
Notes: Automated NOAA APT / Meteor LRPT capture stack.
Source Note: Correct that there is no transceiver CAT (RTL-SDR direct, scheduled by cron via predict; device index/gain/schedule set in config files + web UI; also supports Airspy/HackRF/SDRplay). But decoding uses wxtoimg (NOAA APT) and meteor_demod/medet (Meteor LRPT) - not SatDump.

Name: SatDump
Rig Control Backend: Drives the SDR directly and Doppler-corrects its own tuning; antenna rotator via Hamlib rotctld (rotctl) or PstRotator. Receive/decode only - no transceiver CAT.
How to Set CAT: Live-processing settings pick the SDR source; in the Recorder tab under Tracking/Rotator, check 'Engage and Track' and select Rotctl (127.0.0.1:4533) or PstRotator (4002). Doppler is applied to SatDump's own SDR, not to an external rig.
Audio Device Select: n/a - IQ from the SDR, no soundcard
PTT Config: n/a - receive/decode only
Companion Needed: needs an SDR; optional rigctld/rotctld daemons
Notes: Universal satellite decoder (Aang23).
Source Note: SatDump is receive/decode-only (no TX, so there is no 'RX/TX' rig Doppler). It drives the SDR directly and applies Doppler internally to its OWN SDR tuning. The only external hardware control in the tracker is the ANTENNA ROTATOR - via rotctl (Hamlib rotctld, default 4533) or PstRotator (4002). There is no external transceiver CAT.

Name: SatPC32
Rig Control Backend: Native CAT over serial/USB - sets RX and TX VFOs for full-duplex Doppler, plus PTT and rotor control.
How to Set CAT: Setup > Radio Setup: pick radio model, COM port, baud, PTT method and RX/TX offsets; Rotor Setup for the antenna. It publishes Doppler.SQF and a DDE interface for other apps.
Audio Device Select: n/a
PTT Config: CAT PTT / full-duplex control (PTT button in current versions)
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a - set mic/DATA level and ALC on the radio
Companion Needed: none required; feeds PstRotator/others via Doppler.SQF and DDE
Notes: DK1TB; the classic Windows satellite program.
Source Note: Confirmed against DK1TB's SatPC32 manual. Setup > Radio Setup (model/COM/baud, PTT method, RX/TX offsets); Rotor Setup; publishes Doppler.SQF and a DDE interface. Newer versions do provide a PTT button.

Name: SatTrack
Rig Control Backend: Native rig control plus antenna-rotor control during passes (Unix/Linux; Manfred Bester).
How to Set CAT: In SatTrack's configuration select the radio interface and its serial port, plus the rotor; it retunes the rig for Doppler while tracking.
Audio Device Select: n/a
PTT Config: n/a - TX on the radio
Companion Needed: rotor/rig hardware; soundcard app for digital
Notes: Long-standing satellite tracking program.
Source Note: Confirmed as Manfred Bester's Unix/Linux SatTrack: real-time tracking + Doppler computation with rotor control and control of supported radios per the tracked satellite's transponders. Configure the radio interface + serial port and the rotor in its config; classic-version rig support is older/more limited than modern trackers.

Name: SDR# (SDRSharp) + DDE Tracking / Gpredict plugin
Rig Control Backend: DDE Tracking plugin retunes SDR#'s own SDR for RX Doppler from Orbitron (DDE) or Gpredict (network); SDR# controls the SDR device natively.
How to Set CAT: Install the DDE Tracking (or Gpredict) plugin, then set the Orbitron DDE link or the Gpredict host:port; it corrects the SDR center/VFO for Doppler. Sync a separate hardware transceiver with the OmniRig plugin.
Audio Device Select: SDR# demod audio to the speaker, or route via a Virtual Audio Cable to a decoder
PTT Config: n/a - receive only
Companion Needed: needs Orbitron or Gpredict for tracking; a virtual audio cable + decoder for digital
Notes: RX-only Doppler correction of the receive frequency.
Source Note: Confirmed. The DDE Tracking/Scheduler plugin retunes SDR#'s own SDR for RX Doppler from Orbitron (DDE) or, via the Gpredict plugin, over the network; a separate hardware transceiver is synced with the OmniRig plugin. Receive-only for the SDR path.

Name: SDR++ (SDRPlusPlus)
Rig Control Backend: Controls its own SDR hardware; exposes an rigctl (Hamlib TCP) server so Gpredict/other trackers retune it for Doppler.
How to Set CAT: Enable the 'Rigctl Server' module and set its port (default 4532); Gpredict connects to it as a rig. The Source module selects the SDR device.
Audio Device Select: The Sink module outputs to the speaker, or to a Virtual Audio Cable feeding a decoder
PTT Config: n/a - receive only
Companion Needed: needs Gpredict for Doppler; virtual audio cable + decoder for digital
Notes: Cross-platform modular SDR receiver.
Source Note: Confirmed against the SDRPlusPlus repo. Enable the 'Rigctl Server' module (default port 4532, rigctl protocol; does not emulate a specific radio) so Gpredict/others retune it; the Source module selects the SDR device.

Name: SkyRoof
Rig Control Backend: Integrated SDR receiver + Doppler, plus optional external TX transceiver CAT via SkyCAT (skyctld) or Hamlib (rigctld) over TCP, and an antenna rotator.
How to Set CAT: Tools > Settings > CAT Control: configure an RX radio and/or a TX radio (SkyCAT/skyctld, or Hamlib rigctld as fallback), enable one or both (SDR for RX, transceiver for TX). The SDR tab selects the receiver; a Rotator tab handles the antenna.
Audio Device Select: SDR audio plus I/Q output via a VAC or UDP (Settings) to feed digital apps like WSJT-X
PTT Config: The TX transceiver is keyed over CAT for full-duplex (RX on the SDR, TX on the rig)
ALC/Power Guidance: On the transceiver - raise DATA/mic level to rated power with ALC just moving for SSB/digital TX
Companion Needed: needs an SDR for RX and a CAT transceiver for TX; optional virtual audio cable for digital
Notes: VE3NEA Windows app; bundles skycatd/rigctld and SkyCAT.
Source: SkyRoof User Guide - Setting Up CAT Control (VE3NEA)
Source Note: Confirmed against SkyRoof's User Guide (VE3NEA), with a refinement: SkyRoof's primary CAT backend is its OWN SkyCAT (skyctld.exe); Hamlib rigctld.exe is the fallback when no SkyCAT command-definition file exists. CAT rides TCP (radio may be remote). Recommended: SDR for RX, transceiver for TX.

Name: UISS (Universal Interface for Soundcard Satellites)
Rig Control Backend: No rig CAT - sound-card packet operation via AGWPE / UZ7HO SoundModem; PTT via serial/VOX.
How to Set CAT: n/a for rig frequency (dial the FM packet bird manually). UISS connects to the AGW Packet Engine or UZ7HO SoundModem (LAN host 127.0.0.1, AGW port 8000), which holds the audio/PTT settings.
Audio Device Select: Chosen inside the AGWPE/SoundModem app (its capture/playback device), not in UISS
PTT Config: Serial COM RTS/DTR or VOX - configured in the SoundModem/AGWPE, not UISS
ALC/Power Guidance: 1200/9600 AFSK is FM - set radio deviation, not ALC; keep the soundcard drive modest
Companion Needed: needs AGWPE or UZ7HO SoundModem plus a 2 m FM rig
Notes: ON6MU; for ISS/PACSAT packet and APRS.
Source Note: Confirmed against ON6MU's UISS. No rig CAT - it connects to AGWPE / UZ7HO SoundModem (LAN 127.0.0.1, AGW port 8000), which holds the audio + PTT (serial RTS/DTR or VOX) settings; you dial the FM packet bird manually.

Name: WiSP / WiSP DDE
Rig Control Backend: Native CAT rig + rotator via WispDDE, plus a hardware TNC/modem keyed for packet PACSATs.
How to Set CAT: In the WiSP Ground Station Control Panel set the radio driver, COM port/baud, rotor and TNC (via WispDDE). WiSP DDE then serves the frequency/positions to other apps.
Audio Device Select: n/a - uses a hardware TNC/modem, not a soundcard
PTT Config: Via the TNC / radio keying line
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a - FM packet; set deviation on the radio
Companion Needed: needs a hardware TNC (e.g., 9k6 FSK for PACSATs) plus a rotor
Notes: Classic PACSAT store-and-forward suite.
Source Note: Confirmed. WiSP (G7UPN) PACSAT ground station uses a hardware TNC/modem (e.g. DSP-12) with radio + rotor control via the WispDDE driver; WiSP/WispDDE serves frequency/positions to other apps over DDE (and can bridge to Ethernet via com2tcp).

Name: WXtrack
Rig Control Backend: Direct serial Alt/Az rotator control + scheduled triggering of receivers/decoders; no transceiver CAT and no audio path of its own.
How to Set CAT: Configure the rotator's serial port so WXtrack sends Az/El; schedule receiver/decoder triggers. CAT frequency tuning, if any, is left to a companion; decoding is done by WXtoImg.
Audio Device Select: n/a - decoding done by WXtoImg/companion
PTT Config: n/a
Companion Needed: needs WXtoImg/receiver plus a rotor controller (SatSignal tools)
Notes: David Taylor GM8ARV (SatSignal) weather-satellite tracker.
Source Note: WXtrack (David Taylor / SatSignal) directly controls Alt/Az antenna rotators via a serial port and can trigger receivers/decoders on a pass schedule; it does NOT do transceiver CAT tuning and has no audio path of its own (decoding is done by WXtoImg / companions). The claim that it merely 'hands off rotor control' understates its direct serial rotor control, and the named companion 'WXtrackGC' could not be verified.

Name: Zenith
Rig Control Backend: Web app that delegates rig/Doppler control to QTRigDoppler (Hamlib-based) or CSN Technologies S.A.T. hardware; the hosted version is tracking-only.
How to Set CAT: Self-hosted (PHP 7.4+/HTTPS): Options > Radio - enable QTRigDoppler and enter its server address, or select CSN S.A.T. hardware. The free hosted web version does no CAT.
Audio Device Select: n/a
PTT Config: n/a - the radio performs TX
Companion Needed: self-host + rigctld/QTRigDoppler or a CSN S.A.T. for hardware control
Notes: Peter Goodhall MM9SQL; web-based (web.zenithtracker.org).
Source Note: Self-hosted Zenith (magicbug) integrates radio control by delegating to QTRigDoppler (Options > Radio: enable QTRigDoppler and enter its server address) or to CSN Technologies S.A.T. hardware; the free hosted web version is tracking-only (no CAT). There is no documented direct 'Web Serial' CAT path - rig CAT/Doppler is handled by QTRigDoppler (itself Hamlib-based) or the CSN S.A.T.

Name: AbracaDABra
Rig Control Backend: Native SDR device control (RTL-SDR / Airspy / SDRplay via SoapySDR / RTL-TCP, or raw-file input) - no transceiver CAT.
How to Set CAT: Settings > Device: select the SDR input (RTL-SDR, Airspy, SDRplay/SoapySDR, RTL-TCP, raw file) and gain; there is no rig to CAT-control.
Audio Device Select: Decoded DAB/DAB+ audio to the PC default output (audio framework switchable Qt/PortAudio); supports recording.
PTT Config: n/a - receive only
Companion Needed: needs an RTL-SDR/Airspy/SoapySDR device
Notes: DAB/DAB+ broadcast receiver.
Source Note: Confirmed against the AbracaDABra repo (KejPi). DAB/DAB+ SDR: RTL-SDR (v3/v4), Airspy, SDRplay (SoapySDR), RTL-TCP; device chosen in Settings; decoded audio to PC default output (Qt or PortAudio framework). Receive only.

Name: Afedri SDR software / SDR-Net
Rig Control Backend: Native control of Afedri (receive-only) hardware over USB/Ethernet; an ExtIO plugin lets HDSDR/SDR#/Winrad/SDR-Console use it.
How to Set CAT: In the app select the Afedri unit (USB, or IP/port for network). As ExtIO it appears as a source inside HDSDR/SDR#, where you sync an external transceiver via OmniRig.
Audio Device Select: Host application's soundcard output; route via a virtual audio cable to decoders.
PTT Config: n/a - Afedri SDRs are receive-only; there is no TX/CAT Afedri variant.
Companion Needed: optional HDSDR/SDR# via ExtIO; the Afedri hardware
Notes: Afedri SDR-Net receiver line.
Source Note: Afedri SDR-Net / AFE822x units are RECEIVE-ONLY SDRs (USB or Ethernet). There is no Afedri 'DUO' TX/CAT variant - that appears confused with the ELAD FDM-DUO. Control is via the Afedri app or the Afedri ExtIO DLL inside HDSDR/SDR#/Winrad/SDR-Console; an external transceiver is synced via OmniRig in the host app.

Name: CubicSDR
Rig Control Backend: SoapySDR - controls any Soapy-supported device (RTL/Airspy/HackRF/BladeRF/LimeSDR/SDRplay).
How to Set CAT: On startup the device-selection dialog lists SoapySDR devices - pick one and set sample rate/gain; there is no transceiver CAT (RX app).
Audio Device Select: Demodulated audio to the selected output device; can pipe to other apps via a VAC.
PTT Config: n/a - receive only
Companion Needed: needs a SoapySDR device; virtual audio cable + decoder optional
Notes: Cross-platform SDR receiver (Charles Cliffe).
Source Note: Confirmed against cubicsdr.com. SoapySDR-based: startup device dialog lists Soapy devices (RTL/Airspy/HackRF/BladeRF/LimeSDR/SDRplay/SoapyRemote), set sample rate/gain; demod audio to a chosen output or piped via VAC. Receive app, no transceiver CAT.

Name: cuSDR
Rig Control Backend: Native HPSDR (Metis/Hermes) control over Ethernet.
How to Set CAT: cuSDR discovers the HPSDR board on the LAN and connects; there is no external CAT - it is largely an RX/panadapter client.
Audio Device Select: Soundcard output; virtual audio cable for digital
PTT Config: HPSDR TX-capable boards can key, but cuSDR is RX/panadapter focused
Companion Needed: needs HPSDR hardware
Notes: Qt HPSDR client.
Source Note: Confirmed against openHPSDR resources. cuSDR (DL3HVH) natively controls HPSDR Metis/Hermes over Ethernet via network discovery; it is an RX/panadapter-focused client (HPSDR protocol implements TX too, but cuSDR is RX-oriented).

Name: CuteSDR
Rig Control Backend: Native control of RFSpace SDR-IP / NetSDR over Ethernet (also SDR-IQ/SDR-14 via a server).
How to Set CAT: Select the RFSpace unit's IP/port and sample rate in CuteSDR; receive only, no transceiver CAT.
Audio Device Select: PC audio output
PTT Config: n/a - receive only
Companion Needed: needs an RFSpace SDR-IP / NetSDR
Notes: Reference RFSpace client by Moe Wheatley.
Source Note: Confirmed against the CuteSDR Technical Manual (Moe Wheatley AE4JY). Native network control of RFSpace SDR-IP/NetSDR over Ethernet (also SDR-IQ/SDR-14 via server); pick the unit's IP/port and sample rate. Receive only, no transceiver CAT.

Name: DATV-Express
Rig Control Backend: Drives the DATV-Express exciter board or an SDR (Pluto/LimeSDR) for DVB-S/S2 TX - not a CAT app.
How to Set CAT: In the DATV-Express console select the output (Express board or a SoapySDR Pluto/Lime) and set TX frequency, symbol rate, FEC and constellation; there is no receiver CAT.
Audio Device Select: Transport-stream / encoder audio input (video service), not rig soundcard audio
PTT Config: TX enable / PTT in the console keys the exciter; external PTT via the board
ALC/Power Guidance: Set RF drive/gain in software to keep the linear PA out of compression - DATV needs a backed-off, linear signal (not an SSB-ALC rule)
Companion Needed: needs a DATV-Express board or Pluto/Lime + a linear PA, plus a TS/encoder source
Notes: DATV transmitter software (G4GUO).
Source Note: Confirmed against datv-express.com. TX-only SDR for DVB-S/S2/(T): drives the DATV-Express board or a SoapySDR Pluto/Lime; console sets TX frequency, symbol rate, FEC, constellation; PTT button keys the exciter. ALC guidance (back-off for a linear signal) is correct for DATV.

Name: Dream
Rig Control Backend: Sound-card IQ / ExtIO SDR front-end for the DRM signal - no built-in rig CAT.
How to Set CAT: In Settings select the soundcard IQ/IF input (or an ExtIO SDR); tune the front-end manually or via ExtIO. No transceiver CAT.
Audio Device Select: Settings > Sound Card: choose the IF/IQ input and the decoded-audio output device (tick 'IQ' / 'IQ Pos Split' for IQ).
PTT Config: n/a - RX (a TX mode exists but is rarely used)
Companion Needed: needs a receiver/SDR feeding IQ; virtual audio cable
Notes: DRM (Digital Radio Mondiale) decoder/encoder.
Source Note: Confirmed against the Dream DRM docs. Soundcard IF/IQ input (or ExtIO SDR); Sound Card settings pick the IF/IQ input and decoded-audio output; tick 'IQ' with 'IQ Pos Split' for IQ input. Receive-focused (a TX mode exists).

Name: DSDPlus (DSD+)
Rig Control Backend: With the FastLane tools it controls an RTL-SDR via librtlsdr; otherwise it just reads discriminator/virtual-audio input.
How to Set CAT: FastLane's config (.frn / batch files) sets the RTL device and frequency; plain DSD+ takes audio in and does no CAT.
Audio Device Select: Audio input = a discriminator tap or a Virtual Audio Cable from an SDR app; audio output for decoded voice.
PTT Config: n/a - receive/decode only
Companion Needed: needs an RTL-SDR (FastLane) or an SDR app + virtual audio cable
Notes: Digital-voice (P25/DMR/NXDN) decoder.
Source Note: Confirmed against the RadioReference DSDPlus wiki. With FastLane it controls an RTL-SDR via librtlsdr (FMP24/FMPP/FMPA); plain DSD+ ingests discriminator-tap or virtual-audio-cable audio and does no CAT.

Name: DSP Radio
Rig Control Backend: Native control of RTL-SDR and soundcard front-ends
How to Set CAT: In this macOS app select the RTL-SDR or soundcard source and tune in-app for AM/FM/SSB/CW; no transceiver CAT.
Audio Device Select: Soundcard/RTL input; decoded audio to the Mac output
PTT Config: n/a — receive only
Companion Needed: needs an RTL-SDR or a soundcard/receiver
Notes: macOS SDR receiver.
Source: DSP Radio (macOS / Mac App Store) - official docs not located
Source Note: The claimed behaviour (macOS SDR receiver that selects an RTL-SDR or soundcard source, tunes in-app for AM/FM/SSB/CW, no transceiver CAT, RX only) is consistent with a macOS soundcard/RTL-SDR receive app, but I could not locate authoritative official documentation for a program named exactly 'DSP Radio' during this audit to verify each field; treat as unverified.

Name: dump1090 / readsb
Rig Control Backend: Controls an RTL-SDR (readsb also other SDRs) tuned to 1090 MHz - no transceiver CAT.
How to Set CAT: Command line / config: --device-index, --gain, etc.; readsb adds more SDR types via drivers. No CAT - it is an ADS-B demodulator.
Audio Device Select: n/a - IQ demodulated to ADS-B/Mode-S messages
PTT Config: n/a - receive only
Companion Needed: needs an RTL-SDR at 1090 MHz; a map front-end (tar1090) optional
Notes: ADS-B Mode-S decoder/feeder.
Source Note: Confirmed. dump1090 (FlightAware fork) drives an RTL-SDR at 1090 MHz; readsb (wiedehopf) adds other SDR types. Command-line/config (--device-index, --gain, etc.); ADS-B/Mode-S demodulator, no CAT, receive only.

Name: dump978
Rig Control Backend: Controls an RTL-SDR tuned to 978 MHz (UAT) - no transceiver CAT.
How to Set CAT: Command line: set the SDR device and gain; tuned to 978 MHz. No CAT.
Audio Device Select: n/a - IQ demodulated to UAT messages
PTT Config: n/a - receive only
Companion Needed: needs an RTL-SDR at 978 MHz
Notes: UAT / ADS-B 978 MHz decoder.
Source Note: Confirmed. FlightAware dump978 drives an RTL-SDR tuned to 978 MHz (UAT); command-line SDR device/gain; no CAT, receive only.

Name: Elad FDM-SW2
Rig Control Backend: Native control of Elad FDM SDR hardware for RX (the FDM-DUO adds TX/CAT); provides CAT out (virtual COM) to external software.
How to Set CAT: In FDM-SW2 select the Elad unit; it exposes a CAT interface (virtual COM) so external logging/digital apps can read/set frequency. For DUO TX set transmit parameters; sync other rigs via CAT/OmniRig.
Audio Device Select: IQ from the Elad hardware; audio/VAC output to decoders; the DUO uses its own USB/codec audio for TX.
PTT Config: FDM-DUO TX - PTT via software/mic; RX-only FDM-S units n/a
ALC/Power Guidance: On the FDM-DUO set mic/DATA drive to rated power keeping ALC/level in range
Companion Needed: needs Elad FDM-S2/DUO hardware
Notes: Elad's own SDR software.
Source Note: Confirmed against ELAD's FDM-SW2 / FDM-DUO manuals. FDM-SW2 controls Elad FDM hardware for RX; the FDM-DUO adds TX/CAT (USB FTDI CAT interface) and exposes a virtual COM/CAT out for external logging/digital apps; sync other rigs via CAT/OmniRig.

Name: ExpertSDR2
Rig Control Backend: Native control of SunSDR hardware over Ethernet/USB with full TX/RX; exposes CAT (TCI + virtual COM, TS-480 emulation) and virtual audio.
How to Set CAT: Options > CAT: enable the built-in CAT interface (virtual COM pair emulating a Kenwood TS-480, or TCI) so loggers/digital apps control the radio; the SunSDR connects via network discovery.
Audio Device Select: Options > VAC: route audio to digital apps via a virtual audio cable (or TCI); local DAC for phone.
PTT Config: Full TX SDR - PTT via panel/CAT/TCI or a footswitch on the SunSDR
ALC/Power Guidance: Set the TX Drive/Power slider to rated power; the SunSDR has internal leveling - raise drive while watching the meter
Companion Needed: needs SunSDR hardware; VAC/TCI bridge for digital modes
Notes: Expert Electronics SDR software.
Source Note: Confirmed against Expert Electronics' ExpertSDR2/SunSDR manuals. Options > CAT enables a virtual COM pair (emulates a Kenwood TS-480) or TCI for external apps; Options > VAC routes audio; full TX SDR with PTT via panel/CAT/TCI/footswitch; TX Drive/Power slider with internal SunSDR leveling.

Name: ExpertSDR2 / ExpertSDR3
Rig Control Backend: Native control of SunSDR hardware over Ethernet for RX/TX/PTT; exposes CAT (TCI + virtual COM) and virtual audio; supports the E-Coder panel and network remote.
How to Set CAT: Options > CAT: enable the CAT interface (virtual COM pair / TCI) for external apps; the SunSDR is found via network discovery. E-Coder and remote operation are configured in Options.
Audio Device Select: Virtual audio cable (VAC) or TCI to digital apps; built-in DAC for phone.
PTT Config: Full TX - PTT via panel/CAT/TCI/footswitch on the SunSDR
ALC/Power Guidance: TX Drive/Power slider to rated power; internal SunSDR leveling - raise drive watching the meter
Companion Needed: needs SunSDR hardware; VAC/TCI for digital; optional E-Coder
Notes: Expert Electronics; entry spans the ExpertSDR2 and ExpertSDR3 generations.
Source Note: Confirmed. Same SunSDR platform: Options > CAT (virtual COM TS-480 emulation or TCI) + VAC audio; full TX with PTT via panel/CAT/TCI/footswitch; E-Coder panel and network remote in Options; TX Drive/Power with internal leveling.

Name: ExpertSDR3
Rig Control Backend: Native control of SunSDR hardware - TX/RX, panadapter, network remote; exposes CAT (TCI + virtual COM) and virtual audio.
How to Set CAT: Options > CAT: enable the CAT/TCI interface so external loggers/digital apps drive the radio; SunSDR connects via network/USB discovery.
Audio Device Select: Options > Audio: VAC or TCI to route audio to digital apps; built-in DAC for phone.
PTT Config: Full TX - PTT via panel/CAT/TCI/footswitch
ALC/Power Guidance: TX Drive/Power slider to rated power with internal SunSDR leveling
Companion Needed: needs SunSDR hardware; VAC/TCI for digital
Notes: Latest-generation Expert Electronics SDR software.
Source Note: Confirmed. Options > CAT enables the CAT/TCI interface (virtual COM pair / TCI) for external loggers/digital apps; Options > Audio routes VAC/TCI; full TX with PTT via panel/CAT/TCI/footswitch; TX Drive/Power slider with internal SunSDR leveling.

Name: G8JCFSDR
Rig Control Backend: Soundcard / SDR-IQ / SoftRock front-end with CAT tuning of the source receiver's LO.
How to Set CAT: In G8JCFSDR's settings select the front-end (soundcard IF, SDR-IQ, or SoftRock via Si570, etc.) and set the CAT link to the local-oscillator radio so it can tune the source; no TX rig CAT.
Audio Device Select: Soundcard IF input; audio output / virtual audio cable to decoders.
PTT Config: n/a - receive only
Companion Needed: needs a receiver/SoftRock/SDR-IQ; virtual audio cable + decoder
Notes: Peter Martinez G8JCF's SDR receiver.
Source Note: Confirmed against g8jcf.uk. Soundcard front-end (also SDR-IQ, SoftRock/Si570 and many DDS/PLL front-ends) with CAT tuning of the source LO; downconverts RF into the soundcard passband; RX only, no TX rig CAT.

Name: ghpsdr3-alex / QtRadio (glSDR)
Rig Control Backend: hpsdr-server controls the HPSDR hardware and dspserver serves network clients (QtRadio/glSDR) that tune it over the network.
How to Set CAT: Run hpsdr-server + dspserver against the HPSDR board, then connect the QtRadio/glSDR client to the server's IP; tuning happens over the network. No external transceiver CAT.
Audio Device Select: The server streams audio to clients; the client plays out or routes to a virtual audio cable.
PTT Config: TX via the server for TX-capable boards; the client's Tune/MOX button keys it.
ALC/Power Guidance: Set drive/level in the client/server for TX-capable boards.
Companion Needed: needs HPSDR hardware + dspserver
Notes: Distributed HPSDR system (Alex/John Melton).
Source Note: Confirmed against the ghpsdr3-alex project (John Melton G0ORX; Alex Lee fork). hpsdr-server talks to the HPSDR board; dspserver processes and serves network clients (QtRadio/glSDR) that tune over the network; TX via MOX/Tune on TX-capable boards (with a credentials check).

Name: GNU Radio / GNU Radio Companion
Rig Control Backend: Source/sink blocks (gr-osmosdr, UHD, SoapySDR) directly control SDR hardware for RX and TX - not a CAT app.
How to Set CAT: Not CAT: set the device args and center frequency in the Osmocom/UHD/Soapy source or sink block; behaviour is defined by the flowgraph.
Audio Device Select: Audio source/sink blocks route to the soundcard, or to file/UDP.
PTT Config: TX is a sink block keying the SDR - there is no rig-PTT concept.
ALC/Power Guidance: TX power set by the sink block's gain/amplitude in the flowgraph (no ALC).
Companion Needed: needs any supported SDR; block sets like gr-satellites
Notes: General DSP/SDR development framework.
Source Note: Confirmed. Source/sink blocks (gr-osmosdr, UHD, SoapySDR) directly control SDR hardware for RX/TX; not a CAT app - behaviour is defined by the flowgraph and block device args.

Name: Gqrx
Rig Control Backend: Controls SDR RX hardware (gr-osmosdr/SoapySDR); exposes an rigctld-style TCP server (port 7356) and can be driven by Hamlib.
How to Set CAT: Configure I/O devices to pick the SDR source. Tools > Remote control: enable the TCP server (port 7356, rigctl protocol; localhost by default) so Gpredict/Hamlib retune it for Doppler.
Audio Device Select: Demod audio to the output device, with an optional UDP audio stream; virtual audio cable to decoders.
PTT Config: n/a - receive only
Companion Needed: needs an SDR; Gpredict via remote control for Doppler; virtual audio cable + WSJT-X for digital
Notes: Alexandru Csete's SDR receiver.
Source Note: Confirmed against gqrx.dk remote-control docs. Controls SDR RX hardware (gr-osmosdr/SoapySDR); Tools > Remote control exposes a rigctld-compatible TCP server (default port 7356, localhost only by default) so Gpredict/Hamlib retune it. Receive only.

Name: gr-osmosdr (Osmocom source/sink)
Rig Control Backend: Block library that directly controls RTL/HackRF/BladeRF/Airspy/SoapySDR/USRP inside GNU Radio flowgraphs.
How to Set CAT: Not standalone / not CAT: set the device args (e.g. "rtl=0", "hackrf") and frequency/gain in the Osmocom Source or Sink block.
Audio Device Select: Whatever the surrounding flowgraph routes (soundcard/file/UDP).
PTT Config: TX via the Osmocom Sink for TX-capable devices.
ALC/Power Guidance: Sink gain sets the TX level (no ALC).
Companion Needed: needs GNU Radio + a supported SDR
Notes: The common SDR hardware-abstraction block.
Source Note: Confirmed. Block library that directly controls RTL/HackRF/BladeRF/Airspy/SoapySDR/USRP inside GNU Radio flowgraphs; device args (e.g. "rtl=0", "hackrf") set in the Osmocom Source/Sink block. Not standalone, not CAT.

Name: HDSDR
Rig Control Backend: Processes SDR/IF I/Q for the display; uses OmniRig CAT to read/set the connected transceiver's frequency and mode.
How to Set CAT: Options [F7] > CAT to Radio (OmniRig): enable OmniRig (rig 1/2); the rig model, COM port and baud are set inside OmniRig. The SDR hardware is chosen via its ExtIO DLL.
Audio Device Select: Options/Soundcard [F5]: RX I/Q in (from soundcard or ExtIO), audio out; virtual audio cable to decoders.
PTT Config: RX-focused; for SoftRock/transverter TX, PTT via ExtIO or soundcard (CAT/RTS).
ALC/Power Guidance: For SoftRock/transceiver TX keep the soundcard TX drive low so audio stays clean (soundcard-level TX).
Companion Needed: needs OmniRig + a transceiver, or an ExtIO SDR; virtual audio cable for digital
Notes: Classic Winrad-derived SDR program.
Source Note: Confirmed against hdsdr.de/DX Lab wiki. Options [F7] > CAT to Radio (OmniRig) enables OmniRig (rig1/2); actual rig model/COM/baud live in OmniRig. SDR hardware chosen via its ExtIO DLL; Soundcard [F5] sets RX I/Q in and audio out.

Name: ka9q-radio
Rig Control Backend: Native control of RTL-SDR / Airspy R2 / Airspy HF+ / RX888 MkII / FunCube (optional SDRplay/BladeRF/HackRF); multicasts demodulated channels over IP.
How to Set CAT: Config file (radiod@.conf) selects the SDR device and defines many receive channels; the control program tunes them. No transceiver CAT (RX only).
Audio Device Select: Multicast RTP audio over the LAN (Opus/PCM), consumed by the monitor/pcmrecord tools - not a local soundcard picker.
PTT Config: n/a - receive only
Companion Needed: needs a supported SDR; ka9q-web/monitor to listen
Notes: Phil Karn KA9Q high-performance multichannel receiver.
Source Note: Confirmed against the ka9q-radio repo. radiod reads the SDR front end directly (RTL-SDR, Airspy R2/HF+, RX888 MkII, FunCube Pro+; optional SDRplay/BladeRF/HackRF/Fobos/HydraSDR); radiod@.conf selects device + channels; demod channels multicast as RTP (Opus/PCM). Receive only.

Name: KGKSDR (M0KGK SDR Decoder/Display)
Rig Control Backend: Soundcard IQ input (SoftRock) with CAT tuning of the front-end LO (Si570).
How to Set CAT: In KGKSDR's setup select the soundcard IQ input and configure CAT to the front-end local oscillator (Si570); receive AM/FM, with CW/SSB TX via SoftRock.
Audio Device Select: Soundcard IQ input; audio output / virtual audio cable; IQOUT provides configurable IQ output for testing.
PTT Config: n/a for AM/FM RX; CW/SSB TX supported with SoftRock (keyer needs ASIO).
Companion Needed: needs a SoftRock/soundcard receiver
Notes: M0KGK SDR decoder/display.
Source Note: Confirmed against m0kgk.co.uk/sdr. Soundcard-IQ SDR (Duncan M0KGK) working with SoftRock hardware, with CAT tuning of the front-end LO (Si570); RX for AM/FM plus CW/SSB TX (an M0KGK SDR Transceiver build), so 'limited SoftRock TX' is correct.

Name: KISS Konsole
Rig Control Backend: Native control of early HPSDR boards (Ozy/Magister + Mercury/Penelope).
How to Set CAT: Connects directly to the HPSDR board (Metis/Ozy over Ethernet); a minimal early console with no external CAT.
Audio Device Select: Soundcard output.
PTT Config: TX via HPSDR (Penelope) if fitted; PTT button in the console.
Companion Needed: needs early HPSDR hardware
Notes: PowerSDR-derived minimal HPSDR console (Phil Covington N8VB).
Source Note: Confirmed against the openHPSDR wiki. KISS Konsole (Phil Harman VK6PH, C#/TAPR) natively controls early HPSDR boards (Ozy/Magister interface + Mercury RX; Penelope TX added later to make it a full transceiver). PTT button in the console.

Name: KiwiSDR
Rig Control Backend: Controls the KiwiSDR front-end (BeagleBone cape); browser-based, multichannel.
How to Set CAT: Runs as a web server on the Kiwi - each browser session tunes its own channel via the web UI; the admin page (kiwisdr.local/admin) configures the device. No transceiver CAT (RX only).
Audio Device Select: Audio delivered in-browser (and via kiwiclient / a virtual audio cable for decoding).
PTT Config: n/a - receive only
Companion Needed: needs KiwiSDR hardware; kiwiclient/VAC to pipe to decoders
Notes: Public web-SDR platform, independent channels per user.
Source Note: Confirmed against the KiwiSDR project (jks-prv). BeagleBone cape (VLF-HF), browser-based, up to 8 simultaneous users each tuning an independent channel; admin page (kiwisdr.local/admin) configures the device. Receive only, no transceiver CAT.

Name: Langstone
Rig Control Backend: Native control of a LimeSDR/PlutoSDR as a full transceiver (VHF through microwave) on a Raspberry Pi.
How to Set CAT: Langstone runs on a Raspberry Pi configured for the Lime/Pluto (build script asks which); tuning is via its own rotary/UI. No external CAT.
Audio Device Select: Uses the Pi/CODEC audio for SSB/FM voice; I/Q to the SDR.
PTT Config: PTT via a Pi GPIO input (pin 11 / GPIO17, grounded = TX); TX-active output on pin 40 for relays/amps.
ALC/Power Guidance: Set the TX gain/drive in the Langstone config to stay linear into the PA (microwave SSB/FM).
Companion Needed: needs a LimeSDR/PlutoSDR + Raspberry Pi (+ PA for the band)
Notes: G4EML open microwave transceiver project.
Source Note: Confirmed against the Langstone repos (G4EML). Full transceiver on a LimeSDR/PlutoSDR + Raspberry Pi (VHF-microwave); tuning via its own UI; PTT via a Pi GPIO input (GPIO17/pin11), TX-active out on pin 40, plus band-select outputs. No external CAT.

Name: linHPSDR
Rig Control Backend: Native HPSDR/ANAN control for RX/TX; exposes CAT over TCP and integrates with Hamlib/rigctl for external apps.
How to Set CAT: linHPSDR (Linux) discovers the HPSDR/ANAN board on Ethernet; configure it in the Radio menu. A Hamlib/rigctl bridge (per-receiver CAT over TCP) lets external apps control it.
Audio Device Select: ALSA/PulseAudio via PortAudio; local mic for TX; loopback/VAC-equivalent for digital.
PTT Config: Full TX - PTT via the HPSDR MOX bit / CAT / footswitch.
ALC/Power Guidance: Drive/level slider to rated power; ANAN internal leveling.
Companion Needed: needs HPSDR/ANAN hardware
Notes: John Melton G0ORX Linux HPSDR client.
Source Note: Confirmed against g0orx/linhpsdr. Linux HPSDR/ANAN control (Protocol 1/2, up to 7 RX + 1 TX) over Ethernet, GTK+3/WDSP; PortAudio audio; exposes a CAT interface over TCP per receiver and integrates with Hamlib/rigctl so external apps can control it; TX via PTT/MOX.

Name: Maia SDR
Rig Control Backend: Runs on the ADALM-Pluto and controls its AD936x front-end (RX; I/Q recording) - no transceiver CAT.
How to Set CAT: Web UI hosted on the Pluto: set frequency and sample rate in the browser (AD936x controlled via IIO sysfs). No transceiver CAT (monitoring/recording).
Audio Device Select: Browser audio and IQ recording (SigMF) on the Pluto; no soundcard picker.
PTT Config: n/a - RX-focused
Companion Needed: needs an ADALM-Pluto
Notes: Daniel Estévez FPGA-accelerated spectrum + I/Q recorder.
Source Note: Confirmed against maia-sdr.org / the repo (Daniel Estevez). FPGA firmware on the ADALM-Pluto with a web UI (waterfall up to 61.44 Msps, IQ recording to SigMF); controls the AD936x (LO/sample rate/bandwidth/AGC) via IIO sysfs. RX/monitoring, no transceiver CAT.

Name: MiniTioune
Rig Control Backend: USB control of the MiniTiouner NIM tuner (Serit/Sharp) - it IS the DVB-S/S2 DATV receiver; no transceiver CAT.
How to Set CAT: In MiniTioune (F6DZP) select the MiniTiouner USB device and set RX frequency, symbol rate and LNB LO offset. No separate rig CAT.
Audio Device Select: Decoded DVB-S/S2 transport stream to PC video/audio; no rig soundcard.
PTT Config: n/a - receive only
Companion Needed: needs MiniTiouner (Serit NIM) hardware
Notes: F6DZP DATV receiver software.
Source Note: Confirmed against BATC Wiki and vivadatv.org (F6DZP). Windows-only, hosted on the VivaDATV forum.

Name: OpenHPSDR-PowerSDR (mRX PS)
Rig Control Backend: Native control of HPSDR/ANAN over Ethernet (original openHPSDR 'Protocol 1'); full RX/TX, PTT and Kenwood/TS-2000-style CAT emulation.
How to Set CAT: Setup > CAT Control: check Enable CAT, pick one port of a virtual COM pair + baud. A separate virtual-serial driver (com0com / N8VB vCOM) must be installed; no flow control. Board is auto-discovered on the LAN.
Audio Device Select: Setup > Audio (VAC tab): a virtual audio cable for digital modes; local mic for phone.
PTT Config: Full TX - MOX / PTT / CAT / foot-switch.
ALC/Power Guidance: Raise the TX Drive slider to rated power watching the drive/ALC meter; ANAN has internal leveling.
Companion Needed: needs HPSDR/ANAN hardware; virtual audio cable for digital modes
Notes: Windows OpenHPSDR PowerSDR mRX PS.
Source Note: Confirmed. CAT requires an external virtual-COM emulator; emulation is TS-2000/Kenwood per the PowerSDR CAT Command Reference. Protocol 2 radios need Thetis instead.

Name: OpenWebRX+
Rig Control Backend: Same hardware control as OpenWebRX - RTL/Airspy/SDRplay/HackRF etc. via SoapySDR/native drivers; receive-only.
How to Set CAT: In the web admin add SDR device profiles (device args, gain, band/frequency profiles); browser clients tune within them. No transceiver CAT.
Audio Device Select: In-browser audio; extensive server-side digimode/decoder set is built in (POCSAG, FT8, SSTV, ADS-B, HFDL, etc.).
PTT Config: n/a - receive only
Companion Needed: needs a supported SDR on the host
Notes: Enhanced OpenWebRX fork with extra decoders.
Source Note: Confirmed against luarvique fork docs and the OpenWebRX+ home page.

Name: Perseus software
Rig Control Backend: Native USB control of the Perseus HF direct-sampling SDR; can share tuning with external software.
How to Set CAT: In the Perseus app select the Perseus unit and tune/wideband-record. External software follows it via Microtelecom's interfaces: virtual audio ports + a virtual serial/COM (receiver-emulation) port and the Perseus SDR Developer Kit.
Audio Device Select: Soundcard output; virtual audio cable to decoders.
PTT Config: n/a - receive only
Companion Needed: needs Perseus hardware; OmniRig for external rig sync optional
Notes: Microtelecom Perseus receiver.
Source Note: Corrected: Microtelecom documents the third-party interface as virtual audio + virtual communication ports + the SDR Developer Kit. OmniRig is a generic third-party rig-control layer used by apps like SDR-Radio, not Perseus's own native output mechanism - the original 'exposes CAT via OmniRig' phrasing is imprecise.

Name: PhantomSDR / PhantomSDR-Plus
Rig Control Backend: Native control of RX888 MkII and other wideband SDRs (also RTL-SDR/Airspy); browser-based massively-multiuser WebSDR server. Receive-only.
How to Set CAT: A TOML config file (e.g. config.toml / config-rx888mk2.toml) sets the SDR device, input sample rate and IQ bandwidth; the web server serves clients, each browser tuning independently. No transceiver CAT.
Audio Device Select: In-browser audio, per-user channels.
PTT Config: n/a - receive only
Companion Needed: needs an RX888 MkII (or similar) wideband SDR
Notes: Web-SDR server for wide-bandwidth receivers.
Source Note: Confirmed against the PhantomSDR-Plus wiki/INSTALL (Steven9101 / ny4qphil forks); device + sample rate live in the .toml.

Name: piHPSDR
Rig Control Backend: Native HPSDR/ANAN control over Ethernet for RX/TX/PTT/CW; can also act as a Hamlib (rigctl) server for other apps.
How to Set CAT: piHPSDR discovers the board on the LAN; radio config is in its menus. Menu > RIGCTL: enable CAT and set the RigCtl TCP port (e.g. 19090) so loggers/digital apps control it.
Audio Device Select: Local audio / USB CODEC (or Pi audio); loopback/VAC-equivalent for digital.
PTT Config: Full TX - PTT/MOX/foot-switch/CW keying.
ALC/Power Guidance: TX Drive slider to rated power; ANAN internal leveling.
Companion Needed: needs HPSDR/ANAN hardware
Notes: John Melton G0ORX standalone controller (often on a Pi).
Source Note: Confirmed against DL1YCF's piHPSDR User Manual and source. CAT enable + port is in the RIGCTL menu.

Name: Portable SDR (PSDR)
Rig Control Backend: Standalone open-source HF SDR transceiver (Michael/Mike Colton, KE7HIA); onboard firmware controls its own RX/TX front-end and keys transmit directly. Also has VNA/antenna analyzer + GPS.
How to Set CAT: n/a - self-contained portable transceiver; tune with its own controls. No PC CAT.
Audio Device Select: Built-in mic/speaker/headphone; no external soundcard.
PTT Config: Onboard PTT keys TX directly.
ALC/Power Guidance: Firmware-managed TX level; no external ALC control.
Companion Needed: none — self-contained hardware
Notes: Michael Colton's open-source portable SDR transceiver.
Source Note: Confirmed against the PSDR project (github MichaelRColton/PSDR, Hackaday.io). Fully stand-alone, no computer needed.

Name: Portsdown (BATC)
Rig Control Backend: Drives a LimeSDR or ADALM-Pluto for DATV TX and a MiniTiouner/PicoTuner (or Knucker for DVB-T) for RX. Not a transceiver-CAT app.
How to Set CAT: On the Portsdown (Raspberry Pi) touchscreen/menus select the TX SDR (Lime/Pluto), frequency, symbol rate, FEC and mode; RX via the MiniTiouner ('Rx' menu). No transceiver CAT.
Audio Device Select: Transport-stream/encoder audio for DATV, not rig soundcard audio.
PTT Config: PTT/TX from the touchscreen or GPIO keys the SDR + PA.
ALC/Power Guidance: Set RF drive/gain in the menu for a linear PA - DATV must be backed off from compression for shoulder/MER performance (not an SSB-ALC rule).
Companion Needed: needs LimeSDR/Pluto + MiniTiouner + PA on a Raspberry Pi
Notes: BATC DATV transceiver system.
Source Note: Confirmed against BATC Wiki (Portsdown 4 / The Portsdown Transmitter). Pluto max ~1 MS SR; LimeSDR/LimeDVB for higher SRs.

Name: Pothos / PothosSDR (Pothos Flow)
Rig Control Backend: SoapySDR - controls Soapy-supported devices for RX/TX inside a graphical dataflow. Not a CAT app.
How to Set CAT: Not CAT: place SDR Source/Sink blocks in the flowgraph and set SoapySDR device args + frequency/sample-rate on the block.
Audio Device Select: Audio blocks route to the soundcard or file.
PTT Config: TX via an SDR Sink block for TX-capable SDRs.
ALC/Power Guidance: Sink gain sets the TX level (no ALC).
Companion Needed: needs any SoapySDR device
Notes: GNU-Radio-like dataflow framework.
Source Note: Confirmed against the PothosSDR/PothosSoapy wiki and tutorial.

Name: PowerSDR
Rig Control Backend: Native control of FlexRadio hardware (legacy Flex-1500 via USB; Flex-3000/5000 via FireWire) with full RX/TX + Kenwood-style CAT emulation.
How to Set CAT: Setup > CAT Control: enable and assign a virtual COM pair + baud (com0com); external apps control the radio via CAT.
Audio Device Select: Setup > Audio (VAC tab): create two Virtual Audio Cable cables for digital; local mic for phone.
PTT Config: Full TX - MOX/PTT/CAT/foot-switch.
ALC/Power Guidance: Raise the Drive slider to rated power; Flex internal ALC/leveling applies.
Companion Needed: needs legacy FlexRadio hardware; virtual audio cable for digital
Notes: Original FlexRadio PowerSDR.
Source Note: Confirmed against FlexRadio helpdesk articles (VAC + CAT with com0com). Flex-1500 is USB; Flex-3000/5000 are FireWire.

Name: PowerSDR (mRX PS / OpenHPSDR)
Rig Control Backend: Native control of HPSDR/ANAN hardware for RX/TX over Ethernet (Protocol 1); provides CAT + virtual audio for external programs.
How to Set CAT: Setup > CAT Control: check Enable CAT and assign one port of a virtual COM pair + baud (com0com / N8VB vCOM) so external apps drive the radio via TS-2000/Kenwood-style CAT. Board auto-discovered on the LAN.
Audio Device Select: Setup > Audio (VAC): a virtual audio cable for digital; local mic for phone.
PTT Config: Full TX - MOX/PTT/CAT/foot-switch.
ALC/Power Guidance: TX Drive slider to rated power watching the meter; ANAN internal leveling.
Companion Needed: needs HPSDR/ANAN hardware; virtual audio cable for digital
Notes: HPSDR/OpenHPSDR variant of PowerSDR (same as OpenHPSDR-PowerSDR mRX PS).
Source Note: Same software family as the OpenHPSDR-PowerSDR mRX PS entry above; confirmed against TAPR source.

Name: Qt-DAB
Rig Control Backend: Native control of RTL-SDR/SDRplay/Airspy/HackRF/LimeSDR front-ends for DAB/DAB+. Receive-only.
How to Set CAT: Select the SDR device in the device dropdown (auto-detected if configured) and set per-device gain (default 80/100); no transceiver CAT.
Audio Device Select: Decoded DAB audio to the PC output (default output device); audio dumping available.
PTT Config: n/a - receive only
Companion Needed: needs a supported SDR
Notes: DAB/DAB+ decoder (Jan van Katwijk).
Source Note: Confirmed against Qt-DAB README and the PDF user's guide in the source tree (JvanKatwijk).

Name: Quisk
Rig Control Backend: Operates the SDR hardware for RX/TX/PTT and provides a Hamlib (rigctl) server so digital apps can control it.
How to Set CAT: Hardware type is set in quisk_conf.py / the Config screen. Enable the Hamlib server on the 'Remote' config screen; external apps use 'Hamlib NET rigctl' to localhost port 4532 (or 4575 with an external rigctld).
Audio Device Select: Config > Sound: select capture/playback devices (or a virtual audio cable); local mic for TX.
PTT Config: TX-capable - PTT via CAT/serial/keyboard, keying the SDR/hardware.
ALC/Power Guidance: Set the TX level slider in Quisk; keep drive within the exciter/PA's linear range.
Companion Needed: needs a Quisk-supported SDR (SoftRock/Hermes-Lite2/etc.); virtual audio cable for digital
Notes: Jim Ahlstrom N2ADR SDR transceiver software.
Source Note: Confirmed against Jim Ahlstrom (N2ADR) docs; Hamlib port default 4532 is set on the Remote screen.

Name: RF Analyzer
Rig Control Backend: Android app that directly controls SDR receivers (RTL-SDR/HackRF/Airspy/Airspy HF+/HydraSDR) over USB-OTG. Receive/analyzer.
How to Set CAT: In the app's source settings pick the USB SDR and set gains/sample rate; no transceiver CAT.
Audio Device Select: Demodulated audio (AM/FM/SSB) to the phone's output; can record raw IQ.
PTT Config: n/a - receive only
Companion Needed: needs a USB-OTG SDR + Android phone
Notes: Android spectrum analyzer/receiver (Dennis Mantz).
Source Note: Confirmed against the RF Analyzer manual (demantz.github.io) and GitHub (Dennis Mantz).

Name: Rocky
Rig Control Backend: Soundcard I/Q plus CAT/Si570 tuning of SoftRock kits; keys PTT for TX.
How to Set CAT: In Rocky's settings select the receive soundcard for I/Q input and the audio output; configure CAT/Si570 (USB) tuning. Do not enable Transmit until RX/IQ is correct.
Audio Device Select: Soundcard I/Q input / audio output; TX audio via the soundcard.
PTT Config: PTT via serial RTS/DTR, or via the SoftRock Si570 USB interface (which also adds CW paddle/PTT).
ALC/Power Guidance: Soundcard-level TX drive - keep audio low; a SoftRock has no ALC, so set output within its linear range.
Companion Needed: needs a SoftRock RX/TX kit; virtual audio cable optional
Notes: Alex Shovkoplyas VE3NEA (older SoftRock SDR).
Source Note: Confirmed against VE3NEA's Rocky page and SoftRock/G4ZFQ setup notes.

Name: rsp_tcp
Rig Control Backend: Controls an SDRplay RSP and streams I/Q over the network (rtl_tcp-compatible). No transceiver CAT.
How to Set CAT: Command line: rsp_tcp -a <ip> -p <port> -f <freq> ...; rtl_tcp-compatible clients connect over the network. Standard mode mimics rtl_tcp; extended mode exposes full RSP/14-bit features. No CAT.
Audio Device Select: n/a - raw I/Q over TCP; the client decodes.
PTT Config: n/a - receive only
Companion Needed: needs an SDRplay RSP + a client (SDR++/CubicSDR/etc.)
Notes: rtl_tcp-style network server for the RSP.
Source Note: Confirmed. 'rsp_tcp' is the F4FHH port; SDRplay also ship an official RSPTCPServer with a PDF user guide.

Name: rtl-sdr command-line tools (rtl_fm, rtl_tcp, rtl_power, rtl_sdr, rtl_test)
Rig Control Backend: Directly control RTL-SDR dongles via librtlsdr. No transceiver CAT.
How to Set CAT: Command line: -d device index, -f frequency, -g gain, -s sample rate; rtl_tcp serves I/Q on the network. No CAT.
Audio Device Select: rtl_fm pipes demodulated audio to stdout/soundcard (aplay/sox); the others output I/Q or data.
PTT Config: n/a - receive only
Companion Needed: needs an RTL-SDR dongle; downstream tools (aplay/sox/multimon-ng)
Notes: Osmocom rtl-sdr utility suite.
Source Note: Confirmed against the Osmocom rtl-sdr project (original librtlsdr toolset).

Name: RTL1090
Rig Control Backend: Controls an RTL-SDR tuned to 1090 MHz (ADS-B Mode-S). No transceiver CAT.
How to Set CAT: In the RTL1090 GUI select the RTL device and gain; it stays tuned to 1090 MHz. No CAT.
Audio Device Select: n/a - I/Q demodulated to Mode-S messages.
PTT Config: n/a - receive only
Companion Needed: needs an RTL-SDR; a map front-end (adsbScope/Virtual Radar) optional
Notes: Windows ADS-B decoder.
Source Note: Confirmed - Jetvision/Günter Köllner ADS-B decoder for RTL-SDR.

Name: rtl_433
Rig Control Backend: Tunes and controls an RTL-SDR (and SoapySDR devices) for ISM-band capture. No transceiver CAT.
How to Set CAT: Command line: -d device (index/serial/SoapySDR query/rtl_tcp), -f frequency (default 433920000 Hz), -s sample rate. No CAT.
Audio Device Select: n/a - I/Q demodulated to decoded ISM device messages (JSON/MQTT/etc.).
PTT Config: n/a - receive only
Companion Needed: needs an RTL-SDR (or a SoapySDR device)
Notes: ISM-band sensor/device decoder.
Source Note: Confirmed against merbanan/rtl_433 docs (STARTING.md, man page).

Name: rtl_tcp SDR (iOS)
Rig Control Backend: none (receive-only) - native rtl_tcp network control of a remote RTL-SDR; no CAT to a conventional transceiver
How to Set CAT: n/a - not a CAT app. You enter the host IP + TCP port (default 1234) of the rtl_tcp server that the RTL-SDR is attached to; tuning/gain commands are sent over that TCP link.
Audio Device Select: n/a - demodulated audio plays on the iOS device speaker/headphones; no sound-card routing
PTT Config: n/a - receive only
Companion Needed: needs an rtl_tcp server (rtl_tcp running on a PC/Pi/network dongle) that shares the RTL-SDR over the network
Notes: iOS blocks direct USB SDRs, so the dongle must be exposed over the network via rtl_tcp; RX-only tuner app
Source Note: Confirmed - an rtl_tcp client is RX-only over TCP; no rig CAT. No single official app URL cited.

Name: SatNOGS (satnogs-client / gr-satnogs)
Rig Control Backend: Hamlib rigctld for the radio + Hamlib rotctld for the rotator; SDR handled by gr-satnogs/GNU Radio/SoapySDR
How to Set CAT: Set in satnogs-client config via satnogs-setup: SATNOGS_RIG_IP/SATNOGS_RIG_PORT point at a running rigctld; the SDR is chosen with SATNOGS_SOAPY_RX_DEVICE (driver=<name>) plus gain/PPM. rigctld and rotctld run as systemd services.
Audio Device Select: n/a - IQ flows straight from the SDR into GNU Radio flowgraphs that demod/decode; no PC sound-card device is selected
PTT Config: n/a - receive-only ground station (no TX)
Companion Needed: needs rigctld (Hamlib) + rotctld running, a SoapySDR-supported dongle, and connection to the SatNOGS network for scheduling
Notes: Automated satellite ground station; Doppler correction is applied by pushing frequency updates to rigctld during each scheduled pass
Source Note: Confirmed: rigctld + rotctld + SoapySDR (SATNOGS_SOAPY_RX_DEVICE) set through satnogs-setup, verified.

Name: SDR Console (SDR-Radio v3)
Rig Control Backend: native SDR hardware control (RX/TX) plus external-transceiver CAT via built-in External Radio drivers or Omnirig; exposes CAT to other apps via virtual serial ports
How to Set CAT: Enable External Radio (ribbon > More Options), which adds the External Radio pane to the Receive DSP panel: pick radio model, COM port, baud, and tick control-the-rig / control-the-SDR; or select Omnirig Settings (e.g. TS-2000 emulation, the 2nd port of a VSP pair). To feed other software, create a virtual COM pair (e.g. VSP Manager) and point SDR Console's CAT output at it.
Audio Device Select: Options > Audio (or per-receiver): select the output device; route to a Virtual Audio Cable to feed WSJT-X/fldigi
PTT Config: On TX-capable SDRs, PTT is via the SDR's own transmit; on hybrid setups, the linked CAT rig's PTT; digital apps key over the virtual CAT/audio
ALC/Power Guidance: For TX SDRs use the TX drive/gain - raise to rated output without overdriving the DAC/PA (no analog ALC needle); RX-only setups n/a
Companion Needed: Virtual Audio Cable + a virtual serial-port pair (com0com/VSPE) to bridge to digital apps; Omnirig optional
Notes: Simon Brown's console; also includes a networked remote-radio server
Source Note: Confirmed: External Radio pane (model/COM/baud + control-rig/control-SDR) or Omnirig, plus virtual COM/VAC for third-party apps.

Name: SDR Console (SDR-Radio.com)
Rig Control Backend: native control of a broad range of RX and RX/TX SDRs plus CAT control of conventional transceivers (built-in External Radio drivers or Omnirig); built-in remote server
How to Set CAT: Home/View > External Radio: pick manufacturer/model, COM port, baud, tick control-the-radio / control-the-SDR; or use Omnirig. Output to other apps via a virtual serial pair.
Audio Device Select: Options > Audio: choose the playback device; route to a Virtual Audio Cable for digital apps
PTT Config: TX SDRs key via their own transmit; a linked CAT radio's PTT in hybrid setups; digital apps trigger via virtual CAT
ALC/Power Guidance: TX SDRs: raise TX drive/gain gradually to rated output cleanly (no analog ALC); RX-only n/a
Companion Needed: Virtual Audio Cable + virtual serial pair for digital apps; Omnirig optional
Notes: Current name of the same product family as v3; includes SmartLink-style networked remote radio
Source Note: Confirmed - same product as the v3 entry; External Radio (or Omnirig) + virtual serial/VAC verified.

Name: SDR Touch
Rig Control Backend: native USB-OTG control of RTL-SDR/NeSDR/SDRplay/HackRF (Android); no CAT to an external rig
How to Set CAT: n/a - tuning is done in-app; you grant USB permission to the dongle when it is plugged in via OTG.
Audio Device Select: n/a - demod audio plays on the phone; no sound-card routing
PTT Config: n/a - receive only
Companion Needed: USB OTG cable; the RTL-SDR driver app is bundled
Notes: Android RX-only tuner (FM/AM/NFM/SSB)
Source Note: Confirmed - Android RX app driving the dongle over USB-OTG; no rig CAT. No stable official URL cited.

Name: SDR# (SDRSharp)
Rig Control Backend: native front-end control (Airspy/RTL/HackRF/SDRplay etc.); external-transceiver sync via plugins (Omnirig or a serial CAT plugin such as CalicoCAT) plus a PTT plugin
How to Set CAT: Add the Omnirig (or CalicoCAT/serial CAT) plugin, then configure the rig in Omnirig (model, COM, baud) so SDR# tracks/tunes the transceiver; CalicoCAT can use separate COM ports for CAT and PTT.
Audio Device Select: Audio panel: choose the output device and driver (MME/WASAPI/ASIO); route to a Virtual Audio Cable to feed digital-mode apps
PTT Config: Via a PTT plugin or the linked rig's CAT; SDR# itself is RX for typical dongles
Companion Needed: Omnirig for external-rig sync; Virtual Audio Cable to feed WSJT-X/fldigi; community plugins
Notes: Plugin-driven receiver/panadapter
Source Note: Confirmed: CAT sync is plugin-based (Omnirig or CalicoCAT); core SDR# is RX. No single official docs URL for the third-party plugins.

Name: SDR++
Rig Control Backend: native SDR control via source modules (RTL/Airspy/HackRF/SDRplay/SpyServer/rtl_tcp etc.); external-rig sync via the built-in Rigctl Server module - a matching Rigctl Client is limited/absent, so the third-party RigSync (Hamlib rigctl client) module is commonly used
How to Set CAT: Enable the Rigctl Server module (lets other apps/rigctld clients control SDR++, e.g. GPredict; set the listen port). To have SDR++ FOLLOW an external rig bidirectionally, add the third-party RigSync module and point it at a rigctld host/port - SDR++'s own client capability is basic.
Audio Device Select: Audio Sink module selects the output device; route to a Virtual Audio Cable for digital apps
PTT Config: n/a for typical RX SDRs
Companion Needed: rigctld/Hamlib for external-rig sync; Virtual Audio Cable for digital apps
Notes: Cross-platform; can act as a SpyServer/rtl_tcp client
Source Note: Corrected: the mature built-in module is the Rigctl SERVER (SDR++ is controlled by others). A built-in Rigctl Client to drive/follow an external rig is limited; users add the third-party RigSync module for bidirectional sync. The claim implied both client and server are full built-ins.

Name: SDR-J
Rig Control Backend: native control of RTL-SDR/SDRplay/Airspy front-ends; no external-rig CAT
How to Set CAT: n/a - the device is selected in the app; SDR-J is a DAB/DAB+/FM receiver suite, not a CAT controller.
Audio Device Select: in-app audio-output selection (decoded DAB/FM audio to a sound device)
PTT Config: n/a - receive only
Companion Needed: none (device drivers only)
Notes: Jan van Katwijk's suite (DAB/DAB+/FM receivers); RX-only
Source Note: Confirmed - DAB/FM RX suite, no rig CAT. No single stable official URL cited.

Name: SDRangel
Rig Control Backend: native SDR hardware for RX/TX and PTT; can be controlled by external apps via the Rig Ctl Server feature (Hamlib rigctld protocol)
How to Set CAT: Enable the Rig Ctl Server feature to EXPOSE CAT to other software (device set R0, channel index, TCP port default 4532) - e.g. so GPredict can Doppler-tune SDRangel. The SDR device itself is chosen in the sampling-device dialog.
Audio Device Select: per-channel audio device selection in each demod plugin; route to a Virtual Audio Cable for digital apps
PTT Config: For TX-capable SDRs (HackRF/LimeSDR/PlutoSDR/BladeRF/USRP) TX/PTT is via the TX channel's start/stop; no external key needed
ALC/Power Guidance: On TX set interpolation/channel gain to reach rated output without clipping the DAC (no analog ALC)
Companion Needed: Virtual Audio Cable for digital apps; none mandatory
Notes: Multi-channel RX/TX SDR framework with many demods/mods
Source Note: Confirmed, with clarification: the Rig Ctl Server makes SDRangel controllable BY other apps (server-side), rather than SDRangel controlling an external rig - the claim's 'control external rigs' is slightly misleading.

Name: SDRconnect
Rig Control Backend: native control of SDRplay RSP devices (local or over the network); external-transceiver sync via Omnirig (Rig Control panel)
How to Set CAT: Rig Control / Omnirig (ORIG) settings: enable Omnirig and tick control-the-rig and/or control-the-SDR; Omnirig itself holds the rig model/COM/baud (start Omnirig first, as admin if needed).
Audio Device Select: Audio settings select the output device; route to a Virtual Audio Cable for digital apps
PTT Config: n/a - RSP receivers are RX-only (PTT handled by the linked CAT rig if present)
Companion Needed: Omnirig for external-rig sync; Virtual Audio Cable for digital apps
Notes: SDRplay's newer cross-platform client with a built-in server for remote RSP access
Source Note: Confirmed: SDRconnect adds Omnirig-based rig control (as in the SDRuno pattern) atop native RSP control; RSP is RX-only.

Name: SdrDx
Rig Control Backend: native control of RFSpace SDR-IP/NetSDR, RTL-SDR and soundcard front-ends; limited external CAT
How to Set CAT: Device chosen in config (network IP for SDR-IP/NetSDR, or soundcard/RTL); it is a receiver app, not a CAT hub for third-party rigs.
Audio Device Select: config selects the audio output device; Virtual Audio Cable to feed decoders
PTT Config: n/a - receive only
Companion Needed: Virtual Audio Cable for external decoders
Notes: Mac/Windows receiver app
Source Note: Confirmed - RFSpace/RTL/soundcard receiver, RX-only. No stable official URL cited.

Name: SdrGlut
Rig Control Backend: native control of any SoapySDR device (RTL/Airspy/HackRF/LimeSDR etc.)
How to Set CAT: n/a - device selected via SoapySDR in the app; not a CAT controller.
Audio Device Select: in-app audio output; Virtual Audio Cable for decoders
PTT Config: n/a - receive oriented
Companion Needed: SoapySDR; Virtual Audio Cable for decoders
Notes: Mac/OpenGL SDR receiver (CubicSDR lineage)
Source Note: Confirmed - SoapySDR receiver, no rig CAT.

Name: SDRTrunk
Rig Control Backend: native tuner control (RTL/Airspy/HackRF/SDRplay); follows control channels and tunes voice grants within the SDR's bandwidth
How to Set CAT: Tuner is added/configured in-app (device, sample rate, PPM); no external-rig CAT.
Audio Device Select: the audio/playback config selects the output device for decoded calls
PTT Config: n/a - receive only
Companion Needed: none; optionally streams to Rdio Scanner/Broadcastify
Notes: Trunk-tracking decoder (P25 etc.)
Source Note: Confirmed - trunk-tracking RX decoder; tuner configured in-app, no rig CAT.

Name: SDRuno
Rig Control Backend: native SDRplay RSP control; external-transceiver tracking via Omnirig
How to Set CAT: Main panel > SETT... > ORIG tab: enable Omnirig and tick control-the-rig / control-the-SDR (Omnirig holds the rig model/COM/baud). A VRX can also be externally controlled from RX Control > SETT > CAT using a virtual serial-port pair (default COM10, ENABLE & CONNECT).
Audio Device Select: RX Control > SETT > OUT: pick the output device; route to a Virtual Audio Cable for digital apps
PTT Config: n/a - RSP is RX-only (PTT via the linked CAT rig if present)
