Companion Needed: Omnirig for external-rig sync; Virtual Audio Cable for digital apps
Notes: SDRplay's Windows flagship (derived from Studio1)
Source Note: Confirmed: ORIG (Omnirig) tab for rig tracking, plus per-VRX CAT tab for being controlled; RSP is RX-only.

Name: SigDigger
Rig Control Backend: native SDR control via SoapySDR
How to Set CAT: n/a - device selected via the SoapySDR source dialog; not a CAT controller.
Audio Device Select: in-app audio-preview device; Virtual Audio Cable for further decoding
PTT Config: n/a - RX/analysis oriented
Companion Needed: SoapySDR
Notes: Signal-analysis/inspection SDR tool
Source Note: Confirmed - SoapySDR analysis/receiver, no rig CAT.

Name: SliceMaster
Rig Control Backend: none of its own - drives FlexRadio slices via the SmartSDR API over Ethernet and auto-configures/launches third-party apps with the correct SmartSDR CAT (serial) + DAX (audio) devices
How to Set CAT: In SliceMaster, per slice set the Launch behavior (Never / When active / By mode) and map the app; SliceMaster then automatically selects the rig type + CAT COM port + DAX audio devices for the launched app (SmartSDR CAT provides the virtual COM ports). Requires the slice's mode/DAX to be set correctly in SmartSDR.
Audio Device Select: uses SmartSDR DAX channels as the sound devices for the launched apps (assigned by SliceMaster)
PTT Config: PTT to the Flex is via SmartSDR CAT/DAX - the launched app keys through CAT; MOX/PTT handled by SmartSDR
ALC/Power Guidance: Set RF power in SmartSDR; drive DAX TX audio so SmartSDR's TX meter shows full rated power with no flat-topping (Flex has no classic ALC needle)
Companion Needed: needs SmartSDR + SmartSDR CAT + DAX running with a Flex 6000; launches WSJT-X/fldigi/CW Skimmer etc.
Notes: K1DBO's Slice Master 6000 orchestrator/launcher; not a modem or CAT engine itself
Source Note: Confirmed: SliceMaster auto-launches and auto-configures third-party apps with the right SmartSDR CAT + DAX per slice.

Name: SmartSDR
Rig Control Backend: native control of FlexRadio 6000-series over Ethernet (local or SmartLink); slices, DAX audio and CAT serial provided for digital apps
How to Set CAT: SmartSDR CAT creates FlexVSP virtual serial-port pairs mapped to slices; other software picks that COM (as a Flex/Kenwood TS-2000-like model) at the shown baud.
Audio Device Select: DAX Control Panel assigns DAX RX/TX audio channels which appear as Windows sound devices for digital apps
PTT Config: Digital apps key via a SmartSDR CAT command or via DAX TX (VOX auto-keys MOX); no serial PTT line needed
ALC/Power Guidance: Set the RF Power slider in SmartSDR; raise DAX TX audio to reach full rated power without flat-topping - no analog ALC, so watch power + waveform
Companion Needed: SmartSDR CAT + DAX helper apps; a Flex 6000 radio; SmartLink for remote
Notes: The FlexRadio control suite proper
Source Note: Confirmed against the SmartSDR CAT User Guide: FlexVSP virtual COM ports (CAT) + DAX audio + CAT/DAX-VOX PTT.

Name: SmartSDR (FlexRadio) + SmartSDR CAT / DAX / Maestro
Rig Control Backend: native Flex SDR control over Ethernet (control + I/Q + audio); SmartSDR CAT = virtual serial CAT ports, DAX = sound-card audio to loggers/digital apps, Maestro = hardware front panel, SmartLink = remote
How to Set CAT: In SmartSDR CAT create a FlexVSP port for the desired slice; point the logger/digital app at that COM (Flex/Kenwood-style model) and baud.
Audio Device Select: DAX Control Panel maps DAX RX/TX channels to Windows audio devices; select those in the digital app
PTT Config: App keys via a SmartSDR CAT command or DAX TX; MOX in SmartSDR
ALC/Power Guidance: RF Power slider sets output; increase DAX TX audio to hit rated power cleanly (no analog ALC)
Companion Needed: the CAT + DAX helper apps; a Flex 6000; optional Maestro / SmartLink
Notes: Same suite with all component pieces named
Source: FlexRadio SmartSDR Software & CAT User Guides
Source Note: Confirmed - same Flex ecosystem as the SmartSDR entry, describing the CAT/DAX/Maestro/SmartLink pieces.

Name: SmartSDR for Mac / iOS (dogpark software)
Rig Control Backend: native Flex control over the network (TX/RX, slices, panadapter) - a third-party (dogpark) native Mac client for Flex 6000; SmartLink remote supported
How to Set CAT: Connects to the Flex over LAN/SmartLink; it has no serial CAT of its own. For digital apps on the Mac, use the xDAX and xCAT helper apps (DL3LSM) - xCAT bridges legacy CAT-over-TCP to native Flex commands and xDAX exposes DAX network audio.
Audio Device Select: uses network audio to/from the Flex; for external apps, xDAX exposes DAX audio devices (no separate desktop sound card)
PTT Config: PTT/MOX to the Flex over the network from the app UI (or via xCAT for digital apps)
ALC/Power Guidance: Set RF power in the app; Flex has no analog ALC - drive to rated power cleanly
Companion Needed: a FlexRadio 6000; optional SmartLink for remote
Notes: Independent Mac/iOS/iPadOS client, not FlexRadio's own software
Source Note: Confirmed, refined: on the Mac the digital-mode CAT/audio bridges are xCAT/xDAX (by DL3LSM), not Windows 'SmartSDR CAT/DAX'; dogparkSDR itself is the GUI network client.

Name: SoDiRa
Rig Control Backend: native front-end via ExtIO DLLs (RTL-SDR, SDRplay, Perseus, etc.) plus soundcard/USB IQ and SpyServer
How to Set CAT: Configuration > Receiver: select the ExtIO DLL (or soundcard/USB/SpyServer source), then HW Init + Start; not a CAT controller for external rigs.
Audio Device Select: Configuration audio settings; the 'used sound channel'/IQ-swap must be set correctly or DRM will not decode
PTT Config: n/a - receive only
Companion Needed: the appropriate ExtIO DLL for your hardware

Name: Thetis
Rig Control Backend: native control of ANAN/Hermes-Lite (openHPSDR protocol) for RX/TX; exposes CAT (serial/TCP) and virtual audio to third-party apps (also TCI)
How to Set CAT: Setup > CAT Control > CAT tab: enable CAT and choose the virtual COM port + baud (or TCP), and set the PTT COM port with RTS + Enable PTT, so loggers/digital apps talk to Thetis as a Kenwood-style rig. TCI is an alternative for lower-latency control.
Audio Device Select: Setup > Audio (VAC1/VAC2/VAC3): route TX/RX audio to Virtual Audio Cables that the digital apps use
PTT Config: Digital apps key via the CAT port (RTS), via VAC audio/VOX, or TCI; MOX in Thetis
ALC/Power Guidance: Set Drive/TX level in Thetis; raise VAC TX audio so Drive reaches rated PA output without flat-topping (openHPSDR has no analog ALC needle - watch the TX display)
Companion Needed: Virtual Audio Cable (VAC) + a virtual serial pair (com0com) for CAT to digital apps
Notes: openHPSDR console for ANAN / Hermes-Lite 2
Source Note: Confirmed: Setup > CAT Control (COM/baud + RTS PTT) and Setup > Audio VAC1/2/3 verified; TCI is an additional option.

Audio Device Select: uses Virtual Audio Cable(s) to pass SDR audio into Unitrunker for decoding and out to a speaker device
PTT Config: n/a - receive only

Rig Control Backend: native SDR TX/RX via device backends (RTL/HackRF/USRP/LimeSDR/Airspy/SDRplay/PlutoSDR)

Name: Zeus Radio
Rig Control Backend: Native control of ZS-1 hardware (TX/RX) and other front-ends; presents virtual CAT + virtual audio to external apps in Server Mode.
How to Set CAT: Server Mode: set the CAT/Command manager to a virtual COM and PTT manager to another virtual COM, then start CAT; external apps (WSJT-X/fldigi) use those COM ports as a Kenwood-style rig.

Notes: SSB-Electronic / HFR ZS-1 transceiver software

Name: AGWPE (SV2AGW Packet Engine)
Rig Control Backend: None (sound-card AX.25 modem, or drives a serial hardware TNC); no rig-frequency CAT.
How to Set CAT: Not a CAT app. Right-click tray icon > Properties > New Port; in TNC Setup pick SoundCard (or a serial TNC/COM), TNC type and the TX/PTT serial (COM/LPT) port.
Audio Device Select: In the sound-card port properties select the input/output sound device (and AFSK 1200/300).
PTT Config: PTT via a serial COM/LPT control line (RTS/DTR); VOX also possible.
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a - set the soundcard TX level for clean FM deviation / SSB drive, not ALC.

Notes: SV2AGW Packet Engine (free version)

Name: AGWPE / AGW Packet Engine (Pro)
Rig Control Backend: None (sound-card AX.25 modem, or serial hardware TNC); no rig CAT. Pro is the paid build with the same config model.
How to Set CAT: Not a CAT app. Port setup: pick a SoundCard modem (or serial TNC/COM) and the PTT serial port.
Audio Device Select: Sound-card input/output device chosen in the radio-port properties.
PTT Config: PTT via serial COM RTS/DTR (or VOX).
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a - set clean AFSK TX audio/deviation, not ALC.
Companion Needed: provides AX.25 packet (AGWPE TCP + KISS) to Winlink, APRS, BBS clients
Notes: Paid 'Pro' build with multi-port support

Name: ARDOP (ARDOP_Win / ARDOP TNC)
Rig Control Backend: Sound-card modem presenting a TCP virtual TNC; frequency CAT is handled by the host app (Winlink Express/ARIM), not the TNC.
How to Set CAT: Launched (or in the ARDOP_Win setup) with a capture/playback device + host TCP port; a PTT argument tells it how to key. Freq/mode CAT comes from the host.
Audio Device Select: Choose capture (RX) and playback (TX) sound devices at launch or in the setup dialog.
PTT Config: Native PTT is serial RTS, or CAT on/off hex strings on a serial COM. VOX possible.
ALC/Power Guidance: USB (data) mode; raise TX/playback audio so the radio makes rated power with ALC barely moving.
Companion Needed: a client over the TCP/serial host interface (Winlink Express, ARIM/gARIM, BPQ32)
Notes: Windows ARDOP TNC; presents a virtual TNC to clients
Source: ardop (pflarue) command-line options / John Wiseman ARDOP docs
Source Note: Original listed 'serial RTS/DTR, CAT, or Hamlib.' Native ARDOP TNC PTT is serial RTS or CAT hex strings (and GPIO on ARM builds); DTR and built-in Hamlib/rigctld are not native - Hamlib PTT requires a patched fork (ardop-hamlib) or an external rigctld bridge (ardopctl).; PTT methods overstated

Name: ARDOP (ardopc / ardopcf / ARDOP_Win TNC)
Rig Control Backend: Sound-card modem presenting a TCP virtual TNC with PTT keying; freq/mode CAT usually done by the client.
How to Set CAT: Started with host TCP port + capture/playback device numbers; PTT argument (-p serial, -g GPIO, or -k/-u CAT hex) tells it how to key.
Audio Device Select: Capture and playback device numbers given at launch (ALSA/PortAudio names or Windows device numbers listed at startup).
PTT Config: Native PTT via serial RTS (-p), CAT on/off hex strings (-k/-u), or GPIO on ARM/Pi (-g). VOX possible.
ALC/Power Guidance: USB-data mode; raise TX audio to rated power with ALC barely moving.
Companion Needed: a TCP client (Winlink, ARIM, BPQ32)
Notes: Cross-platform C implementations (ardopc/ardopcf) plus the Windows build
Source Note: Native ardopcf PTT is serial RTS / CAT hex / ARM-GPIO. DTR and built-in Hamlib/rigctld/flrig are NOT native (available only via a patched fork or external rigctld bridge).; PTT methods overstated

Name: ARDOP_GUI
Rig Control Backend: none of its own - it is a monitor/control GUI for a running ARDOP TNC (the TNC handles audio + PTT)
How to Set CAT: n/a - connects to the ARDOP TNC's host TCP port; radio CAT/PTT is configured in the TNC/host, not here
Audio Device Select: n/a - audio devices are chosen in the ARDOP TNC, not in the GUI
PTT Config: n/a - PTT is handled by the ARDOP TNC

Companion Needed: a running ARDOP TNC (ardopc/ardopcf) that it attaches to
Notes: Waterfall/status GUI for ARDOP
Source Note: Could not locate an official standalone tool literally named 'ARDOP_GUI' that is purely a monitor. Note: the Windows ARDOP TNC (ARDOP_Win) has its own GUI window and DOES handle audio + PTT itself (configured in its setup), which would contradict 'none of its own.' Verify what 'ARDOP_GUI' refers to.

Name: ardopc / ardopcf
Rig Control Backend: Sound-card modem presenting an ARDOP TCP host TNC. No SCS-Dragon/PACTOR emulation.
How to Set CAT: Command-line: host TCP port + capture/playback device numbers + PTT method (-p serial RTS, -g GPIO on ARM, or -k/-u CAT hex strings).
Audio Device Select: Capture and playback device numbers (ALSA/PortAudio) given as arguments.
PTT Config: PTT via serial RTS, CAT on/off hex strings, or ARM/Pi GPIO. Hamlib/rigctld PTT only via a patched fork or external bridge.
ALC/Power Guidance: USB-data mode; raise TX (playback) audio to rated power with ALC minimal.
Companion Needed: a TCP host (BPQ32/Winlink/ARIM) or, via Dragon emulation, PACTOR apps
Notes: John Wiseman's cross-platform ARDOP (ardopcf is the maintained fork)
Source Note: Two errors in the original: (1) ardopcf/ardopc does NOT emulate an SCS Dragon so PACTOR-only apps can use it - no such feature is in the docs; (2) native PTT is serial RTS / CAT hex / ARM-GPIO, not Hamlib/rigctld/flrig (those need a fork or external ardopctl/rigctld bridge).; SCS-Dragon emulation and Hamlib claims unsupported

Notes: Amateur Radio Instant Messaging + file/beacon over ARDOP

Name: VARA FM / VARA HF
Rig Control Backend: Standalone sound-card modem; CAT + PTT set within the modem (or delegated to the host app). Frequency usually set by the host.
How to Set CAT: Settings > PTT (and Rig Control): PTT via VOX, CAT, or RTS/DTR on a COM; for CAT set the rig command set/port there.

PTT Config: PTT via VOX, CAT, or RTS/DTR on a COM port (Settings > PTT).
ALC/Power Guidance: FM: clean deviation. HF/SSB: USB mode, raise Settings > SoundCard TX drive so the radio makes rated power with ALC barely moving (VARA is sensitive to overdrive/IMD; keep ALC no more than ~1/3).
Companion Needed: apps connect over TCP (Winlink Express, VarAC, vARIM) - VARA HF/FM command port 8300
Notes: EA5HVK modems; a registration key unlocks full speed

Name: VARA HF
Rig Control Backend: Standalone sound-card SSB modem; PTT via CAT/RTS-DTR/VOX; IP host interface to clients.
How to Set CAT: Settings > PTT: select CAT/RTS/DTR (COM port + rig for CAT) or VOX; frequency is normally commanded by the host app.
Audio Device Select: Settings > SoundCard: input/output device.
PTT Config: PTT via VOX, CAT, or RTS/DTR (COM).
ALC/Power Guidance: USB (data); raise Settings > SoundCard TX drive to rated power with ALC minimal (≈1/3 max) - VARA HF is very sensitive to distortion, keep it clean.
Companion Needed: clients over TCP 8300 (Winlink Express, VarAC, vARIM)
Notes: HF variant (500/2300/2750 Hz bandwidths)

Name: VARA SAT
Rig Control Backend: Standalone sound-card modem (varasat.exe) through a satellite-capable SSB station; PTT/CAT set in Settings (host usually keys).

PTT Config: PTT via VOX, CAT, or RTS/DTR; test via Settings > SoundCard TUNE.
ALC/Power Guidance: USB linear operation into the sat uplink; keep TX drive low/clean to protect linearity and avoid IMD on the transponder.
Companion Needed: Winlink/VarAC over TCP; note VarAC must be set to 'VaraFM' type to key VARA SAT (SAT/FM share a command set)
Notes: For QO-100/satellite Winlink; same modem family as VARA FM

Name: WinRPR

Name: Black Cat SSTV

Companion Needed: optional OmniRig or HRD if sharing the rig with other apps
Notes: Modern FT8 decoder with a WSJT-X-style workflow (ft2.it).
Source Note: Backends (Hamlib/OmniRig/HRD bridge) confirmed against the official manual. The specific 'Hamlib 4.7' version wasn't independently verified but is plausible for the current release.

Name: DigiRite
Rig Control Backend: Via WriteLog - inherits WriteLog's rig control (configured in WriteLog). Runs standalone too, but then has NO rig control.
How to Set CAT: No independent CAT UI - configure the radio in WriteLog (Setup > Ports / rig type); DigiRite is launched from WriteLog and uses its rig/PTT. It also commands the rig to USB for FT8.
Audio Device Select: In DigiRite, select the sound-card input/output (the rig's USB codec); WriteLog handles rig/PTT.
PTT Config: PTT keyed via WriteLog's rig control (CAT, DATA-mode PTT); DigiRite triggers TX through WriteLog.
ALC/Power Guidance: DATA/USB-D mode; set the soundcard TX level so ALC barely moves.
Companion Needed: needs WriteLog (DigiRite is its FT8/RTTY engine)
Notes: Multi-decoder FT8 window for WriteLog contest logging (K1GQ).
Source Note: Confirmed. Added caveat: DigiRite can run stand-alone but then has no rig control/PTT/logging - rig + DATA-mode PTT come from WriteLog (Setup > Ports).

Name: DigiSkimmer
Rig Control Backend: None (network SDR control) - tunes networked KiwiSDR receivers via kiwirecorder; not a CAT rig.
How to Set CAT: No rig CAT - edit the Python settings file (config.py, based on the settings.py sample: SETTINGS / STATIONS / SCHEDULES) to list KiwiSDR host(s)/port/password and the bands to skim; DigiSkimmer tunes the KiwiSDR over the network.
Audio Device Select: n/a - pulls audio/IQ from KiwiSDR over the network; no local sound card.
PTT Config: n/a - receive/skim only.
Companion Needed: needs one or more KiwiSDR receivers + kiwirecorder; uses WSJT-X's decoders; spots to PSKReporter/WSPRnet
Notes: Headless multi-band FT8/FT4/WSPR spotting (lazywalker); typically runs as a Docker container on a Pi.
Source Note: Original said the config is YAML. It is actually a Python settings file (config.py), not YAML.; config format is Python, not YAML

Name: Faros
Rig Control Backend: OmniRig (CAT) — VE3NEA's own CAT engine
How to Set CAT: Faros ships with OmniRig; the rig model/port/baud are set once in OmniRig. Faros then commands band changes to follow the NCDXF/IBP beacon schedule and forces the rig to CW mode.
Audio Device Select: Choose the sound-card input device fed from the receiver (sound input selection is under the View menu).
PTT Config: n/a — receive/monitoring only (no TX).
Companion Needed: needs OmniRig for CAT; monitors the NCDXF/IBP beacon chain
Notes: VE3NEA ionospheric propagation monitor; decodes beacon signals and measures propagation.
Source: DX Atlas — Faros (VE3NEA); K9LA 'Monitoring Beacons with FAROS'
Source Note: Confirmed. Minor additions from docs: Faros also forces the rig to CW mode, and sound-card input is selected under the View menu (not a separate Setup dialog).

Name: FT Ham (FT8/FT4 transceiver)
Rig Control Backend: none (audio only) — no CAT/USB rig control
How to Set CAT: n/a — no rig CAT; set the radio's frequency/mode (USB) manually. The app states 'no USB control, no CAT interface, no computer required'.
Audio Device Select: iOS audio routing — decode via built-in mic or a connected USB audio interface; TX audio via speaker or the interface. External USB audio devices are fully supported.
PTT Config: VOX — the radio keys on the transmitted FT8 audio. No CAT PTT.
ALC/Power Guidance: USB/DATA mode; keep the iOS output/interface level low so ALC barely moves.
Companion Needed: none (optional audio interface for a cabled link)
Notes: Self-contained iPhone FT8/FT4 transceiver with waterfall, auto-sequencing, ADIF log; fully offline, on-device DSP (turrion.dev).
Source: FT Ham official site (ftham.turrion.dev) + App Store listing

Name: FT8 Decoder (Bunzee)
Rig Control Backend: none (audio only, RX)
How to Set CAT: n/a — no CAT/TX; tune the SSB/SDR receiver to the FT8 frequency (USB) manually. Correct time (e.g. via ClockSync) is critical for decoding.
Audio Device Select: Android audio input — device mic or an audio interface (e.g. iRig 2) fed from the SSB/SDR receiver.
PTT Config: n/a — receive/decode only.
Companion Needed: none (needs an SSB or SDR receiver to provide audio)
Notes: Decode-only FT8 for Android (com.bunzee.digitalradioreceiver); no transmit.

Name: FT8 Radio (Bunzee)
Rig Control Backend: Built-in CAT rig control over USB (select rig model, e.g. IC-7300 / IC-705) OR 'VOX' audio-only mode — handles RX, TX, QSO logging and CAT itself with no PC/Pi.
How to Set CAT: In app settings, pick the radio model: choose your rig (e.g. IC-7300, IC-705) for USB CAT control, or select 'VOX' for audio-only keying. Set callsign/grid/band (callsign & grid in UPPERCASE).
Audio Device Select: Android audio via a USB audio interface (or the rig's USB codec) in CAT mode; acoustic/mic keying in VOX mode.
PTT Config: PTT via CAT over USB when a CAT rig is selected; VOX when 'VOX' mode is selected.
ALC/Power Guidance: USB/DATA; keep TX audio low so ALC barely moves.
Companion Needed: none (works with an external SSB/SDR radio; optional audio interface)
Notes: Experimental portable FT8 TX/RX app for SOTA/POTA (com.bunzee.ft8radio); RX plus limited TX.
Source Note: Original claimed 'no CAT / VOX only'. FT8 Radio actually provides self-contained CAT rig control — you select the rig model (e.g. IC-7300 for CAT, or 'VOX' for AF/acoustic mode); the app does FT8 RX/TX, QSO logging and CAT with no PC required. Verified via kholia/DigitalRadioReceiverSupport and Google Play.; has self-contained CAT, not VOX-only

Name: FT8AF
Rig Control Backend: native USB CAT (built-in; 75+ Icom/Yaesu/Kenwood/Xiegu and more) over USB-serial
How to Set CAT: Open FT8AF, grant USB & mic permissions, pick your rig model; connect via a single USB-C cable (OTG) — CAT (and audio) run over that one cable. Set band/mode in-app. Can also decode from the phone mic before wiring CAT.
Audio Device Select: Audio over the same USB-C connection (rig's USB codec) or the device's built-in mic; selected in app settings.
PTT Config: PTT via CAT over USB (auto-sequenced); VOX possible for audio-only rigs.
ALC/Power Guidance: Confirmed — FT8AF provides automatic ALC auto-leveling and SWR protection across 75+ rigs; use DATA/USB-D and let the app manage drive/ALC.
Companion Needed: none (single USB-C cable to the rig)
Notes: All-native Android FT8 (ft8af.app): decode, CAT drive, auto-sequence, ALC/SWR management — no PC or WSJT-X.
Source Note: Confirmed against the official site: single USB-C cable carries CAT + audio + ALC auto-leveling + SWR protection across 75+ Icom/Yaesu/Kenwood/Xiegu models.

Name: FT8CN
Rig Control Backend: built-in CAT (its own rig library) over USB-serial, Bluetooth, or WiFi/network (TCP)
How to Set CAT: In-app — pick the radio model and connection: USB serial (choose baud), Bluetooth (pair the rig, e.g. Xiegu X6200), or TCP/WiFi (host:port). Set band/frequency in-app.
Audio Device Select: Phone mic/speaker (acoustic/VOX) or a USB-C sound interface (e.g. DigiRig) via Android audio routing; audio can also run over the rig's USB.
PTT Config: PTT via CAT (preferred); or VOX when using acoustic/USB audio without CAT PTT.
ALC/Power Guidance: DATA/USB-D mode; keep audio drive low so ALC barely moves (or let CAT-set power manage it).
Companion Needed: none required; a USB-C audio interface (DigiRig) or CAT cable improves reliability over acoustic/VOX
Notes: Popular free Android FT8 app (BG7YOZ); flexible USB/Bluetooth/WiFi connectivity.

Name: ft8modem
Rig Control Backend: Command-line sound-card modem; CAT — both T/R (PTT) switching AND frequency control — is provided by the companion `ft8cat` helper driving Hamlib's rigctld. Not 'no CAT'.
How to Set CAT: No GUI. Run Hamlib rigctld for your rig; the `ft8cat` utility connects the modem to the rig's CAT for T/R switching, frequency control and logging decodes. ft8modem is launched with the mode + sound-device number; ft8report uploads spots to PSKReporter.
Audio Device Select: Command-line — pass the sound-device number to ft8modem (48 kHz device, decimated to 12 kHz for jt9).
PTT Config: PTT/T-R via CAT (ft8cat + rigctld); serial RTS/DTR or VOX also possible.
ALC/Power Guidance: USB/DATA; keep TX audio low so ALC barely moves (uses GFSK-like smoothed keying to reduce splatter).
Companion Needed: none (a soundcard/PTT interface to the rig)
Notes: Lightweight FT8 modem engine; audio in/out plus PTT keying only.
Source: KK5JY — ft8modem: a command-line software modem for FT8
Source Note: Original said 'no full CAT; only PTT port configured'. The ft8modem package (KK5JY) includes `ft8cat`, which uses Hamlib rigctld for full CAT — T/R switching AND frequency control — plus `ft8report` for PSKReporter. So it does have CAT, not just PTT.; does support full CAT via ft8cat + rigctld

Name: ft8mon
Rig Control Backend: RX-only decoder. Input is a sound card (PortAudio) OR a supported SDR — Airspy HF+ Discovery, RFspace SDR-IP/NetSDR/CloudIQ/CloudSDR, or HPSDR/ANAN-7000dle. No transceiver CAT; RTL-SDR is not among the supported devices.
How to Set CAT: n/a — no rig CAT. Tune the receiver manually, or pass the SDR device + frequency on the command line (e.g. `-card airspy ,14.074` or `-card hpsdr 192.168.x.x,14.074`).
Audio Device Select: Command-line — select the PortAudio sound-card capture device, or one of the supported SDR devices.
PTT Config: n/a — receive/decode only.
Companion Needed: none (SSB receiver audio or an RTL-SDR)
Notes: Command-line FT8 monitor/decoder (ab1hl); RX only.
Source Note: Original listed 'SDR (RTL-SDR/receiver)'. ft8mon's README documents sound card (PortAudio) + Airspy HF+ Discovery, RFspace (SDR-IP/NetSDR/CloudIQ/CloudSDR) and HPSDR/ANAN-7000dle — RTL-SDR is NOT listed. RX-only (no TX/CAT) confirmed.; SDR list is specific; RTL-SDR not supported

Name: GridTracker
Rig Control Backend: none of its own; optional Hamlib (rigctld) for tuning from the map
How to Set CAT: No native CAT — it reads/writes UDP to WSJT-X/JTDX (which owns the rig). Optionally point it at a Hamlib rigctld instance to command frequency/band from spots.
Audio Device Select: n/a — no audio (mapping/spotting/logging companion).
PTT Config: n/a — does not key the radio (WSJT-X does).
Companion Needed: needs WSJT-X/JTDX (UDP source); optional Hamlib rigctld for tuning
Notes: Map/logging/alerting companion; the digital engine handles CAT/audio/PTT.

Name: GridTracker2
Rig Control Backend: none of its own; optional Hamlib (rigctld) for tuning from the map
How to Set CAT: No native CAT — same UDP bridge to WSJT-X/JTDX; optionally command a Hamlib rigctld to tune the transceiver from spots.
Audio Device Select: n/a — no audio.
PTT Config: n/a — does not key the radio (WSJT-X does).
Companion Needed: needs WSJT-X/JTDX (UDP source); optional Hamlib rigctld for tuning
Notes: Rewritten GridTracker; same radio model — UDP companion plus optional rigctld tuning.

Name: JS8Call
Rig Control Backend: Hamlib (built-in)
How to Set CAT: File > Settings > Radio — choose Rig (Hamlib model), Serial Port, Baud, data/stop bits, handshake; set PTT method; poll ~1s; Split Operation 'Rig' or 'Fake It'.
Audio Device Select: File > Settings > Audio — Soundcard Input/Output.
PTT Config: PTT via CAT, RTS/DTR on a serial port, or VOX.
ALC/Power Guidance: DATA/USB-D; raise the Pwr slider until desired RF out with ALC barely moving.
Companion Needed: none (standalone); can share via Hamlib net
Notes: WSJT-X-derived keyboard/messaging mode (JS8).

Name: JT65-HF
Rig Control Backend: Hamlib (built-in)
How to Set CAT: Setup/Station — choose CAT: Hamlib rig model (or 'None'), COM port, baud; enable a PTT method.
Audio Device Select: Setup — select sound-card input/output devices.
PTT Config: PTT via CAT, RTS/DTR (COM), or VOX.
ALC/Power Guidance: USB/DATA; keep TX audio low so ALC barely moves.
Companion Needed: none
Notes: Legacy standalone JT65 app (W6CQZ), predecessor workflow to WSJT-X.

Name: JT65-HF HB9HQX Edition
Rig Control Backend: Hamlib (built-in, on board — no separate install); CAT tab also offers OmniRig, Ham Radio Deluxe, DXLab Commander and TRX-Manager
How to Set CAT: On the CAT tab select the backend (Disabled / OmniRig / HamLib / Ham Radio Deluxe / Commander / TRX-Manager). For Hamlib pick the serial port + rig manufacturer/model; OmniRig must be installed/configured first.
Audio Device Select: Setup — sound-card input/output devices.
PTT Config: PTT via CAT command, or COM port RTS/DTR; VOX also possible.
ALC/Power Guidance: USB/DATA; keep TX audio low so ALC barely moves.
Companion Needed: optional OmniRig for rig sharing
Notes: Feature-extended JT65-HF (band scheduler, decode tweaks) by HB9HQX.
Source Note: Confirmed and expanded: this edition's CAT tab supports not only Hamlib and OmniRig but also Ham Radio Deluxe, DXLab Commander and TRX-Manager; Hamlib 3 is bundled (no separate install). Source: SourceForge project + HB9HQX handbook.

Name: JTAlert
Rig Control Backend: none of its own for TX; uses WSJT-X's rig (Hamlib/OmniRig). Can use DXLab Commander (or OmniRig/HRD) for its own logging/frequency reads
How to Set CAT: Settings — enable the WSJT-X/JTDX UDP link; CAT relies on WSJT-X (which owns the rig). Optionally configure DXLab Commander (or OmniRig/HRD) so JTAlert can read frequency/mode for logging and trigger band/freq changes.
Audio Device Select: n/a — no audio (alerting/logging only).
PTT Config: n/a — does not key the radio (WSJT-X does).
Companion Needed: needs WSJT-X or JTDX; optional DXLab Commander/OmniRig and a logger (ACLog/HRD/Log4OM)
Notes: Callsign/alert/logging companion for WSJT-X/JTDX.

Name: JTDX
Rig Control Backend: Hamlib (built-in); also OmniRig on Windows
How to Set CAT: File > Settings > Radio — Rig (Hamlib model or OmniRig), Serial Port, Baud, data/stop/handshake, PTT method, poll 1s; Split 'Rig' or 'Fake It'.
Audio Device Select: File > Settings > Audio — Soundcard Input/Output.
PTT Config: PTT via CAT, RTS/DTR, or VOX.
ALC/Power Guidance: DATA/USB-D; raise Pwr until desired RF out with ALC barely moving.
Companion Needed: none; pairs well with JTAlert/GridTracker
Notes: WSJT-X fork optimized for FT8/JT decoding.

Name: JTDX Improved
Rig Control Backend: Hamlib (built-in); also OmniRig on Windows
How to Set CAT: File > Settings > Radio — identical to JTDX: Rig (Hamlib/OmniRig), Serial Port, Baud, data/stop/handshake, PTT method, poll 1s; Split 'Rig' or 'Fake It'.
Audio Device Select: File > Settings > Audio — Soundcard Input/Output.
PTT Config: PTT via CAT, RTS/DTR, or VOX.
ALC/Power Guidance: DATA/USB-D; raise Pwr until desired RF out with ALC barely moving.
Companion Needed: none
Notes: Community-enhanced JTDX build; radio configuration identical to JTDX.
Source: JTDX documentation (config identical for the JTDX Improved fork)
Source Note: Radio/CAT configuration is inherited unchanged from JTDX (this is a JTDX fork); verified against JTDX documentation.

Name: Linrad
Rig Control Backend: native SDR/soundcard hardware control (A/D + D/A devices, SDR/network IQ) — not a classic transceiver CAT
How to Set CAT: Configured in Linrad's own setup menus: pick the RX/TX A/D and D/A sound devices, the SDR/network IQ source, and sample rates. For a conventional rig it is the audio/IF source; frequency is set on the SDR/rig.
Audio Device Select: In Linrad's setup menus — select the A/D (input) and D/A (output) sound devices or SDR device and sample rates.
PTT Config: Primarily an RX/DSP platform; TX/PTT only in specific transceiver setups (hardware-dependent).
Companion Needed: often used as the RX front-end/network IQ source for MAP65 and other decoders
Notes: SM5BSZ advanced SDR receiver/DSP; frequently the wideband RX engine behind MAP65.

Name: MAP65
Rig Control Backend: wideband IQ input (Linrad network / SDR-IQ / soundcard IQ); controls the SDR/receiver LO — no transceiver CAT for TX
How to Set CAT: Setup — configure the IQ source (Linrad network, SDR-IQ, or soundcard IQ) and the receiver/downconverter LO frequency; MAP65 tunes within the wideband slice. TX is handed to a companion WSJT-X.
Audio Device Select: Not a normal soundcard-audio app — it ingests IQ; select the IQ input (network/soundcard) in setup.
PTT Config: n/a for MAP65 itself — TX/PTT handled by a companion WSJT-X instance.
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a (RX). For TX via WSJT-X, follow WSJT-X ALC guidance.
Companion Needed: typically Linrad (RX front-end) and WSJT-X (for TX); used for 2 m EME
Notes: K1JT wideband dual-pol JT65/Q65 EME decoder covering a whole 2 m sub-band.

Name: MSHV
Rig Control Backend: Hamlib (built-in)
How to Set CAT: Options/Configurations > Rig/CAT (Interface Control) — select the Hamlib rig model, Serial Port (CAT port), Baud, data/stop/handshake, PTT method, and poll interval; a separate port can be used for PTT.
Audio Device Select: Options > Sound Card — select input/output devices.
PTT Config: PTT via CAT, RTS/DTR (serial), or VOX.
ALC/Power Guidance: DATA/USB-D; raise TX audio/Pwr until desired RF out with ALC barely moving.
Companion Needed: none (standalone; supports multi-stream/multi-answer for DXpeditions)
Notes: LZ2HV FT8/FT4/JT/MSK144 multi-decoder; popular for DXpedition multi-answer.

Name: pico_ft8_xcvr
Rig Control Backend: none — it IS the radio (self-contained Raspberry Pi Pico FT8 transceiver)
How to Set CAT: n/a — no external CAT; band/frequency is set on-device via the Si5351 (menu/keypad).
Audio Device Select: n/a — on-board DSP generates/decodes FT8 internally; no sound card.
PTT Config: n/a — the firmware keys its own Class-E PA directly.
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a — fixed low-power (QRP) direct RF; no ALC.
Companion Needed: none (a hardware build: antenna + LPF; time is synced on the startup screen)
Notes: AA1GD standalone RP2040 FT8 rig with ST7789 LCD waterfall + 4x4 keypad; Si5351 DDS RX/TX; no PC.

Name: PropNetPSK
Rig Control Backend: CAT (serial rig control) for band/frequency selection + PC sound-card PSK31 modem
How to Set CAT: Setup — select the rig/CAT COM port and baud for frequency/mode control; configure the PSK31 propagation-beacon band/schedule so the app IDs and reports on the assigned band.
Audio Device Select: Setup — sound-card input/output for PSK31 RX/TX audio.
PTT Config: PTT via CAT, serial RTS/DTR, or VOX.
ALC/Power Guidance: USB/DATA; keep single-tone PSK audio low so ALC barely moves — overdrive causes IMD/splatter.
Companion Needed: none (part of the PropNET propagation-reporting network)
Notes: Automated PSK31 propagation beacon/reporter.
Source Note: Architecture confirmed (Windows PSK31 propagation-beacon app with sound-card modem + serial rig/CAT + PTT). The official propnet.org site blocks automated fetch (HTTP 403), so exact per-rig CAT model coverage was not re-verified.

Name: QMAP
Rig Control Backend: wideband SDR IQ input (like MAP65); no transceiver CAT of its own
How to Set CAT: Setup — configure the IQ/SDR input and LO; QMAP decodes the wideband slice. CAT/TX is handled by a linked WSJT-X.
Audio Device Select: IQ input (network/soundcard), not normal audio.
PTT Config: n/a — RX only (TX via WSJT-X).
Companion Needed: needs WSJT-X (TX + orchestration) and an SDR/Linrad IQ source; used for Q65/JT65
Notes: K1JT wideband Q65/JT65 decoder that feeds decodes to WSJT-X.

Name: ROS
Rig Control Backend: none (audio only; PTT via serial/VOX) — no CAT frequency control
How to Set CAT: n/a for CAT — set the radio frequency/mode manually; configure only the PTT COM port.
Audio Device Select: Setup — sound-card input/output devices.
PTT Config: PTT via serial COM RTS (typical) / DTR, or VOX.
ALC/Power Guidance: USB/DATA; keep TX audio low so ALC barely moves.
Companion Needed: none
Notes: EA5HVK ROS MFSK/DSSS weak-signal chat mode; audio + PTT.

Name: rpitx
Rig Control Backend: none — the Raspberry Pi GPIO IS the transmitter (direct RF synthesis)
How to Set CAT: n/a — no CAT; frequency is a command-line/software parameter to rpitx.
Audio Device Select: n/a — generates RF directly from data/audio files in software; no sound card to a rig.
PTT Config: n/a — software keys the GPIO carrier directly.
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a — raw GPIO output; no ALC (needs external filtering/attenuation).
Companion Needed: needs a band-pass/low-pass filter on GPIO18 (rich spurious output); TX only, use legally/attenuated
Notes: F5OEO general-purpose SDR transmitter on bare Pi GPIO (FT8/SSB/FM/etc.); experimental, requires filtering.

Name: rtlsdr-ft8d
Rig Control Backend: native RTL-SDR control (RX only) — tunes the dongle directly; no transceiver CAT
How to Set CAT: n/a — command-line options set the RTL-SDR frequency/gain/ppm; no rig CAT.
Audio Device Select: n/a — reads IQ from the RTL-SDR USB device (no sound card).
PTT Config: n/a — receive only.
Companion Needed: needs an RTL-SDR dongle; spots to PSKReporter
Notes: Lightweight command-line FT8 decoder direct from an RTL-SDR (Guenael).

Name: rtlsdr-wsprd
Rig Control Backend: native RTL-SDR control (RX only)
How to Set CAT: n/a — command-line options set RTL-SDR frequency/gain/ppm; no rig CAT.
Audio Device Select: n/a — IQ direct from the RTL-SDR (no sound card).
PTT Config: n/a — receive only.
Companion Needed: needs an RTL-SDR; uploads spots to WSPRnet
Notes: Standalone WSPR decoder for RTL-SDR (VA2GKA); no PC sound card needed.

Name: SDR++ Brown
Rig Control Backend: native SDR hardware control (RTL-SDR, Airspy, HackRF, SDRplay, etc.); can also run a Rigctl Server module so other apps follow its tuning
How to Set CAT: In-app — select the SDR source module and set frequency/gain; no external transceiver CAT. Enable the 'Rigctl Server' module to act as a Hamlib rigctl server for other software.
Audio Device Select: SDR++ handles IQ/demod internally; audio output is via the Sinks (audio device) menu. The Brown fork adds an FT8/decoder capability working on the IF; no separate rig soundcard needed.
PTT Config: n/a for RX; TX only on TX-capable SDRs (HackRF/Pluto) via the app.
Companion Needed: none (all-in-one SDR receiver + decoders); the 'Brown' fork adds the FT8/extra modules
Notes: Full SDR receiver with built-in FT8/decoders and a rigctl server.
Source Note: Confirmed core (native SDR source control + Rigctl Server module). Note: FT8/other decode features are additions in the 'Brown' fork (sannysanoff) on top of upstream SDR++.

Name: SDR-Control for Icom
Rig Control Backend: native Icom network control (CI-V over IP) to network-capable Icoms; supported models include IC-705, IC-7300, IC-7300 MK2, IC-7610, IC-7760, IC-9700 and IC-R8600
How to Set CAT: In-app — add the radio by IP/host with username/password; enable 'Network Control' on the radio (LAN cable for IC-7610/7760/9700/7300mk2/R8600, WiFi for IC-705). The app does CI-V control over the network — no COM/baud or Hamlib.
Audio Device Select: n/a — radio audio is streamed over the network (Icom codec); no local sound card or virtual audio cable.
PTT Config: PTT over the network CI-V (app-controlled); no external PTT wiring.
ALC/Power Guidance: DATA mode; the app sets drive over CI-V — keep TX gain so ALC stays low (managed in-app).
Companion Needed: none — needs a network-capable Icom (or IC-705 via WLAN/USB)
Notes: Marcus Roskosch iOS/macOS all-in-one remote Icom control with built-in FT8/FT4, SSB, memories; no cables/audio drivers.
Source Note: Confirmed (DL8MRE, Marcus Roskosch). Model list corrected: supported radios are IC-705, IC-7300, IC-7300 MK2, IC-7610, IC-7760, IC-9700, IC-R8600 (the original list's 'IC-905' is not among those in the user guide).

Name: SparkSDR
Rig Control Backend: native SDR (HPSDR/Hermes-Lite over Ethernet); auto-detects the radio; can also present a Hamlib NET rigctl server (port 51111)
How to Set CAT: Open the app — it auto-detects the Hermes-Lite on the network; open the radio and tune. For an external WSJT-X/JS8Call/fldigi, set rig type 'Hamlib NET rigctl' + PTT 'CAT' and point it at SparkSDR's IP:51111 (127.0.0.1:51111 if same machine); enable the rigctl server in the virtual-receiver settings.
Audio Device Select: No virtual audio needed — FT8 encode/decode is internal (multiple RX slices). For non-SparkSDR decoders, route via a Virtual Audio Cable.
PTT Config: TX/PTT handled internally by SparkSDR driving the Hermes-Lite PA; external decoders key it via 'PTT by CAT' over the rigctl server.
ALC/Power Guidance: Set TX drive/power in SparkSDR; the Hermes-Lite is a clean SDR PA — no conventional ALC.
Companion Needed: none for built-in FT8; needs a Hermes-Lite/HPSDR SDR
Notes: Multi-slice SDR that can decode many FT8 bands simultaneously; built-in digi modes (sparksdr.com).
Source Note: Confirmed against the SparkSDR manual, including the Hamlib NET rigctl server on port 51111.

Name: Spectran
Rig Control Backend: none (audio only, RX) — spectrum/waterfall analyzer
How to Set CAT: n/a — no CAT; tune the receiver manually.
Audio Device Select: Sound-card input from the receiver selected in the program.
PTT Config: n/a — receive/analysis only.
Companion Needed: none (receiver audio)
Notes: I2PHD/IK2CZL real-time audio spectrum analyzer/filter (QRSS/weak-signal viewing); RX only.

Name: Spectrum Lab
Rig Control Backend: optional CAT (serial/Hamlib) — primarily an audio DSP/analyzer; can read/set a rig's frequency
How to Set CAT: Options — optional radio control (serial/Hamlib interface) to read/set frequency; otherwise tune the rig manually. It is primarily an audio-driven analyzer/generator.
Audio Device Select: Options > Audio Settings/Devices — select sound-card input and output.
PTT Config: Optional PTT via serial COM (RTS/DTR) or via its command interpreter; often VOX; used for beacon/tone TX.
ALC/Power Guidance: For tone/beacon TX in USB, keep audio low so ALC barely moves.
Companion Needed: none
Notes: DL4YHF highly flexible audio spectrum analyzer/generator (QRSS beacons, frequency calibration, scripting).

Name: weakmon
Rig Control Backend: optional Hamlib (for band changes) — mainly RX/decode
How to Set CAT: Command-line/config — optionally set a Hamlib rig type + serial device so weakmon can change bands; otherwise tune the receiver manually.
Audio Device Select: Config/command-line — select the sound-card capture device fed from the SSB/SDR receiver.
PTT Config: n/a — receive/decode focused (no TX).
Companion Needed: none (an SSB/SDR receiver); decodes JT65/FT8/WWV etc.
Notes: ab1hl Python monitoring/decoding suite; RX-focused with optional Hamlib band-hopping.

Name: WOLF / WOLF GUI
Rig Control Backend: none (audio only; PTT via serial) — no CAT
How to Set CAT: n/a — no rig CAT; the LF/MF TX frequency is set on the transmitter/exciter manually.
Audio Device Select: Select the sound-card input/output devices (in the WOLF GUI wrapper) for RX/TX audio.
PTT Config: PTT via serial COM RTS/DTR, or VOX, to key the LF transmitter.
ALC/Power Guidance: LF exciter — set drive appropriately; n/a for conventional ALC.
Companion Needed: none (an LF transmitter/exciter)
Notes: KK7KA coherent weak-signal mode for LF (136 kHz); WOLF GUI is a front-end to the WOLF engine.
Source: WOLF (Weak-signal Operation on Ludicrously-low Frequency), Stewart Nelson KK7KA; WOLF GUI wrapper

Name: WSJT (original)
Rig Control Backend: basic/limited built-in CAT + serial-or-VOX PTT (earlier releases; later versions added Hamlib)
How to Set CAT: Setup > Options — set the CAT/PTT COM port; basic rig frequency control (limited vs modern Hamlib). The rig is often tuned manually.
Audio Device Select: Setup — select sound-card input/output devices.
PTT Config: PTT via serial COM RTS/DTR, or VOX.
ALC/Power Guidance: USB/DATA; keep TX audio low so ALC barely moves.
Companion Needed: none
Notes: Original WSJT (K1JT) meteor-scatter/EME modes FSK441, JT65, JT6M; predecessor to WSJT-X.

Name: WSJT-X
Rig Control Backend: Hamlib (built-in); OmniRig on Windows
How to Set CAT: File > Settings > Radio — Rig (Hamlib model or OmniRig), Serial Port, Baud, data/stop/handshake, PTT method, poll 1s; Split Operation 'Rig' or 'Fake It'; use Test CAT/PTT.
Audio Device Select: File > Settings > Audio — Soundcard Input/Output.
PTT Config: PTT via CAT, RTS/DTR on a serial port, or VOX.
ALC/Power Guidance: Radio in DATA/USB-D; raise the Pwr slider until desired RF out with ALC barely moving (keep ALC near zero).
Companion Needed: none; pairs with JTAlert/GridTracker/Log4OM
Notes: The reference FT8/FT4/JT/WSPR/Q65 application (K1JT et al.).

Name: WSJT-X Improved
Rig Control Backend: Hamlib (built-in); OmniRig on Windows
How to Set CAT: File > Settings > Radio — identical to WSJT-X: Rig (Hamlib/OmniRig), Serial Port, Baud, data/stop/handshake, PTT method, poll 1s; Split 'Rig' or 'Fake It'.
Audio Device Select: File > Settings > Audio — Soundcard Input/Output.
PTT Config: PTT via CAT, RTS/DTR, or VOX.
ALC/Power Guidance: DATA/USB-D; raise Pwr until desired RF out with ALC barely moving.
Companion Needed: none
Notes: DG2YCB community 'Improved' build (extra decoders/contest aids); radio config identical to WSJT-X.
Source: WSJT-X Improved (DG2YCB); radio config per WSJT-X User Guide
Source Note: Radio/CAT config inherited unchanged from WSJT-X (this is DG2YCB's WSJT-X fork); verified against the WSJT-X User Guide.

Name: WSJT-Z
Rig Control Backend: Hamlib (built-in) — WSJT-X fork (OmniRig on Windows)
How to Set CAT: File > Settings > Radio — same as WSJT-X (Rig/Hamlib model or OmniRig, Serial Port, Baud, PTT method, Split).
Audio Device Select: File > Settings > Audio — Soundcard Input/Output.
PTT Config: PTT via CAT, RTS/DTR, or VOX.
ALC/Power Guidance: DATA/USB-D; raise Pwr until desired RF out with ALC barely moving.
Companion Needed: none (adds automation/'robot' QSO features)
Notes: Automation-focused WSJT-X fork (auto-CQ, rule-based auto-sequencing); can drive the transceiver fully automatically.
Source: WSJT-Z (WSJT-X fork); radio config per WSJT-X User Guide
Source Note: Radio/CAT config inherited from WSJT-X (WSJT-Z is a WSJT-X fork); verified against the WSJT-X User Guide.

Name: WSPR (standalone)
Rig Control Backend: Hamlib (built-in) — for band hopping/scheduling
How to Set CAT: In WSPR's setup — select the CAT rig (Hamlib) + COM/baud so it can change bands on the schedule; set the band-hop table.
Audio Device Select: Setup — sound-card input/output devices.
PTT Config: PTT via CAT, serial RTS/DTR, or VOX.
ALC/Power Guidance: DATA/USB-D; keep TX audio low so ALC barely moves (WSPR is ~2 min of 100% duty — mind heat/power).
Companion Needed: none; uploads spots to WSPRnet
Notes: Original standalone WSPR beacon/monitor (K1JT).

Name: WSPR-X
Rig Control Backend: Hamlib (built-in)
How to Set CAT: Settings > Radio — Rig (Hamlib model), COM/baud, PTT method (WSJT-X-style dialog).
Audio Device Select: Settings > Audio — Soundcard Input/Output.
PTT Config: PTT via CAT, RTS/DTR, or VOX.
ALC/Power Guidance: DATA/USB-D; keep TX audio low so ALC barely moves.
Companion Needed: none; WSPRnet reporting
Notes: Experimental WSPR variant (adds WSPR-15); WSJT-X-lineage UI.

Name: wsprd
Rig Control Backend: none — pure decoder; no rig control
How to Set CAT: n/a — no CAT; it is fed a .wav/audio stream captured at the WSPR frequency (tuning done by a separate front-end).
Audio Device Select: n/a — reads a WAV file or piped stream, not a live device directly.
PTT Config: n/a — decode only.
Companion Needed: needs a capture front-end (WSJT-X/WSPR, an SDR script, or rtlsdr-wsprd) to provide audio
Notes: The core WSPR decode engine used by many front-ends (part of WSJT-X).
Source: wsprd (command-line WSPR decoder from the WSJT-X suite, K1JT)

Name: wsprdaemon (WD)
Rig Control Backend: native SDR control (KiwiSDR / RX888 / RTL-SDR) over network/USB — no transceiver CAT
How to Set CAT: No rig CAT — edit wsprdaemon.conf to list receivers (KiwiSDR host, RX888, RTL-SDR) and the bands/schedules; WD tunes each SDR.
Audio Device Select: n/a — pulls audio/IQ from the SDRs (KiwiSDR via kiwirecorder, RX888 via KA9Q-radio); no sound card.
PTT Config: n/a — receive only.
Companion Needed: needs SDR hardware (KiwiSDR/RX888/RTL-SDR); uses wsprd/jt9; uploads to WSPRnet + wsprdaemon.org
Notes: High-performance multi-band/multi-receiver WSPR/FST4W spotting server (Linux).

Name: WsprryPi
Rig Control Backend: none — the Raspberry Pi GPIO IS the transmitter
How to Set CAT: n/a — no CAT; band/frequency, callsign and power are command-line/config parameters.
Audio Device Select: n/a — RF synthesized directly on GPIO (no sound card).
PTT Config: n/a — software keys the GPIO carrier directly on the WSPR schedule.
ALC/Power Guidance: n/a — raw GPIO output (~10 dBm); requires an external low-pass filter to suppress harmonics.
Companion Needed: needs an external low-pass filter (and antenna); TX only
Notes: WSPR beacon transmitter on bare Pi GPIO; NTP-synced timing.

Name: WSQCall / WSQ2
Rig Control Backend: WSQ2/WSQ: audio only with serial PTT (no CAT). WSQCall (the newer program): adds CAT transceiver control in addition to serial PTT and VOX.
How to Set CAT: WSQ2/WSQ: no CAT — set the LF/MF frequency on the rig/exciter manually and configure only the serial PTT port. WSQCall: offers CAT rig control (plus PTT via a serial port's RTS/DTR).
Audio Device Select: Tools > Audio devices — select sound-card input/output (quit and restart for changes to stick).
PTT Config: PTT via serial RTS/DTR, or VOX (transmit tone stops on receive). WSQCall additionally supports CAT.
ALC/Power Guidance: USB mode; keep TX audio low so ALC barely moves (multi-tone IFK — avoid overdrive/IMD).
Companion Needed: none (an LF/MF-capable rig or transverter)
Notes: ZL2AFP 50 Hz-wide multi-tone chat mode for LF/MF (630/2200 m); DL4YHF extended variant; FSQ is its faster HF derivative.
Source Note: Original said the combined entry has 'no CAT'. Correct for WSQ2/WSQ, but WSQCall explicitly adds CAT transceiver control alongside serial RTS/DTR PTT and VOX (per ZL2AFP/ZL1BPU documentation).; WSQCall adds CAT; WSQ2 does not

Name: AirMail
Rig Control Backend: built-in CAT (rig-control table for many HF SSB rigs) via serial + SCS PACTOR (PTC) modem
How to Set CAT: Tools > Options > Connection / Ham Rig Control — pick the transceiver model, COM port, baud; configure the PTC/PACTOR modem port.
Audio Device Select: For sound-card modes select the audio device; PACTOR audio is handled by the SCS modem hardware.
PTT Config: PTT via the PACTOR modem, or via CAT/serial RTS/DTR for sound-card modes.
ALC/Power Guidance: PACTOR/SSB — set drive so ALC barely moves (PACTOR is high-duty; avoid overdrive/splatter).
Companion Needed: typically an SCS PTC PACTOR modem; used with SailMail/Winlink
Notes: Long-standing HF email client (Jim Corenman) for marine (SailMail) and ham (Winlink).

Name: Flarq
Rig Control Backend: via fldigi — no CAT of its own; fldigi owns the radio (flrig/Hamlib/RigCAT)
How to Set CAT: No rig CAT in Flarq — configure the radio in fldigi (Configure > Rig Control); Flarq connects to fldigi over its ARQ/XML-RPC link.
Audio Device Select: n/a in Flarq — audio is fldigi's soundcard (Configure > Soundcard > Devices).
PTT Config: n/a in Flarq — PTT is fldigi's (CAT/RTS/DTR/flrig).
ALC/Power Guidance: Follow fldigi/mode ALC guidance (keep TX audio low so ALC barely moves).
Companion Needed: needs fldigi (the on-air modem/rig engine)
Notes: ARQ email/file-transfer companion layered on fldigi.
Source: Flarq (part of the fldigi suite, W1HKJ)

Name: ICOM Marine Radio Control Program
Rig Control Backend: native Icom CI-V (serial) — control only (frequency/mode/channels) for Icom marine HF SSB (M700Pro/M710/M710RT/M802)
How to Set CAT: In the program — select the COM port and the CI-V address/baud for the Icom marine SSB; set frequency/mode/DSC channels. Used alongside AirMail/Winlink to steer the marine SSB.
Audio Device Select: n/a — control program only; no audio/modem.
PTT Config: n/a — control only (no data PTT).
Companion Needed: none (an Icom marine SSB radio); a separate PACTOR/soundcard modem would carry data
Notes: Manufacturer control/programming utility for Icom marine SSB sets; CAT/CI-V only.
Source: Winlink — ICOM Marine Radio Control Program
Source Note: Confirmed as the Winlink-hosted (public-domain) Icom marine control program that supports M700Pro/M710/M710RT/M802 HF SSB radios via CI-V.

Name: JNOS 2.0
Rig Control Backend: KISS/AX.25 TNC (hardware TNC or soundmodem) — not CAT
How to Set CAT: No rig CAT — configure the TNC/KISS interface in jnos autoexec.nos (attach a KISS port to the TNC or a Direwolf pseudo-tty); the radio is tuned/fixed manually.
Audio Device Select: Audio via the soundmodem (Direwolf/soundmodem) attached over KISS, not JNOS directly.
PTT Config: PTT keyed by the TNC/soundmodem (hardware line, RTS, GPIO).
ALC/Power Guidance: FM packet — set deviation via soundcard level, not ALC (n/a).
Companion Needed: needs a TNC or Direwolf/soundmodem for the RF link
Notes: Classic JNOS TCP/IP + AX.25 packet BBS/mail server; radio via KISS TNC.

Name: LinBPQ / BPQ32
Rig Control Backend: multi — KISS/JKISS/hardware TNCs and sound modems; also drives PACTOR/WINMOR/ARDOP/VARA over TCP; optional rig CAT via Hamlib/rigctld per port
How to Set CAT: In bpq32.cfg — define PORTs: KISS TNC (COM/host), soundmodem, ARDOP/VARA (TCP host:port), or PACTOR (SCS). For CAT frequency control, attach a rig-control (Hamlib) definition to the port.
Audio Device Select: Audio is handled by the attached modem (Direwolf/UZ7HO/VARA/ARDOP), configured in that modem.
PTT Config: PTT via the TNC/soundmodem/modem (RTS/CAT/VOX) as configured per port.
ALC/Power Guidance: For HF ARDOP/VARA/PACTOR keep drive so ALC barely moves; FM packet uses deviation not ALC.
Companion Needed: needs modems/TNCs (Direwolf, VARA, ARDOP, SCS PACTOR) per port
Notes: G8BPQ packet-switch/node stack (BBS, node, chat, Winlink RMS gateway) tying many RF modems together.

Name: Outpost Packet Message Manager
Rig Control Backend: AX.25 TNC / KISS / AGWPE — not CAT
How to Set CAT: Setup > TNC — choose the TNC type (KISS, native/command TNC, or AGWPE) and its COM port/host; radio frequency set manually.
Audio Device Select: Audio via the soundmodem/AGWPE (Direwolf/UZ7HO/AGW), not Outpost directly.
PTT Config: PTT keyed by the TNC/soundmodem (hardware line/RTS/VOX).
ALC/Power Guidance: FM packet — deviation via soundcard level, not ALC (n/a).
Companion Needed: needs a TNC or AGWPE/soundmodem for the RF link
Notes: KN6PE store-and-forward packet messaging for ARES/RACES; talks to any AX.25 TNC/AGWPE.

Name: Paclink
Rig Control Backend: packet TNC/soundmodem (+ HF modems); optional rig CAT for HF
How to Set CAT: Configure the TNC/modem port (KISS/AGWPE) and, for HF, the modem + rig control (COM/CAT); frequency per channel list.
Audio Device Select: Audio via the soundmodem/HF modem, configured there.
PTT Config: PTT via TNC/soundmodem, or CAT/serial for HF.
ALC/Power Guidance: HF — keep drive so ALC barely moves; FM packet uses deviation (n/a for ALC).
Companion Needed: needs a TNC/soundmodem (and HF modem); a Winlink 'Post Office' client
Notes: Older Winlink post-office/gateway client (Windows); largely superseded by newer tools.

Name: Paclink-unix
Rig Control Backend: AX.25/KISS packet TNC, ARDOP, SCS PACTOR; rig CAT via Hamlib for HF
How to Set CAT: Config files — set the AX.25 port (kissattach/Direwolf) for packet, or ARDOP/PACTOR for HF; use Hamlib (rigctld) for frequency/PTT on HF.
Audio Device Select: Audio via the soundmodem (Direwolf) / ARDOP, configured there; no direct soundcard UI.
PTT Config: PTT via the TNC/soundmodem, or Hamlib (rigctld) for HF modes.
ALC/Power Guidance: HF ARDOP/PACTOR — keep drive so ALC barely moves.
Companion Needed: needs AX.25 tools/Direwolf, ARDOP (ardopc) or SCS PACTOR; a Linux Winlink client
Notes: Unix/Linux Winlink client; command-line/cron-friendly.

Name: Pat
Rig Control Backend: Hamlib (rigctld or direct serial/USB) for CAT freq/PTT; transports: ARDOP (built-in), VARA HF/FM, PACTOR, AX.25/KISS, and telnet
How to Set CAT: config.json (or web UI) — set 'hamlib_rigs' with a rigctld network address (or direct serial/USB) + model per transport; define ardop/varahf/varafm addresses (TCP host:port) and ax25/serial-tnc for packet.
Audio Device Select: n/a in Pat — audio belongs to the external modem (ARDOP/VARA/Direwolf); Pat only talks TCP/KISS to them.
PTT Config: PTT via Hamlib rigctld (CAT), or the modem's own PTT (VARA/ARDOP RTS/CAT), or a KISS TNC.
ALC/Power Guidance: Set in the rig/modem — keep HF drive so ALC barely moves; Pat itself doesn't set audio level.
Companion Needed: needs a modem: ARDOP (ardopc), VARA HF/FM, or a KISS packet TNC (Direwolf); + rigctld for CAT
Notes: Popular Go-based Winlink client (CLI + web UI, LA5NTA); ties Hamlib + external modems together.
Source Note: Confirmed and expanded: Pat also supports PACTOR as a transport (not only ARDOP/VARA/AX.25/telnet), and rig control can be rigctld OR a direct serial/USB Hamlib connection.

Name: PSKmail
Rig Control Backend: via fldigi — fldigi owns the rig (flrig/Hamlib/RigCAT)
How to Set CAT: No rig CAT in PSKmail — configure the radio in fldigi (Configure > Rig Control); PSKmail drives fldigi over its XML-RPC/ARQ interface.
Audio Device Select: n/a in PSKmail — audio is fldigi's soundcard (Configure > Soundcard).
PTT Config: n/a in PSKmail — PTT is fldigi's (CAT/RTS/DTR/flrig).
ALC/Power Guidance: Follow fldigi/mode ALC (keep TX audio low so ALC barely moves).
Companion Needed: needs fldigi (the on-air modem/rig control)
Notes: PSK/MFSK ARQ email-over-radio system (client/server) layered on fldigi.

Name: RadioMail
Rig Control Backend: Modem-driven: VARA HF/FM (via a VARA host on a separate computer, over WiFi/network), a BUILT-IN ARDOP modem (using a USB audio interface — Digirig Lite/AIOC/IC-705), or KISS packet (Mobilinkd / Dire Wolf, over Bluetooth/TCP). SCS PACTOR is NOT supported. Rig control (frequency/PTT) via Hamlib rigctld.
How to Set CAT: In-app Settings — add a connection: a VARA modem host (IP:port over WiFi; newer versions can remote-start VARA via 'Varanny'), the built-in ARDOP modem with a compatible USB audio interface, or a KISS packet TNC (Bluetooth). Optional radio control via a rigctld address for PTT/frequency.
Audio Device Select: VARA audio lives on the VARA host; for the BUILT-IN ARDOP modem the iPhone connects to the rig via a USB audio interface (Digirig Lite / AIOC / IC-705).
PTT Config: PTT via the VARA host, the KISS TNC, or Hamlib rigctld (CAT); the built-in ARDOP keys through the interface/CAT.
ALC/Power Guidance: Set on the rig/VARA host — keep HF drive so ALC barely moves.
Companion Needed: needs a VARA modem host (over WiFi) or a Bluetooth/USB PACTOR/KISS TNC
Notes: iOS Winlink client for portable HF/VHF email; no PC needed if paired with a networked VARA or Bluetooth modem.
Source Note: Original listed 'SCS PACTOR (Bluetooth/USB)' and said 'no direct soundcard link'. RadioMail does NOT support SCS PACTOR; it offers VARA (external host), a BUILT-IN ARDOP modem, and KISS packet. The built-in ARDOP DOES use a direct USB audio interface (Digirig Lite/AIOC/IC-705), and rig control is via Hamlib rigctld. Source: winlink.org RadioMail + radiomail.app.; built-in ARDOP; no PACTOR; direct USB audio possible; rigctld

Name: RMS Packet
Rig Control Backend: KISS TNC / soundmodem — not CAT (VHF/UHF packet Winlink gateway)
How to Set CAT: Site Properties — configure the TNC (KISS COM port or AGWPE/soundmodem host) and the channel frequency; the gateway serves packet Winlink.
Audio Device Select: Audio via the soundmodem (UZ7HO/Direwolf/AGWPE), configured there.
PTT Config: PTT via the TNC/soundmodem (hardware line/RTS/VOX).
ALC/Power Guidance: FM packet — set deviation via soundcard level, not ALC (n/a).
Companion Needed: needs a KISS TNC or AGWPE/UZ7HO soundmodem; part of a Winlink RMS gateway station
Notes: Sysop-side VHF/UHF packet Winlink gateway (CMS-connected).

Name: RMS Relay
Rig Control Backend: none directly — a message relay/queue; the RF link is owned by RMS Packet / RMS Trimode
How to Set CAT: No rig config in RMS Relay — radio/CAT/PTT are set in the paired RMS Packet or RMS Trimode; RMS Relay sits between them and the CMS/local delivery.
Audio Device Select: n/a — no audio (relay/queue service).
PTT Config: n/a — handled by RMS Packet/Trimode.
Companion Needed: needs RMS Packet and/or RMS Trimode (the actual RF channels)
Notes: Caching/relay layer for a multi-channel RMS gateway (offline queue, hybrid network).

Name: RMS Trimode
Rig Control Backend: CAT rig control (rig table/serial) + SCS PACTOR modem + soundcard modems (ARDOP/WINMOR/VARA)
How to Set CAT: Site Properties / Radio — select the transceiver model, COM port, baud, PTT method; configure the PACTOR (SCS) and/or soundcard modem (ARDOP/VARA).
Audio Device Select: For soundcard modes select the audio device (rig's USB codec); PACTOR audio is on the SCS modem.
PTT Config: PTT via CAT/serial RTS/DTR, or via the PACTOR modem.
ALC/Power Guidance: HF PACTOR/ARDOP/VARA — keep drive so ALC barely moves (high duty; avoid splatter).
Companion Needed: SCS PACTOR modem (for PACTOR) and/or soundcard modems; a Winlink HF RMS gateway
Notes: Multi-mode HF (+packet) Winlink gateway sysop software.

Name: SailMail (AirMail)
Rig Control Backend: same as AirMail — built-in CAT (rig table) via serial + SCS PACTOR (PTC) modem
How to Set CAT: AirMail — Tools > Options > Connection / Rig Control: select the SSB transceiver, COM port, baud; configure the PTC/PACTOR modem.
Audio Device Select: PACTOR audio via the SCS modem; soundcard modes select the audio device.
PTT Config: PTT via the PACTOR modem, or CAT/serial RTS/DTR.
ALC/Power Guidance: PACTOR/SSB — set drive so ALC barely moves.
Companion Needed: SCS PACTOR modem; SailMail membership + the AirMail client
Notes: SailMail is the marine HF-email network; AirMail is the client used to access it — same radio setup as AirMail.
Source: SailMail (uses the AirMail client, Jim Corenman) — sailmail.com

Name: SCS PACTOR software (PTC / P4dragon tools & WA8DED terminal)
Rig Control Backend: SCS hardware PACTOR modem (PTC-II/P4dragon) over USB/serial — the modem does CAT/PTT
How to Set CAT: In the terminal/tools — set the modem's COM port/baud; the PTC modem can be told the rig type so it does CAT frequency control and keys PTT (or PTT via the modem's PTT line).
Audio Device Select: n/a — audio/DSP is inside the SCS modem hardware (not a PC soundcard).
PTT Config: PTT via the SCS modem's PTT output (and/or rig CAT through the modem).
ALC/Power Guidance: PACTOR — set the modem's audio drive so the rig's ALC barely moves (avoid overdrive).
Companion Needed: needs an SCS PTC-II/P4dragon PACTOR modem; used with WA8DED/host-mode terminals
Notes: Firmware/terminal utilities for SCS PACTOR modems; WA8DED host mode.

Name: SCS Trimode
Rig Control Backend: SCS hardware PACTOR/ARDOP/VARA modem — the modem owns audio; CAT/PTT via the modem
How to Set CAT: In SCS Trimode — set the modem COM port; configure rig control so the modem/software commands the transceiver (frequency) and keys PTT.
Audio Device Select: n/a — audio is inside the SCS modem (no PC soundcard).
PTT Config: PTT via the SCS modem PTT line (and rig CAT through the modem).
ALC/Power Guidance: Set modem drive so the rig's ALC barely moves.
Companion Needed: needs an SCS Dragon/PTC modem; presents PACTOR/ARDOP/VARA as a virtual TNC to Winlink
Notes: SCS tri-mode (PACTOR/ARDOP/VARA) host software; a TCP TNC for Winlink Express/Pat.

Name: Telpac
Rig Control Backend: AX.25 packet TNC (KISS/AGWPE) — not CAT
How to Set CAT: No rig CAT — configure the TNC/AGWPE port and packet frequency; Telpac gateways VHF packet users into the Winlink network.
Audio Device Select: Audio via the soundmodem/AGWPE, configured there.
PTT Config: PTT via the TNC/soundmodem (hardware/RTS/VOX).
ALC/Power Guidance: FM packet — deviation via soundcard level, not ALC (n/a).
Companion Needed: needs a packet TNC/AGWPE; legacy Winlink Telpac/gateway node
Notes: Older Winlink VHF packet gateway node software (predecessor to RMS Packet).

Name: Winlink Express (RMS Express)
Rig Control Backend: per-session CAT (its own rig-control table) via serial/OmniRig; transport modem varies by session (ARDOP/WINMOR/VARA/Packet/PACTOR)
How to Set CAT: Open a session (VARA HF / VARA FM / Packet / ARDOP / Pactor) > Settings — Radio Setup: select the rig model, COM port, baud, data bits, PTT (via CAT/RTS/DTR or external); each session remembers its own radio + modem.
Audio Device Select: In each session's modem (VARA/ARDOP/soundmodem) select the capture/playback sound device (the rig's USB codec).
PTT Config: PTT via CAT/serial RTS/DTR set in Radio Setup, or by the modem/PACTOR hardware.
ALC/Power Guidance: HF (ARDOP/VARA/PACTOR) DATA mode — set the modem 'Drive Level'/soundcard TX so the rig's ALC barely moves.
Companion Needed: bundled ARDOP/WINMOR/Packet; needs VARA HF/FM (separate install), UZ7HO/Direwolf for packet, or an SCS PACTOR modem depending on session
Notes: The standard Windows Winlink client; go-to for Winlink email over many transports.

Name: WoAD (Winlink on Android)
Rig Control Backend: external modem-driven — VARA HF/FM (TCP/WiFi), ARDOP (TCP), or KISS packet TNC (Bluetooth/USB/TCP); no direct CAT
How to Set CAT: In WoAD Settings > Transport — add the modem: VARA/ARDOP host IP:port (over WiFi/TCP), or a Bluetooth/USB KISS TNC (e.g. Mobilinkd); the rig frequency is set manually (or via the modem host).
Audio Device Select: n/a — audio is in the external modem (VARA host / KISS TNC), not the phone.
PTT Config: PTT handled by the paired modem/TNC (VARA host PTT, or the KISS TNC's PTT line/Bluetooth).
ALC/Power Guidance: Set on the rig/VARA host — keep HF drive so ALC barely moves.
Companion Needed: needs a VARA HF/FM host, an ARDOP host, or a KISS packet TNC (e.g. Mobilinkd)
Notes: Full Winlink client for Android; pairs with a hardware/soft TNC or a networked VARA/ARDOP modem.
Source: WoAD — Winlink on Android user guide (F5VH)
